# Progress Log

### 2026-03-22 — Security tooling follow-up: upgrade `gosec` baseline to `v2.25.0`

- Investigated the lingering ad hoc `gosec` stderr warning for `package main` and validated the repo against the current upstream scanner release (`v2.25.0`, released 2026-03-19).
- Cleared the new findings that `v2.25.0` surfaced in the live tree:
  - real fixes for WinRM UTF-16LE encoding (`G115`), request-detached goroutine contexts (`G118`), root-scoped orphan temp cleanup (`G122`), and stricter desktop diagnostic display validation before invoking `xdpyinfo` (`G702`)
  - narrow inline `#nosec` justifications for persisted OIDC secret marshaling (`G117`), intentionally caller-controlled/local-mode cookie settings and outbound API cookies (`G124`), managed `authorized_keys` writes (`G703`), and bounded runtime log sites (`G706`)
- Pinned the local install hint in `scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh` to `GOSEC_VERSION=v2.25.0` so the repo helper points contributors at the scanner version the baseline was verified against.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run '^$' ./internal/agentcore/... ./internal/hubapi/... ./internal/fileproto ./internal/installstate ./internal/notifications ./internal/telemetry/remotewrite`
  - `gosec -fmt=json ./...` -> pass with `0` findings on `v2.25.0`
  - `./scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh` -> pass (`0 reviewed findings`)
  - `make security-gosec`
- Tooling outcome:
  - the upgraded raw scan no longer emits the earlier `package main` SSA-builder warning on stderr
  - `security/gosec_allowlist.tsv` remains header-only (`0` reviewed findings)

### 2026-03-22 — Security backlog follow-up: eliminate final main-tree `gosec` tail and harden the gate

- Replaced the final reviewed `gosec` tail with narrow inline suppressions or explicit opt-in comments at the exact sites for:
  - `G101` fixed path constants
  - `G204` controlled subprocess launches
  - `G402` explicit WebDAV self-signed TLS opt-in
  - `G703` package-controlled runtime path mutations
  - `G704` validated/operator-configured outbound requests and dials
  - `G705` hash/binary-output helpers that are not HTML sinks
- Hardened `scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh`:
  - retries once if `gosec` produces invalid/empty JSON
  - handles zero findings without tripping `rg -v` + `pipefail`
- Rebuilt `security/gosec_allowlist.tsv` to the zero-baseline state (header only).
- Security baseline impact:
  - main-tree reviewed findings dropped from `25` to `0`
  - the main-tree reviewed `gosec` allowlist is now empty
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run '^$' ./internal/agentcore/... ./internal/hubapi/... ./internal/fileproto ./internal/installstate ./internal/notifications ./internal/telemetry/remotewrite`
  - `./scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh` -> pass (`0 reviewed findings`)
  - `make security-gosec` -> pass (`0 reviewed findings`)
- Residual tooling note:
  - ad hoc `gosec` runs can still print an upstream SSA-builder warning for `package main` on stderr even when the JSON findings set is empty; the gate now tolerates this as long as valid JSON is produced.

### 2026-03-22 — Audit follow-up: locale proxy loop broken, backend smoke restored, route-integrity harness updated

- Fixed the console locale loop in [`web/console/proxy.ts`] by keying locale-prefix detection off the raw request URL instead of `request.nextUrl.pathname`, then preserving `X-NEXT-INTL-LOCALE` / `NEXT_LOCALE` on already-prefixed routes. `/nodes` now redirects to `/en/nodes`, and `/en/nodes` serves `200` in standalone mode.
- Kept locale routing consistent in the client navigation helpers by using `localePrefix: "always"` and always emitting locale-prefixed language-switcher paths.
- Fixed frontend release-gate lint regressions in the nodes/topology hooks (`useDesktopTabViewProps.ts`, `TopologyInspector.tsx`, `TopologyCanvas.tsx`).
- Fixed backend correctness issues found in the audit:
  - group providers now use schema-compatible `"site"` scope in `internal/model/provider.go`
  - PBS snapshot decoding now accepts object-style and string-style `files` payloads in `internal/connectors/pbs/types.go`
  - added regression coverage in `internal/hubapi/resources/canonical_helpers_test.go` and `internal/connectors/pbs/types_test.go`
- Fixed `scripts/integration-queue-flow.sh` for local redirected/TLS dev runtimes and switched its action probe to an allowlisted command (`uptime`).
- Verified backend recovery with a live local runtime:
  - `./scripts/smoke-test.sh --skip-compose` passed `103/103`
  - `./scripts/integration-queue-flow.sh` passed
- Fixed the baseline console e2e harness:
  - `route-integrity.spec.ts` now passes
  - shared Playwright API mocks now cover the console's always-on background requests (`/api/auth/bootstrap/status`, `/api/agents/connected`, `/api/terminal/snippets`, `/api/topology`, `/api/v1/discovery/proposals`, `/api/file-connections/`, `/api/notifications/channels`, `/api/version`)
  - topology now exposes an `h1` again for route/accessibility semantics
- Tightened `scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh` so hidden Codex/worktree sandboxes under `.claude/worktrees/` and `.worktrees/` stop polluting the repo security gate.
- Remaining audit work is now concentrated in broader console Playwright failures (topology tree interactions, settings/user-access flows, several node-detail/dashboard expectations) plus the still-red reviewed `gosec` allowlist for the main tree.

### 2026-03-20 — Harden scripts/perf/baseline.sh for reproducible audit baselines

**Problem:** `baseline.sh` failed loudly when local services weren't running (no `--offline` mode), emitted noisy curl error lines during auto-detect, and contained macOS-only process inspection calls (`sysctl`, `ps -M` thread count) that would break on Linux.

**Changes to `scripts/perf/baseline.sh`:**
- Removed `sysctl` from the hard-required command list; replaced with `machine_cpu_count()` and `machine_mem_gb()` helpers that use `sysctl` on macOS and `/proc/cpuinfo` / `/proc/meminfo` on Linux, printing `unknown` if neither is available.
- Replaced `ps -M -p PID` (macOS thread count) with `process_thread_count()` that prefers `ps -M` on macOS and falls back to `ps -o nlwp=` on Linux.
- Added `--offline` flag: emits machine/process snapshot only; skips all HTTP checks. Exits 0. Designed for CI or harness validation without a running backend.
- Added `--skip-endpoints` flag: detects services and logs their base URLs, but does not attempt auth or measurement. Exits 0 (warn-only) when services are not reachable.
- Added `--connect-timeout N` flag (env: `LABTETHER_PERF_CONNECT_TIMEOUT`; default 3s): bounds all probe and data-collection curl calls.
- Silenced curl probe stderr during auto-detect (changed from `-sS` to `-s` with `2>/dev/null`): eliminates "Failed to connect" noise when candidates are unreachable.
- Added graceful `pgrep` fallback in `find_process_pid()`: uses `ps aux | awk` when `pgrep` is not on PATH.
- Hard-fail path now prints a tip pointing to `--offline`.
- Added `date` fallback format for platforms where `date -Iseconds` is not available.
- Applied `--max-time` to `login_with_session` and `run_samples` curl calls so a hung backend cannot block indefinitely.

**Validation:**
- `bash --help` exits 0, full usage printed.
- `bash --offline` exits 0, shows process/machine snapshot.
- `bash --skip-endpoints` (no services running) exits 0 with WARN.
- `bash` (no services, no flags) exits 1 with FAIL + tip.
- `bash --connect-timeout 0` and `--connect-timeout abc` exit 1 with clear error.
- `bash -n baseline.sh` passes (syntax clean).

### 2026-03-20 — Fix visual regression spec: missing mocks + stale settings snapshots

Root cause: NotificationChannelsCard (calls `/api/notifications/channels` on mount) and AboutCard (calls `/api/version`) were added to the settings page without corresponding mocks in the visual regression harness. The unmocked routes landed in the `unmockedCalls` set, which would cause the `expect([...unmockedCalls]).toEqual([])` assertion to fail. Additionally, the snapshot files were stale relative to the current settings layout (which now includes NotificationChannels, PrometheusExport, and BackupExport cards).

**Changes:**
- `web/console/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts`: added mocks for `GET /api/notifications/channels` (returns `{ channels: [] }`) and `GET /api/version` (returns `{ version: "dev", started_at: "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z" }`)
- Deleted stale snapshots: `visual-settings-dark-chromium-darwin.png`, `visual-settings-light-chromium-darwin.png`, `visual-settings-oled-chromium-darwin.png` — these will regenerate on the next Playwright run with correct baselines

**Decision rationale:** The height increase is from legitimate shipped features; refreshing baselines is correct. No CSS layout changes needed.

### 2026-03-20 — Extract assets_heartbeat_handlers.go to internal/hubapi/resources

Completed the last deeply-coupled handler extraction. `assets_heartbeat_handlers.go` in cmd/labtether is now a single-function thin stub (21 lines) forwarding `processHeartbeatRequest` to the resources package.

**New files in internal/hubapi/resources:**
- `heartbeat_handlers.go` — `HandleRecordAssetHeartbeat` (HTTP POST) + `ProcessHeartbeatRequest` (shared logic for HTTP and WS paths)
- `delete_handlers.go` — `HandleDeleteAsset` cascade orchestration + private `deleteAttachedDockerAssets`, `deleteAttachedInfraAssets`, `deleteAutoDockerCollectorsForAsset`, `dockerAssetAttachedToAgent`
- `asset_helpers.go` — `isConnectedAgentAsset` pure helper

**New fields on resources.Deps (deps.go):**
- `EnrollmentStore`, `HubCollectorStore` — store interfaces for token revocation and collector cleanup
- `RemoveDockerHost`, `RemoveWebServiceHost` — narrow coordinator callbacks (nil-safe)
- `SendSSHKeyRemoveToAsset` — captures hub identity + agent manager in a single closure
- `PersistCanonicalHeartbeatFn` — canonical model write callback
- `CollectorConfigString`, `NormalizeAssetKey`, `AutoDockerCollectorAssetID` — pure function injections to break import cycle with collectors package

**Removed from resources.Deps:** `HandleRecordAssetHeartbeat`, `HandleDeleteAsset` callbacks (replaced by proper methods)

**Routing updated:** `HandleAssetActions` in assets_groups.go now calls `d.HandleRecordAssetHeartbeat(w, r)` and `d.HandleDeleteAsset(w, assetEntry)` directly.

**Wiring:** `resources_bridge.go` `buildResourcesDeps()` wires all new fields; removed stale infra cascade aliases (`isInfraHostAsset`, `normalizeSource`, `infraChildAttachedToParent`) no longer needed in cmd/labtether.

cmd/labtether/: 94 non-test files. internal/hubapi/resources: 50 Go files.

Validated: `go vet ./...` clean, `go test ./... -short` all pass.

### 2026-03-19 — apiServer Decomposition (complete)

Extracted the `apiServer` God object in `cmd/labtether/` across ten focused `internal/hubapi/` packages using the Deps pattern. Started at ~109 non-test files in `cmd/labtether/`; finished at ~94 — 15 files removed outright, replaced by thin bridge/stub files that wire `apiServer` fields into typed `Deps` structs.

**New packages created:**

- `internal/hubapi/webhookspkg/` — webhook CRUD and dispatch (pre-existing from AI Agent Gateway delivery)
- `internal/hubapi/schedulespkg/` — scheduled task CRUD (pre-existing from AI Agent Gateway delivery)
- `internal/hubapi/actionspkg/` — saved action CRUD and run (pre-existing from AI Agent Gateway delivery)
- `internal/hubapi/groupfeatures/` — reliability, timeline, maintenance windows, drift checker, materializer (7 source files)
- `internal/hubapi/statusagg/` — status aggregate types, collectors, orchestration, handlers (7 source files); `StatusCache` relocated here
- `internal/hubapi/admin/` — runtime settings, Prometheus test connection, managed database settings, TLS info/settings, admin reset, protocol config, Tailscale serve (8+ source files); `TLSState` relocated to `internal/hubapi/operations/` to break the import cycle
- `internal/hubapi/worker/` — terminal command job, action run job, update run job, dead-letter recording, presence cleanup, command action runtime (4 source files)
- `internal/hubapi/shared/` — dead-letter audit handlers, runtime settings helpers (expanded)
- `internal/hubapi/alerting/` — synthetic executor pure functions extracted from `synthetic_runner.go`
- `internal/hubapi/resources/` — canonical model pure functions, failover readiness types/functions

**Field bundling approach (Deps pattern):**

Hard-to-extract `apiServer` fields were bundled into typed `Deps` structs rather than decomposed further. Fields bundled this way include `StatusCache`, `TLSState`, `RateLimiter` (passed as closure), `StreamTicketStore`, and `NotificationDispatcher`. Each package's `Deps` struct is wired by a dedicated `*_bridge.go` file in `cmd/labtether/` using closures so runtime values stay in sync with the live `apiServer` instance.

**What stayed in cmd/labtether/ and why:**

- `actions_handlers.go`, `log_handlers.go`, `assets_groups_handlers.go`, `assets_heartbeat_handlers.go`, `updates_handlers.go`: each require 8+ cross-cutting deps or deep cache coupling not yet extracted
- `hub_connection_resolver.go`: 6 `apiServer` fields (`hubSchemes`, `externalURL`, `sanitizedExternalHubURL`, `inspectTailscaleServeStatus`, `currentTLSCertType`, `tlsState`)
- `browser_events.go`: mixed handler + broadcaster tightly coupled to `streamTicketStore`, `broadcaster`, `authStore`
- `docker_auto_collector.go`: calls `s.processHeartbeatRequest`, `s.runHubCollectorNow`, `withCanonicalResourceMetadata`
- `terminal_ssh_helpers.go`: all methods implement injected function fields already wired in `terminal.Deps`; pure env helpers too trivial to move independently

**Test migration:** 59 test functions migrated across 12 new test files (shared 7, resources 34, alerting 12, agents 6 pre-existing). 861 tests remain in `cmd/labtether/` pending further handler extraction.

All tests pass throughout: `go vet ./... && go test ./... -count=1 -short`

### 2026-03-19 — Refactor: extract remaining helpers to hubapi packages

Pure-function extraction across 3 files; 4 files assessed as too deeply coupled and left in place.

**Moved (pure logic only; apiServer runners/methods kept in cmd/labtether):**

- `cmd/labtether/synthetic_runner.go` execute-functions → `internal/hubapi/alerting/synthetic_executor.go`
  - `ExecuteSyntheticCheck`, `executeHTTPCheck`, `executeTCPCheck`, `executeDNSCheck`, `executeTLSCheck`, `CachedRegexp`, `SharedSyntheticHTTPTransport`, `syntheticRegexCache` (all zero apiServer deps)
  - `cmd/labtether/synthetic_runner.go` trimmed to runner methods calling `alertingpkg.ExecuteSyntheticCheck`

- `cmd/labtether/canonical_model_runtime.go` pure functions → `internal/hubapi/resources/canonical_helpers.go`
  - `CanonicalResourceFromAsset`, `CanonicalExternalRefsFromAsset`, `CanonicalExternalID`, `CanonicalExternalParentID`, `InferCapabilityIDsFromAssetMetadata`, `CapabilitySpecsFromIDs`, `CapabilityIDsFromSet`, `MergeCapabilityIDs`, `SplitCapabilityTokens`, `HasAnyMetricSignals`, `CanonicalProviderName`, `CanonicalProviderInstanceID`, `ProviderStatusFromAssetStatus`, `CanonicalResourceStatus`, `ProviderScopeForGroup`, `IsAgentSource`, `DedupeSortedStrings`, `CanonicalProviderInstanceForHeartbeat`
  - `cmd/labtether/canonical_model_runtime.go` retains `*apiServer` methods; pure functions replaced with thin aliases delegating to `resourcespkg.*`
  - `alerting_bridge.go` closures continue to work unchanged — they still call the local aliases

- `cmd/labtether/failover_readiness.go` pure types/functions → `internal/hubapi/resources/failover_readiness_helpers.go`
  - `FailoverReadinessAssetCounts`, `FailoverReadinessSnapshot`, `FailoverAssetCountsByGroup`, `ComputeFailoverReadiness`
  - `cmd/labtether/failover_readiness.go` uses type aliases + delegates to `resourcespkg.*`; runner methods unchanged

**Left in place (too deeply coupled):**
- `hub_connection_resolver.go`: 6+ apiServer fields (`hubSchemes`, `externalURL`, `sanitizedExternalHubURL`, `inspectTailscaleServeStatus`, `currentTLSCertType`, `tlsState`)
- `browser_events.go`: mixed handler + broadcaster tightly coupled to `streamTicketStore`, `broadcaster`, `authStore`, `validateOwnerTokenRequest`, `issueStreamTicket`
- `docker_auto_collector.go`: calls `s.processHeartbeatRequest`, `s.runHubCollectorNow`, and `withCanonicalResourceMetadata` — all deep apiServer logic
- `terminal_ssh_helpers.go`: all methods implement injected function fields already wired in terminal.Deps; pure env helpers (`resolveEnvSSHConfig`) too trivial to move independently

### 2026-03-19 — Refactor: extract worker/job handlers to internal/hubapi/worker

- Extracted 4 source files from `cmd/labtether/` into new `internal/hubapi/worker/` package using the Deps pattern
- New package files: `deps.go`, `job_handlers.go`, `presence_cleanup.go`, `action_runtime_command.go`
- `Deps` receives: `TerminalStore`, `ActionStore`, `UpdateStore`, `AuditStore`, `LogStore`, `PresenceStore`, `AgentMgr`, `ExecuteViaAgent` func, `ExecuteActionInProcess` func, `ExecuteUpdateScope` func, `Broadcast` func, `MapCommandLevel` func, `IntToUint64NonNegative` func
- `job_handlers.go` implements `HandleTerminalCommandJob`, `HandleActionRunJob`, `HandleUpdateRunJob`, `RecordDeadLetter` using `opspkg.ExecuteCommand` and `opspkg.ExecuteUpdateWithExecutor` directly
- `presence_cleanup.go` exposes `RunPresenceCleanup` and exports `PresenceCleanupInterval`/`PresenceStaleThreshold` constants
- `action_runtime_command.go` exposes `ExecuteCommandAction` (agent-routing path with in-process fallback via `opspkg.ExecuteActionInProcess`)
- Bridge file `cmd/labtether/worker_bridge.go` wires `apiServer` fields into `Deps`; `workerDeps *workerpkg.Deps` added to `apiServer` struct
- `worker_executors.go` (stats handler) and constant/func aliases in `operations_bridge.go` unchanged — they depend on `workerRuntimeState`/`retentionState` and remain in `cmd/labtether/`
- 4 original source files replaced with thin stubs or one-line forwarders
- All tests pass: `go vet ./... && go test ./cmd/labtether/... -count=1 -short`

### 2026-03-19 — Refactor: extract admin/settings/TLS handlers to internal/hubapi/admin

- Extracted 8 handler files from `cmd/labtether/` into new `internal/hubapi/admin/` package using the Deps pattern
- New package files: `deps.go`, `settings_runtime.go`, `settings_prometheus.go`, `settings_managed_database.go`, `tls.go`, `tls_settings.go`, `admin.go`, `protocol_config.go`
- `TLSState` struct relocated from `cmd/labtether/server_types.go` to `internal/hubapi/operations/tls_state.go` to break the import cycle; `server_types.go` retains a type alias
- `TailscaleCertReloader` field changed to `any` on `TLSState` to avoid the admin/operations package importing cmd-package types; two call sites updated to use type assertions
- Runtime settings helpers (`NormalizeRuntimeOverrides`, `SanitizeRuntimeSettingKeys`, `WriteRuntimeSettingsPayload`) promoted to `internal/hubapi/shared/runtime_settings_helpers.go`
- Bridge file `cmd/labtether/admin_bridge.go` wires `apiServer` fields into `Deps`; provides type/constant aliases for test visibility of exported admin-package identifiers
- `loadHubPrivateKeyPEM` method moved to `cmd/labtether/hub_identity.go` (was in protocol_config_handlers.go which was stubbed)
- 8 original handler files replaced with thin stubs
- All tests pass: `go vet ./... && go test ./cmd/labtether/... -count=1 -short`

### 2026-03-19 — Refactor: extract status aggregate to internal/hubapi/statusagg

- `ProbeEndpointFunc` exported as package-level variable to support test injection without changing the call site

- `buildStatusAggregateResponse` signature reduced from 4 args to 2 (ctx, groupFilter); test call sites updated
- All tests pass: `go vet ./... && go test ./cmd/labtether/... -count=1 -short`

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Post-Plan Polish

- MCP tools expanded from 5 to 23: added services, files, docker, system info (processes, network, disks, packages), alerts, groups, metrics, power management (reboot, shutdown, wake)
- V2 response consistency: all delegated v1 handlers now wrapped in v2 envelope via `WrapV1Handler`
- Integration tests: 5 tests covering full API key lifecycle, scope enforcement, session auth, key expiry, key deletion
- Webhooks system: new `webhooks` table (migration 66), CRUD endpoints at `/api/v2/webhooks`
- Scheduled tasks system: new `scheduled_tasks` table (migration 67), CRUD endpoints at `/api/v2/schedules`
- Saved actions system: new `saved_actions` table (migration 68), CRUD + run endpoints at `/api/v2/actions`
- Unified search: `GET /api/v2/search?q=` spanning assets and groups
- Bulk service action: `POST /api/v2/bulk/service-action` with parallel fan-out
- Real-time event stream: `GET /api/v2/events/stream` WebSocket endpoint
- CLI expanded from 9 to 40+ commands covering all API domains
- CLI exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=auth, 3=not-found, 4=config

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Plan 6 — labtether-cli

- New Go repo at `/Users/michael/Development/LabTether/labtether-cli/`
- API client (`internal/client/`) wrapping the v2 REST API with auth, JSON envelope, error handling
- Config management: `~/.config/labtether/config.json`, env vars (`LABTETHER_HOST`, `LABTETHER_API_KEY`), CLI flags
- 9 top-level commands: `config`, `whoami`, `assets`, `exec`, `services`, `ps`, `files`, `docker`
- Single + multi-target exec with `--targets` and `--group` flags
- `--json` global flag for machine-readable output
- 3 unit tests for the API client
- Built with `cobra` CLI framework, single static binary

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Plan 5 — MCP Endpoint

- MCP server (`internal/mcpserver/`) using `mark3labs/mcp-go` v0.45.0 with streamable HTTP transport
- 5 MCP tools: `whoami`, `assets_list`, `assets_get`, `exec`, `exec_multi` — all scope-enforced
- 1 MCP resource: `labtether://assets` — asset inventory with status
- Mounted at `/mcp` on the hub, protected by `withAuth` — AI agents connect with Bearer token
- Tool handlers use injected dependencies (interfaces for testability)
- Claude Desktop / OpenClaw can connect by pointing MCP config at `https://hub:8443/mcp`

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Plan 4 — REST API v2 Advanced

- Home Assistant v2 endpoints: entities, automations, scenes (stubs with scope enforcement — HA connector not yet fully implemented)
- Credentials: profile CRUD, scope-enforced
- Terminal: sessions, history, snippets
- Agent lifecycle: list connected, pending enrollment, approve/reject, per-agent settings
- Hub status: running status, agent count, TLS info, Tailscale status
- Web services: list, sync trigger
- Hub collectors: list, CRUD
- Notifications: channels, history
- Synthetic checks: CRUD
- Discovery: run, proposals list, accept/reject
- Topology: dependencies CRUD, edges, composites, blast-radius
- Failover: HA pair CRUD, manual failover trigger
- Dead letters: list, retry, dismiss
- Audit: event query
- Log views: saved query CRUD
- Prometheus settings: runtime settings access
- 53 new routes registered — completing the full v2 API surface (150+ endpoints total)

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Plan 3 — REST API v2 Extended

- System info endpoints: network, disks, packages, cron, users, logs, per-asset metrics — all scope-enforced under `/api/v2/assets/{id}/`
- Power management: wake (WoL), reboot, shutdown via agent exec
- Docker endpoints: hosts, containers (start/stop/restart/logs), stacks — scope-enforced under `/api/v2/docker/`
- OS Updates: plans CRUD + execute, runs listing — scope-enforced under `/api/v2/updates/`
- Groups: CRUD — scope-enforced under `/api/v2/groups/`
- Alerts & Incidents: alerts list/actions/rules, incidents list/actions — scope-enforced
- Connectors: generic list/actions, Proxmox (cluster status/resources/assets/nodes/ceph/tasks), TrueNAS, PBS, Portainer — all scope-enforced
- Metrics overview endpoint at `/api/v2/metrics/overview`
- 28+ new routes registered, all following the v2 scope→delegate pattern

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Plan 2 — REST API v2 Core

- V2 response framework (`internal/apiv2/`): consistent envelope with `request_id`, `data`, `meta` fields; `WriteJSON`, `WriteList`, `WriteError` helpers
- Scope enforcement middleware: `ScopeCheck`, `AssetCheck`, `WriteScopeForbidden`, `WriteAssetForbidden`
- `GET /api/v2/whoami`: discovery endpoint showing key info, scopes, allowed assets with status — the first thing an AI agent calls
- `GET/POST /api/v2/assets`: list assets (with status/platform filters, asset-allowlist filtering), create manual devices
- `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v2/assets/{id}`: get, update, delete individual assets with scope + allowlist enforcement
- `POST /api/v2/assets/{id}/exec`: one-shot command execution via agent, 409 if offline with last_seen
- `POST /api/v2/exec`: multi-target fan-out execution (parallel), aggregated results with summary
- Files/processes/services: v2 scope-enforced wrappers delegating to existing resource handlers
- Sub-path routing in `handleV2AssetActions`: exec, files, processes, services all routed under `/api/v2/assets/{id}/`

### 2026-03-17 — AI Agent Gateway: Plan 1 — API Key Infrastructure

- New `internal/apikeys/` package: key generation (`lt_<prefix>_<secret>` format), SHA-256 hashing, prefix extraction, format validation
- Scope enforcement: `ScopeAllows` (exact, wildcard, category wildcard), `AssetAllowed` (allowlist), `ValidateScopes` (38 known scope categories)
- Database: migration 65 (`api_keys` table with `secret_hash` unique index, `prefix` index), `APIKeyStore` interface, Postgres + in-memory implementations
- Auth middleware: `withAuth` extended with third auth path for `lt_`-prefixed Bearer tokens, TLS enforcement (reject on plain HTTP), per-key rate limiting (600 req/min)
- Context propagation: scopes, allowed assets, and key ID injected into request context for downstream scope enforcement
- Admin CRUD: `POST/GET /api/v2/keys`, `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v2/keys/{id}` — all behind `withAdminAuth`
- Audit logging: `api_key.created` and `api_key.deleted` events
- Full test coverage across all packages
- This is Plan 1 of 6 for the AI Agent Gateway feature (design spec: `notes/2026-03-17-ai-agent-gateway-design.md`)

### 2026-03-17 — Universal Metrics Export & Container Fleet Dashboard

- Universal metrics persistence: `MetricsBridge` interface + `MetricsBridgeRegistry` wiring all data sources through a unified pipeline into `metric_samples`
- Bridge adapters implemented for 12 data sources: host agents (pre-existing), Docker stats, Proxmox RRD, PBS datastores, per-disk mounts, per-network interfaces, web service health, synthetic checks, site reliability, alert state, agent presence, process-level (opt-in)
- ~41 Prometheus-prefixed metric names with rich label sets (`docker_host`, `docker_image`, `mount_point`, `interface`, `datastore`, etc.)
- Schema extension: `labels` JSONB column on `metric_samples` (migration 58) with label-aware index
- Metrics query layer generalized: `Snapshot`/`SnapshotMany`/`Series` now serve all persisted metrics, not just the 6 hardcoded host metrics
- Prometheus `/metrics` scrape endpoint: unauthenticated, exact-match auth skip, disabled by default
- `labtether_asset_info` metadata join metric on every scrape
- Prometheus remote_write push client: standard protobuf + snappy wire format, Postgres as replay buffer, `last_pushed_at` watermark survives restarts, exponential backoff on failure
- Container fleet dashboard (`/containers`): summary bar, top-10 CPU/memory tables with sparklines, per-host collapsible cards, Docker host/state/stack filters
- Hub settings UI: Prometheus scrape toggle + URL display, remote_write config (URL, credentials, interval, test connection, last-push status), process metrics toggle + top-N selector
- New packages: `internal/telemetry/bridge/`, `internal/telemetry/promexport/`, `internal/telemetry/remotewrite/`
- Docs: `docs/internal/prometheus-integration.md`, `docs/internal/prometheus-metrics-reference.md`

### 2026-03-17 — Service Configuration Page

- New `/services/config` page with four tabs: Manual Services, Overrides, Grouping & Merge, Icons
- Manual Services tab supports standalone services (no host asset required); `host_asset_id` is now nullable on `web_services_manual` (migration 57)
- Persistence: `PromoteManualServicesToStandalone` method nulls the host FK; coordinator promotes instead of deleting manual services when a host is removed
- Overrides tab: filter + bulk reset; supersedes the old overrides modal
- Grouping & Merge tab: lifted URL grouping and service merge settings from old inline modals
- Icons tab: custom icon library management
- Entry point: gear icon on the Services page header
- Deferred: standalone service health monitoring (out of scope per spec)

### 2026-03-16 — Manual Device & Protocol Management

- Added "Manual Device" source type for protocol-only devices (SSH, Telnet, VNC, RDP, ARD)
- New `asset_protocol_configs` table (migration 56) — one row per protocol per asset
- `POST /assets/manual` endpoint for manual device creation
- Protocol config CRUD API (`/assets/{id}/protocols/...`) + test connection with concurrency guard
- SSH hub key push with Ed25519, platform detection (Unix/Windows), authorized_keys management
- Telnet WebSocket-to-TCP stream handler with IAC negotiation
- Terminal routing updated: `asset_protocol_configs` takes priority over legacy `asset_terminal_configs`
- Desktop routing expanded: manual device VNC/RDP/ARD bypasses env gate
- Frontend: Manual Device in Add Device modal + ProtocolsPanel on device detail page
- New credential kinds: `telnet_password`, `rdp_password`
- Deferred: RSA 4096 fallback, SSH key rotation endpoint, legacy table migration

### 2026-03-15 — Test Migration to hubapi Packages

Migrated test files from cmd/labtether/ to their proper internal/hubapi/ package locations:

**Phase 1: Infrastructure**
- Created `internal/hubapi/testutil/testutil.go` with shared store factories, no-op middleware helpers, and test asset utilities
- Designed to avoid import cycles (testutil does NOT import hubapi sub-packages)

**Phase 2: Migrated tests (59 test functions across 12 new test files)**
- `shared/`: http_helpers (2), stream_trace (3), stream_websocket_keepalive (2) -- 7 functions
- `resources/`: groups_handlers (17), frontend_perf (3), mobile_client_telemetry (3), restart_handlers (2), link_suggestions + bulk_move (9) -- 34 functions
- `alerting/`: alert_instances (3), incidents (4), notification_handlers (1), alert_templates (4) -- 12 functions
- Plus 6 pre-existing tests in agents/agent_install_script_execution_test.go
- Remaining tests that require cmd/labtether bridge routing (timeline, reliability) or call unexported bridge functions left in place with reduced scope

**Key patterns:**
- Tests that call `sut.handleX(w,r)` where handleX is a 1-line bridge delegate to `deps.HandleX(w,r)` can be migrated
- Tests that access apiServer internal fields (`sut.policyState`, `sut.terminalDeps`) or call unexported functions (`validateX`, `sanitizeX`) remain in cmd/labtether
- Each hubapi test file contains a `newTestXDeps(t)` factory using testutil helpers

**Left behind (too entangled with apiServer):**
- Terminal tests (deep auth context, issueStreamTicket, policy state mutation)
- Desktop/WebRTC tests (agent manager, bridge maps, session options)
- Connector handler tests (unexported sanitize/validate helpers)
- Auth tests (zero-value apiServer pattern, auth store not in memory persistence)
- Most alerting CRUD tests (call unexported validation in bridge file)

## 2026-03-15 (Connector audit hardening follow-up)
- Fixed the recent connector audit findings across Portainer, TrueNAS, Proxmox, and the shared device-capabilities UI flow.
- **Backend hardening:**
  - `cmd/labtether/portainer_api_exec.go` now requires admin privileges for container exec and rejects non-WebSocket requests before creating upstream exec state.
  - `cmd/labtether/truenas_api_disks.go` now requires admin privileges before starting SMART tests.
  - `cmd/labtether/portainer_api_capabilities.go` now reports `can_exec` based on the collector auth method so the UI can hide the shell action for API-key-only collectors.
  - `cmd/labtether/truenas_api_capabilities.go` now treats `vm.query` / `app.query` failures conservatively and surfaces warnings instead of misclassifying transient failures as SCALE/app support.
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_api_capabilities.go` no longer performs extra Ceph/HA/replication probes on the hot path; it now returns a kind-scoped tab set without extra upstream RPCs.
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_api_task_node_handlers.go` no longer lets storage assets inherit node-level HA matches.
- **Frontend hardening:**
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useDeviceCapabilities.ts` now aborts/replaces in-flight requests safely during fast node switches and preserves last-good data on transient failures.
  - `PBSTab.tsx` was restored to a static tab shell to remove an unnecessary capabilities round-trip.
  - `ProxmoxDetailsTab.tsx` now uses loaded Proxmox details for Ceph/HA tab visibility instead of relying on a new capability request.
  - `TrueNASTab.tsx` now falls back to the historical full tab set when capabilities are unavailable instead of hiding the VMs tab on transient failures.
  - `PortainerPanel.tsx` / `PortainerContainersTab.tsx` now hide the Shell action unless backend capabilities report `can_exec=true`.
- **Validation:**
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*(PBS|Proxmox|TrueNAS|Portainer|Capabilities|Exec|SMART)'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

## 2026-03-14 (Device detail pages — final polish)
- Implemented Portainer container exec WebSocket proxy — bidirectional WS bridge through Portainer API with Shell button and xterm.js modal in the Containers tab. Note: requires username/password credentials (not API key only) due to Portainer's WebSocket auth requirement.
- Updated spec status to Complete. Updated wiki connector docs (Portainer, TrueNAS, PBS, Proxmox) with Device Management sections documenting all sub-tabs and capabilities. Updated node-triage workflow.

## 2026-03-14 (Capability detection endpoints for all device types)
- Added formal `GET /api/{platform}/assets/{id}/capabilities` endpoints for Portainer, TrueNAS, PBS, and Proxmox.
- **Backend:**
  - `portainer_api_capabilities.go` — returns `tabs` and `kind`; host assets get all 6 tabs, containers/stacks return empty tab list.
  - `truenas_api_capabilities.go` — probes `vm.query` to detect SCALE edition; conditionally adds `vms` tab; probes `app.query` for SCALE apps flag.
  - `pbs_api_capabilities.go` — always returns all 11 tabs; includes `kind` ("server" or "datastore").
  - `proxmox_api_capabilities.go` — concurrently probes `GetCephStatus`, `ListHAResources`, and `ListReplication` to populate `has_ceph`, `has_ha`, `has_replication`; builds ordered tab list.
  - Wired `capabilities` case into each dispatcher (portainer, truenas, pbs, proxmox).
- **Frontend:**
  - `useDeviceCapabilities.ts` — shared `useCapabilities<T>` hook with per-platform typed exports (`usePortainerCapabilities`, `useTrueNASCapabilities`, `usePBSCapabilities`, `useProxmoxCapabilities`).
  - Updated `TrueNASTab.tsx`, `PBSTab.tsx`, `PortainerPanel.tsx`, `ProxmoxDetailsTab.tsx` to filter visible tabs from capabilities response with graceful fallback while loading.
  - 4 new Next.js API proxy routes: `/api/{portainer,truenas,pbs,proxmox}/assets/[assetId]/capabilities/route.ts`.
- **Validation:** `go build`, `go vet`, `go test ./cmd/labtether/...` all clean; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

- **Backend:** Added ~20 new client methods (firewall CRUD, network CRUD, replication, updates, certificates, node syslog, HA config, storage content, Ceph pool/OSD management). Created 11 new handler files for metrics, firewall, backup, storage content, network, HA, Ceph, replication, logs, updates, certificates, cluster.
- **Frontend:** 16 sub-tab components, many wrapping existing section components. New tabs: Metrics (RRD time-series), Console, Replication, Logs (syslog viewer), Updates, Certificates. Conditionally hides tabs based on asset kind (Ceph only if available, Console only for VMs/CTs, etc.).
- **Validation:** go vet, go test (1 test updated for new POST behavior), tsc all clean.
- **Plan:** `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-14-device-pages-proxmox.md`

## 2026-03-14 (TrueNAS device detail page — full management panel)
- Expanded the TrueNAS device detail page from 3 cards (SMART, Filesystem, Events) to a full management panel with 10 sub-tabs.
- **Backend:**
  - Created 9 new handler files: overview (system.info + pool/service/alert aggregation), pools (list + scrub), datasets (CRUD), shares (SMB + NFS CRUD), disks (list + SMART test + details), services (list + start/stop/restart + enable), snapshots (CRUD + rollback + clone), replication (list + run), VMs (SCALE-only with CORE graceful degradation).
  - All handlers use existing JSON-RPC client infrastructure (`callTrueNASQueryWithRetries`, `callTrueNASMethodWithRetries`).
- **Frontend:**
  - Rebuilt `TrueNASTab.tsx` with SubTabBar and 10 sub-tab components.
  - Existing SMART health, Events, and Filesystem cards preserved and integrated into their respective sub-tabs.
  - Pools tab features expandable vdev topology and severity sorting.
  - Datasets tab shows hierarchical indentation with quota usage bars.
  - Snapshots tab has dataset filter and checkbox bulk-delete.
  - Services tab sorts key services (SMB, NFS, SSH, iSCSI) to top.
  - 20 new Next.js API route files for endpoint proxying.
- **Validation:**
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether/...` — clean
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether/... -count=1` — all tests pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` — clean
- **Plan:** `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-14-device-pages-truenas.md`

## 2026-03-14 (Portainer device detail page — full management panel)
- Rebuilt the Portainer device detail page from a thin metadata display into a full management panel with 6 sub-tabs: Overview, Containers, Stacks, Images, Volumes, Networks.
- **Backend:**
  - Refactored Proxmox, PBS, TrueNAS handler dispatchers to support per-action HTTP method routing (prerequisite for POST/PUT/DELETE actions across all device types).
  - Added 13 new methods to the Portainer client: container logs/inspect, image list/pull/remove, volume list/create/remove, network list/create/remove, stack compose get/update.
  - Created Portainer asset resolver and runtime loader following the PBS pattern, with client caching on the apiServer struct.
  - Registered `/portainer/assets/` route and implemented 6 handler files: overview, containers (list + logs + inspect + start/stop/restart/kill/remove), stacks (list + compose get/put + start/stop/redeploy/remove), images (list + pull + remove), volumes (list + create + remove), networks (list + create + remove).
- **Frontend:**
  - Created reusable `SubTabBar` component for horizontal sub-tab navigation within device panels.
  - Rebuilt `PortainerPanel.tsx` with sub-tab navigation for container-host assets while preserving existing relationship-aware views for individual container/stack assets.
  - Created 7 new sub-tab components with data fetching, table views, action buttons, and create/pull modals.
- **Validation:**
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether/...` — clean
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether/... -count=1` — all tests pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` — clean
- **Design spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-device-detail-pages-design.md`
- **Implementation plan:** `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-14-device-pages-portainer.md`
- **Remaining:** TrueNAS, PBS, Proxmox device page enhancements (separate plans per the vertical slice strategy). Container exec WebSocket deferred.

## 2026-03-14 (Linux Wayland desktop backend detection + experimental WebRTC path)
- Added an explicit Linux desktop backend model so agents, diagnostics, and the console can distinguish real X11 desktops, Wayland desktops, and headless `Xvfb` fallback sessions.
- Root cause:
  - Remote-view routing only looked at `webrtc_available`, which made Wayland and headless fallback look indistinguishable from a real X11 desktop until the user landed in a misleading VNC fallback shell.
  - The Linux capture stack remained X11-specific (`x11vnc`, `xrandr`, `ximagesrc`, `xdotool`), so there was no first-class Wayland capability model or initial Wayland real-desktop path.
- Implemented:
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_session.go` now detects the active Linux desktop session via `loginctl` and publishes `desktop_session_type` / `desktop_backend`.
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect.go`, `internal/agentcore/run.go`, `cmd/labtether/agent_runtime_signal_handlers.go`, and `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go` now surface backend-aware metadata and real-desktop support flags for VNC/WebRTC.
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_routing_proxy.go` and `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useDesktopProtocolState.ts` now stop treating Wayland the same as X11: WebRTC remains preferred, automatic fallback to VNC is suppressed when VNC would only open a fallback desktop, and VNC is labeled as a fallback desktop path on Wayland.
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc.go` now refuses a default real-desktop VNC attach on Wayland instead of silently dropping straight into `Xvfb`.
  - Added the first experimental Wayland WebRTC backend behind agent settings/env:
    - `LABTETHER_WEBRTC_WAYLAND_EXPERIMENTAL_ENABLED=true`
    - `LABTETHER_WEBRTC_WAYLAND_PIPEWIRE_NODE_ID=<node-id>`
    - optional `LABTETHER_WEBRTC_WAYLAND_INPUT_BACKEND=auto|ydotool|none`
  - The experimental backend uses PipeWire capture (`pipewiresrc`) plus optional `ydotool` input injection, and the browser now forwards DOM `code` / `key` data alongside legacy keycodes for future backend parity.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(BuildGStreamerVideoPipeline|BuildWaylandPipeWireVideoPipeline|DetectWebRTCCapabilities|ResolveWebRTCDisplay|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStart|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStop|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCInput|StartLinuxVNCServerRejectsWaylandRealDesktopFallback|HandleDesktopStreamRejectsDirectProxyWhenDisabled|HandleDesktopStreamRejectsWaylandVNCFallbackWhenWebRTCUnavailable|ProcessAgentWebRTCCapabilitiesStoresUnavailableReason)'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
- Remaining gap:
  - This is still an experimental Wayland path. It depends on an operator-provided PipeWire capture node ID and does not yet implement zero-config portal/session orchestration or real monitor enumeration.

## 2026-03-14 (Linux desktop fallback root cause isolation)
- Traced the current "real desktop" miss on Linux remote view back to two X11-specific assumptions that survived the earlier fallback fixes.
- Root cause:
  - The Linux agent decides whether the existing desktop is usable by checking for an X11 lock file like `/tmp/.X0-lock` before VNC/WebRTC attach, so a host can have GUI packages installed and still be treated as headless if there is no live capturable X11 display.
  - The capture stack is still X11-based (`x11vnc`, `xrandr`, `ximagesrc`), so Wayland-only sessions and package-only desktop installs do not currently attach to the real desktop.
  - A separate plumbing bug let the console forward monitor labels like `DP-1` as the VNC `display` value even though the agent expects X display ids like `:0`; that could trigger unnecessary fallback/failure on older builds.
- Landed:
  - agent-side guard in `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc.go` to ignore non-X11 display identifiers instead of passing them to `x11vnc`
  - regression coverage in `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc_test.go`
  - console-side `resolveDesktopDisplay()` hardening so VNC only forwards X11 display ids
  - docs/wiki/ADR updates clarifying that installed desktop packages are not sufficient without a live X11 session
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(StartLinuxVNCServerUsesExistingDisplayWhenAvailable|StartLinuxVNCServerIgnoresNonX11DisplaySelection|StartLinuxVNCServerFallsBackToXvfbOnDisplayError|StartLinuxVNCServerSkipsToXvfbOnHeadless|StartLinuxVNCServerReturnsPrimaryErrorWhenDisplayIsHealthyButStartupFails|StartLinuxVNCServerBootstrapFailureStillStartsVNC)'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

## 2026-03-14 (Comprehensive audit-driven bug fix pass)
- Applied 15 bug fixes identified by five parallel audit agents scanning `cmd/labtether/`, `internal/agentcore/`, `internal/agentmgr/`, and the install script templates.
- **Hub fixes:**
  - **BUG-2/3 — pendingAgent concurrent WebSocket write race**: `pendingAgent.conn` was written from both the approval handler and the read-loop goroutine without synchronization. Fix: added `connMu sync.Mutex` to `pendingAgent` struct, acquired in `sendPendingEnrollmentDecision` and `sendPendingEnrollmentChallenge`.
  - **BUG-5 — Unsanitized hostname as asset ID**: `enrollmentHandler` used raw `req.Hostname` as asset ID without normalization. Fix: added `normalizeHostnameForAssetID()` (lowercase, alphanumeric + separators, length-bounded to 64 chars) with empty-result validation.
  - **BUG-6 — Token TTL floor missing**: `configuredAgentTokenTTLHours()` allowed zero/negative values from env config. Fix: added `ttlHours < 1` floor check.
  - **BUG-7 — Zombie connection on approval send failure**: When `sendPendingEnrollmentDecision` failed during approval, the pending connection remained open. Fix: added `agent.conn.Close()` after send failure.
  - **BUG-10 — No hostname length limit in URL sanitization**: `sanitizeHostPort` accepted arbitrarily long hostnames. Fix: added 253-character limit per DNS spec.
  - **BUG-11 — Goroutine leak in TouchAgentTokenLastUsed**: Nested goroutine-in-goroutine pattern leaked goroutines. Fix: simplified to single goroutine.
  - **BUG-13 — AgentConn public fields read without lock**: `LastMessageAt` and `ConnectedAt` were read without mutex protection. Fix: added `GetLastMessageAt()` and `GetConnectedAt()` thread-safe getter methods.
  - **BUG-4 — Duplicate agentWriteDeadline constant**: Same 10s constant defined in both `internal/agentmgr/conn.go` and `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`. Fix: exported `AgentWriteDeadline` from `agentmgr`, changed `cmd/labtether` to reference it.
- **Agent runtime fixes:**
  - **BUG-R1 — logWarning race on runtime fields**: `lastMessage` and `lastAt` fields read/written without lock in `logWarning`. Fix: added `r.mu.Lock()/Unlock()`.
  - **BUG-E3 — ps command without timeout**: `exec.Command("ps", ...)` ran without context timeout; could hang on stalled /proc. Fix: changed to `exec.CommandContext` with 15s timeout.
  - **BUG-G5 — apt-get without DEBIAN_FRONTEND**: Remote update handler ran apt-get without suppressing interactive prompts. Fix: added `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` env var.
  - **BUG-RC5 — WebService inventory not resent after reconnect**: `webServiceCollector` cached state survived reconnect, so the hub never received the report. Fix: added `ResetPublishedState()` method and wired into `onConnect` callback.
  - **BUG-G7 — Settings file write not atomic**: `SaveAgentSettingsFile` wrote directly to the target path. Fix: changed to temp-file + `os.Rename` for atomic writes.
  - **BUG-RC1 — Stale terminal/desktop/WebRTC sessions after reconnect**: Session managers were created after the reconnect loop, so `onConnect` could not clean up orphaned sessions. Fix: moved `termMgr`, `deskMgr`, `fileMgr`, and `webrtcMgr` creation before the reconnect loop and added `closeAll()` calls in the `onConnect` callback.
- **Install script fixes:**
  - **BUG-16 — Unquoted DOCKER_ENDPOINT in env file heredoc**: `LABTETHER_DOCKER_SOCKET=${DOCKER_ENDPOINT}` in the generated env file could break with paths containing spaces. Fix: changed to `"${DOCKER_ENDPOINT}"`. Updated install script execution test expectation.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` — clean
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether/ ./internal/agentcore/ ./internal/agentmgr/` — all pass
  - `go test -race ./cmd/labtether/ ./internal/agentcore/ ./internal/agentmgr/` — all clean, zero races
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` change needed; these are bug fixes within existing architecture.

## 2026-03-14 (Comprehensive debug pass: race conditions, test isolation, bridge security, resilience)
- Ran full race detection (`go test -race`) across all key packages; found and fixed three data races in `cmd/labtether/`.
- **Race #1 — Tailscale global function vars**: `TestHandleAgentInstallScript` was marked `t.Parallel()` but mutated package-level `tailscaleLookPath`/`tailscaleFallbackPaths` globals, racing with `TestHandleAgentBootstrapScript` reading them concurrently. Fix: removed `t.Parallel()` from the mutating test.
- **Race #2 — Pending enrollment identity poll**: `TestHandlePendingEnrollmentAcceptsLargeValidProof` polled `agent.IdentityVerified` (a struct field) without the lock after `pendingAgents.Get()` returned a pointer, while the handler goroutine wrote it under the lock. Fix: added `IsIdentityVerified(assetID)` method to `pendingAgents` that reads under the lock, updated the test to use it.
- **Hidden test dependency bug**: `TestHandleAgentBootstrapScript` passed in the full suite but failed in isolation because it accidentally depended on another parallel test stubbing out `tailscaleLookPath`/`tailscaleFallbackPaths` first. Fix: added explicit tailscale stubs to the test.
- **Security — journal bridge spoofing (LT-AUD-2026-03-12-005 follow-up)**: `journalBridge` lacked `expectedAssetID` validation, allowing any connected agent to inject responses into another agent's journal query via a matching `RequestID`. Fix: added `expectedAssetID` field to `journalBridge`, set it from the HTTP request's asset ID, and added the stricter `conn == nil || !strings.EqualFold(...)` guard in `processAgentJournalEntries`. Added regression test `TestProcessAgentJournalEntriesRejectsMismatchedSender` in `bridge_hardening_test.go`.
- Confirmed via subagent audit that the five originally-flagged bridges (network, packages, services, display, file) have already been hardened. Only `journalBridge` (now fixed) and `desktopDiagnosticWaiters` (remote desktop — out of scope) remained unhardened.
- **Resilience — log streaming restart loop** (`internal/agentcore/log_handler.go`): log streaming permanently died after the first `journalctl` crash because `StreamEntries` returned an error with no retry. Fix: wrapped in a `for {}` loop with exponential backoff (1s→30s cap); only retries on non-nil errors (clean nil return exits cleanly). Added `TestLogManagerRestartsOnStreamError` test.
- **Resilience — Docker inventory after WebSocket reconnect** (`internal/agentcore/docker_collector.go`, `internal/agentcore/run.go`): After WebSocket reconnect, Docker container inventory stayed stale for up to 30 minutes (the discovery interval) because `hasPublishedFull` was never reset. Fix: added `ResetPublishedState()` method, wired into the `onConnect` callback in `run.go`.
- **Accuracy — Linux disk usage** (`internal/agentplatform/linux/provider_telemetry.go`): `readDiskUsagePercent` used `stats.Bfree` (includes root-reserved blocks) instead of `stats.Bavail` (available to non-root users), understating disk usage by ~5% on typical systems. Fix: changed to `Bavail`.
- **Stability — worker nil guards** (`cmd/labtether/worker_job_handlers.go`): `handleActionRunJob` and `handleUpdateRunJob` dereferenced `s.auditStore` and `s.logStore` without nil checks, panicking in degraded-observability mode (e.g. audit store disabled). Fix: added nil guards matching the existing pattern in `handleTerminalCommandJob`.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` — clean
  - `go test -race ./cmd/labtether/ ./internal/agentcore/ ./internal/agentplatform/linux/` — all clean, zero races
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` change needed; these are bug fixes, resilience improvements, and security hardening within existing architecture.

## 2026-03-14 (Linux remote view Xauthority propagation follow-up)
- Finished the Linux remote-view diagnosis by tracing the browser entry path, hub routing, and agent desktop/WebRTC startup end to end.
- Confirmed the console stream-ticket path was not the shared failure: the backend still returns both `ticket` and `stream_path`, so the break was inside the Linux agent runtime.
- Root cause:
  - managed Xvfb displays now start with an auth cookie, but the fallback X11 clients (`xterm`, `xsetroot`) still launched with only `DISPLAY`, so the headless VNC fallback desktop stayed black even though `x11vnc` itself could attach with `-auth`.
  - WebRTC dynamic-display sessions acquired the managed Xvfb display but dropped the returned Xauthority path before starting `gst-launch-1.0` and `xdotool`, so capture/input could not connect to the secured X server.
- Fixes implemented:
  - replaced the old Xvfb-only env helper with a shared X11 client env builder that always sets `DISPLAY` and injects `XAUTHORITY` when a real cookie file exists
  - wired that env into the Linux fallback bootstrap shell and fallback background path
  - wired the same env into WebRTC GStreamer startup and WebRTC input injection for dynamically-acquired Xvfb displays
  - added regression coverage for X11 env construction and for WebRTC managed-display startup carrying `DISPLAY` + `XAUTHORITY`
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - updated `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(BuildX11ClientEnv|StartLinuxVNCServer|WaitForXvfbReady|ResolveWebRTCDisplay|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStart|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStop|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCInput|DecodeWebRTCInputEvent|ICECandidateSendDelay)'`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDesktopStreamRejectsDirectProxyWhenDisabled|TestHandleAgentDesktopStreamClosesBeforeReadyWhenAgentCloses|TestDesktopBridgeCloseReasonRoundTrip'`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore ./cmd/labtether`
  - `go vet ./internal/agentcore ./cmd/labtether`
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change was needed because this work fixes X11 auth propagation inside the existing Linux remote-view architecture rather than introducing a new architecture or product contract.

## 2026-03-14 (Linux remote view live redeploy + final WebRTC fixes)
- Built fresh Linux agent binaries with `make build-agent-linux`, forced a live self-update on `containervm-deltaserver`, and re-ran real browser checks against `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net/nodes/containervm-deltaserver?panel=desktop`.
- Live sequence after the Xauthority fix exposed two additional blockers:
  - **Linux agent VP8 pipeline bug**: `buildGStreamerVideoPipeline(...)` always appended `config-interval=-1`, which is valid for `rtph264pay` but invalid for `rtpvp8pay`. On the live host the agent preferred VP8, so signaling completed but `gst-launch-1.0` exited immediately with `no property "config-interval" in element "rtpvp8pay"`.
  - **Console viewer stream merge bug**: the browser `WebRTCViewer` replaced `srcObject` with whichever incoming stream arrived last. When the browser received separate video and audio track events, the later audio-only stream could overwrite the earlier video stream and leave the session "connected" but visually blank.
- Fixes implemented:
  - updated the Linux WebRTC video pipeline builder so H.264 payloaders keep `config-interval=-1` while VP8 payloaders use only `pt=96`
  - kept WebRTC audio best-effort on Linux so a failed `pulsesrc` path no longer tears down an otherwise healthy video session
  - updated the console `WebRTCViewer` to maintain one combined `MediaStream` and merge incoming tracks instead of replacing the active stream on each `ontrack` callback
  - added lightweight `data-webrtc-video-tracks` / `data-webrtc-audio-tracks` debug attributes on the viewer container to aid future live diagnostics
- Live validation:
  - VNC: connected cleanly against the real console host with `.vncConnected` and a live canvas
  - WebRTC: connected cleanly against the real console host and stayed up for a sustained window with `data-webrtc-video-tracks=1`, `data-webrtc-audio-tracks=1`, and a decoded `1920x1080` video element advancing in real time
- Additional validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(BuildGStreamerVideoPipeline|BuildGStreamerAudioPipeline|BuildGStreamerVideoPipelineEncoderVariantsAndDefaults|ResolveWebRTCDisplay|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStart|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStop|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCInput|DecodeWebRTCInputEvent|ICECandidateSendDelay|WebRTCManagerWatchPipelineExitKeepsSessionOnAudioFailure)'`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCheckSameOrigin'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
- Validation gap:
  - the existing Playwright desktop mock suite on the already-running local dev server stalled in `Authenticating...`, so the final WebRTC confirmation for the split-track case came from the real-host browser run rather than the local mocked spec harness.

## 2026-03-14 (Local desktop Playwright mock gap closed)
- Closed the remaining local validation gap for the desktop WebRTC mock flow on the already-running `next dev` server.
- Root cause:
  - the desktop Playwright helper replaced `window.WebSocket` globally, which also intercepted the Next.js HMR socket on the dev server
  - the mock websocket also allowed a closed stale socket to emit its delayed `ready` event, so under React Strict Mode the viewer could build an offer against a superseded signaling socket and stay stuck in `Authenticating...`
- Fixes implemented:
  - `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts` now delegates non-desktop websocket URLs back to the native browser `WebSocket`
  - the desktop websocket mock now suppresses delayed `ready` emission after `close()`
  - added lightweight WebRTC audit breadcrumbs in the desktop browser mock so the spec can assert that the mock peer reaches `pc:connected`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npx playwright test e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts --grep "desktop WebRTC toolbar supports browser recording and clipboard sync"`

## 2026-03-13 (Remote view Linux agent diagnostics and fixes)
- Diagnosed and fixed two remote view bugs on headless Linux agents (Xvfb): VNC black screen and WebRTC connection never establishing.
- Root cause (VNC black screen): `startDesktopBootstrapShell()` failure was non-fatal — x11vnc connected to an empty Xvfb framebuffer. Also `-auth guess` failed for dynamically-created Xvfb displays without Xauthority.
- Root cause (WebRTC not establishing): `detectX11DisplayIdentifiers()` ran once at agent startup, reading `$DISPLAY` (empty on systemd service) and falling back to `:0` (non-existent on headless). GStreamer pipeline failed silently; hub fell back to VNC without notifying browser.
- Fixes implemented:
  - **Diagnostic endpoint**: `POST /api/desktop/diagnose/{id}` — returns full desktop stack state (Xvfb, x11vnc, bootstrap shell, GStreamer, WebRTC caps, framebuffer content check) for live introspection.
  - **Wire protocol**: Added `desktop.diagnose` / `desktop.diagnosed` message types.
  - **VNC Fix A**: Visible Xvfb fallback background (`xsetroot -solid`) when bootstrap shell is unavailable, replacing pure black.
  - **VNC Fix B+C**: Generate Xauthority file for Xvfb displays (replacing `-auth guess`); replace 500ms `time.Sleep` with active lock-file probe for Xvfb readiness.
  - **Xvfb lifecycle monitoring**: `watchXvfbExit` goroutine cleans up sessions when Xvfb crashes instead of leaving x11vnc connected to a dead framebuffer.
  - **Shared displayManager**: Reference-counted Xvfb lifecycle shared between VNC and WebRTC sessions. WebRTC sessions dynamically acquire Xvfb displays at session start (not just at agent startup). Placeholder pattern prevents TOCTOU race on concurrent acquire.
  - **WebRTC fallback reporting**: When WebRTC falls back to VNC, the reason is stored in session options and sent as a first-message JSON notification (`protocol_fallback`) on the VNC WebSocket before binary data begins.
  - **Trace logging**: `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_DEBUG=true` env var enables byte/frame counters in VNC output, agent data logging on hub bridge, GStreamer pipeline command logging, and WebRTC signaling message logging.
- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-remote-view-linux-diagnostics-design.md`
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-13-remote-view-linux-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` — clean
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore/ ./cmd/labtether/` — all tests pass
  - New tests: display manager ref counting, diagnostic handler, hub diagnostic endpoint, Xvfb fallback background, Xauthority generation, readiness probe, concurrent acquire race prevention

## 2026-03-13 (TLS consistency audit: frontend and dev runtime alignment)
- Audited and fixed the frontend dev script and Docker compose to align with the backend's TLS source precedence (ADR-071/ADR-072).
- Root cause: `scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh` hardcoded `https://localhost:8443` + `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, breaking when the backend served Tailscale or other non-built-in certs (hostname mismatch → 502 on `/api/auth/me`).
- Backend changes:
  - Whitelisted `/api/v1/tls/info` on the HTTP redirect port so the dev script can probe TLS info without needing TLS.
  - Added `https_port` and `http_port` fields to the `/api/v1/tls/info` response for frontend auto-configuration.
- Dev script changes:
  - `scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh` now probes `http://localhost:8080/api/v1/tls/info` before launching Next.js.
  - Auto-detects TLS source (tailscale, built_in, deployment_external, ui_uploaded, disabled) and sets the correct `LABTETHER_API_BASE_URL`, `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_HUB_API_PORT`.
  - Falls back to built-in CA behavior with a warning if the backend is unreachable.
  - Explicit `LABTETHER_API_BASE_URL` env var still wins (skip probe).
- Docker compose changes:
  - `web-console` service switched from `https://labtether:8443` to `http://labtether:8080` (matching deploy compose).
  - Removed `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` and CA volume from web-console. Agent service unchanged (still verifies TLS end-to-end).
- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-tls-consistency-audit-design.md`
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-13-tls-consistency-audit.md`
- Browser-facing TLS fix:
  - When TLS source is `tailscale`, the dev script now passes the Tailscale cert (`data/certs/tailscale/server.{crt,key}`) to Next.js via `--experimental-https-cert`/`--experimental-https-key`.
  - Previously, Next.js always used the built-in self-signed cert (valid for `localhost` only), causing `ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID` when accessing the console via the `*.ts.net` hostname.
  - Falls back to built-in certs for non-Tailscale TLS sources, or plain HTTP if no certs exist.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether/ ./internal/servicehttp/` — clean
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether/ ./internal/servicehttp/` — all pass (15 tests across both packages)
  - `bash -n scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh` — syntax valid
  - `openssl s_client` confirms Next.js serves Let's Encrypt cert for `*.ts.net` hostname on :3000
  - `curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net:3000` — 200
- ADR: ADR-072

## 2026-03-13 (groups proxy fetch-failed fallback)
- Fixed the console-side group management proxy so create/update/move requests no longer die with a raw `fetch failed` when the resolved backend origin is temporarily unreachable.
- Updated frontend proxy behavior:
  - `web/console/app/api/groups/route.ts`, `web/console/app/api/groups/[groupId]/route.ts`, and `web/console/app/api/groups/[groupId]/move/route.ts` now retry the local `LABTETHER_API_BASE_URL` backend after a network failure against the resolved runtime override origin
  - this keeps the groups tab aligned with local-dev and self-hosted setups where browser-facing routing overrides are valid for clients but not always usable from the Next.js server runtime
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

## 2026-03-13 (clean first-run tailscale serve verification)
- Fixed the clean-room remote-access path after reproducing it end to end on a wiped local database and fresh TLS/install state.
- Updated runtime behavior:
  - preserved intentionally blank `LABTETHER_ADMIN_PASSWORD` values in the local backend launch path so browser-first `/setup` onboarding can be exercised instead of silently falling back to `password`
  - taught host Tailscale detection to find the macOS `Tailscale.app` binary even when `tailscale` is not on `PATH`
  - changed the default managed/guided `tailscale serve` upstream target from the raw hub API to the local web console (`http://127.0.0.1:3000`)
  - `Setup -> Remote Access -> Open LabTether` now redirects to the detected `https://<host>.<tailnet>.ts.net` URL once Tailscale HTTPS is healthy
  - fixed a duplicate Postgres migration version collision (`53`) that blocked clean startup on fresh databases
- Live verification:
  - reset the real host-backed `labtether` Postgres schema, removed local `data/`, restarted the hub in production first-run mode, created a fresh owner account through `/setup`, enabled managed Tailscale Serve, confirmed `tailscale serve status` proxied `/` to `http://127.0.0.1:3000`, verified `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net` served the Next.js console, and completed sign-in on that HTTPS origin as the new owner account
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleTailscaleServeStatus' -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/persistence -run 'Test(PostgresSchemaMigrationsHaveUniqueVersions|NormalizedSchemaMigrations)' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - live browser automation against `http://localhost:3000/setup`, `https://localhost:8443`, and `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net`

## 2026-03-13 (tailscale frontend-origin agent bootstrap fix)
- Fixed the public agent bootstrap gap on the Tailscale HTTPS origin after reproducing `curl https://<host>.<tailnet>.ts.net/install.sh` returning a Next.js 404.
- Updated frontend/runtime behavior:
  - added a public frontend proxy for `/install.sh` and allowed public `/api/v1/*` agent bootstrap endpoints while preserving the original public host when forwarding to the backend
  - added `/ws/agent` frontend rewrite so agent `wss://.../ws/agent` traffic works on the same Tailscale cert/origin as the console
  - ensured backend-generated install/enrollment/update URLs continue to advertise the `ts.net` hostname when those requests arrive through the frontend origin

## 2026-03-13 (agent installer summary layout cleanup)
- Cleaned up the Linux agent installer completion summary after live output showed the fixed-width box breaking on long hostnames, fingerprints, and path values.
- Updated installer/runtime behavior:
  - replaced the hard-coded summary box renderer with a width-aware renderer that clamps to terminal size and wraps long values instead of overrunning the right border
  - kept the existing summary content and approval guidance while making the wrapped output stable in both TTY and non-TTY test runs
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestInstallScript(ExecutesInstallFlow|SummaryWrapsLongValues|DesktopPrereqsInstallIncludesGStreamerAndInputTools)' -count=1`

## 2026-03-13 (tailscale cert trust for enrolled agents)
- Fixed the agent TLS trust regression that left Tailscale-served hubs reconnecting as `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority` after a successful enroll.
- Updated agent/runtime behavior:
  - changed agent TLS CA loading to merge the saved LabTether CA with the host system cert pool instead of replacing system roots outright
  - preserved the existing private-CA bootstrap path for direct hub TLS while allowing later reconnects to public Tailscale `https://...ts.net` / `wss://...ts.net` endpoints to validate normally
  - rebuilt fresh Linux agent binaries so the served installer/update artifacts include the trust-store fix
  - fixed the macOS background backend launcher so the detached `nohup env ... ./build/labtether` path no longer gets cut off by an inline shell comment before starting the hub
  - raised the pending-enrollment websocket read limit from 1 KiB to 8 KiB so real signed `enrollment.proof` payloads no longer trip `close 1009 (message too big)` during Tailscale WSS enrollment fallback
  - aligned the pending-enrollment websocket read limit with the normal agent websocket limit after proving pre-approval heartbeat/telemetry frames could still exceed the smaller cap
  - fixed the Add Device agent step so it resolves only when the wizard's own one-time enrollment token is consumed or a genuinely new asset appears, instead of closing on unrelated `agentTokens` refresh churn
  - fixed runtime policy updates so `Settings -> Advanced Settings -> policy` applies immediately to the in-memory policy state instead of waiting for the background refresh loop
  - restored the intended new-install default for interactive/connector policy bootstrap so backend behavior matches the settings metadata and product expectation
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestBuildTLSConfig|TestEnrollment' -count=1`
  - `make build-agent-linux`
  - `bash -n scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandlePendingEnrollment|TestVerifyPendingEnrollmentProof' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(RuntimeSettingsGetUpdateAndReset|RuntimeSettingsUpdateAppliesPolicyStateImmediately|LoadPolicyConfigFromEnvUsesProductDefaults)' -count=1`

## 2026-03-13 (group-scoped workflow canonicalization)
- Finished the next group-model cleanup wave so group-scoped workflows no longer keep live `site_id` contracts in incidents, alert targets, reliability, maintenance, profiles, and failover surfaces.
- Finished the strict follow-up cleanup so active grouped workflows no longer return or depend on legacy `site` payload keys.
- Updated runtime/API behavior:
  - `/groups/reliability` now returns `{ "groups": [...] }`, `/groups/:id/reliability` returns `{ "group": ... }`, and `/groups/:id/timeline` returns `{ "group": ..., "impact": ..., "reliability": ..., "events": [...] }`
  - reliability/timeline handlers now read groups from `groupStore` and use group-native helper names end to end
  - maintenance window guardrails, failover readiness scoring, and drift checking now resolve grouped state through group-first helpers instead of site-named runtime helpers
  - alert predicate + notification route matching now use `group_id` selectors and group ID sets
  - grouped log/source aggregation paths now project `group_id` instead of `site_id`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/persistence -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- Updated runtime/API behavior:
  - incidents now use `group_id` in request/response payloads and filtering
  - alert rule targets now use `group_id` and `target_scope=group`
  - added group-native operational routes for reliability, timeline, and maintenance windows under `/groups/...`
  - added group-native profile and failover endpoints under `/group-profiles` and `/group-failover-pairs`
  - redirected the duplicate console `/sites` page to `/groups`
  - removed backend registration for legacy `/sites`, `/site-profiles`, and `/site-failover-pairs` CRUD routes so the active API surface is group-native
  - deleted dead Next `/api/sites/*` proxies and unused site-management client components that were left behind after the console moved to `/groups`
- Updated persistence/schema behavior:
  - added migration `52` (`group_columns_canonicalization`) to rename the remaining live `site_id` / `primary_site_id` / `backup_site_id` columns in active alert, incident, maintenance, reliability, profile-assignment, drift-check, and failover tables
  - updated Postgres and memory stores to read/write canonical `group_id`, `primary_group_id`, and `backup_group_id` columns/fields
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/smoke-test.sh`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleIncidents|TestAlertsAndIncidentsAPI|TestActionExecuteReturnsLockedWhenGroupMaintenanceBlocksActions|TestUpdatePlanExecuteReturnsLockedWhenGroupMaintenanceBlocksUpdates' -count=1`

  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence -run '^$'`

  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
  - `./scripts/db-migrate.sh`
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence`

- Docs/wiki updates:
  - updated `docs/API.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - updated `docs/ADR.md`

## 2026-03-13 (canonical group_id runtime migration)
- Finished the runtime-side `site_id` to `group_id` migration pass so grouped workflows now behave correctly on live databases instead of only in the happy-path schema.
- Updated backend request/query handling:
  - normalized grouped heartbeat writes so `POST /assets/heartbeat` prefers `group_id` and accepts legacy `site_id` only as an input alias
  - switched grouped filters for metrics, logs, incidents, actions, and update runs to canonical `group_id` lookup with `site_id` fallback compatibility
  - updated site-profile assignment and site-failover handlers to accept canonical `group_id` / `primary_group_id` / `backup_group_id` request fields while preserving legacy aliases
  - wired action and update execution through maintenance preflight against the resolved target group before queueing
- Updated persistence/runtime behavior:
  - repaired Postgres site/group compatibility with new forward-only migrations `50` (`group_id_site_compatibility_repair`) and `51` (`group_id_foreign_key_retargeting`)
  - normalized memory-store asset aliases so stale `SiteID` reads mirror canonical `GroupID`
  - fixed blank group timezone/location writes in `postgres_sites_store.go` to match the `groups` schema contract
- Added regression coverage:

## 2026-03-13 (tailscale https settings slice)
- Implemented the first Docker-first Tailscale HTTPS product slice without making Tailscale mandatory.
- Updated backend:
  - added read-only host inspection endpoint `GET /settings/tailscale/serve`
  - added `cmd/labtether/tailscale_serve_status_handlers.go`
  - registered the route in `cmd/labtether/http_handlers.go`
  - added focused handler coverage in `cmd/labtether/tailscale_serve_status_handlers_test.go`
- Updated console:
  - added proxy route `web/console/app/api/settings/tailscale/serve/route.ts`
  - added `web/console/app/(console)/settings/components/TailscaleServeCard.tsx`
  - wired the new card into `web/console/app/(console)/settings/page.tsx`
- Product/UX behavior:
  - Tailscale is treated as optional but strongly recommended.
  - Settings now surfaces whether the Docker host has Tailscale installed, whether it is logged in, the detected `ts.net` URL, current Serve state, the suggested localhost upstream target, and the recommended host command when Serve is not configured.
  - The card intentionally stays read-only for now so we do not fake host control before the managed host integration boundary exists.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - updated `docs/API.md`
  - updated `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleTailscaleServeStatus|TestBuildHTTPHandlers_DevModePprofRequiresAuth' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update was made because this slice adds inspect-only API/UI support and follows the already-tracked Docker-first Tailscale Serve plan rather than locking in a new mutation/host-integration architecture.

## 2026-03-13 (tailscale https setup flow)
- Extended the first-run setup flow so Tailscale HTTPS is part of onboarding without becoming a requirement.
- Updated console:
  - added shared status hook `web/console/app/hooks/useTailscaleServeStatus.ts`
  - extended `web/console/app/setup/page.tsx` into a two-step flow:
    - step 1: create the first admin account
    - step 2: optional but strongly recommended Tailscale HTTPS guidance with host-command copy, live status, and verify action
  - enhanced `web/console/app/(console)/settings/components/TailscaleServeCard.tsx` with a `Verify` action so operators can re-check host state from Settings after making host-side changes
- Product/UX behavior:
  - setup remains non-blocking if the operator skips Tailscale
  - the post-bootstrap Tailscale step now explains the Docker-host model, shows the recommended host command when available, and makes `Continue Without Tailscale` explicit
  - if the operator completes the host-side step immediately, they can verify before opening the console
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - updated `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/generated-secrets-and-first-run.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/install-docker-compose.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/first-login-checklist.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/start-here/new-user-setup-guide.md`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleTailscaleServeStatus' -count=1`
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update was made because this is a user-flow extension of the already-chosen optional/recommended Tailscale direction, not a new architecture or trust-boundary decision.

## 2026-03-13 (tailscale desired-state controls)
- Added real runtime/env controls for Tailscale preference without crossing into host mutation yet.
- Updated backend/runtime model:
  - added `remote_access.mode` and `remote_access.tailscale_serve_target` to `internal/runtimesettings/types.go`
  - added env-backed support for `LABTETHER_REMOTE_ACCESS_MODE` and `LABTETHER_TAILSCALE_SERVE_TARGET`

  - added test coverage in `cmd/labtether/settings_api_test.go` and `cmd/labtether/tailscale_serve_status_handlers_test.go`
- Updated console:
  - extended `useSettingsForm()` so targeted runtime-setting saves can be issued from focused settings cards
  - updated `Settings -> Tailscale HTTPS` so operators can save:
    - preferred remote-access mode (`Tailscale HTTPS`, `Manual / Existing TLS`, `Off For Now`)
    - optional custom host-local Serve upstream target
  - updated the setup flow so continuing with or without Tailscale now saves that preference best-effort before entering the console
- Docs/wiki updates:

  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`

- ADR rationale:
