## 2026-03-13 (tailscale managed actions + ts.net discovery preference)
- Added the next operational slice for deployments that explicitly opt into host-managed Tailscale control.
- Updated backend:
  - extended `GET /settings/tailscale/serve` handling with admin-only `POST /settings/tailscale/serve`
  - managed actions now support:
    - `apply` -> `tailscale serve --bg ...`
    - `disable` -> `tailscale serve reset`
  - action path is fail-closed:
    - admin/owner only
    - requires `LABTETHER_TAILSCALE_MANAGED=true`
    - validates custom upstream targets before apply
  - updated `cmd/labtether/hub_connection_resolver.go` so healthy `ts.net` HTTPS endpoints are preferred first in hub candidate selection when `remote_access.mode=serve` and Serve is actually configured
- Updated console:
  - `Settings -> Tailscale HTTPS` now shows managed `Enable HTTPS` / `Disable HTTPS` actions for admin/owner users when the deployment explicitly exposes host control
  - `Setup -> Remote Access` now shows the same one-click managed host action path when available
- Added/updated tests:
  - `cmd/labtether/tailscale_serve_status_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_connection_resolver_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/settings_api_test.go`
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - updated `docs/API.md`
  - updated `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestRuntimeSettingsGetUpdateAndReset|TestHandleTailscaleServeStatus|TestResolveHubConnectionSelection|TestDiscoverEndpoint' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update was made because the host-control boundary itself is still opt-in and env-gated (`LABTETHER_TAILSCALE_MANAGED=true`), not a mandatory architectural shift for all deployments.

## 2026-03-12 (systematic website + backend audit)
- Executed a fresh audit wave across the website and backend surfaces using the existing functionality audit tracker as the source of truth.
- Validation baseline:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint` failed with 8 hook/dependency errors in `PBSDatastoreDrilldown.tsx`, `PortainerPanel.tsx`, and `StatusContext.tsx`.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e` failed with `24` failures (`92` passed): `9` functional regressions and `15` visual snapshot diffs.
- Added new active findings to `notes/FUNCTIONALITY_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md`:
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-001` migration `46` still drops tables that live site/site-profile/failover code paths query.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-002` Postgres `ListAssetsBySite` still reads removed `assets.site_id`.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-003` schema migration execution is not serialized across hub + migrator startup.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-004` group deletion can orphan alert/incident references.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-005` network/package/service/display/file bridges are not asset-bound on responses.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-010` persistent terminal sessions bypass the normal policy gate.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-011` terminal create/queue paths dereference audit/log stores without nil guards.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-006` file download streaming can silently return truncated `200 OK` downloads.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-007` frontend lint gate is red on live hook issues.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-008` frontend functional Playwright coverage is red.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-012` auth bootstrap currently redirects on transient `/api/auth/me` failures.
  - `LT-AUD-2026-03-12-009` visual snapshots have drifted.
- Notes/process updates:
  - updated `notes/FUNCTIONALITY_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md`
  - updated `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - updated `notes/TODO.md`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:

## 2026-03-12 (performance audit kickoff)
- Started a fresh performance audit workstream for the website and backend runtime.
- Created:
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-plan.md`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-findings.md`
- Validation baseline:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint` passed.
  - `./scripts/perf/backend-hotspot-gate.sh` passed.
  - hidden-tab polling resilience check passed.
- Runtime/tooling observations:
  - direct unauthenticated `https://127.0.0.1:3000/api/status*` requests returned `401`, so route timing needs an authenticated or mocked harness.
  - `scripts/perf/baseline.sh` exited early before producing a usable baseline.
  - `scripts/perf/profile.sh` still assumes unauthenticated `:8080` pprof access that does not match the current auth/dev-mode setup.
- Converted the provisional hotspot list into a fix-first shortlist:
  - instrumentation-first order now starts with `/status` and `/status/live`, then `StatusContext`, then route-local churn (`logs`, `services`), then agent websocket/browser fanout.
  - top likely p95 wins are:
    - reduce `StatusContext` refresh acceptance and fanout cost
    - shrink and cheapen the `/status` aggregate path
    - move synchronous agent websocket side effects off the read path
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were made because this workstream is still audit planning, measurement setup, and prioritization rather than shipped product behavior.

## 2026-03-13 (performance audit wave 1 baseline)
- Hardened the local perf harnesses so they match the current runtime instead of the old HTTP-only assumptions.
- Updated:
  - `scripts/perf/baseline.sh`
  - `scripts/perf/profile.sh`
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_schema_migrations.go`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-plan.md`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-findings.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Runtime blocker found and removed:
  - the local Postgres schema claimed migration `48` was applied while `groups` was missing and legacy `sites` still existed.
  - added recovery steps to migration `49` so it can recreate `groups`, backfill from `sites`, and recover the `assets.site_id -> assets.group_id` rename when that drift exists.
  - `./scripts/db-migrate.sh` then succeeded and unblocked the backend runtime.
- Measurement baseline captured:
  - `./scripts/perf/baseline.sh --console-base https://127.0.0.1:3000 --api-base https://127.0.0.1:8443 --samples 3`
  - artifact: `tmp/perf/runs/20260313-002650-status-baseline/summary.json`
  - current local dataset is tiny (`assetCount=0`), but the path attribution is still useful:
    - console `/api/status` mean `78.804 ms`, p95 `181.865 ms`
    - console `/api/status/live` mean `31.512 ms`, p95 `68.124 ms`
    - backend `/status/aggregate` mean `7.013 ms`, p95 `12.249 ms`
    - backend `/status/aggregate/live` mean `3.805 ms`, p95 `6.273 ms`
- Loaded backend pass captured:
  - `./scripts/perf/backend-hotspot-apples.sh --label perf-audit-wave1 --api-base https://127.0.0.1:8443 --aggregate-calls 240 --live-calls 480 --concurrency 12 --cpu-seconds 10`
  - artifact: `tmp/perf/runs/20260313-002806-perf-audit-wave1/summary.json`
  - results:
    - `/status/aggregate` p95 `24.996 ms`
    - `/status/aggregate/live` p95 `15.550 ms`
  - query/heap evidence still points at `listAssets`, session/user lookups, groups listing, audit events, and web-service summary snapshotting as the recurring backend contributors under load.
- Profiling notes:
  - `./scripts/perf/profile.sh 10 --api-base https://127.0.0.1:8443` passed once the backend was restarted with `DEV_MODE=true`.
  - direct pprof capture worked, but the standalone CPU sample was mostly idle.
  - the apples harness still captures pprof after the request batches, so CPU profiling is not yet aligned with peak load.
- Interpretation after wave 1:
  - on the current local dataset, the backend aggregate path is not the dominant p95 bottleneck.
  - the larger visible gap is between direct backend timing and console-proxy timing, so the next wave should focus on `StatusContext`, route rerender breadth, and proxy/request-path overhead.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were made because this wave changed internal migration recovery and local perf tooling only; no operator-facing workflow or public API contract changed.

## 2026-03-13 (performance audit wave 2 instrumentation)
- Added the next layer of frontend-side attribution so the audit can separate request cost, compare/merge cost, and route-local compute cost.
- Updated:
  - `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useRoutePerfTelemetry.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/useDerivedServiceCollections.ts`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/useTopologySceneData.ts`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/TopologyPageClient.tsx`
  - `cmd/labtether/frontend_perf_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/frontend_perf_handlers_test.go`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-plan.md`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-findings.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - `StatusContext` now emits route-scoped metrics for request, parse, compare, merge, and paint phases for both live and full status refreshes.
  - route perf telemetry can now attribute metrics to `dashboard`, `services`, and `topology` in addition to the existing `devices` and `logs` coverage.
  - `useWebServices` and the services page now emit fetch, derived-collection, and page-render metrics.
  - topology scene building now records hierarchy, graph/tree model compute time, and page-render telemetry.
  - backend frontend-perf ingestion now accepts the expanded route set and has focused test coverage for it.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleFrontendPerfTelemetry' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- Measurement caveat:
  - a normal-user-agent interactive browser pass now populated the new telemetry path, confirming the metrics are emitted and persisted through `frontend_perf`.
  - on the current empty local dataset, route-local compute remained tiny while `status.*.paint` exceeded raw request time on `dashboard`, `services`, and `topology`, which points the next investigation back toward status fanout/render cost instead of backend latency.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were made because this wave added internal telemetry and audit instrumentation only; no operator-facing workflow changed.

## 2026-03-13 (performance audit wave 3 status fanout remediation)
- Followed the new frontend telemetry back into `StatusContext` and found a structural fanout leak in the current fast/slow split.
- Updated:
  - `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/page.tsx`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-plan.md`
  - `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-findings.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- What changed:
  - narrowed `useFastStatus` to a fast-summary shape (`servicesUp`, `servicesTotal`, `assetCount`, `staleAssetCount`) instead of exposing the full `summary` object
  - narrowed `useSlowStatus` to slow-summary fields and rebuilt both fast/slow slice memos from slice-local dependencies instead of the whole `status` object
  - composed the dashboard `NarrativeSummary` input from `status.summary + slowStatus.summary` so the dashboard keeps its full narrative counts without forcing the fast slice to carry slow-only fields
- Why this matters:
  - before this change, accepted live updates could still invalidate slow consumers and accepted full updates could still invalidate fast consumers because both context slice wrappers were recreated from the full `status` object
  - this makes the context split behave more like the intended architecture and should reduce unnecessary rerender breadth on status refresh
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint` passed
- Measurement blocker:
  - I was not able to run the required post-fix live telemetry pass from this tool shell because localhost runtime access failed during this step (`:3000`, `:8443`, and direct backend startup socket access were unavailable here)
  - the follow-up remeasurement is still required before counting this as a proven p95 win
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were made because this wave was internal performance remediation and audit tracking only; no operator-facing workflow or architecture contract changed.

## 2026-03-10 (backend perf: web service summary snapshot cache)
- Measured the backend in dev mode against the same local Postgres using a clean `HEAD` worktree baseline and the current workspace candidate.
  - Baseline run (`HEAD`): `tmp/perf/runs/20260310-225314-devmode-head-pre/summary.json`
  - Candidate run (workspace): `tmp/perf/runs/20260310-225408-devmode-worktree-post/summary.json`
  - `/status/aggregate`: p95 `186.910 ms -> 10.769 ms`, p99 `299.064 ms -> 19.569 ms`, mean `23.491 ms -> 3.192 ms`
  - `/status/aggregate/live`: p95 `13.280 ms -> 6.467 ms`, p99 `39.779 ms -> 14.220 ms`, mean `4.400 ms -> 1.711 ms`
- Hotspot evidence:
  - Clean-`HEAD` `pg_stat_statements` deltas showed repeated full-table reads of `web_service_overrides` (`calls_delta: 2791`, `total_ms_delta: 191.313`) and `web_services_manual` (`calls_delta: 2791`, `total_ms_delta: 85.618`) during one apples run.
  - Clean-`HEAD` heap alloc profile showed `ListWebServiceOverrides` (`25.73 MB` flat), `ListManualWebServices` (`13.56 MB` flat), and `SummaryByHosts` / `statusWebServiceSummary` as dominant allocators.
- Implemented a coordinator-owned cached store snapshot for manual services + overrides in `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator.go`.
  - Snapshot loads are singleflight-deduplicated.
  - Cache invalidates on manual-service writes/deletes, override writes/deletes, host removal, and `ClearAll`.
  - Added focused tests in `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator_test.go` covering cache reuse, invalidation, and "do not cache failed loads" behavior.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/connectors/webservice`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestStatusAggregate|TestPerformanceHotspots|TestWebService' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether` currently fails in an unrelated existing TrueNAS collector test; the touched performance paths passed.
- After the change, reran the same apples workload:
  - Query deltas for the targeted full-table reads fell to `0` calls each in `tmp/perf/runs/20260310-225408-devmode-worktree-post/worker-top-queries-delta.json`.
  - Post-change heap alloc profile (`tmp/perf/runs/20260310-225408-devmode-worktree-post/heap-alloc-top.txt`) no longer shows `ListWebServiceOverrides` or `ListManualWebServices` among dominant allocators.

## 2026-03-09 (PBS device page — complete information display)
- Transformed the PBS device page from a minimal health/task viewer into a comprehensive backup management dashboard.
- Branch: `feat/pbs-device-page` (12 commits).
- Backend (Go):
  - Refactored `handlePBSAssets` dispatcher from hardcoded "details" to switch-based action routing.
  - Added `GET /pbs/assets/{assetID}/groups` — returns backup groups per datastore with staleness sorting.
  - Added `GET /pbs/assets/{assetID}/snapshots?store=X&type=Y&id=Z` — returns snapshots with type/id filtering.
  - Added `GET /pbs/assets/{assetID}/verification` — returns aggregated verification counts (verified/unverified/failed) per datastore.
  - Added `resolvePBSStoreList` helper for single-store vs multi-store dispatch.
  - Tests for all three new endpoints.
- Frontend (Next.js + React):
  - Three new Next.js proxy routes (groups, snapshots, verification).
  - TypeScript types + normalizers for all new response shapes.
  - `PBSServerOverviewCard` — replaces PBSHealthCard with aggregate storage bar, total groups/snapshots, most recent backup.
  - `PBSDatastoresCard` — promoted datastore table with visual usage bars, clickable drill-down.
  - `PBSDatastoreDrilldown` — inline expanded view with storage detail, backup groups, and expandable snapshot list.
  - `PBSBackupCoverageCard` — backup groups across all datastores with staleness indicators (>24h warn, >72h bad).
  - `PBSVerificationCard` — verification integrity dashboard per datastore.
  - Enhanced `PBSRecentTasksCard` with filter tabs (All/Errors/Running), duration display, human-readable type labels.
  - Deleted replaced `PBSHealthCard.tsx`.
- Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-09-pbs-device-page-design.md`.
- Implementation plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-09-pbs-device-page-plan.md`.
- API docs updated.

## 2026-03-10 (performance audit)
- **Performance audit**: Comprehensive backend + frontend optimization pass addressing 58 audit findings.
  - Plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-10-performance-audit-fixes.md`
  - Branch: `feature/performance-audit`
  - **Backend (Phase 1):**
    - Added gzip compression middleware for API responses (~70-80% payload reduction).
    - Parallelized 11 independent status aggregate collection queries.
    - Removed redundant `SELECT EXISTS` from alert evaluations (FK constraint already guarantees).
    - Added 5s timeout to `TouchAgentTokenLastUsed` fire-and-forget goroutine.
    - Increased telemetry cache TTL to 10s, collector concurrency to 8.
    - Bounded alert regex cache to 256 entries with LRU-style eviction.
    - Split `PruneExpiredData` into independent delete statements (reduced lock contention).
    - Added singleflight to endpoint probe cache.
    - Replaced per-request fingerprint DB queries with atomic generation counter.
    - Expanded canonical cache from 1 to 4 slots to prevent thrashing.
    - Used dedicated HTTP client for endpoint health probes.
  - **Frontend (Phase 2-4):**
    - Fixed `areUnknownValuesEqual` comparison: removed O(n log n) sorted-entries allocation → O(n) key iteration.
    - Memoized dashboard computations (`allAssets`, device counts, problem workloads, fleet avg, hotspot items, timeline).
    - Added `React.memo` to `KpiCard`, `NarrativeSummary`, `DashboardProblemWorkloadsBanner`, `Sidebar`.
    - Replaced O(n^2) host lookup in `DashboardProblemWorkloadsBanner` with `Map`.
    - Consolidated 5 topology validation effects into 2, skip graph model computation in list view.
    - Replaced `framer-motion` (~90-120KB gzipped) with CSS `@keyframes` animations via Radix `data-state`.
    - Extracted 9+ inline style objects to module-level constants across `layout.tsx`, `Sidebar.tsx`, `page.tsx`.
    - Added `will-change` and `contain: strict` hints for compositor promotion.
    - Increased `ConnectedAgentsContext` poll interval from 10s to 30s.
    - Narrowed `useNodeDetailPageModel` dependencies from full `status` to `assets[]` + `telemetry[]`.
    - Removed `backdrop-filter` from `Card.tsx` (eliminates 6-12 simultaneous compositor blur layers).
    - Replaced `transition-all` with specific transition properties in `Card.tsx`.
    - Memoized dead token count computation in `ConnectAgentsCard`.
    - Added `avif`/`webp` image format config to `next.config.ts`.
  - **Skipped (deferred):**
    - Task 14: StatusContext split into sub-contexts (large refactor, medium risk).
    - Task 27: Add `context.Context` to all Store interface methods (mechanical but ~100 methods across ~20 files).
  - Validation: `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, `tsc --noEmit` all clean.

## 2026-03-10 (TOTP two-factor authentication)
- **TOTP 2FA**: Added optional per-user TOTP-based two-factor authentication across all clients.
  - Backend: 5 new API endpoints (`/auth/login/2fa`, `/auth/2fa/setup`, `/auth/2fa/verify`, `DELETE /auth/2fa`, `/auth/2fa/recovery-codes`), challenge token flow with 5-min TTL, AES-256-GCM secret encryption, bcrypt recovery codes.
  - Database: Migration v48 adds `totp_secret`, `totp_verified_at`, `totp_recovery_codes` to users table.
  - Web console: 2FA code input on login page, setup/disable/recovery code management in Settings.
  - iOS app: 2FA code input on login, setup/disable/recovery in Settings (manual key entry, no QR).
  - Core auth helpers: `internal/auth/totp.go` (encrypt/decrypt/validate/recovery codes), `internal/auth/challenge.go` (ephemeral challenge store).
  - Modified `handleAuthLogin` to return challenge token when 2FA enabled, `handleAuthMe` to include `totp_enabled`.
  - Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-09-totp-2fa-design.md`
  - Plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-09-totp-2fa-plan.md`
  - Validation: `go vet`, `go test`, `tsc --noEmit`, iOS `xcodebuild` all pass.

## 2026-03-10 (groups hierarchy UI + site→group frontend migration)
- **Site→Group frontend migration**: Renamed `app/(console)/sites/` → `app/(console)/groups/`, updated all component names (SiteListItem→GroupListItem, etc.), deleted dead `app/api/sites/` proxy routes (replaced by existing `app/api/groups/`), renamed `siteLabelByID`→`groupLabelByID` in topology + node detail, updated e2e test mock data.
- **Groups page tree view**: Replaced flat groups list with full hierarchy tree view.
  - `useGroupTree` hook: builds tree from flat groups, manages expansion state (localStorage-persisted), cumulative device counts.
  - `GroupTreeNode` component: recursive renderer with chevron toggle, folder icons, inline rename, location badge, device count, drag-and-drop reparenting, "Move to..." inline panel with GroupParentSelect.
  - Groups page: manage mode toggle, expand/collapse toolbar, root drop zone, edit modal (name + parent selector).
- **GroupParentSelect component**: Reusable `<select>` dropdown showing hierarchical groups with indented options, self-exclusion for cycle prevention. Used in GroupCreateModal, Groups page edit modal, GroupTreeNode move action, and NodeEditModal.
- **GroupCreateModal enhanced**: Added parent group selector field to the create modal, pre-filled when creating child groups from the tree.
- **Node detail edit modal**: Added `NodeEditModal` with device name + group assignment fields. Pencil edit button added to `DeviceIdentityBar`. PATCH to `/api/assets/{id}` with only changed fields.
- Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-09-groups-hierarchy-ui-design.md`
- Plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-10-groups-hierarchy-ui.md`
- Validation: `tsc --noEmit` clean, no orphaned imports, all GroupParentSelect usages verified.

## 2026-03-09 (devices page deep debug: Proxmox + TrueNAS — Phase 5 edge cases)
- Phase 5 edge case audit identified and fixed:
  - **formatBytes fractional byte crash**: 4 copies of `formatBytes()` had `bytes === 0` guard but fractional bytes (0 < bytes < 1) caused `Math.log` to produce `-Infinity` index → `units[-Infinity]` → undefined. Fixed with `bytes < 1` guard and `Math.max(0, ...)` clamp in `InterfacesTab.tsx`, `DisksTab.tsx`, `truenasTabModel.ts`, `pbsTabModel.ts`.
  - **days_since_backup raw float**: `parseIntValue` truncated `0.7757` to `0` showing "Backup today" when backup was ~18h ago. Switched to `parseFloatValue` with human-friendly formatting ("18h since backup" for sub-day values).
  - **Unguarded Date constructors**: Added null guards for `run.created_at` in `NodeActionsTabCard.tsx` and `event.timestamp` in `TrueNASEventsCard.tsx` to prevent "Invalid Date" display.
  - **CPU temperature drilldown key**: Already fixed in earlier phase (`temp_celsius` → `temperature_celsius`).
- Confirmed: all panels have proper empty states, loading states, and error states. No crashes under degraded conditions.

## 2026-03-09 (devices page deep debug: Proxmox + TrueNAS)
- Conducted comprehensive 6-phase debug of all device detail pages for Proxmox and TrueNAS connector assets.
- **P0 — Proxmox network rate bug**: `/cluster/resources` reports `netin`/`netout` as cumulative total bytes since boot, not per-second rates. Storing these as `network_rx_bytes_per_sec` produced absurd values (~11.6 TB/s). Removed the emission entirely with explanatory comment.
- **P0 — Desktop eligibility for non-desktop types**: `deriveShouldShowDesktop` had a gap where `!isProxmoxAsset` was `true` for ALL non-Proxmox assets, causing storage-pool, dataset, disk, share-smb, service, app, etc. to erroneously show Remote View. Added `DESKTOP_ELIGIBLE_TYPES` allowlist restricting to host, hypervisor-node, vm, container.
- **P1 — Proxmox storage "available" status**: `normalizeProxmoxStatus` didn't map `"available"` to `"online"`, causing storage pools to show as "stale". Added to the online case.
- **P1 — TrueNAS NAS host missing metrics**: The NAS host asset lacked aggregate disk/temperature metrics and canonical key names for CPU/memory. Added disk_used_percent aggregation from pool data, max temperature_celsius from disk data, and canonical key mappings (cores→cpu_cores_physical, physmem→memory_total_bytes, model→cpu_model).
- **P1 — Proxmox absolute values**: Added `maxcpu` to Resource struct and emitted `cpu_cores_physical` and `memory_total_bytes` from Proxmox cluster resources API for all nodes and VMs.
- **P2 — Empty connector-cluster names**: `keepConnectorClusterAssetAlive` didn't pass a Name to the heartbeat. Added extraction of `cluster_name` or `display_name` from collector config.
- **P2 — CPU temperature drilldown**: System panel CPU drilldown read `temp_celsius` instead of `temperature_celsius`. Fixed with fallback chain.
- Updated files: `hub_collector_connector_exec_proxmox.go`, `hub_collector_utils.go`, `hub_collector_truenas_exec.go`, `hub_collector_lifecycle_helpers.go`, `hub_collector_runner_proxmox_test.go`, `internal/connectors/proxmox/client_types.go`, `internal/connectors/truenas/connector_discovery.go`, `internal/connectors/truenas/connector_utils.go`, `nodeDetailPageModelBuilders.ts`, `useNodeDetailPageModel.ts`, `systemPanelDrilldownContent.ts`.
- Validation: `go vet ./...` clean, `go test ./cmd/labtether/...` pass, `go test ./internal/connectors/truenas/...` pass, `tsc --noEmit` clean, live API verification confirmed all metadata keys present.

  - no `docs/ADR.md` change was needed because this work stays within the existing connector/detail-page architecture

## 2026-03-09 (default Proxmox QEMU desktop sessions to VNC)
- Adjusted the web console desktop protocol chooser so Proxmox QEMU VMs default to `VNC` and keep `SPICE` as an explicit optional path instead of assuming every reachable Proxmox VM is ready for SPICE.
- Updated:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodeDetailPageModel.ts`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useDesktopProtocolState.ts`

  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/protocol-selection.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/spice-proxmox.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - Proxmox QEMU desktop tabs now open with `VNC` preselected, reducing dead-end `SPICE` first attempts on VMs that do not expose a live SPICE port
  - `SPICE` remains available as an explicit operator choice when the VM is configured for it
  - the node detail page no longer trips its error boundary when the target asset has not been resolved yet during early bootstrap, which keeps the Proxmox desktop tab reachable while status data hydrates
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts --grep "desktop Proxmox QEMU defaults to VNC while still offering SPICE"`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated docs/wiki because the operator-facing default desktop workflow changed for Proxmox VMs
  - updated `docs/ADR.md` because this is a durable product-level protocol selection rule, not just a one-off copy change

  - no `docs/ADR.md` change was needed because this was a UI classification fix, not an architecture or tradeoff change

## 2026-03-09 (fix Proxmox SPICE upstream TLS bridging)
- Fixed a Proxmox SPICE regression where the hub successfully issued same-origin SPICE tickets but then dialed the upstream Proxmox `tls-port` as raw TCP instead of TLS, causing the viewer to fail immediately after ticket issuance.
- Updated:
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_spice_proxy.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_socket.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/protocol-selection.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/spice-proxmox.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - hub-side SPICE bridging now negotiates TLS to the Proxmox SPICE endpoint and reuses the collector trust model (`ca_pem` / `skip_verify`) instead of treating the upstream as plaintext
  - SPICE ticket responses now retain the effective CA/trust context needed for the later one-time stream open
  - SPICE ticket failures caused by Proxmox returning `no spice port` now surface as an explicit VM-configuration error instead of the generic `failed to open SPICE proxy`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDesktopStreamSPICERequiresPreissuedTicketFlow|TestNewProxmoxTLSConfig'`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDesktopSessionActionsDeleteCleansUpSessionState'`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDesktop'`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated operator docs because this changes the practical SPICE troubleshooting path and required Proxmox TLS expectations
  - updated `docs/ADR.md` because the same-origin SPICE bridge contract now explicitly depends on TLS to the upstream Proxmox `tls-port`

## 2026-03-09 (fix Proxmox password-auth username persistence on existing collectors)
- Fixed a Proxmox connector regression where editing an existing collector and switching to username/password auth could still leave the runtime authenticating with the old credential profile username or token ID, which surfaced as `401 unauthorized` from `/api2/json/access/ticket` even when TLS reachability and `skip_verify` were correct.
- Updated:
  - `web/console/app/api/settings/proxmox/route.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/settings/proxmox/[collectorId]/route.ts`
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_target_runtime.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_helpers_test.go`
  - `docs/CONNECTORS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - Proxmox password-auth collectors now persist the selected username in collector config.
  - runtime auth now prefers the collector-stored username over stale credential-profile identity metadata.
  - switching an existing Proxmox collector from API token auth to username/password no longer reuses the old token ID as the password-login username.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestLoadProxmoxRuntime' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/CONNECTORS.md` and `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md` because this changes shipped Proxmox connector behavior and the operator-facing settings flow.
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is a regression fix within the existing collector/credential architecture, not a new architectural decision.

## 2026-03-09 (fix macOS TCC Local Network denial for hub dev runtime)
- Diagnosed and fixed "no route to host" errors for all LAN connections (Proxmox, Home Assistant) from the hub when running via `make dev-backend-bg` on macOS.
- Root cause: tmux runs as a `launchd` daemon without Local Network permission. Go binaries that link CoreFoundation/Security frameworks (via `crypto/x509` system cert pool) trigger full macOS TCC enforcement. In tmux's headless context, the Local Network permission prompt cannot display and is auto-denied, blocking all LAN connections silently. Ad-hoc signed CLI tools like tmux are never listed in System Settings > Privacy > Local Network.
- Updated:
  - `scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh`
    - on macOS, the backend now launches as a background process (`&`) in the current terminal instead of a tmux session, preserving the calling terminal's TCC Local Network permission
    - PID file management at `/tmp/labtether-dev-backend.pid` for stop/restart
    - falls back to tmux on non-macOS or when `LABTETHER_USE_TMUX=true`
  - `cmd/labtether/startup_bootstrap.go`
    - added `LABTETHER_DISABLE_MDNS` env var to skip mDNS/Bonjour advertising in headless contexts where multicast would trigger additional TCC prompts
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
    - added macOS Local Network / TCC troubleshooting section documenting the tmux issue and the dev-backend-bg.sh workaround
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - hub can now reach all LAN devices (Proxmox, Home Assistant, etc.) when started via `make dev-backend-bg` on macOS
  - no workflow change for non-macOS developers
- Validation:
  - connector test to Proxmox at 192.168.0.32:8006 returns HTTP 401 (authentication failure) instead of "no route to host", confirming TCP/TLS/HTTP connectivity works
  - `go vet ./...` — passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md` because this is an operator-facing macOS development workflow issue
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is an environment/tooling fix, not an architecture decision

## 2026-03-09 (mac-agent 120Hz smoothness: static hero chrome and no staged section entry)
- Continued the measured ProMotion smoothness pass by removing the remaining entrance choreography and hot hero chrome animations from the menu-bar popover and pop-out, so scrolling no longer retriggers staggered section reveals or carries the breathing/pulsing status treatment on those surfaces.

  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/Components.swift`
    - added a non-animated mode to `LTHealthOrb` so hot surfaces can keep the same visual language without the continuous breathe loop

    - removed `ltStaggered` usage from the popover sections
    - made the hero orb static, removed the metrics insert transition, and flattened the restart banner to a non-pulsing/non-animated state
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutView.swift`
    - removed the hero scale/fade intro animation and `ltStaggered` usage from the pop-out sections
    - switched the pop-out hero orb to the static health-orb mode and removed the glowing PID badge treatment
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AlertsView.swift`
    - removed staggered per-row entry animation from active alert rows
  - `notes/MAC_AGENT_120HZ_SMOOTHNESS_PLAN.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the menu/pop-out now present as mostly static surfaces during live scrolling instead of replaying staged entry effects as sections and rows come into view
  - hero/status chrome remains readable and branded, but it no longer spends frame budget on the breathing orb loop or restart-banner attention animation in the hot path
  - this moves the remaining 120Hz work toward smaller residual polish costs instead of broad animation churn
- Validation:
  - `./scripts/perf/mac-agent-120hz-trace.sh --no-build --scenario scroll --seconds 4`
    - saved fresh post-static-hero artifacts at `/tmp/labtether-mac-agent-120hz-20260309-143754/scroll/{logging,time-profiler,swiftui}.trace`
  - `./scripts/perf/mac-agent-120hz-trace.sh --no-build --scenario log-burst --seconds 4`
    - saved fresh post-static-hero artifacts at `/tmp/labtether-mac-agent-120hz-20260309-143754/log-burst/{logging,time-profiler,swiftui}.trace`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
    - passed
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is internal mac-agent performance/refinement work, not an operator-facing workflow or architecture contract change

## 2026-03-09 (mac-agent 120Hz smoothness: cheaper hero metric primitives)
- Continued the measured ProMotion smoothness work by cutting more cost out of the hero/system metric path, so the menu-bar popover and pop-out no longer spend each poll on per-gauge geometry sizing, live numeric transitions, and glow-heavy sparkline rendering.
- Updated:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/Components.swift`
    - added shared `LTMetricBar` for low-cost live gauge fills
    - rewrote `LTSparkline` to a lightweight async `Canvas` path without endpoint glow/shadow work
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MetricsView.swift`
    - switched the compact system card to `LTMetricBar` and removed live metric value-flash / numeric transition churn
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`
    - switched hero mini-gauges to `LTMetricBar` and removed scan shimmer from the live system card
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutView.swift`
    - removed live metric numeric/value-flash churn from the pop-out metric cards so the shared sparkline/bar path is the only changing visual
  - `notes/MAC_AGENT_120HZ_SMOOTHNESS_PLAN.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the hottest metric surfaces now use one cheaper bar primitive across menu + compact card + pop-out instead of repeated per-view fill logic
  - sparkline rendering keeps the trend signal but drops the endpoint glow and extra shadow work that was attached to every metrics refresh
  - the live metrics path is now much closer to a stable “read values, draw simple shapes” model during 5-second polling
- Validation:
  - `./scripts/perf/mac-agent-120hz-trace.sh --no-build --scenario scroll --seconds 4`
    - passed; artifact set: `/tmp/labtether-mac-agent-120hz-20260309-141147/scroll/{logging,time-profiler,swiftui}.trace`
  - `./scripts/perf/mac-agent-120hz-trace.sh --no-build --scenario log-burst --seconds 4`
    - passed; artifact set: `/tmp/labtether-mac-agent-120hz-20260309-141147/log-burst/{logging,time-profiler,swiftui}.trace`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
    - passed
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is internal mac-agent performance/refinement work, not an operator-facing workflow or architecture contract change

## 2026-03-09 (Home Assistant latest-core audit follow-up)
- Audited the custom integration against current Home Assistant config-flow guidance and closed the remaining recovery/compatibility gaps that were still visible after the setup-flow refresh.
- Updated:
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/config_flow.py`
    - removed URL-based unique-id handling for duplicate prevention
    - added manual duplicate-host checks
    - added reconfiguration flow for connection-detail changes
    - added reauthentication flow for API-key failures
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/strings.json`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/translations/en.json`
    - added reconfigure/reauth copy and success abort messaging
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/conftest.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_config_flow.py`
    - expanded the HA mock harness and added coverage for duplicate-host abort, reconfigure, and reauth flows
  - `integrations/homeassistant/README.md`
  - `docs/INTEGRATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/connectors/home-assistant.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the custom integration now supports Home Assistant-native recovery paths instead of forcing remove/re-add when credentials or connection details change
  - duplicate-entry prevention now avoids using the hub URL itself as a config-flow unique ID and instead compares configured hosts directly
  - the integration is in better shape for current Home Assistant releases and current config-flow quality expectations
- Validation:
  - `python -m pytest integrations/homeassistant/tests -q`
    - passed (`43` tests)
  - `python -m compileall integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated docs and wiki because this changes how operators recover and reconfigure the Home Assistant integration
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is a compatibility/UX hardening pass on an existing integration, not a new architecture decision

## 2026-03-09 (Home Assistant Core 2026.3.1 runtime audit)
- Followed through on the latest-core audit by checking the custom integration against the current Home Assistant package/runtime shape instead of stopping at the mocked harness.

    - switched reconfigure/reauth updates to `async_update_reload_and_abort`

  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/conftest.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_config_flow.py`
    - expanded the local HA mock harness to cover the newer helper-based update path
    - added assertions that TLS option state persists through review, reconfigure, and reauth
  - `integrations/homeassistant/README.md`
  - `docs/INTEGRATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/connectors/home-assistant.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the custom integration now survives a real import/smoke check against Home Assistant Core `2026.3.1`
  - the options flow no longer trips on the newer Home Assistant `config_entry` property behavior
  - reauth/reconfigure behavior is closer to current Home Assistant helper conventions and reload behavior

    - passed (`43` tests)

    - passed (`smoke-ok`)

## 2026-03-09 (Home Assistant deeper runtime audit)
- Ran a broader audit across the Home Assistant integration runtime and found a few non-config-flow issues that mattered in real use: dynamic asset discovery after setup, switch exposure for unsupported sources, asset-parent device creation, and multi-entry action routing safety.
- Updated:
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/__init__.py`
    - ensured the hub device is always created in the HA device registry during setup
    - removed the arbitrary first-entry fallback from `labtether.run_action` routing
    - added `async_remove_config_entry_device` support so stale asset devices can be removed safely
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/binary_sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/switch.py`
    - added coordinator listeners so newly discovered assets create entities without an integration reload
    - limited power-switch creation to supported controllable asset sources
    - declared explicit `PARALLEL_UPDATES` values for the platforms
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/conftest.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_binary_sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_switch.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_runtime.py`
    - added regression coverage for dynamic entity creation, safer service targeting, stale-device removal, and hub-device bootstrap
  - `integrations/homeassistant/README.md`
  - `docs/INTEGRATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/connectors/home-assistant.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - new LabTether assets now show up in Home Assistant without waiting for a reload
  - unsupported controllable assets no longer expose dead-end power switches
  - asset devices now have a valid hub parent even when hub entity categories are disabled
  - action service routing is safer in multi-hub setups because unknown asset IDs no longer fall back to an unrelated entry
- Validation:
  - `python -m pytest integrations/homeassistant/tests -q`
    - passed (`50` tests)
  - `python -m compileall integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether`
    - passed
  - `/tmp/labtether-ha-2026.3.1/bin/python /tmp/labtether_ha_smoke_runtime.py`
    - passed (`runtime-smoke-ok`)
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated docs and wiki because this changes operator expectations around live discovery, stale-device cleanup, and multi-entry safety
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is still a runtime hardening pass inside the existing HA integration shape

## 2026-03-09 (Home Assistant deep-dive: multi-entry identity isolation)
- Continued the runtime audit with a deeper focus on what happens when multiple LabTether hubs are configured in the same Home Assistant instance.
- Updated:
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/const.py`
    - added helpers for namespaced hub and asset registry keys
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/coordinator.py`
    - attached `entry_id` to the coordinator so entities can derive per-entry identities safely
    - hardened `get_asset()` against malformed asset rows without `id`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/__init__.py`
    - namespaced hub device creation and stale-device checks per config entry
    - added migration of older global entity unique IDs to the per-entry namespaced scheme during setup
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/entity.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/binary_sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/switch.py`
    - namespaced entity unique IDs and device identifiers per config entry
  - `integrations/homeassistant/custom_components/labtether/api.py`
    - filtered malformed asset rows without IDs at the API client boundary
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_api.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_entity.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_binary_sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_sensor.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_switch.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_runtime.py`
    - added regression coverage for multi-entry unique IDs/device identities and malformed asset filtering
  - `integrations/homeassistant/README.md`
  - `docs/INTEGRATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/connectors/home-assistant.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - multiple LabTether hubs can now coexist in one Home Assistant instance without hub-device collisions or same-asset-ID collisions
  - stale-device cleanup now checks the correct per-entry identifiers
  - malformed asset rows are discarded before they can trip entity/device lookups
  - older installs migrate their entity unique IDs forward instead of ending up with duplicate old/new entities
- Validation:

    - made runtime client/coordinator setup respect options and improved `labtether.run_action` service registration to follow enabled entries

    - added user/import/review/options copy plus specific URL/auth/TLS error strings

  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_api.py`
  - `integrations/homeassistant/tests/test_config_flow.py`

  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/connectors/home-assistant.md`

  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`

  - Home Assistant setup is now materially closer to the guided iOS-style flow in spirit, while staying native to HA conventions
  - testing the integration is simpler because operators now see a preview before committing and can scope what gets imported

## 2026-03-09 (dev-up install-script readiness check fix)
- Fixed the false install-script compatibility warning that still appeared in `make dev-up` even though the generated installer already supported `--install-vnc-prereqs`.
- Updated:
  - `scripts/dev-up.sh`
    - changed the readiness verification to fetch the install script body first and then grep the captured text, instead of using a `curl | grep -q` pipeline under `set -o pipefail`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - `make dev-up` now reports the expected success line when `/install.sh` contains `--install-vnc-prereqs`
  - the root cause was pipeline semantics, not installer drift: `grep -q` exited on the first match, `curl` received `SIGPIPE`, and `pipefail` converted that into a false negative
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/dev-up.sh`
    - passed
  - `make dev-up`
    - passed (`PASS: Install script is ready and supports --install-vnc-prereqs.`)
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no docs, wiki, or ADR change required because this was a developer helper-script correctness fix with no workflow or architecture change

## 2026-03-09 (linux agent rollout follow-up: docker socket + TLS noise verification)
- Closed the remaining Linux-agent rollout follow-ups by rebuilding the published Linux binaries, redeploying the live host through the supported self-update path, and re-running the discovery/TLS-noise verification loop against `containervm-deltaserver`.
- Updated:
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the hub now serves a fresh Linux amd64 agent release `sha256:b408ba26e3a3`
  - `POST /api/v1/agents/containervm-deltaserver/settings/update-agent` succeeded with `updated agent binary to sha256:b408ba26e3a3`
  - the live Linux agent reconnected cleanly on fresh websocket sessions (`ztg4ma6wpihxpceqmDRDjQ -> JnfAKlvSOr8xlixYfLrxsg`, then `zhYeZBOIyESDhY2Ir9aHSw` after the local backend restart window)
  - post-rollout presence still reports the expected local connectors as reachable on the host (`docker` via `unix:///var/run/docker.sock`, `truenas` via `https://127.0.0.1:443`)
  - `POST /api/v1/services/web/sync` accepted the host-targeted rediscovery request, and the refreshed `discovery_stats` row for `containervm-deltaserver` now shows `total_services=129` with `docker=7`, `proxy=112`, and `scan=10`
  - a focused backend log sweep after redeploy found no `TLS handshake error` or `bad certificate` lines during the verification window
- Validation:
  - `make build-agent-linux`
    - passed
  - `GET /api/v1/agent/releases/latest?os=linux&arch=amd64`
    - passed (`version=sha256:b408ba26e3a3`)
  - `POST /api/v1/agents/containervm-deltaserver/settings/update-agent`
    - passed (`status=succeeded`)
  - `GET /agents/presence`
    - passed (live Linux agent reconnected with reachable `docker` and `truenas` connectors)
  - `POST /api/v1/services/web/sync`
    - passed (`queued=1`, `sent_to=["containervm-deltaserver"]`)
  - `GET /api/v1/services/web`
    - passed (`containervm-deltaserver` discovery row refreshed; `129` total services)
  - `tmux capture-pane -p -t labtether-backend -S -240 -J | rg 'TLS handshake error|bad certificate|EOF'`
    - passed for the TLS-noise check (`TLS handshake error` / `bad certificate` had no matches; only expected non-TLS agent reconnect churn from the self-update cycle)
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this was an operational rollout verification pass with no behavior or contract changes

- Replaced the earlier scroll-workaround idea with a proper rendering pass that makes the scrolling regions cheaper to draw and less stateful while moving.
- Updated:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/Components.swift`
    - simplified `LTGlassCard` down to a lighter gradient/tint glass treatment with a cheaper shadow profile, removing the heavier per-card live-material/highlight stack
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`
    - converted the menu-bar root into a mostly static shell and moved hot `AgentStatus`/`AgentProcess`/`AgentSettings`/`LocalAPIClient`/`LogBuffer` observation into section-scoped subviews so polling/log updates repaint smaller islands
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutView.swift`
    - mirrored the same observation-isolation pattern in the pop-out dashboard so hero, metrics, alerts, screen sharing, and action-bar sections update independently instead of invalidating the full scroll tree
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AlertsView.swift`
    - simplified alert rows to a stable tinted style instead of hover-reactive styling inside the scrolling list
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the menu-bar app now scrolls against lighter card compositing and fewer hover-driven state transitions in the list content itself
  - this is a structural fix rather than an input-event workaround: the expensive behavior was removed from the scrolling surfaces, and the hottest observers now invalidate section-level view islands instead of the full scrolling shell
- Validation:
  - `cd apps/mac-agent && swift build`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this was a native interaction-performance refinement, not a new workflow or architecture decision

## 2026-03-09 (mac-agent 120Hz smoothness program: plan + phase 1)
- Started a concrete measured smoothness program for the mac-agent instead of continuing one-off visual tweaks.

    - captured the first-principles execution plan, frame-budget targets, phased work, and fallback path if SwiftUI still misses 120 Hz after profiling/state-split work
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`
    - introduced diffed `MenuBarLabelPresentation` derivation for the menu-bar glyph + metric text
    - coalesced upstream label refresh requests onto the next main-runloop turn and only republish `AppState` when the rendered label content actually changes
    - switched the menu-bar label view to the diffed presentation model instead of rebuilding straight from broad upstream object state
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/MenuBarIconResolverTests.swift`
    - added coverage for compact/unreachable label suppression, standard/verbose metric text formatting, and icon-state carry-through in the derived presentation
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - same-turn `objectWillChange` bursts from `LocalAPIClient`, `AgentStatus`, and `AgentSettings` are now coalesced before the menu-bar label decides whether it needs a redraw
  - unrelated upstream churn no longer forces `AppState` to republish unless the visible menu-bar label output actually changed
  - the remaining 120 Hz work is now tracked as an explicit multi-phase program rather than an unbounded polish thread
- Validation:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
    - passed (`41` tests, `0` failures)
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this begins an internal mac-agent performance program and ships a redraw-throttling refinement without changing operator workflow or system architecture

    - passed (verified both the live-session stop and already-stopped no-op cases)

- Closed the remaining live-host/manual Linux-agent audit work on the real systemd Docker host and fixed the churn defect the replay exposed.
- Updated:
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_connector_exec_docker.go`
    - added stale Docker discovery-asset pruning so removed containers/stacks/hosts are deleted when later collector snapshots no longer report them

- Result:
  - live Docker churn proof on `containervm-deltaserver` now has concrete evidence:
    - `container.create` for disposable BusyBox container `lt-audit-wave2b-20260309` expanded host inventory from `11 -> 12` containers and hub Docker assets from `23 -> 24`
    - `container.remove` contracted host inventory back to `11`, and the patched collector later pruned the stale persisted asset so the hub Docker slice returned to `23`
  - completed a detached CA-pinned bootstrap/reinstall on the live host through `systemd-run`, with:
    - bootstrap URL pinned to hub CA fingerprint `63a45e7d8a2e6b7799b92aa571725e2fa412fa464ad697bfbcc0420e533bdf61`
    - transient unit `lt-wave2-reinstall-20260309.service` finishing with `Result=success`
    - agent websocket session changing from `l859Exz_-NGxeZbjT4PhGA` to `ztg4ma6wpihxpceqmDRDjQ`
    - `/etc/labtether/agent-token` keeping its original `2026-03-08 23:42` timestamp while `/etc/labtether/device-fingerprint` refreshed at `2026-03-09 10:39`
  - the former wave-2 TODO is now closed because both remaining slices have direct live evidence instead of placeholders
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestExecuteDockerCollector(AddsCanonicalResourceMetadata|PrunesStaleDiscoveredAssets|EmptyDiscoveryIsOK)' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `go test ./internal/connectors/docker -run 'TestCoordinatorHandleDiscoveryDeltaAppliesContainerDiff|TestCoordinatorRemoveHost|TestCoordinatorExecuteActionContainerCreateUsesHostTarget' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether`
    - passed
  - `make dev-backend-bg-restart`
    - passed
  - live evidence:
    - `POST /api/v1/docker/hosts/docker-host-containervm-deltaserver/action` with `image.pull`, `container.create`, and `container.remove`
    - `GET /api/v1/docker/hosts/docker-host-containervm-deltaserver`
    - `GET /assets?limit=300`
    - detached `systemd-run` execution of the CA-pinned bootstrap installer on `containervm-deltaserver`
    - `GET /agents/presence`
    - `journalctl -u lt-wave2-reinstall-20260309 --no-pager -n 200`
    - `journalctl -u labtether-agent --no-pager -n 80`

## 2026-03-09 (persistent terminal audit fixes: attach/delete correctness)
- Closed the follow-up audit findings on saved terminal workspaces so attach/delete behavior now matches the intended operator contract instead of only the happy path.
- Updated:
  - `internal/agentmgr/message.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/command_handler.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/terminal_handler.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/capability_handlers_test.go`
    - added a terminal-scoped `terminal.tmux.kill` agent protocol path and agent-side coverage for ending saved tmux sessions without relying on generic remote command execution
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_persistent_session_cleanup.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_persistent_session_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_api_test.go`
    - made persistent-session attach update state only after the fresh ephemeral session exists, return the updated attached record, and fail closed if attached-session cleanup cannot finish before metadata deletion
  - `docs/API.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/terminal-workflow.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - saved shells no longer get marked `Attached` when the fresh hub transport session fails to create
  - `attach` responses now carry the updated persistent-session status and timestamps
  - `Delete Everywhere` now fails closed when attached ephemeral session cleanup fails, instead of deleting metadata and leaving orphaned hub session rows
  - connected-agent saved-shell cleanup now rides a terminal-scoped agent message, removing the surprising dependency on generic remote command execution capability
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestPersistentSession|TestDeletePersistentSession|TestSessionDeleteRemovesSession' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestTerminalManager(ProbeReportsTmuxAvailability|TmuxKillEndsSavedSession)' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalConnectionsStoreTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalConnectionsViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests`
    - passed (`19` tests)
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, and `docs/ADR.md` because the saved-shell attach/delete contract and agent cleanup protocol are operator-visible and architecture-relevant behavior, not just internal cleanup

## 2026-03-09 (linux agent audit live-host manual validation wave 1)

  - queried `GET /disks/containervm-deltaserver`, `GET /cron/containervm-deltaserver`, and `GET /users/containervm-deltaserver` to verify live disk, timer, and user-session truth on the Debian host
  - wrote a sentinel terminal config to `/assets/containervm-deltaserver/terminal/config`, restarted the backend, and confirmed the config survived reconnect unchanged before restoring it to `null`
  - seeded a disposable offline WoL target with a valid MAC and proved the real agent-assisted wake path via backend logs, including successful relays from `containervm-deltaserver`

  - reopened `LT-LAGT-30` as `LAGT-2026-03-09-09` because repeated identical wake requests alternated between `containervm-deltaserver` and `Michael-MBP.local`; the hub currently chooses the first sendable relay from unsorted `AgentManager.ConnectedAssets()` map iteration

    - passed (`status=succeeded`)
  - `GET /disks/containervm-deltaserver`
    - passed (`22` mounts; `/dev/sda1` on `/`)
  - `GET /cron/containervm-deltaserver`
    - passed (`19` entries; sample `man-db.timer`)
  - `GET /users/containervm-deltaserver`
    - passed (`1` session; `michael` on `seat0`)

## 2026-03-09 (persistent terminal workspaces across all phases)

  - `cmd/labtether/startup_server.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/http_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_persistent_session_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_persistent_session_cleanup.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_handlers.go`

    - wired new persistent-session APIs through the server, made attach create fresh ephemeral transport sessions, taught both agent and SSH runtimes to reuse stable tmux session names, and now require remote tmux cleanup before `Delete Everywhere` removes hub metadata
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/APIClient.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift`

    - added iOS persistent-session API models, saved-shell resume flows, previous-session filtering fixes, local hide/delete semantics that work for persistent shells as well as saved SSH configs, and fixed missing Xcode test-target membership for `TerminalConnectionsViewModelTests`
  - `docs/API.md`

  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`

  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - the hub now distinguishes durable terminal workspaces from live transport sessions
  - persistent shells can be resumed with a fresh stream ticket while landing in the same tmux workspace
  - iOS Previous Sessions now keeps detached saved shells visible, supports `Resume Saved Shell` vs `New Temporary Shell`, and lets operators hide or delete hub-backed shell state coherently
- Validation:

    - passed
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestPersistentSession|TestCreateSession|TestSessionStreamTicketEndpoint|TestHandleSessionsFiltersListForNonOwnerActor' -count=1`
    - passed

## 2026-03-09 (linux agent audit phase 5: runtime and identity wave 16)
- Closed the remaining runtime and identity frontier by adding direct CLI/config/runtime coverage, fixing two latent correctness bugs, and validating that one-time token enrollment was already evidence-complete.
- Updated:
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_cli.go`
    - added small seams for docker ping, identity load, and self-update so the local CLI contract can be exercised directly
    - unknown top-level CLI commands now fail fast with usage output instead of silently falling through to daemon startup
    - `settings set` now canonicalizes keys before save so mixed-case operator input does not persist unusable setting names
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_file.go`
    - canonicalizes agent setting keys on both save and load so persisted mixed-case keys still map back to the runtime’s canonical setting names
  - `internal/agentcore/runtime.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_runtime.go`
    - now track baseline collect/heartbeat intervals separately from transient overrides so legacy clears can restore the correct runtime baseline
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_ws_handler.go`
    - `config.update` now applies valid intervals to the effective runtime config, acknowledges effective values instead of raw atomics, and treats explicit zeroes as clear-to-baseline requests
  - `internal/agentcore/config_persist.go`
    - restored interval overrides now update the effective startup config before the first loop tick
    - stale `applied-config.json` state is now removed when overrides are cleared
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_runtime_config_handlers.go`
    - now sends explicit zero-valued interval clears so agents can withdraw stale legacy overrides when the hub removes them
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_cli_test.go`
    - added direct coverage for unknown-command rejection, `settings show/set/wizard/test docker`, `identity show`, and `update self --force`
  - `internal/agentcore/config_layering_test.go`
    - added direct config-layering coverage for settings-file-over-env precedence, mixed-case key canonicalization, and explicit-secret-env-over-file precedence
  - `internal/agentcore/config_persist_test.go`
    - added startup restore and stale-file removal coverage for `applied-config.json`
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_ws_handler_test.go`
    - added direct `config.update` coverage for valid apply, invalid ack semantics, and clear-to-baseline behavior
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_runtime_config_handlers_test.go`
    - added hub-side proof that runtime override pushes now send both real values and explicit zero clears
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`
    - updated the known-message allowlist to include `process.kill_result`, which surfaced during the broader package validation run
  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `notes/LINUX_AGENT_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md`
  - `notes/LINUX_AGENT_AUDIT_TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`

  - `LT-LAGT-06` config layering moved to `pass`
  - `LT-LAGT-08` one-time token enrollment moved to `pass`

## 2026-03-08 (connected agent delete guard for decommission safety)
- Fixed a decommissioning footgun where deleting a live connected agent asset could look successful but immediately bounce back through automatic re-enrollment and Docker rediscovery.
- Updated:
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_heartbeat_handlers.go`
    - block deletion of connected agent-backed assets with `409 Conflict` instead of returning a false-success delete
  - `cmd/labtether/asset_delete_test.go`
    - added regression coverage for the connected-agent conflict path
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Live runtime finding that drove the change:
  - deleting `containervm-deltaserver` revoked its old agent token, but the still-running agent auto-enrolled again and minted a new token because the host still had a valid enrollment path
  - that made the delete endpoint look successful even though the asset and Docker children reappeared minutes later
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestDeleteAsset' -count=1`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/` and `docs/wiki/` because this changes operator-visible delete semantics for connected agents
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is an operational safety guard, not an architecture decision

## 2026-03-09 (iOS remote desktop scaling modes)
- Added desktop-style remote view modes to the iOS remote viewer so operators can switch between `Fit`, `1:1`, and `Fill` without reconnecting a session.
- Updated:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Config/AppSettings.swift`
    - added a persisted app setting key for the iOS desktop scaling preference
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - introduced `DesktopScalingMode`
    - added `Fit`, `1:1`, and `Fill` controls to the iOS desktop dock
    - persisted the selected mode and bridged it into the embedded desktop web client
    - added script-building helpers and regression coverage hooks for the scaling bridge
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - taught the bundled desktop runtime to apply live scaling changes across VNC, RDP, SPICE, and WebRTC layouts
    - enabled native `1:1` VNC viewport behavior with no reconnect, including direct-mode viewport panning through noVNC when native scale is active
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
    - added coverage for scaling bridge script generation and invalid-mode fallback
  - `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/desktop-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`

## 2026-03-08 (iOS remote desktop audit follow-up fixes)
- Closed the main reliability gaps uncovered in the remote-view audit so keyboard shortcuts, drag gestures, and queued actions behave predictably under interruption.
- Updated:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - stopped dropping queued desktop actions on blind `evaluateJavaScript` completion by only clearing batches after an explicit JS success signal
    - added action-batch delivery helpers and page-reload reset behavior so a WKWebView navigation reconnects the same desktop session instead of assuming the old JS bridge still exists
    - stopped trimming leading/trailing newlines from local clipboard paste before forwarding text to the remote session
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - fixed SPICE key injection so modifier chords send real key-down/key-up ordering instead of duplicate full key taps
    - added drag cleanup for touch cancel, scroll-transition, view-only toggle, and viewer cleanup paths so interrupted gestures release the remote pointer cleanly
    - reworked iOS VNC input delivery to target the live noVNC canvas for mouse/wheel routing instead of relying on wrapper-relative private mouse calls
    - added proper DOM `code` values for VNC key dispatch so noVNC can translate special keys and modifiers more reliably on mobile
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
    - added coverage for action-batch delivery signaling and delivered-prefix queue removal
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/desktop-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`

## 2026-03-08 (iOS remote desktop ergonomics tuning)
- Tightened the new iOS remote-desktop controls so the viewer behaves more like a real mobile remote client during longer sessions.
- Updated:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - extended desktop input actions with modifier payloads so remote key chords can be sent intentionally instead of only single keys
    - added sticky `ctrl` / `alt` / `shift` / `cmd` pills to the keyboard accessory row with one-tap clear
    - automatically applies active modifiers to typed text and special-key accessory actions, then clears them after the next remote keypress
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - taught the bundled desktop client to send key chords with modifier down/up ordering across VNC, RDP, WebRTC, and SPICE key injection paths
    - tuned touch handling so trackpad mode uses adaptive pointer gain for short-vs-long drags and direct mode ignores tiny accidental movement before taps/long-presses
    - tightened double-tap drag recognition to nearby taps only, which reduces accidental drag starts
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
    - added coverage for desktop action payloads that include keyboard modifiers
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/desktop-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`

## 2026-03-08 (remote viewer VNC input control follow-up)
- Separated VNC keyboard capture from pointer lock so the toolbar `Keyboard` action no longer drags the browser into mouse-capture mode.
- Updated `web/console/app/components/RemoteViewToolbar.tsx` to render explicit `Mouse` and `Keyboard` pills for VNC input controls instead of icon-only affordances.
- Root cause: the VNC wrapper was focusing and pointer-locking the outer container div while noVNC binds real input handlers to its internal canvas, so toolbar handoff could look active without the canvas actually receiving events.
- Updated `web/console/app/components/VNCViewer.tsx` to target the noVNC canvas for focus and pointer lock, and to redirect non-canvas viewer clicks back into that canvas.
- Removed browser pointer lock from the normal VNC `Mouse` toolbar path because hiding the browser cursor is the wrong interaction model for a desktop session; the button now just restores mouse focus to the remote viewer.
- Added a viewer-side fallback cursor for Linux/x11vnc cases where noVNC leaves the canvas at `cursor: none`; the fallback stays visible until a real cursor image arrives.
- Added focused Playwright coverage for VNC mouse focus handoff, keyboard capture, and hidden-cursor fallback behavior.

## 2026-03-08 (linux agent service writable system view for package actions and self-update)
- Closed the Linux runtime gap where package actions, desktop prerequisite repair, and agent self-update could still fail on older installs even after the installer/package improvements.
- Updated:
  - `deploy/agents/systemd/labtether-agent.service`
    - changed the shipped Linux service unit from `ProtectSystem=full` to `ProtectSystem=off`
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template.go`
    - changed the generated Linux systemd unit to `ProtectSystem=off`
    - removed the narrower `ReadWritePaths=` workaround in favor of a consistent writable system view for package managers and self-update
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
    - updated assertions to match the new systemd hardening contract
  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestInstallScriptExecutesInstallFlow|TestInstallScriptDesktopPrereqsInstallIncludesGStreamerAndInputTools|TestBootstrapScriptExecutesPinnedInstallFlow|TestInstallScriptReinstallPreservesPersistedTokenWithoutEnrollmentToken' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestBuildLinuxPackageActionCommands|TestBuildDarwinPackageActionArgs|TestBuildGStreamer|TestUnsupportedPlatformWebRTCReason' -count=1`
    - passed
- Live runtime findings that drove the change:
  - interactive repair attempts on `containervm-deltaserver` failed with `read-only file system` while writing `gst-inspect-1.0`, `libxdo3`, and `xdotool`
  - that host was still running an older service unit with `ProtectSystem=full`, so the agent could not repair package state or complete self-update/package operations from inside the sandbox
- Operator consequence:
  - new installs and refreshed installs will ship the corrected service unit
  - older Linux installs still need a reinstall or service-file refresh outside the running agent sandbox before package actions, desktop prerequisite repair, and self-update can succeed reliably

## 2026-03-08 (iOS remote desktop usability pass)
- Dramatically upgraded the iOS remote desktop interaction model so the session is workable on touch devices instead of behaving like a bare embedded web page.
- Shipped in:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - replaced the old one-shot desktop command plumbing with queued structured viewer actions

    - added a native hidden keyboard proxy so the iOS software keyboard can feed remote desktop input more reliably
    - added a compact keyboard accessory row for escape/tab/enter/navigation/page keys and clipboard paste
    - synced remote clipboard events back into `UIPasteboard` with inline success/warning banners
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - added a persistent virtual pointer overlay and touch gesture bridge for VNC/RDP/WebRTC
    - implemented direct-vs-trackpad cursor handling, tap/long-press/double-tap-hold gestures, and two-finger scroll
    - added richer action handling for native keyboard text, special keys, clipboard paste, and clipboard fetch
    - enabled remote clipboard receive/send handling for VNC, Guacamole/RDP, and WebRTC clipboard data channels
    - stopped abusing noVNC `dragViewport` as a fake trackpad mode; mobile pointer motion is now handled explicitly
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/IOSProfileHarnessView.swift`
    - updated the harness to use the new desktop action queue interface
