  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this wave added test seams and coverage only; no desktop operator workflow or product contract changed

## 2026-03-08 (linux package backend apt refresh + desktop host package triage)
- Hardened the Linux package-action runtime for Debian-family hosts and pushed the live desktop audit one step further on the current Linux node.
- Updated:
  - `internal/agentcore/package_backend_linuxpm.go`
    - Debian-family package installs/upgrades now run `apt-get update` before the package action instead of assuming package metadata is already fresh
  - `internal/agentcore/package_backend_linuxpm_test.go`
    - added focused coverage for Debian install/upgrade command sequencing and non-apt command behavior
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestBuildLinuxPackageActionCommands|TestBuildDarwinPackageActionArgs|TestBuildGStreamer|TestUnsupportedPlatformWebRTCReason' -count=1`
    - passed
- Live runtime findings on `containervm-deltaserver`:
  - retrying `/packages/containervm-deltaserver/install` still returns `apt` exit `100`, even after the package-backend refresh fix
  - `/packages/containervm-deltaserver` shows the host already has most of the needed stack installed:
    - `x11vnc`, `xvfb`, `gstreamer1.0-plugins-base`, `gstreamer1.0-plugins-good`, `gstreamer1.0-plugins-bad`, `gstreamer1.0-plugins-ugly`, `gstreamer1.0-libav`, and `gstreamer1.0-x`
  - but two critical packages are in a broken state instead of fully installed:
    - `gstreamer1.0-tools`: `half-installed`
    - `xdotool`: `half-installed`
  - that matches the live runtime symptom:
    - `webrtc_unavailable_reason=gst_launch_not_found`
    - `/usr/bin/gst-launch-1.0` is still absent on the host
  - `apt-daily` / `apt-daily-upgrade` are inactive, so the remaining blocker is not an active apt lock; it is a broken package state on the host
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this wave hardened an internal package-runtime path and captured live audit evidence, but it did not change the operator-facing product contract beyond the installer work already documented separately

## 2026-03-08 (linux agent installer desktop prerequisites widened)
- Closed the installer/runtime gap where Linux desktop installs could ship VNC prerequisites but still miss the GStreamer and input tooling needed for WebRTC-capable remote view.
- Updated:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template_preflight.go`
    - widened the Linux desktop prerequisite package set to install `x11vnc`, `Xvfb`, `xdotool`, and GStreamer packages on supported package managers instead of only `x11vnc` + `Xvfb`
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template.go`
    - updated generated installer/help/prompt text so `--install-vnc-prereqs` clearly means the full Linux desktop stack
    - fixed the empty TLS-args shell path so the generated installer no longer crashes on macOS Bash with `set -u` when no custom CA / skip-verify options are passed
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_execution_test.go`
    - added coverage that the generated install script now includes the GStreamer + `xdotool` package list and that the desktop prerequisite install path executes successfully
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestInstallScriptExecutesInstallFlow|TestInstallScriptDesktopPrereqsInstallIncludesGStreamerAndInputTools|TestBootstrapScriptExecutesPinnedInstallFlow|TestInstallScriptReinstallPreservesPersistedTokenWithoutEnrollmentToken' -count=1`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/` and `docs/wiki/` because the installer’s operator-facing contract changed: Linux desktop prerequisite auto-install now covers the WebRTC/input stack as well as VNC
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is an installer/runtime packaging refinement, not an architectural change

## 2026-03-08 (ios shell performance remediation: hidden refresh fan-out, telemetry batching, motion reduction)
- Closed the first iOS thermal/performance wave from the audit by removing three confirmed steady-state hotspots in the shell and observability stack.
- Runtime changes shipped in:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/MainTabView.swift`
    - limited heavy realtime subscriptions to the active tab while keeping alerts and live-activity hooks global
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Home/HomeViewModel.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Nodes/NodesViewModel.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Services/ServicesViewModel.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Alerts/AlertsViewModel.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Incidents/IncidentsViewModel.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/MoreView.swift`
    - added explicit realtime unregister paths so hidden tabs and More-tab subflows stop receiving background event work
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Observability/MobileTelemetryCenter.swift`
    - batched mobile telemetry delivery, added bounded flush cadence, and prevented pre-auth/config drain reschedule churn
  - `cmd/labtether/mobile_client_telemetry_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/mobile_client_telemetry_handlers_test.go`
    - widened hub ingest to accept batched mobile telemetry payloads in addition to the original single-event shape
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/Components.swift`
    - replaced the shared drifting background with a static ambient composition and gated continuous motion for the Home/Devices shell indicators behind the existing platform motion/power posture
- Expected impact:
  - idle foreground shells no longer fan out heartbeat-driven refreshes across Home, Devices, Services, and More at the same time
  - mobile observability no longer amplifies steady-state API traffic with one POST per envelope or spin before auth/config is ready
  - the default shell backdrop and common status affordances run with lower continuous GPU/CPU cost, especially on Home and Devices
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleMobileClientTelemetry'`
    - passed
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA'`
    - passed (`176` tests, `0` failures)
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/ADR.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/API.md`, `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`, `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`, and `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`

## 2026-03-08 (linux agent audit phase 4: settings lifecycle wave 9)
- Closed the settings apply/state audit gap by adding direct lifecycle coverage across the agent and hub paths and fixing two same-wave correctness defects uncovered during the audit.
- Coverage shipped in:
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_runtime_lifecycle_test.go`
    - added direct agent-side settings coverage for local-only key rejection, denied remote overrides, fingerprint mismatch rejection, and successful restart-required apply with persisted normalized values
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_runtime_state.go`
    - preserved `applied` and `failed` metadata when a same-revision `agent.settings.state` report follows the apply ack so `applied_at`, `restart_required`, and `last_error` do not get erased immediately
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_resolution.go`
    - fixed drift detection so an explicitly empty reported value still registers against a non-empty desired hub setting
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_runtime_state_test.go`
    - added hub-side settings coverage for local-only normalization rejection, expected-fingerprint propagation, same-revision lifecycle preservation, empty-string drift detection, and history payload correctness after failed apply plus state report
- Workflow status after this wave:
  - `LT-LAGT-14` settings apply/state lifecycle moved to `pass`
  - highest remaining audit priorities are now `LT-LAGT-17` to `LT-LAGT-19`, then `LT-LAGT-25`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(ApplyAgentSettingsRejectsLocalOnlyKey|HandleAgentSettingsApplyRejectsDisabledRemoteOverridesAndReportsState|HandleAgentSettingsApplyRejectsFingerprintMismatchAndReportsCurrentState|HandleAgentSettingsApplyReportsRestartRequiredAndPersistsValues|NormalizeAgentSettingValuesRejectsLocalOnlyForHubApply|PushAgentSettingsApplyIncludesStoredFingerprint|ProcessAgentSettingsStatePreservesApplyLifecycleForMatchingRevision|BuildAgentSettingsPayloadMarksDriftWhenAgentReportsEmptyValue|HandleAgentSettingsHistoryPreservesApplyFailureAfterStateReport)' -count=1`
    - passed

    - passed (`4.1%` targeted coverage)
  - `go test -cover ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(NormalizeAgentSettingValuesRejectsLocalOnlyForHubApply|PushAgentSettingsApplyIncludesStoredFingerprint|ProcessAgentSettingsStatePreservesApplyLifecycleForMatchingRevision|BuildAgentSettingsPayloadMarksDriftWhenAgentReportsEmptyValue|HandleAgentSettingsHistoryPreservesApplyFailureAfterStateReport)' -count=1`
    - passed (`0.8%` targeted coverage)

## 2026-03-08 (ios tailscale hub upgrade prompt)
- Added a post-login iOS prompt that offers switching a saved LAN hub origin to the hub's advertised Tailscale endpoint instead of silently rewriting the stored URL.
- The prompt appears only after successful authentication, shows both the current and suggested origins, and makes it explicit that accepting the upgrade returns the app to login on the new origin.
- Refined the prompt to better match the iOS app's premium glass aesthetic, with a clearer "why switch" explanation for roaming/mobile use and stronger visual separation between the current LAN URL and the recommended Tailnet URL.
- Followed up with a spacing and hierarchy pass so the full-screen upgrade prompt is easier to scan and remains comfortable on smaller iPhones by using a scroll-safe layout and tighter copy.
- Added a debug-only screenshot harness for the Hub Upgrade prompt: launch arguments can now force the screen open with fixed sample URLs, and `scripts/ios-console-screenshot.sh` builds, boots, launches, and captures a simulator screenshot in one step.
- While validating the screenshot harness, fixed an existing clean arm64 simulator compile issue in `MobileTelemetryCenter` by replacing invalid `Duration.timeInterval` usage with an explicit `Duration` to seconds conversion.
- Reused the existing `/api/v1/discover` metadata and Tailscale candidate parsing in the mobile client to decide when an upgrade suggestion is warranted.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' build`
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' test -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/HubConfigTests`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` and `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/auth-login-issues.md`
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is a mobile auth UX refinement around existing discovery metadata, not an architectural change

## 2026-03-08 (remote viewer toolbar layout rethink)
- Reworked the embedded remote viewer layout so LabTether controls no longer sit on top of the remote desktop canvas during normal page use.
- The viewer shell now splits into a clean viewer stage plus a bottom control dock for embedded sessions.
- Fullscreen now keeps the viewer stretched to the full viewport and uses a floating overlay toolbar anchored to the bottom by default, with explicit `TOP` / `BOT` and `AUTO` controls for position and auto-hide.
- Collapsed the shortcut-heavy fullscreen actions into a `More` popover so the overlay stays compact during fullscreen sessions.
- Tightened the fullscreen overlay further by switching to compact status badges and moving display selection into `More`.
- Replaced the fullscreen text toggles with cleaner icon buttons plus tooltips for top/bottom placement and auto-hide.
- Restored VNC remote-input readiness after toolbar interaction by refocusing the active viewer before mouse capture, keyboard capture, and shortcut-send actions; clarified the mouse/keyboard tooltips to describe remote capture behavior.
- Updated remote desktop docs/wiki to describe the dock-first embedded layout and fullscreen overlay behavior.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts --grep "desktop WebRTC toolbar supports browser recording and clipboard sync|desktop fullscreen toolbar defaults to bottom and supports top toggle plus auto-hide"`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`, `docs/wiki/core-workflows/desktop-workflow.md`, and `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/vnc.md`
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is a remote viewer UX layout refinement, not an architectural change

## 2026-03-08 (ios login tls compatibility toggle)
- Added the existing `Allow Untrusted TLS` preference directly to the iOS login card so self-signed/private-cert labs can recover from trust failures before reaching the full settings screen.
- Reused the persisted `lt.settings.allowUntrustedTLS` app setting rather than introducing a second login-only flag, keeping login, terminal, desktop, and Settings behavior aligned.
- Updated operator docs and troubleshooting references to point at the login-screen toggle in addition to the Settings path.

  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is an operator-accessibility improvement on an existing trust-toggle behavior, not an architectural change

## 2026-03-08 (smoke test cleanup hardening)
- Hardened the shared smoke/integration cleanup contract so smoke validation stops leaving durable API state behind.
- Backend cleanup support shipped in:
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_session_handlers.go`
    - added `DELETE /terminal/sessions/{id}` so shell smoke/integration flows can remove created terminal sessions and command history.
  - `cmd/labtether/incidents_handlers.go`
    - added `DELETE /incidents/{id}` for smoke-created incidents, including best-effort unlinking of incident asset links before removal when a dependency store is present.
  - `cmd/labtether/actions_handlers.go`
    - added `DELETE /actions/runs/{id}` so smoke-created action runs are removable instead of waiting on retention.
  - `cmd/labtether/updates_handlers.go`
    - added `DELETE /updates/runs/{id}` for explicit smoke/update run cleanup in addition to plan-delete cascades.
  - `internal/persistence/types.go`
  - `internal/persistence/memory_action_update_store.go`
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_actions_updates.go`
  - `internal/persistence/memory_incident_store.go`
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_incidents_store.go`
    - added corresponding persistence delete methods for action runs, update runs, and incidents.
- Smoke harness cleanup shipped in:
  - `scripts/lib/smoke-common.sh`
    - added nested JSON run-id extraction helper for queued job responses.
  - `scripts/smoke-test.sh`
    - switched fixture IDs to a per-run token instead of shared hard-coded smoke asset IDs.
    - smoke runs now register cleanup for terminal sessions, incidents, action runs, update runs, and existing created fixtures.
    - retention settings are snapshotted before mutation and restored during `EXIT` cleanup.
    - smoke now fails loudly if it cannot capture the IDs/settings required for teardown.
  - `scripts/integration-queue-flow.sh`
    - now registers cleanup for its created terminal session, action run, update run, and update plan.
- Test/teardown support:
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_api_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/incidents_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/actions_updates_api_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/test_helpers_test.go`
    - added focused delete-handler coverage and extended test cleanup to remove action runs, update runs, incidents, and terminal sessions.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
    - documented that smoke validation now uses per-run fixture IDs and cleans up its created resources/settings on exit.
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/smoke-test.sh`
    - passed
  - `bash -n scripts/integration-queue-flow.sh`
    - passed
  - `bash -n scripts/lib/smoke-common.sh`
    - passed
  - `./scripts/smoke-test.sh --help`
    - passed
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestSessionDeleteRemovesSession|TestHandleIncidentActionsDeleteRemovesIncident|TestActionRunDeleteByID|TestUpdateRunDeleteByID|TestCreateSession|TestHandleSessionsFiltersListForNonOwnerActor|TestActionRunGetByID|TestUpdateRunGetByID|TestRetentionSettingsGetAndUpdate' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `go test ./internal/persistence ./internal/terminal -count=1`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/` and `docs/wiki/` because smoke-validation behavior changed for operators.
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is cleanup/validation tooling hardening, not an architectural change.

## 2026-03-08 (desktop runtime smoke harness with cleanup)
- Added a cleanup-safe live desktop smoke workflow so runtime desktop verification is no longer a manual sequence.
- Runtime cleanup shipped in:
  - `internal/terminal/store.go`
    - added session deletion so desktop smoke runs can remove the created session record and any associated command list state
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers.go`
    - added `DELETE /desktop/sessions/{id}` for owner-scoped desktop session cleanup
    - desktop deletion now tears down agent desktop bridges, WebRTC signaling bridges, stored desktop session options, and cached SPICE proxy targets before removing the session
  - `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go`
    - factored a reusable `sendWebRTCStop` helper so explicit desktop-session deletion can notify an attached agent to stop a live WebRTC session
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers_test.go`
    - added cleanup coverage proving desktop session delete removes the session, clears reconnect/runtime state, and closes both desktop + WebRTC bridges
- Operator workflow shipped in:
  - `scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh`
    - added a live desktop smoke command that creates a desktop session, validates protocol-specific ticket responses, probes the returned desktop WebSocket, optionally probes the agent-backed VNC audio sideband, deletes the session, and verifies the session is no longer accessible
  - `Makefile`
    - added `make desktop-smoke-test`
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/operations/release-readiness-checklist.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
    - documented how to run the desktop smoke target with `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET` and agent-backed VNC expectations
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/terminal -run 'TestHandleDesktopSessionActionsDeleteCleansUpSessionState|TestHandleDesktopStreamTicketIssuesAgentVNCPasswordWithoutQueryLeak|TestStore' -count=1`
    - passed
  - `./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --help`
    - passed
  - `bash -n scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh`
    - passed
  - live runtime verification:
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=containervm-deltaserver LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_EXPECT_AGENT_VNC=1 ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - passed
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=containervm-deltaserver LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_EXPECT_AGENT_VNC=1 LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROBE_AUDIO=1 ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - passed
    - runtime notes:
      - the harness now auto-detects local HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect mode, probes websocket upgrades with bearer auth against the direct hub endpoint, and successfully validated VNC video + audio sideband cleanup on `containervm-deltaserver`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:

  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is an operational cleanup path and smoke harness, not an architecture decision

## 2026-03-08 (desktop smoke preflight for stale agent inventory)
- Hardened the live desktop smoke harness so agent-backed and WebRTC validation fails early when inventory is stale instead of surfacing a stream-time `502`.
- Updated:
  - `scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh`
    - now requires `jq` and preflights `GET /assets/{id}` plus `GET /agents/connected` before opening a desktop session
    - added `--list-targets` so operators can enumerate likely desktop smoke candidates with source/platform/status/connected-agent state before choosing a target
    - `--list-targets` now also shows advertised `webrtc_available` status, any published `webrtc_unavailable_reason`, and agent-reported local connector reachability from `/agents/presence`
    - fails early for:
      - WebRTC targets whose agent is not currently connected
      - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_EXPECT_AGENT_VNC=1` targets whose agent is not currently connected
      - agent-sourced VNC targets whose agent is not connected when direct VNC proxy is disabled
    - prints the target source/status/platform/connected-agent state as part of the smoke result for easier runtime triage
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/operations/release-readiness-checklist.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Agent/runtime WebRTC diagnostics shipped in:
  - `internal/agentmgr/message.go`
    - added `unavailable_reason` to `WebRTCCapabilitiesData`
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect.go`
    - now classifies unavailable WebRTC states as:
      - `unsupported_platform:<goos>`
      - `gst_launch_not_found`
      - `gst_inspect_not_found`
      - `no_supported_video_encoder`
  - `internal/agentcore/run.go`
    - now publishes `webrtc_unavailable_reason` in heartbeat metadata when WebRTC is unavailable
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_runtime_signal_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go`
    - now preserve and log the WebRTC unavailable reason on the live agent connection metadata
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers_test.go`
    - added focused coverage for unsupported-platform reason generation plus heartbeat/capability propagation of the reason field
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh`
    - passed
  - `./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --help`
    - passed
  - `./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --list-targets`
    - passed
  - focused runtime/tests:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(VideoEncoderPriority|BuildGStreamerVideoPipeline|BuildGStreamerAudioPipeline|DetectX11DisplayIdentifiers|UnsupportedPlatformWebRTCReason|ResolveWebRTCDisplay)$' -count=1`
      - passed
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestProcessAgentHeartbeatStoresWebRTCUnavailabilityReason|TestProcessAgentWebRTCCapabilitiesStoresUnavailableReason|TestProcessAgentHeartbeatIgnoresPayloadAssetID' -count=1`
      - passed
  - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=containervm-deltaserver LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_EXPECT_AGENT_VNC=1 ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
    - passed
  - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=Michael-MBP.local ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
    - failed at preflight as intended with `asset ... is agent-sourced but not currently in /agents/connected`
  - after relaunching `build/LabTether Agent.app`:
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=Michael-MBP.local LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_EXPECT_AGENT_VNC=1 ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - passed
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=Michael-MBP.local LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_EXPECT_AGENT_VNC=1 LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROBE_AUDIO=1 ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - passed
  - corrected WebRTC verification behavior:
    - the harness now refuses `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROTOCOL=webrtc` when the target does not currently advertise `webrtc_available=true`
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=containervm-deltaserver LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROTOCOL=webrtc ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - fails at preflight as intended because `webrtc_available=false`
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=Michael-MBP.local LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROTOCOL=webrtc ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - fails at preflight as intended because `webrtc_available=false`
- Runtime note:
  - `Michael-MBP.local` is still present in asset inventory as `status=online`, but it is not in `/agents/connected`; the new preflight makes that mismatch explicit before any desktop session or stream probe is attempted
  - relaunching the local macOS menu bar app restored `Michael-MBP.local` to `/agents/connected`, and the same cross-platform smoke workflow then passed for VNC video, audio sideband, and session cleanup
  - current `--list-targets` output in this environment shows two live agent-backed desktop candidates:
    - `Michael-MBP.local`
    - `containervm-deltaserver`
  - both currently advertise `webrtc_available=false`, so the environment does not have real live WebRTC coverage yet even though a raw `protocol=webrtc` request would otherwise fall back to VNC at stream time
  - after rebuilding the local mac menu bar app with `make mac-agent` and relaunching it from the rebuilt bundle executable, `Michael-MBP.local` now publishes:
    - `webrtc_available=false`
    - `webrtc_unavailable_reason=unsupported_platform:darwin`
  - corresponding live preflight evidence:
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=Michael-MBP.local LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROTOCOL=webrtc ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - fails at preflight with `reason=unsupported_platform:darwin`
  - after building fresh Linux agent binaries with `make build-agent-linux` and triggering the supported self-update flow via `POST /api/v1/agents/containervm-deltaserver/settings/update-agent`, `containervm-deltaserver` now republishes:
    - `webrtc_available=false`
    - `webrtc_unavailable_reason=gst_launch_not_found`
  - corresponding live Linux verification:
    - `./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --list-targets`
      - now shows `containervm-deltaserver` with `WEBRTC REASON gst_launch_not_found`
    - `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_TARGET=containervm-deltaserver LABTETHER_DESKTOP_SMOKE_PROTOCOL=webrtc ./scripts/desktop-smoke-test.sh --verbose`
      - fails at preflight with `reason=gst_launch_not_found`
    - remote terminal inspection against `containervm-deltaserver` confirmed:
      - `systemctl status labtether-agent --no-pager` shows the refreshed service restarted cleanly and reconnected to `wss://100.100.216.70:8443/ws/agent`
      - `ls /usr/bin/gst-launch-1.0`
        - fails with `No such file or directory`
      - `ls /usr/bin/gst-inspect-1.0`
        - fails with `No such file or directory`
  - this narrows the live Linux WebRTC blocker from "stale agent metadata" to a concrete host dependency gap: GStreamer CLI tools are not installed on `containervm-deltaserver`
  - `containervm-deltaserver` currently reports reachable local `docker,truenas` connectors via `/agents/presence`, but only the `docker` hub collector is actually configured
  - `--list-targets` also now prints configured hub collectors, which makes the current blocker explicit here:
    - only the `docker` collector is configured
    - there are no connector-backed desktop targets in `/assets` yet, so non-agent desktop smoke remains blocked on environment setup rather than script/runtime correctness
  - with the current live stack, desktop smoke now has real end-to-end evidence for:
    - VNC on Linux
    - VNC + audio sideband on Linux
    - VNC on macOS
    - VNC + audio sideband on macOS
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/` and `docs/wiki/` because the operator-facing smoke workflow now has a clearer preflight/readiness contract
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is runtime validation hardening, not an architecture change

## 2026-03-08 (frontend audit wave: VNC reconnect, exhaustion, credentials, auto-password, auto-password fallback, and audio coverage)
- Closed the remaining desktop remote-view audit follow-up on `main` by fixing the stored-password reconnect regression and adding dedicated Playwright coverage for VNC reconnect lifecycle behavior, reconnect exhaustion/`Try Again`, VNC credentials/auth-failure recovery, stored session-password auto-submit, stored session-password auto-submit reset on reconnect, stored session-password fallback-to-prompt behavior, and VNC audio unavailable/recovery behavior.
- Coverage shipped in:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useDesktopCredentials.ts`
    - reset the one-shot stored-password auto-submit guard whenever a new desktop connection attempt starts so reconnects on the same node continue to auto-authenticate when the stream ticket carries the same VNC password
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts`
    - fixed desktop reconnect exhaustion so the final failed automatic retry now lands on the `Try Again` overlay instead of silently falling back to an idle/error state
    - marked auth/credential/password disconnect reasons as non-retryable so VNC auth failures stay on the operator-facing `Retry` path instead of being immediately obscured by auto-reconnect
  - `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`
    - reintroduced the dedicated desktop remote-view suite in this checkout and expanded it to cover:
      - protocol switching that clears stale VNC display state before WebRTC connect
      - VNC monitor-picker forwarding in session creation
      - WebRTC recording and clipboard toolbar flows
      - VNC `Reconnect Now` behavior after an abnormal disconnect, including preservation of the chosen monitor and cancellation of the queued retry timer
      - VNC reconnect exhaustion after all `5` retries, including the `Try Again` recovery path
      - VNC credentials-required prompt, auth failure, and successful retry flow
      - VNC password-only `credentialsrequired` challenge that auto-submits the stream-ticket session password without showing the credential overlay
      - VNC password-only auto-submit succeeding again after a disconnect/reconnect on the same mounted desktop tab
      - VNC password-only auto-submit that falls back to the visible password prompt when the first stored-password attempt does not satisfy the server on the same connection
      - VNC audio unavailable state followed by recovery on reconnect, including toolbar disable/restore behavior for mute and volume controls
    - added a lightweight browser-side noVNC/RFB shim plus desktop audio mocks so the suite can exercise real reconnect and sideband state transitions without needing a live VNC backend in Playwright
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
    - passed
  - `cd web/console && npm run build`
    - passed
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npm run test:e2e -- e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`
    - passed (`10` tests).
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this pass adds frontend audit coverage, a browser-test seam, stored-password verification, and reconnect-state correctness fixes without changing the documented product contract.

## 2026-03-08 (frontend audit wave: deep desktop + heavy-suite closure)
- Closed the remaining frontend audit gates on `main` by fixing the desktop WebRTC fallback timer regression, expanding the desktop audit coverage, and refreshing the visual baselines after bringing the visual harness mocks back into parity with the current console routes.
- Fixed the desktop fallback regression:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useProtocolSwitch.ts`
    - stabilized the poor-quality WebRTC fallback timer with `useEffectEvent` and stable fallback-condition dependencies so background rerenders no longer prevent the `12s` VNC fallback window from completing.
  - `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`
    - added a dedicated poor-quality WebRTC fallback failure regression alongside the existing protocol defaulting, VNC display forwarding, and WebRTC recording/clipboard coverage.
- Closed the heavy visual/e2e follow-up:
  - `web/console/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts`
    - added missing mocks for `GET /api/auth/users`, `GET /api/services/web`, and `POST /api/telemetry/perf`.
    - refreshed the chromium/darwin visual baselines for settings, alerts, logs, health, and devices across `oled`, `dark`, and `light`.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npm run test:e2e -- e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`
    - passed (`4` tests).
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npm run test:e2e -- e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts`
    - passed (`15` tests).
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npm run test:e2e -- e2e/soak.spec.ts`
    - passed (`1` test).
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this pass fixed frontend timing correctness, completed audit coverage, and refreshed test baselines without changing the shipped product contract.

## 2026-03-08 (frontend audit wave: desktop remote-view follow-up)
- Added dedicated desktop remote-view coverage in `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts` for:

    - persisted whether a saved protocol was auto-selected vs explicit and now only restores explicit protocol choices from `sessionStorage`.

    - corrected the WebRTC mock track event typing and upgraded the mock stream path so the browser-side recording/clipboard assertions run against a realistic `MediaStream`.

## 2026-03-08 (ios terminal saved sessions: favorites, recents, and hub-backed SSH profiles)

## 2026-03-08 (systematic codebase audit remediation wave 1)
- Closed the first batch of audit findings on `main` across backend routing, remote-viewer frontend behavior, agent/platform parity, and the security gate.
- Backend fixes:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`
    - restored router-contract coverage for clipboard message types so the backend baseline matches the live agent router again.
  - `cmd/labtether/clipboard_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/clipboard_handlers_test.go`
    - bound clipboard replies to the expected agent asset ID and added regressions for mismatched vs matching agents.
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_routing_proxy.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_spice_proxy.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`
    - stopped clearing desktop session options on the first stream/ticket attempt so reconnects retain protocol/display state.
- Frontend fixes:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useAudioSideband.ts`
    - mute/volume changes now update the active audio element without reconnecting the VNC audio sideband.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useProtocolSwitch.ts`
    - automatic WebRTC fallback now uses an in-progress notice instead of claiming VNC already connected before the reconnect completes.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/DesktopTab.tsx`
    - removed the stale `browserRecording` hook dependency.
    - limited display forwarding to VNC-only flows so WebRTC no longer receives monitor-picker labels as X11 display IDs.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/DesktopConnectionControlsCard.tsx`
    - limited the pre-connect monitor picker to VNC so the control no longer implies unsupported WebRTC per-display targeting.
- Agent/platform fixes:
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler_test.go`
    - sanitized requested WebRTC display values so non-X11 monitor-picker labels fall back to an advertised X display instead of breaking capture startup.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`
    - macOS now treats WebRTC as unsupported in the embedded runtime and presents the settings as a Linux-only informational disabled state instead of implying unsupported local capture parity.
  - `internal/agentcore/display_list_windows.go`
    - Windows display enumeration now uses PowerShell `System.Windows.Forms.Screen` data instead of `Win32_VideoController`.
  - `internal/agentcore/display_parse.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/display_handler_test.go`
    - added Windows screen-parsing coverage for monitor count, geometry, offsets, and primary display flags.
  - `security/gosec_allowlist.tsv`
    - refreshed reviewed `gosec` allowlist entries to current file/line locations so the audited security gate reflects the live tree again.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestKnownMessageTypes|TestProcessAgentClipboardDataIgnoresUnexpectedAgent|TestProcessAgentClipboardDataAcceptsExpectedAgent|TestHandleDesktopStreamRejectsDirectProxyWhenDisabled' -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestParsePowerShellScreenDisplays|TestParseXrandrMonitors' -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore/... -count=1`
  - `go test ./...`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `make security-gosec`
- Closure follow-up:
  - re-ran the previously suspected flaky TrueNAS backoff subtest ten times and it stayed green, so that finding was closed as non-reproducible on current `main`.
  - removed the reintroduced dead terminal replay buffer file and verified `go run honnef.co/go/tools/cmd/staticcheck@latest ./cmd/labtether/...` passes.
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` update required because the operator docs already matched the intended behavior and this wave fixed implementation drift rather than changing the product contract.

## 2026-03-08 (backend audit remediation: hot-path fan-out, backpressure, incident/reliability query reductions)
- Finished the remaining backend audit fixes after the security/auth slice, focusing on blocking fan-out paths and avoidable query amplification.
- Hot-path and transport changes:
  - `cmd/labtether/notification_delivery.go`
    - alert notification delivery now runs asynchronously behind a bounded dispatcher so slow channels do not stall alert evaluation.
  - `cmd/labtether/browser_events.go`
    - browser websocket fan-out now uses per-client queues and evicts backpressured clients instead of serially blocking broadcasts.
  - `cmd/labtether/file_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/file_handlers_read_write.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/file_handlers_manage.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/file_handlers_responses.go`
    - file-response bridges now keep messages instead of silently dropping chunks under backpressure, including download paths.
  - `cmd/labtether/startup_worker.go`
    - worker concurrency is now configurable via `JOB_WORKERS` and defaults to 4 goroutines instead of 1.
- Query/load reductions:
  - `cmd/labtether/alert_evaluator_instances.go`
  - `internal/persistence/memory_incident_store.go`
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_incidents_store.go`
  - `internal/persistence/types.go`
    - auto-incident dedupe now uses direct incident-link existence checks instead of per-incident link scans.
  - `cmd/labtether/site_features_reliability.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/reliability_materializer.go`
    - reliability materialization now computes shared inputs once per pass and reuses prefetched site guardrails.
  - `cmd/labtether/alert_evaluator_checks.go`
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_telemetry_logs.go`
  - `internal/persistence/memory_telemetry_store.go`
    - alert metric/deadman/log-pattern evaluation now uses narrower telemetry/log queries and batch presence checks where available.
- Tests:
  - `cmd/labtether/notification_delivery_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/performance_hotspots_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/audit_fixes_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/recording_handlers_test.go`

## 2026-03-08 (web console perf follow-up: status rerender churn and terminal session reuse)
- Closed the next frontend performance follow-up after the robustness sweep by removing two sources of repeated work in the operator console.
- Status/runtime updates:
  - `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`
    - normalized live/full status payload parsing before merge so malformed responses still degrade safely.
    - added structural equality guards so unchanged live polls and unchanged full-status refreshes reuse the previous `status` object instead of forcing whole-console rerenders every cycle.
    - restored the visibility reconnect path so a hidden-tab websocket close reconnects promptly when the tab becomes visible again.
- Terminal workspace updates:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/terminal/page.tsx`
    - kept terminal tab panels mounted and keyed pane refs by `tabId + paneIndex` so switching tabs no longer remounts panes or recreates existing sessions.
    - limited broadcast/snippet targeting to the active tab and stabilized pane callback dependencies.
  - `web/console/app/components/terminal/TerminalPane.tsx`
    - only auto-connects an idle pane once its tab is active, which preserves hidden tabs without eagerly opening extra sessions.
- Tests:
  - `web/console/e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts`
    - added coverage to verify returning to an existing terminal tab reuses the prior session instead of creating a second one.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts -g 'terminal multipane layout switching updates pane count and persists|terminal creates a default workspace tab when none exist|terminal creates a default workspace tab when the tabs payload is malformed|terminal tab switches reuse an existing session when returning to a pane'`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/resilience.spec.ts -g 'status websocket retries after connection failures|hidden-tab pauses fast polling and visible-tab resumes without duplicate loops'`
- Docs/wiki/ADR:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this was an internal frontend perf/resilience pass with no operator workflow or API contract change.

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent audit follow-up: safe diagnostics export, masked enrollment token, trusted hero-state presentation)
- Closed the next set of mac-agent audit issues around operator-facing status truth and secret handling.
- Shared presentation/reporting helpers:

    - hero label/subtitle/color now follow the shared presentation resolver.
    - `Copy Diagnostics` now exports buffered log counts and an explicit redaction note instead of raw recent log lines.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutView.swift`
    - hero presentation now matches the menu view and no longer claims `Connected` when the authenticated local-status layer is unavailable or auth-failed.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`
    - enrollment token entry is now rendered as a secure field instead of plaintext.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`
    - forwards `status` and `settings` changes into the menu bar label refresh path and forces a refresh when the menu closes so display-mode/icon changes do not stay stale.

    - added hero-presentation precedence coverage for auth-failed/unavailable states.

## 2026-03-08 (remote viewer parity — Phase 2 VNC enhancements)
- Continued the remote viewer parity branch with the first Phase 2 VNC feature wave across hub, agent, and web console.
- Delivered:

  - adaptive quality suggestions in the shared remote viewer shell,
  - remote file download from the viewer toolbar,
  - multi-monitor display enumeration + picker,
  - desktop audio sideband for agent-backed VNC using a dedicated `/desktop/sessions/{id}/audio` WebSocket.

    - added clipboard + desktop-audio protocol messages/data payloads.
  - `internal/agentcore/clipboard_*.go`

    - added Linux desktop-audio capture with ffmpeg/GStreamer fallback and unavailable reporting on unsupported platforms.
  - `cmd/labtether/clipboard_handlers.go`
    - added hub clipboard relay endpoints for agent-backed nodes.
  - `cmd/labtether/display_handlers.go`
    - added monitor enumeration endpoint for desktop sessions.
  - `cmd/labtether/audio_sideband_handlers.go`
    - added dedicated audio WebSocket relay for VNC sideband playback.
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers.go`
    - desktop stream-ticket responses now include `audio_stream_path` for agent-backed VNC sessions.
- Web console:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useClipboardSync.ts`
    - added toolbar-driven clipboard pull/push flow.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useDisplayList.ts`
    - added remote monitor enumeration for the display picker.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useAdaptiveQuality.ts`
    - added viewer quality suggestions + auto-apply mode.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useFileDownload.ts`
    - added remote file download helper.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useAudioSideband.ts`
    - added VNC audio sideband playback via a separate browser WebSocket and streamed Opus/Ogg playback.
  - `web/console/app/components/RemoteViewToolbar.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/components/RemoteViewerShell.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/DesktopTab.tsx`
    - wired clipboard, display picker, file download, adaptive quality, and VNC audio controls into the shared remote viewer shell.
- Docs/wiki/ADR:

  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/vnc.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`

## 2026-03-08 (backend security/auth remediation slice: aggregate scoping, desktop boundaries, outbound DNS hardening)
- Closed the requested backend security/auth findings without widening scope into the separate performance work.
- Terminal aggregate scoping:
  - `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_orchestration.go`
    - `/status/aggregate` now filters terminal sessions, recent commands, and recent audit by actor for non-owner callers.
    - aggregate fingerprints, ETags, and singleflight keys now include terminal-data scope so cached aggregate state cannot bleed across operators.
- Desktop recordings:
  - `cmd/labtether/recording_handlers.go`
    - recording start/stop now enforce desktop-session ownership with admin override semantics.
    - recording list visibility now follows desktop-session ownership semantics, with a fallback to recording actor if the session cannot be resolved.
    - recording API responses no longer expose raw `file_path` or `actor_id`.
- Desktop auth boundary consistency:
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/credentials_handlers.go`
    - desktop session creation and desktop credential get/retrieve/save/delete now all pass through the existing interactive `session_start` policy evaluation path.
    - managed-asset checks still happen before credential-policy denial so missing assets continue to return `404`.
- Bootstrap and outbound runtime hardening:
  - `cmd/labtether/auth_bootstrap.go`
    - removed plaintext generated-admin-password output to `stderr`; only a masked hint is logged.
  - `internal/securityruntime/outbound.go`
    - outbound validation now rejects hostnames whose DNS results land on loopback/private/link-local addresses when policy disallows them, including name-allowlisted hosts.
- Tests:
  - `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/recording_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/credentials_handlers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/auth_bootstrap_test.go`
  - `internal/securityruntime/outbound_test.go`
- Validation:
  - `gofmt -w cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_handlers.go cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_orchestration.go cmd/labtether/recording_handlers.go cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers.go cmd/labtether/credentials_handlers.go cmd/labtether/auth_bootstrap.go cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_handlers_test.go cmd/labtether/recording_handlers_test.go cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers_test.go cmd/labtether/credentials_handlers_test.go cmd/labtether/auth_bootstrap_test.go internal/securityruntime/outbound.go internal/securityruntime/outbound_test.go`
  - `go test ./internal/securityruntime`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleStatusAggregateScopesTerminalDataAndETagsByActor|TestAuthorizeRecordingSessionAccessHonorsOwnerAndAdminSemantics|TestCanAccessRecordingMetadataUsesDesktopSessionOwnership|TestRecordingResponsePayloadOmitsSensitiveMetadata|TestHandleDesktopSessionsAppliesInteractivePolicy|TestDesktopCredentialEndpointsApplyDesktopAuthorizationBoundary|TestBootstrapAdminUserDoesNotPrintGeneratedPasswordBannerToStderr'`

## 2026-03-09 (outbound policy follow-up: secure private HTTPS/WSS targets)
- Relaxed the outbound hostname policy just enough to fit normal homelab connector reality after a Home Assistant endpoint on a private-resolving HTTPS hostname was rejected.
- Updated:
  - `internal/securityruntime/command.go`
    - added bool-env parsing with presence detection so default policy can differ from an explicit operator override
  - `internal/securityruntime/outbound.go`
    - secure `https://` / `wss://` URLs now allow private-network targets by default
    - explicit `LABTETHER_OUTBOUND_ALLOW_PRIVATE=false` still restores the stricter deny-private behavior
    - loopback blocking and insecure-transport gating remain unchanged
  - `internal/securityruntime/outbound_test.go`
    - added coverage for secure private-host allow-by-default, explicit private disable, and loopback staying blocked
  - `docs/API.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - Home Assistant and similar secure connector URLs can point at legitimate private-network HTTPS/WSS hosts without extra env changes
  - operators still have an explicit env escape hatch when they want a stricter outbound posture
- Validation:
  - `gofmt -w internal/securityruntime/command.go internal/securityruntime/outbound.go internal/securityruntime/outbound_test.go`
  - `go test ./internal/securityruntime ./internal/connectors/homeassistant ./cmd/labtether`
    - passed
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated docs and ADR because this changes the effective outbound security posture for secure private-network connector targets

## 2026-03-08 (iOS LAN discovery follow-up: bounded subnet fallback plus Docker LAN publish defaults)
- Investigated report: iOS discovery was returning no LAN hubs even when the hub was healthy.
- Root cause was a two-part deployment/reachability gap:
  - Bonjour/mDNS is still best-effort and can disappear behind Docker bridge networking or multicast-hostile Wi-Fi gear.
  - the default Docker Compose stack published hub HTTPS only on `127.0.0.1`, so even a discovered host could be unreachable from a phone/tablet on the LAN.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Discovery/HubScanner.swift`
  - when Bonjour and MagicDNS both come back empty, discovery now runs a bounded fallback probe across the device's current private IPv4 `/24` on the default secure hub port (`8443`).
  - added interface filtering and subnet helpers so the sweep stays on private LAN addresses and avoids loopback/point-to-point interfaces.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/HubScannerTests.swift`
  - added coverage for `/24` host enumeration, broad-netmask clamping, and invalid-input rejection.
- `docker-compose.yml`
  - keeps hub HTTP redirect/health on loopback by default, but now publishes hub HTTPS (`8443`) on the LAN by default via `LABTETHER_HUB_HTTPS_PUBLISH_IP` so native/mobile clients can actually connect.
- `.env.example`
  - documented the new compose publish IP knobs.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/DiscoveryView.swift`
  - updated discovery copy to reflect Bonjour + local subnet probe + MagicDNS behavior.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/install-docker-compose.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/HubScannerTests -quiet`
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' -quiet`
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture decision change; this is a targeted discovery/reachability hardening pass across the mobile client and compose defaults.

## 2026-03-08 (web console frontend audit: null-payload hardening, terminal layering, focused e2e verification)
- Audited the `web/console` frontend for runtime crashes, stale locator drift, and recent lag/double-load regressions.
- Hardened API-boundary normalization so malformed/null collections no longer break operator flows:
  - `web/console/app/lib/responseGuards.ts`
    - added shared array/object/string guards for frontend fetch boundaries.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts`
    - normalized service discovery stats, grouping suggestions, overrides, and icon-library payloads.
    - fixed the LabTether bootstrap classifier so missing metadata no longer crashes `.trim()` checks.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWorkspaceTabs.ts`
    - normalized workspace tab and pane payloads so malformed tab collections fall back safely and can still auto-create a default tab.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useSettingsForm.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useReliability.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useEnrollment.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useIncidents.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useLogs.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useActions.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useCron.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useProcesses.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useServices.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useDisks.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useNetworkInterfaces.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/usePackages.ts`
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useUsers.ts`
    - normalized fetch payloads so `null` or malformed list/object responses degrade to safe empty states instead of crashing route components.
  - `web/console/app/components/AddDeviceModal/index.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/components/AddDeviceModal/collectorSync.ts`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/nodesPageComponents.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodeLogsData.ts`
    - removed remaining trust in nullable add-device, pending-agent, and node-log payloads.
- Closed interaction regressions uncovered by the audit:
  - `web/console/app/components/terminal/TerminalWorkspaceHeader.tsx`
    - raised the tab context menu overlay above the terminal header so rename/duplicate/close actions stay clickable after the earlier header z-index fix.
  - `web/console/e2e/resilience.spec.ts`
    - made the PBS task error-path assertion wait for the task details panel before checking backend error copy, removing a parallel-load flake.
- Added/kept regression coverage for the new edge cases:
  - `web/console/e2e/services-discovery-policy.spec.ts`
    - malformed service discovery collections plus `metadata: null` on the LabTether bootstrap service.
  - `web/console/e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts`
    - malformed terminal workspace tab payloads.
  - `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts`
    - null telemetry series points and null Docker stack container id lists.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint`
  - focused Playwright verification:
    - `npx playwright test e2e/services-discovery-policy.spec.ts`
    - `npx playwright test e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts -g 'terminal multipane layout switching updates pane count and persists|terminal creates a default workspace tab when none exist|terminal creates a default workspace tab when the tabs payload is malformed'`
    - `npx playwright test e2e/files-browser.spec.ts`
    - `npx playwright test e2e/resilience.spec.ts -g 'services triage filters and unstable sorting prioritize actionable items|status websocket retries after connection failures|hidden-tab pauses fast polling and visible-tab resumes without duplicate loops'`
    - `npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts -g 'dashboard fleet focus|device card navigation uses a single history entry per click|node metrics tolerates telemetry series with null points|docker stack detail tolerates null container id lists|add device portainer flow surfaces test-connection failures|add device pbs flow tests and saves connector settings|add device pbs flow surfaces test-connection failures|add device truenas flow tests and saves connector settings'`
  - note: the single large 74-test Playwright bundle hit a dev-server `ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` cascade under concurrency, so final verification was completed in focused slices to separate real regressions from harness instability.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` update required because this work hardens existing frontend behavior and tests without changing operator workflows, APIs, or architecture.

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent audit follow-up: legacy secret purge, stale-status invalidation, bounded session history)
- Closed the next mac-agent audit pass after the earlier security/lifecycle fixes and targeted the remaining operator-trust gaps.
- Secret lifecycle hardening:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettingsMigration.swift`
    - exposed the legacy-secret removal helper for reuse from the live settings layer.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`
    - clearing or re-saving a secret now also removes any surviving legacy `UserDefaults` fallback copy so deleted credentials cannot come back on relaunch.
- Local status truthfulness and session reset:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`
    - poll/auth failures now clear the cached `status` snapshot and related ETag/shadow state so stale alerts and update badges disappear when loopback status is no longer trustworthy.
    - starting/stopping a session now resets metrics history so charts do not blend data from an old embedded-agent run into the next one.
    - `MetricsHistory` now uses a fixed-capacity circular buffer instead of repeated `removeFirst()` eviction.
- Long-session log hygiene:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LogBufferView.swift`
    - replaced front-trimming log storage with a fixed-capacity circular buffer so long-lived log windows stop paying O(n) eviction costs.
- Tests:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/AgentSettingsMigrationTests.swift`
    - added coverage for removing a legacy secret from every defaults store consulted by migration/fallback logic.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/LocalAPIClientTests.swift`
    - added coverage for stale-status invalidation on poll failure, session history reset on stop, and fixed-capacity metrics retention.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/LogRenderingTests.swift`
    - added fixed-capacity log-buffer retention coverage.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updated:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md`
  - `docs/PLATFORM.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `make mac-agent`

## 2026-03-08 (iOS auth manual hub entry hardening: scheme-less URLs, inline validation, less misleading TLS errors)
- Investigated report: manually entering the hub address could dump operators into a broken login path and follow-on login failures were surfacing as misleading TLS trust guidance.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Config/HubConfig.swift`
  - added `normalizedManualHubURL(_:)` so scheme-less manual input like `labtether.local:8443` or `192.168.1.50:8443` normalizes to HTTPS before the existing origin-only validation rules run.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/AuthViewModel.swift`
  - split discovery/manual-entry validation errors from login failures via `discoveryErrorMessage` so bad manual input stays on the discovery screen instead of bouncing into the login card state.
  - updated auth transport messaging so certificate-like failures no longer tell operators to install a CA when `Allow Untrusted TLS` is already enabled; the app now points them back to the hub address / secure port path (`:8443`) instead.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/DiscoveryView.swift`
  - shows manual-entry validation errors inline under the Connect controls and clears them as the operator edits the address.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/APIClient.swift`
  - removed the remaining `URLComponents(... )!` / `components.url!` request-builder assumptions on query-bearing paths so malformed stored hub URLs cannot turn into a force-unwrap crash.
- Tests:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/HubConfigTests.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests.swift`
  - added coverage for scheme-less manual URLs, inline discovery errors, refined TLS error text, and malformed configured hub URL rejection.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/auth-login-issues.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/HubConfigTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests -quiet`
  - full-suite validation running at handoff time from the same simulator destination.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture decision change; this is a targeted iOS auth/manual-entry robustness fix.

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent audit follow-up: token precedence, hidden-state truthfulness, login-item reconciliation)
- Closed the remaining mac-agent audit findings that were still visible after the first hardening batch.
- Runtime/auth cleanup:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`
    - clearing the API token now always removes the runtime `agent-token` file instead of preserving a stale bearer token when enrollment is still configured.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`
    - reconciles `Start at Login` against the current `SMAppService` registration state on launch instead of only forcing the enabled path.
- Background status and auth clarity:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`
    - hidden polls now still publish lightweight status/reachability/auth state so the menu bar icon and label stay current while the menu is closed.
    - local `/agent/status` `401` responses now surface as `auth_failed`.
    - stopping the local client clears alert dedupe state so a fresh session can notify again.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarStatusIcon.swift`
    - treats `auth_failed` as an error badge even when no fresh metrics payload is available.
- Log correctness:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentProcess.swift`
    - switched `LogPipeChunkDecoder` from string-chunk assembly to byte buffering so split UTF-8 scalars are not dropped at pipe chunk boundaries.
- Tests:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/AgentSettingsNormalizationTests.swift`
    - added runtime API-token file action coverage.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/LocalAPIClientTests.swift`
    - added coverage for hidden poll publishing, auth-failed summary state, and alert dedupe reset on stop.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/LogRenderingTests.swift`

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent audit remediation: secret lifecycle, authenticated loopback status, restart/logging hardening)
- Turned the earlier mac-agent audit into a coordinated hardening pass across the Swift wrapper and shared `agentcore` runtime.
- Secret/runtime posture:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`
    - stopped exporting raw API/enrollment/TURN secrets to the child process environment.
    - now writes runtime secret files under `~/Library/Application Support/LabTether/` with `0700` parent / `0600` file permissions.
    - added file-backed local API auth token generation for the current app runtime.
    - surfaces keychain/runtime secret persistence failures as validation errors.
    - removes stale runtime token files when credentials are cleared.
    - defaulted remote hub overrides to off.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/KeychainSecretStore.swift`
    - added status/error reporting helpers so keychain save/delete failures are no longer silent.
  - `internal/agentcore/config.go`
    - loads API token, enrollment token, and TURN password from file-based env paths when direct env values are absent.
  - `internal/agentcore/runtime.go`
    - added `LABTETHER_AGENT_LOCAL_AUTH_TOKEN_FILE` support for the local status API auth token.
- Local status / diagnostics:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`
    - now attaches `Authorization: Bearer ...` when polling the loopback status API.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`
    - wired the generated local auth token into the local API client and cleaned up ephemeral local auth state on app termination.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`
    - redacted diagnostics export down to hub-origin summary plus configured/not-configured credential state.
- Process / lifecycle hardening:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentProcess.swift`
    - made restart termination-driven instead of fixed-delay polling.
    - classifies user-initiated stops/restarts as stopped rather than crashed.
    - parses log batches off-main and applies them to the UI in one main-actor hop.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LogBufferView.swift`
    - added batched append support and amortized overflow trimming for long log sessions.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutWindowController.swift`
    - removed top-level log-buffer observation from the detached panel root and cleans up the close observer token.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/ScreenSharingMonitor.swift`
    - fixed the enabled->disabled notification edge case and reduced hidden-surface polling cadence.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettingsNormalization.swift`
    - remote Docker endpoints must now use `https://`.
- Tests:
  - `internal/agentcore/config_secret_files_test.go`
    - added coverage for file-based config secret loading and file-based local auth token resolution.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/AgentSettingsNormalizationTests.swift`
    - updated Docker endpoint normalization coverage for the new `https://` requirement.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updated:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md`
  - `docs/PLATFORM.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore/...`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `make mac-agent`

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent log viewer polish + chunked line fix)
- Fixed a real mac wrapper log correctness bug: pipe reads no longer emit partial lines when `availableData` splits a log line across chunk boundaries.
- Added `LogPipeChunkDecoder` buffering in `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentProcess.swift` so trailing fragments are held until the next read and flushed cleanly on EOF.
- Reworked `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LogBufferView.swift` to match the premium mac-agent UI better:
  - structured rows with timestamp, source badge, severity edge, and cleaner message rendering,
  - ANSI/control-character cleanup for display,
  - richer glass toolbar/status bars, live-tail indicator, and per-level counters,
  - labeled gap separators and improved empty-state treatment.
- Added regression coverage in `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/LogRenderingTests.swift` for timestamp stripping, source extraction, ANSI cleanup, and chunked read reassembly.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`

## 2026-03-08 (web console node-detail null-list hardening sweep)
- Expanded the original monitoring crash fix into a broader node-detail payload hardening pass after finding the same frontend assumption pattern in Docker-backed asset detail routes and adjacent infrastructure tabs.
- Root cause pattern:
  - several node-detail fetch paths assumed backend list fields always arrived as concrete arrays and immediately consumed them with array operations or list-oriented rendering.
  - Go nil slices can serialize as `null`, so sparse Docker, Proxmox, PBS, and TrueNAS payloads could trigger client-side crashes even when the empty state was legitimate.
- Hardened Docker node-detail boundaries in `web/console/lib/docker.ts`:
  - added normalization for container, image, stack, and host detail payloads before they enter the UI.
  - `container_ids`, `tags`, `labels`, port arrays, mounts, and similar list fields now degrade to empty arrays instead of propagating `null`.
  - updated host/container/stack fetch helpers to return normalized models consistently.
- Hardened other node-detail tabs that consume backend detail payloads directly:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodeDetailTypes.ts`
    - added Proxmox normalization helpers for cluster status, network interfaces, snapshots, tasks, HA resources, firewall rules, backups, Ceph lists, storage content, and warnings.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodeProxmoxData.ts`
    - normalized Proxmox detail/cluster/network fetch responses at the fetch boundary before state updates.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/pbsTabModel.ts` and `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/PBSTab.tsx`
    - normalized PBS asset details, task status payloads, and task log lines.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/truenasTabModel.ts` and `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/TrueNASTab.tsx`
    - normalized SMART, events, and filesystem responses before rendering.
- Added a focused Docker regression in `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts` that opens a Docker stack detail route with `container_ids: null` and verifies the page renders without client-side errors.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx eslint --no-ignore --no-warn-ignored 'lib/docker.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodeDetailTypes.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodeProxmoxData.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/pbsTabModel.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/PBSTab.tsx' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/truenasTabModel.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/TrueNASTab.tsx' 'e2e/console.spec.ts'`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts -g 'docker stack detail tolerates null container id lists|node metrics tolerates telemetry series with null points'`
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this work only hardens frontend parsing of existing API payload edge cases and does not change workflow, setup, or contracts.

## 2026-03-08 (web console metrics null-points crash)
- Fixed a node monitoring crash in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodeMetricsAnalysisModel.ts` that happened when backend telemetry series serialized a Go nil slice as `"points": null`.
- Root cause:
  - `analyzeTelemetryDetails()` assumed every series carried a concrete `points` array and immediately called `.reduce()` on it.
  - container and sparse telemetry payloads can legitimately arrive with `points: null`, which caused the node detail monitoring panel to throw before rendering.
- Implemented a defensive normalization pass before analysis:
  - non-array `series` now collapse to an empty list.
  - malformed series entries are skipped.
  - `points: null` and other non-array point payloads now normalize to `[]`.
  - malformed point values are dropped instead of poisoning the whole metric card.
- Added a focused Playwright regression in `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts` that loads `/nodes/<id>?panel=monitoring` with a mocked telemetry response containing `points: null` and verifies the page renders metric panels without page errors.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx eslint --no-ignore --no-warn-ignored 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodeMetricsAnalysisModel.ts' 'e2e/console.spec.ts'`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts -g 'node metrics tolerates telemetry series with null points'`
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this was a frontend robustness fix for an existing telemetry payload edge case with no workflow or API contract change.

## 2026-03-08 (web console device navigation lag + double-load feel)
- Traced the sluggish console navigation to two layout-level causes in `web/console/app/(console)/layout.tsx`:
  - the ambient cursor glow was driven by React state on every pointer move, which re-rendered the whole console tree continuously while the mouse moved.
  - the console shell wrapped every route in a pathname-keyed `AnimatePresence` transition, forcing an exit-then-enter remount animation on every page change and making device click-through feel like a second load.
- Implemented the fix in the shared console shell:
  - replaced cursor glow state updates with a ref-backed `requestAnimationFrame` transform update so the effect stays smooth without invalidating page content.
  - removed the pathname-keyed route transition wrapper so page changes render directly instead of waiting through an extra exit/enter cycle.
- Added a device navigation regression in `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts` to verify each device-card click produces a single browser history entry and returns to `/nodes` with one Back action.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx eslint --no-ignore --no-warn-ignored 'app/(console)/layout.tsx'`
  - `cd web/console && npx eslint --no-ignore --no-warn-ignored 'e2e/console.spec.ts'`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts -g 'device card navigation uses a single history entry per click'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint` still fails on pre-existing unrelated issues in `app/(console)/topology/TopologyGraphPanel.tsx`, `app/(console)/topology/topologyGraphModel.tsx`, and `app/hooks/useWorkspaceTabs.ts`.
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this was an internal responsiveness/navigation fix with no workflow, setup, API, or architecture change.

- Replaced the mac-agent menu bar's disconnected/broken-looking SF Symbol path with a compact custom LabTether tether glyph plus small state badge.
- State mapping now stays visually consistent across healthy, reconnecting, starting, warning, critical, alert, disconnected, error, and stopped conditions.
- Reachable-but-disconnected hub state now renders as a muted hollow badge instead of a slashed antenna, making the "not connected" state look intentional rather than broken.
- Added resolver regression coverage in `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/MenuBarIconResolverTests.swift`.
- Docs/wiki updated:

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent scroll jank remediation)
- Traced the remaining mac-agent scroll lag to three hot paths in the current SwiftUI tree:
  - large `LTGlassCard` surfaces inside `ScrollView` were hover-reactive by default, so macOS hover enter/exit churn was animating fill, border, shadow, and offset while content moved under a stationary pointer.
  - long menu/pop-out/settings scroll stacks still used eager `VStack` layout.
  - `PopOutView` was observing `LogBuffer` at the root, so incoming log lines could invalidate the whole dashboard while scrolling.
- Implemented the fix in the mac client:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/Components.swift`
    - made `LTGlassCard` hover lift opt-in so non-interactive cards remain visually stable while scrolling.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`
    - switched the popover scroll content container to `LazyVStack`.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutView.swift`
    - switched the main scroll content container to `LazyVStack`.
    - isolated recent-events rendering into a dedicated child view and stopped observing `LogBuffer` at the pop-out root.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`
    - converted the tab content and per-tab section stacks to `LazyVStack` so off-screen settings sections do not layout/render during scroll.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`
    - added `assignIfChanged` gating for visible-state publishes and `Equatable` payload models so no-op `304`/disconnect assignments do not trigger extra redraw churn.
- Validation:
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/mac-agent`
  - `make mac-agent`
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were needed because this was an internal mac-agent responsiveness fix with no workflow, setup, API, or architecture change.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Services edit parity: rename + alternative URLs + override sync)
- Implemented iOS service-settings parity flow in `ServiceDetailView` with the existing native visual style:

  - override fields: display name, category, URL override, tags override, hidden toggle.
  - alternative URL management: list, add, remove (manual source), with live source badges.
  - reset-to-auto path that deletes persisted overrides.
- Added iOS-side backend sync plumbing:
  - `ServicesViewModel` mutation methods for save/reset overrides and alt URL list/add/remove.
  - `APIClient.send(_:method:body:)` for non-POST mutation verbs without response decoding.
  - `WebService` model expansion for `service_key`, `source`, `host_asset_id`, `metadata`, and `alt_urls`.
  - added override/alt-url request-response models in networking layer.
- Added decoding regression coverage in `LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests.swift` for current backend service shape including `alt_urls`, metadata tags/hidden, and source/service key fields.
- Validation:
