## 2026-03-08 (remote viewer Parsec/TeamViewer parity — Phase 1: UX Foundation)
- Designed and implemented Phase 1 of remote viewer parity initiative to bring the web console's desktop viewer up to feature parity with Parsec/TeamViewer.
- Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-08-remote-viewer-parity-design.md`
- Implementation plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-08-remote-viewer-parity-plan.md`
- **13 new files / significant modifications** across types, hooks, and components:
  - `web/console/app/types/viewer.ts` — shared ViewerHandle interface, ViewerMetrics, KeyboardGrabState, REMOTE_SHORTCUTS (5 shortcuts with X11 keysyms), ReconnectState types.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useKeyboardGrab.ts` — Keyboard Lock API + pointer lock with Ctrl+Alt+Shift release combo.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useViewerMetrics.ts` — unified metrics: VNC (rAF frame counting), WebRTC (getStats() 1s polling).
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useCursorAutoHide.ts` — hides cursor after 3s inactivity in fullscreen.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useTouchGestures.ts` — tap/double-tap/long-press/scroll/pinch/drag gesture mapping.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useVirtualKeyboard.ts` — hidden input element for on-screen keyboard on touch devices.
  - `web/console/app/components/PerformanceOverlay.tsx` — real-time HUD (FPS/latency/bitrate/codec/resolution) with Ctrl+F1 toggle.
  - `web/console/app/components/ReconnectOverlay.tsx` — full-screen reconnection overlay with retry attempts and manual reconnect.
  - `web/console/app/components/RemoteViewerShell.tsx` — unified wrapper composing toolbar + overlays + session panel.
  - `web/console/app/components/RemoteViewToolbar.tsx` — added 5 shortcut buttons (CAD, Alt+Tab, Alt+F4, Win, PrtSc), keyboard grab toggle, perf overlay toggle, virtual keyboard button.
  - `web/console/app/components/VNCViewer.tsx` — exposed `sendKey(keysym, down)` on handle.
  - `web/console/app/components/WebRTCViewer.tsx` — exposed `sendKey(keysym, down)` on handle.
  - `web/console/app/components/FileDropOverlay.tsx` — XMLHttpRequest progress, speed tracking, multi-file queue.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts` — 5 reconnect attempts with 1s-16s exponential backoff.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/DesktopTab.tsx` — replaced DesktopRemoteViewSection with RemoteViewerShell, wired all Phase 1 hooks.
- **Validation**: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `go vet ./...` clean, `go test ./...` all pass.
- Branch `feat/remote-viewer-parity-phase1` merged to main (fast-forward, 22 commits).

## 2026-03-08 (backend hotspot remediation — pprof-driven)
- Profiled running backend with pprof (CPU 30s, heap, alloc_objects, alloc_space, goroutines).
- Evidence: 298MB / 1.25M alloc objects lifetime; 72% from log ingestion path (`processAgentLogBatch` → `AppendEvents`); 88K `fmt.Sprintf` allocations (4MB) in INSERT builder; per-call `regexp.Compile` in 4 hot paths; unbounded `MemoryTelemetryStore` per-asset slices.
- **6 targeted fixes** (all validated with `go vet ./...` + `go test ./...`):
  1. `internal/persistence/memory_telemetry_store.go` — per-asset eviction (1000 cap, 20% headroom drop, scoped to written assets).
  2. `internal/persistence/postgres_telemetry_logs.go` — replaced `fmt.Sprintf("($%d,$%d,...)")` with `strconv.Itoa` + `strings.Builder` in AppendEvents INSERT builder.
  3. `cmd/labtether/synthetic_runner.go` — shared `http.Transport` (connection pooling) + `sync.Map` regex cache.
  4. `cmd/labtether/alert_evaluator_checks.go` — `sync.Map` regex cache for log-pattern rule evaluation.
  5. `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_status_helpers.go` — replaced per-call `regexp.MustCompile` with `strings.ToLower` + `strings.Contains` in `hasNormalizedToken`.
  5b. `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_winrm.go` — `sync.Map` regex cache in `extractXMLTagValue`.
  6. `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_runner.go` — empty-result backoff (12 empty runs → 30s poll, resets on collector found).
- Plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-08-backend-hotspot-remediation-plan.md`

## 2026-03-08 (macOS menu-agent CPU hotspot evidence pass + remediation)
- Captured live evidence on macOS where `LabTetherAgent` stayed high CPU while background-only and windowless.
- Evidence highlights:
  - process sample stacks repeatedly showed CoreAnimation/CoreGraphics conic-gradient render work (for example `CGContextDrawConicGradient`, `rgba64_shade_conic_RGB`, `CA::Layer::display_if_needed`),
  - Swift-side hotspots correlated with always-on rotating `AngularGradient` border/spinner paths and previously hardcoded pop-out animation activation,
  - local status conditional polling headers were present in Swift but `/agent/status` did not yet support ETag/304 server behavior.
- Implemented remediation:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`
    - added shared visibility policy across menu + detached panel (`setMenuVisible`, `setPanelVisible`, `animationsActive`) for polling cadence and animation gating.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`
    - wired pop-out panel visibility (`popOutController.$panelVisible`) into local API client visibility state.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/PopOutWindowController.swift`
    - replaced hardcoded pop-out `animationsActive=true` with controller-bound visibility environment.
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/Components.swift`
    - replaced rotating conic-gradient border modifiers with static gradient borders,
    - replaced custom conic spinner arc with system circular `ProgressView`,
    - reduced LTHealthOrb animated shadow churn.
  - `internal/agentcore/local_status.go`
    - added `/agent/status` ETag support + `If-None-Match` 304 behavior,
    - introduced ETag uptime bucketing (1-minute granularity) to avoid per-request cache busting while preserving full uptime in JSON payload.
  - `internal/agentcore/local_status_test.go`
    - added conditional request regression tests and ETag token matching coverage.
  - `internal/agentcore/log_backend_darwin.go`
    - reduced parse-path allocations via shared trimmed-field and source-path fallback helpers.
  - `internal/agentcore/log_backend_darwin_test.go`
    - added source fallback coverage for process/sender path basenames.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` -> pass.
  - `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent` -> pass.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates:
  - `docs/PLATFORM.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-057)

## 2026-03-08 (performance optimization — Mac agent + hub)
- Investigated excessive CPU/memory in both hub and Mac agent via deep profiling audit.
- **Mac agent fixes** (6 commits):
  - Added `animationsActive` EnvironmentKey gating all `repeatForever` animations on popover/panel visibility.
  - Retrofitted 9 animated components + 2 alert components with visibility-aware lifecycle.
  - Removed 3 ambient drifting RadialGradient orbs from PopOutView (replaced with static gradients).
  - Replaced random Canvas noise with deterministic LCG + `.drawingGroup()` for GPU rasterization.
  - Cached `DateFormatter` as static in LogBufferView (was allocating per log line).
  - Batched log line dispatch in AgentProcess (one `Task` per `availableData` read instead of per line).
  - Reduced LTHealthOrb from dual shadows to single shadow.
  - Cached MetricsHistory arrays as stored properties (recomputed on append, not on every access).
  - Removed GeometryReader from LTSectionHeader.
- **Hub fixes** (2 commits):
  - Added ring buffer eviction to MemoryLogStore (10K cap, 20% batch drop) and audit Store (5K cap).
  - Fixed N+1 queries in incident correlator — prefetch all correlation data once per cycle.
  - Multi-row INSERT for `AppendSamples` (was N individual INSERTs).
  - Added 24-hour window to `ListSources` query.
  - Replaced `time.Sleep` with `time.NewTimer` + `defer timer.Stop()` in desktop session handler.
  - Moved stale job claims recovery from per-`Claim()` to periodic 30s ticker in worker goroutine.
- **Validation**: `go vet ./...` clean, `go test ./...` all pass, `swift build` clean.
- Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-08-performance-optimization-design.md`
- Implementation plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-08-performance-optimization-plan.md`

## 2026-03-08 (server discovery audit + LAN-to-Tailscale promotion)
- Audited iOS + hub discovery flow for post-sign-out bootstrap reliability and found a contract regression:
  - route table still wrapped `GET /api/v1/discover` with auth middleware even though discover is intended as a pre-login bootstrap endpoint.
- Backend fixes:
  - `cmd/labtether/http_handlers.go`
    - removed auth wrapper for `"/api/v1/discover"` so discover is reachable without a session.
  - `cmd/labtether/enrollment_handlers.go`
    - `handleDiscover` now returns resolver-backed endpoint metadata:
      - legacy keys: `api_url`, `ws_url`, `enroll_url`,
      - explicit keys: `hub_url`, `hub_ws_url`,
      - ordered `hub_candidates` list for transport-aware client selection.
  - `cmd/labtether/enrollment_api_test.go`
    - upgraded discover response assertions to include candidate payload contract.
    - added `TestDiscoverEndpoint_PrefersTailscaleCandidateWhenAvailable`.
    - added `TestBuildHTTPHandlers_DiscoverEndpointAllowsUnauthenticatedRequests`.
- iOS discovery behavior:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Discovery/HubScanner.swift`
    - LAN results now call `/api/v1/discover`,
    - when discover advertises reachable Tailscale candidates, scanner promotes LAN -> Tailscale before presenting choices.
    - promotion now iterates all advertised tailscale candidates, skipping malformed entries and deduplicating repeated endpoints.
    - preserves LAN fallback when candidate probe fails.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/HubScannerTests.swift`
    - added tests for tailscale candidate selection + endpoint parsing/fallback/safety checks.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS auth flow: sign-out keeps hub, explicit Change forgets hub)
- Fixed post-sign-out re-login UX where users had to rely on rediscovery to reach the same hub again.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/AuthViewModel.swift`:
  - `logout()` now clears session artifacts/token while retaining `hubURL`,
  - added `logoutAndForgetHub()` for explicit hub reset back to discovery.
- `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/LoginView.swift`:
  - hub `Change` action now calls `logoutAndForgetHub()` to preserve explicit hub switch semantics.
- Tests:
  - updated `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests.swift` coverage for both flows:
    - sign-out keeps hub + clears session,
    - forget-hub clears hub + session and returns to discovery.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/auth-login-issues.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests test` passed (`12 tests, 0 failures`).
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture decision change; this is a targeted iOS auth/session UX fix.

## 2026-03-08 (hub + agent inefficiency fix: log ingest throughput and macOS stream churn)
- Investigated live CPU/resource pressure and targeted the sustained log-ingest hot path rather than adding a low-power mode toggle.
- Live signals captured:
  - active labtether processes showed steady CPU on hub + mac agent runtime.
  - `GET /worker/stats?query_limit=all` showed dominant `INSERT INTO log_events` call volume in `performance.top_queries`.
  - macOS unified-log source volume check:
    - `log stream --style ndjson --type log --level info --timeout 2s | wc -l` ~= `2013` lines
    - `log stream --style ndjson --type log --level default --timeout 2s | wc -l` ~= `336` lines
- Hub ingest optimization:
  - `internal/persistence/types.go`
    - added optional `LogBatchAppendStore` interface (`AppendEvents([]logs.Event)`).
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_telemetry_logs.go`
    - added `AppendEvents` bulk insert path for `log_events` with conflict-safe multi-row writes.
  - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store.go`
    - added `AppendEvents` implementation and shared append helper.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_runtime_signal_handlers.go`
    - `processAgentLogBatch` now uses batch append when available, with backward-compatible fallback.
- macOS agent stream optimization:
  - `internal/agentcore/log_backend_darwin.go`
    - stream parser now uses a fast path that skips timestamp parsing for live `log.batch` emission,
    - reordered timestamp parse layouts for query path hot format first,
    - switched stream args from `--level info` to `--level default`.
  - `internal/agentcore/log_backend_darwin_test.go`
    - added stream fast-path timestamp-skip test,
    - updated stream arg expectation to `--level default`.
- Added/updated regression coverage:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`
    - `TestProcessAgentLogBatchUsesBatchAppenderWhenAvailable`
  - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store_test.go`
    - `TestMemoryLogStoreAppendEvents`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/persistence -run 'TestMemoryLogStore(AppendEvents|QueryEventsExcludeFields|QueryEventsFieldKeysProjection|QueryEventsSiteFilterWithAssetFallback)$' -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(ParseOSLogLine|ParseOSLogLineForStreamSkipsTimestampParsing|BuildDarwinLogStreamArgs)$' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestProcessAgentLogHandlersIgnorePayloadAssetID|TestProcessAgentLogBatchUsesBatchAppenderWhenAvailable' -count=1`
- Runtime apply:
  - backend restarted with `make dev-backend-bg-restart`.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updated:
  - `docs/PLATFORM.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/os-command-cheatsheet.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-055)

## 2026-03-08 (Mac Agent Premium Polish — Iteration 11-15: Layout Density + Cross-Platform Parity)
- **MenuBarView hero horizontal layout**: Redesigned hero from vertical centered (48pt orb + centered text) to compact horizontal HStack (36pt orb + left-aligned status + PID badge inline). Saves ~40pt vertical space.
- **Agent controls integrated into hero**: Replaced separate agent control section (with dividers) with compact `LTMiniActionButton` circular icons (28pt) in the hero row's trailing edge. Stop/restart when running, play when stopped, spinner when starting.
- **New component `LTMiniActionButton`**: 28pt circular icon button with tinted background, hover brightening, press-scale feedback, and glow shadow. Used for inline agent controls.
- **Mini metric gauges in hero**: Added tiny CPU/MEM/DISK gauge bars (3pt height) with glowing tips directly below the hero status line. Shows system load at a glance without scrolling.
- **ScrollView for overflow**: Wrapped all content below hero in `ScrollView(.vertical, showsIndicators: false)` so sections scroll naturally when content overflows the popover.
- **LTGlassCard hover effects**: Cards subtly brighten on hover (elevated glass fill + brighter specular edge + stronger border glow). Smooth `animFast` transition.
- **LTGlassCard inner glow**: Cards with `glowColor` now have a subtle radial gradient wash inside (6% opacity, positioned upper-left) for depth and warmth.
- **PopOutView hero glass card**: Wrapped hero in `LTGlassCard(glowColor: orbColor)` with horizontal layout matching MenuBarView pattern. 48pt orb + left-aligned text + inline agent control buttons.
- **SettingsView sliding tab pill**: Replaced static tab background with `matchedGeometryEffect`-animated sliding pill indicator + accent border stroke + glow shadow. Premium tab switching animation.
- **MetricsView gauge glowing tips**: Added a 4pt glowing circle dot at the tip of each gauge fill bar. Tip inherits the threshold color with double-layer shadow for premium sparkle.
- **LogBufferView severity edge bars**: Added 2pt colored severity bars on the left edge of every log row. Error=red (full opacity), warning=amber, info=subtle. Row hover highlighting added.
- **LogBufferView toolbar icon**: Added terminal.fill icon in accent color before "Logs" title.
- **Footer minimization**: Reduced footer from full "LabTether" + capsule badge + "Quit" text button to minimal "LabTether · v1.0" text + circular power icon. Saves vertical space.
- **Design token `LT.space6`**: Added 6pt compact spacing for tighter section gaps (was using space8 between sections, now space6).
- **Capsule badge tightening**: Reduced `LTCapsuleBadge` padding from `space8` horizontal / 3pt vertical to `space6` / 2pt for more compact metadata tags.
- Build: all changes compile clean on first try via `swift build --package-path apps/mac-agent`.
- Deploy cycle: `make mac-agent-app` → `pkill` → `open` (no password required).
- Docs/wiki rationale: mac-agent-only UI changes; no hub API, deployment, or operator workflow changes.

## 2026-03-08 (Mac Agent Premium Visual Overhaul — Full Glass-Morphism Redesign)
- **Design system**: Created `DesignTokens.swift` (LT namespace) with OLED-black palette (#0a0a0c), Neon Rose accent (#ff0080), emerald/amber/red status colors with glow variants, Sora/Inter/JetBrains Mono typography helpers, spacing/radius/animation constants.
- **Bundled fonts**: Sora (Regular/Medium/SemiBold/Bold), Inter (Regular/Medium/SemiBold), JetBrains Mono (Regular/Medium/SemiBold) — 10 TTF files via SPM `.copy()` resource bundling. `FontLoader` registers via `CTFontManagerRegisterFontsForURL` at launch.
- **Component library**: Created `Components.swift` with LTGlassCard (specular highlight, glow border), LTSectionHeader (terminal `// TITLE` style), LTHealthOrb (3-layer breathing orb), LTStatusDot (dual-shadow glow), LTPillButton, LTMenuRow, LTSeparator, LTSparkline, LTStaggeredEntrance modifier.
- **MenuBarView** (340pt, was 280pt): Centered health orb + narrative line hero, glass cards, gradient metric bars with threshold glow, `// SECTION` terminal headers, staggered entrance animation, OLED background.
- **MetricsView**: Gradient-fill bars (accent→threshold color) with glow shadows, JetBrains Mono values, LT token colors throughout.
- **AlertsView**: Glass card per alert, pulsing LTStatusDot for critical severity, severity color mapping to LT tokens, empty state with green checkmark orb.
- **PopOutView** (new, ~400pt floating NSPanel): Extended dashboard with 48pt health orb, 3-column metric cards with sparklines (60-sample history), recent events feed, ambient floating orb effect, horizontal action bar.
- **SettingsView** (520pt, was 480pt): Custom pill tab bar replacing system segmented picker, glass cards per section, accent-focus input fields, inline agent control with LTStatusDot + LTPillButton, `.tint(LT.accent)` toggle tracks.
- **LogBufferView**: Full JetBrains Mono terminal, glass toolbar with accent-focus search, LTStatusDot level indicators, timestamp gap separators, breathing auto-scroll dot, glass status bar.
- **Final polish**: Updated ConnectionState.color to return LT tokens, menu bar label fonts to JetBrains Mono, zero remaining system colors in view files.
- Build: `swift build` — `Build complete!` across all changes.
- Docs/wiki rationale: mac-agent-only UI changes; no hub API, deployment, or operator workflow changes.

## 2026-03-08 (mac-agent Task 7: Pop-Out Floating Dashboard + Sparklines)
- Added `MetricsHistory` ring buffer (60-sample, ~5 min) to `LocalAPIClient.swift` accumulating `MetricsSnapshot` on each successful poll.
- Added `@Published var metricsHistory` to `LocalAPIClient`; appended on every decoded poll response.
- Added `LTSparkline` to `Components.swift`: normalised Y-axis, 1.5 pt stroke, gradient fill below the line.
- Created `PopOutView.swift`: scrollable floating dashboard with:
  - 48 pt `LTHealthOrb` hero section with narrative text and PID badge.
  - Three-column metric cards (CPU / MEM / DISK) each with `LTSparkline` history and gauge-coloured values.
  - Network RX/TX row (temperature if available).
  - Conditional firing alerts section via existing `AlertsView`.
  - Recent Events section — last 10 non-routine log lines with timestamp, `LTStatusDot` level indicator, stripped message.
  - Screen sharing status card.
  - Horizontal action bar (`LTPillButton` set: Console, Device, Logs, Settings).
  - Ambient background: two large drifting radial gradient orbs (20 s / 15 s easing loops).
- Created `PopOutWindowController.swift`: `@MainActor` class managing an `NSPanel` with `.nonactivatingPanel`, `.floating` level, transparent titlebar, `LT.bg` background, min size 380×500, toggle/show/close API.
- Updated `AppState` in `App.swift`: added `let popOutController = PopOutWindowController()`.
- Updated `MenuBarView` creation in `App.swift`: passes `onPopOut` closure wired to `AppState.shared.popOutController.toggle(appState:)`.
- Updated `MenuBarView.swift`: added `var onPopOut: () -> Void = {}` property; Pop Out Window row now calls `onPopOut()`.
- Build: `swift build` — `Build complete!` (zero warnings, zero errors).
- Docs/wiki rationale: mac-agent-only UI feature; no operator workflow, hub API, or deployment config changes.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Visual Polish — Iterations 33-37: Final LabeledContent, Premium Micro-Interactions)
- Iteration 33: Final LabeledContent migration — replaced all remaining user-facing `LabeledContent` with icon-based rows in HomeView (Queued Actions), AlertsView (Queued Alert Actions), IncidentsView (Queued Updates, Checklist Progress, Current status, timeline rows). Added `statusIcon()` helper with per-status SF Symbols.
- Iteration 34: DiagnosticsView upgrade — replaced all 17 `LabeledContent` instances in Mobile Observability and Live Activity Diagnostics sections with icon-based rows. Context-aware colors (failure counts turn red when > 0, queue depth turns amber when non-zero). Added `diagRow()` and `failColor()` helpers.
- Iteration 35: Command palette glow ring — the ⌘ button now displays a pulsing glow ring when there are firing alerts or down services. Ring color adapts (red for alerts, amber for down services). Extracted `commandPaletteButton` computed property with `hasCriticalCondition` and `criticalColor` helpers.
- Iteration 36: Metric card scan-line — `LTMetricCard` now features a one-shot horizontal light sweep on first appearance (0.8s ease with 0.3s delay). Premium fintech-style reveal effect.
- Iteration 37: Animated progress rings — `LTProgressRing` now animates fill from 0 to target value on appearance (0.8s ease). Center percentage uses `numericText()` content transition. Value changes animate smoothly. Standardized remaining `ProgressView` tint to `LT.accent` in IncidentsView and UpdatesView.
- Zero `LabeledContent` in user-facing views (only form-context pickers in AlertsView editor and debug tools remain).
- Zero untinted `ProgressView()` across the app — all use `LT.accent` or context-appropriate tint.
- Docs/wiki rationale: iOS-only visual polish; no operator workflow, API, or config changes.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Visual Polish — Iterations 27-32: Loading Overlays + Detail Views + Creative Effects)
- Iteration 27: Premium loading overlays — created `LTLoadingOverlay` component with animated spinning arc, glass-morphism background, and monospaced label. Replaced `ProgressView` overlays in 7 views (Recordings, Topology, Sites, Actions, Updates, Telemetry, Reliability). Replaced full-screen `ProgressView` with `LTLoadingShimmer` in Logs, Files, CommandPalette. Upgraded empty states to `LTEmptyState` in Recordings, Sites, Actions (connectors + runs), Updates (plans + runs). Added haptic feedback to refreshable in 6 views.
- Iteration 28: Incident detail views + staggered animations — replaced `LabeledContent` in `IncidentLinkedAlertDetailView` and `IncidentLinkedAssetDetailView` with icon-based rows, color-coded status indicators, and glass backgrounds. Added staggered entrance animations to Sites and Recordings lists. Replaced inline `ProgressView("Syncing...")` with shimmer-based indicators in Incidents, Alerts, and Home queue sections. Upgraded `IncidentDetailView` overlay to `LTLoadingOverlay`.
- Iteration 29: Settings view — replaced `LabeledContent` in Account and About sections with icon-based info rows. Upgraded Hub Connection section with custom icon-row layout and `LTStatusDot`. Added `settingsInfoRow` helper function. Added haptic on refreshable.
- Iteration 30: More view + AlertDetail — replaced `LabeledContent` in MoreView connection, about, and Docker host summary sections with icon-based rows. Upgraded `AlertDetailView` status section to capsule badge format. Added `dockerInfoRow` helper.
- Iteration 31: Animated gradient border — created `LTAnimatedBorder` component with sweeping `AngularGradient` effect (similar to Arc Browser, Linear). Applied to Home hero briefing card, activates when system needs attention.
- Iteration 32: Batch empty state upgrades — replaced remaining plain `Text("No ...")` empty states with `LTEmptyState` in Topology (outgoing, incoming, relationships), Docker (containers, stacks), and Agents (pending, connected) views.
- New components: `LTLoadingOverlay`, `LTAnimatedBorder` (with `.animatedBorder()` modifier).
- Docs/wiki rationale: iOS-only visual polish; no operator workflow, API, or config changes.

## 2026-03-08 (mac agent menubar + code 9 stability pass)
- Fixed two regressions observed during mac agent relaunch cycles:
  - menubar click would appear to do nothing (popover collapsing during redraw churn),
  - agent shutdown paths surfaced misleading `Exited with code 9` errors during intentional stop/quit.
- `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`:
  - restored/kept the original `MenuBarExtra(...).menuBarExtraStyle(.window)` UX,
  - gated `AppState` label-forwarding redraws while the menu window is visible to avoid mid-click label churn.
- `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`:
  - defaulted menu visibility to hidden (`false`) for lower idle polling load,
  - exposed `isMenuVisible` for redraw gating.
- `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentProcess.swift`:
  - treat user/app-initiated terminations as clean stops (no error state on signal exits),
  - set `userInitiatedStop` in `forceKill()` and cancel crash cooldown to avoid false crash-loop behavior,
  - suppress disconnected-notification noise on intentional stop.
- `Makefile`:
  - `mac-agent-app` now rebuilds bundle from a clean directory (`rm -rf`),
  - clears extended attributes and ad-hoc signs bundle every build (`xattr -cr` + `codesign --force --deep --sign -`),
  - prevents invalid signature/tainted-binary launch failures during rebuild/relaunch loops.
- Validation:
  - `make mac-agent` passed after Makefile changes.
  - `codesign --verify --verbose=2 "build/LabTether Agent.app"` passed.
  - runtime relaunch succeeded (`pgrep -fl LabTetherAgent` shows new process from `build/LabTether Agent.app`).
  - UI scripting check on the LabTether status item confirmed open behavior (`before=0,after=1` windows).
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` update required; this is internal mac app lifecycle/packaging reliability work with no user-facing API/config changes.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Visual Polish — Iterations 21-26: Alert Rules + Loading + Detail Views)
- Iteration 21: Alert rule system polish — `alertRuleRow` upgraded with `LTIconBox` (kind-specific icons: gauge, heart.slash, clock, doc.text.magnifyingglass, globe), monospaced uppercase kind badges, bold status capsule. `AlertRuleDetailView` summary section replaced plain `LabeledContent` with icon-based rows. Evaluation rows upgraded with `LTIconBox` (bell.badge, bell.slash, xmark.octagon, checkmark.circle), monospaced status text, candidate/triggered counts. Home dashboard critical alerts section upgraded with `LTIconBox`, staggered animation, trailing count badge. Home triage alert/incident/service rows upgraded with `LTIconBox`. Status badges standardized to monospaced bold font.
- Iteration 22: Premium shimmer loading states — replaced `ProgressView("Loading...")` with animated shimmer skeleton placeholders in NodesView, ServicesView, AlertsView, IncidentsView. Matches the shimmer pattern already used in HomeView's briefing section.
- Iteration 23: Numeric text transitions + state label consistency — added `.contentTransition(.numericText())` to alert current values, threshold values, and service response times across list and detail views. AlertsView state labels upgraded to monospaced uppercase (FIRING, ACK'D, RESOLVED).
- Iteration 24: Breathing empty states + triage headers — `LTEmptyState` component enhanced with breathing radial glow animation behind the icon (all empty states benefit automatically). Home triage category headers upgraded with `LTIconBox` + monospaced uppercase text + letter tracking.
- Iteration 25: Detail view icon rows + micro-interactions — `NodeDetailView` details section replaced plain text rows with icon-based layout (network, platform, shippingbox, clock, antenna). Agent status uses CONNECTED/NOT CONNECTED capsule badge. `ServiceDetailView` details section replaced plain text rows with icon-based layout (tag, shippingbox, cube.box, arrow.triangle.branch, clock). Added haptic on ServiceDetailView refresh and Home triage filter pills.
- Iteration 26: Reusable shimmer loading — added `LTLoadingShimmer` component to `Components.swift`. Batch-replaced remaining `ProgressView("Loading...")` in TerminalNodePickerView, DockerDashboardView, WebServicesByHostView, AgentsManagementView, AlertRulesView, IncidentDetailView.
- docs/wiki rationale: iOS-only visual polish; no operator workflow, API, or config changes.

## 2026-03-08 (docker discovery phase completion: event-driven deltas + adaptive stats)
- Completed the Docker discovery optimization phases for Linux agent runtime to reduce idle power, payload churn, and Docker API pressure.
- `internal/agentcore/docker_collector.go`:
  - replaced periodic full-scan-only loop with a trigger-driven discovery manager,
  - added event-coalesced container refresh + incremental `docker.discovery.delta` publishing,
  - added periodic full reconciliation snapshots for correctness/backward compatibility,
  - split container refresh cadence from heavier image/network/volume reconciliation,
  - added adaptive per-container stats sampling with backoff for quiet containers.
- `internal/agentcore/docker_events.go`:
  - Docker events now enqueue discovery hints (container delta vs full reconcile) in addition to event forwarding.
- `internal/agentcore/docker_actions.go`:
  - post-action refresh now queues immediate discovery/stats triggers instead of forcing synchronous full scans.
- Protocol + hub wiring:
  - `internal/agentmgr/message.go`:
    - added `MsgDockerDiscoveryDelta` and `DockerDiscoveryDeltaData`.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go` + `cmd/labtether/docker_runtime_agent_handlers.go`:
    - added route/handler for `docker.discovery.delta`.
  - `internal/connectors/docker/message_handlers.go`:
    - added coordinator delta application (`HandleDiscoveryDelta`) with container/image/network/volume upsert/remove support and compose replacement handling.
- Added/expanded regression coverage:
  - `internal/connectors/docker/coordinator_test.go`:
    - delta apply + host bootstrap delta tests.
  - `internal/agentcore/docker_collector_test.go`:
    - container diff/fallback/stats-interval/build-map tests.
  - `internal/agentcore/docker_events_test.go`:
    - event-to-discovery-trigger tests.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go` + `internal/agentmgr/message_test.go`:
    - new message-type coverage.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updated:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-054)
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore ./internal/connectors/docker ./internal/agentmgr ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.
  - `go vet ./internal/agentcore ./internal/connectors/docker ./internal/agentmgr ./cmd/labtether` passed.

## 2026-03-08 (agent runtime efficiency pass: Linux ultra low-power profile + mac hotspot hardening)
- Continued the thermal/hotspot pass and shipped additive low-power controls in the shared agent runtime.
- `internal/agentcore/config.go`:
  - added `LABTETHER_LOW_POWER_MODE` profile defaults:
    - collect interval `30s`,
    - heartbeat `120s`,
    - Docker discovery `5m`,
    - web-service discovery `10m`,
    - local port scan + listening-port enrichment disabled by default,
    - background log streaming disabled by default.
  - explicit env values still override profile defaults.
- `internal/agentcore/types.go`:
  - added `RuntimeConfig` fields `LowPowerMode`, `WebServiceDiscoveryInterval`, and `LogStreamEnabled`.
- `internal/agentcore/run.go`:
  - web-service collector now uses configurable `cfg.WebServiceDiscoveryInterval`.
  - background log streamer now starts only when `cfg.LogStreamEnabled` is true.
- `internal/agentcore/log_handler.go`:
  - pre-sized log batch buffer,
  - skip batching/marshal/send when transport is disconnected to reduce reconnect-window overhead.
- Tests:
  - added `internal/agentcore/config_low_power_test.go` (default mode, low-power defaults, explicit override precedence).
  - validated with `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1`.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/PLATFORM.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/os-command-cheatsheet.md`
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update required; this is an additive runtime tuning profile, not an architecture-direction change.

## 2026-03-08 (linux agent low-power + low-memory hotspot pass)
- Identified and optimized Linux agent hotspots in service discovery, Docker collection, and telemetry sampling paths.
- `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`:
  - added disconnected-transport fast exit for discovery cycles,
  - added short-lived health-probe cache with bounded size/expiry pruning,
  - reduced per-cycle clone churn by cloning previous-service snapshots only on fallback paths,
  - reused already-normalized discovery config in the hot cycle path.
- `internal/agentcore/webservice_fingerprinting.go`:
  - added short-lived fingerprint cache (hit/miss TTL) with bounded size/expiry pruning.
- `internal/agentcore/webservice_compat.go`:
  - switched compatibility cache timestamps to shared collector clock helper and added bounded-size pruning.
- `internal/agentcore/webservice_scan_discovery.go`:
  - added internal `...WithConfig` discovery helpers so run-cycle scan phases avoid redundant config normalization passes.
- `internal/agentcore/docker_collector.go` + `docker_events.go`:
  - skip discovery/stats/event forwarding when transport is disconnected,
  - rewrote compose-stack inference to linear aggregation (`O(n)`) instead of nested container matching.
- `internal/agentplatform/linux/provider.go` + `provider_telemetry.go`:
  - reduced `/proc` parser allocations (CPU/memory/network),
  - added cached sampling for disk usage (1m) and temperature probes (30s) to lower steady-state overhead.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`:
    - health-cache reuse test,
    - health-cache TTL expiry reprobe test,
    - disconnected run-cycle skip test.
  - `internal/agentcore/docker_collector_test.go`:
    - `BenchmarkInferComposeStacksLarge`.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updated:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-053)
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore ./internal/agentplatform/linux -count=1` passed.
  - `go vet ./internal/agentcore ./internal/agentplatform/linux` passed.
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run TestNonExistent -bench 'BenchmarkInferComposeStacksLarge' -benchmem -count=1` passed.

    - added short-lived cache (30s) for runtime routing URL resolution used by status endpoint probes.

    - added cache state fields for routing URL and endpoint probe caches.

    - `tmp/perf/runs/20260308-115110-live-ttl15-30-pass3-tuned`
  - measured deltas:
    - runtime settings query delta: `953 -> 1 -> 0`
    - `/status/aggregate/live` p95: `50.2ms -> 53.4ms -> 45.2ms`
    - steady 1Hz/75s poll simulation: probe hits `8 -> 5`, runtime-setting reads `7 -> 3`

## 2026-03-08 (macOS agent thermal hotspot reduction: Darwin unified-log stream path)
- Investigated live thermal report from the running `labtether-agent` process and profiled with `sample` against the active PID.
- Hot path identified in Darwin log ingestion:
  - `internal/agentcore.darwinLogBackend.StreamEntries`
  - `internal/agentcore.parseOSLogLine`
  - repeated JSON parse churn due line-scanner + non-line-delimited stream format mismatch.
- Fixes shipped:
  - `internal/agentcore/log_backend_darwin.go`
    - switched Darwin stream command to `log stream --style ndjson --type log --level info --color none`.
    - switched Darwin historical query command to `log show --style ndjson --color none --debug`.
    - added `buildDarwinLogStreamArgs()` helper to centralize stream args.
  - `internal/agentcore/log_backend_darwin_test.go`
    - extended arg coverage to assert `--style ndjson` for query path.
    - added stream-arg regression test for `--style ndjson --type log --level info`.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/PLATFORM.md` (Darwin log ingestion mode and capability matrix wording)
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/os-command-cheatsheet.md` (macOS heat/hotspot command workflow)
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.
  - synthetic stream-volume check with identical tagged events:
    - `--style json`: `1861` lines
    - `--style ndjson`: `62` lines
- ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update required; this is a runtime efficiency correction of existing Darwin log ingestion, not an architectural change.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Visual Polish — Iterations 17-20: Final View Polish & Haptic Feedback Pass)
- Iteration 17: ReliabilityView outage rows with `LTIconBox` + event kind/color mapping + staggered animation; impact section replaced `LabeledContent` with icon-based rows; haptic on refresh. TelemetryView fleet snapshot rows use `LTIconBox`; haptic on refresh. DockerDashboardView host rows with `LTIconBox` + count badges; container rows with `LTIconBox` + state capsule badges; stack rows with `LTIconBox`. AgentsManagementView connected agents with `LTIconBox` + platform badges; pending agents with `LTIconBox` + monospaced metadata. WebServicesByHostView service rows with globe `LTIconBox` + status badges + monospaced URLs.
- Iteration 18: NodeDetailView alert rows with severity `LTIconBox`, status badge monospaced uppercase, haptic on refresh. AlertDetailView timeline section with icon-based rows and color-coded date values; metrics section with icon indicators.
- Iteration 19: IncidentDetailView incident room section uses icon-based rows (Owner, Opened, Elapsed, Severity Clock) instead of plain `LabeledContent`; haptic on refresh for incidents list and detail.
- Iteration 20: IncidentDetailView linked alert rows with bell `LTIconBox` + uppercase type badges; linked asset rows with server.rack `LTIconBox` + role badges. Haptic feedback on incident status transitions and delete. AlertDetailView haptic on acknowledge, resolve, and delete buttons.
- All remaining user-facing views now have consistent `LTIconBox`, monospaced capsule badges, haptic feedback on mutations.
- docs/wiki rationale: iOS-only visual polish; no operator workflow, API, or config changes.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Visual Polish — Iterations 11-15: Full App Premium Pass)
- Iteration 11: SettingsView replaced custom `iconPill` with `LTIconBox`; CommandPaletteView upgraded to `LTIconBox` with section-colored icons; TopologyView hero focused node with platform icon + monospaced capsule badges; RecordingsView added LTIconBox per recording with protocol-specific icons; ActionsView added LTIconBox per action run; SitesView added LTIconBox with building icon and smart pluralization.

- Iteration 14: Command palette button redesigned with glass pill styling and `LTPressStyle`; added `LTHaptic` utility for settings-aware feedback; haptic selection on tab switches; haptic impact on command palette; haptic success notification on Home pull-to-refresh.
- Iteration 15: IncidentDetailView hero header — 52px severity orb with filled icon variants, bold title, monospaced severity badge, inline assignee/source metadata.

## 2026-03-08 (iOS Visual Polish — Iterations 5-6: Deep Detail View Polish)
- Iteration 5: Docker views — `LTSectionHeader` for Host/Containers/Stacks, `LTStatusDot` for container/stack status indicators, staggered entrance animations on container and stack rows.

- Zero `ContentUnavailableView`, zero `borderedProminent`, zero status-indicator `Circle().fill()` in feature views.

## 2026-03-07 (iOS Visual Polish — Web Console Parity)

## 2026-03-07 (iOS Design Language Refresh)
- Ported the web console's LabTether design language to the iOS app.
- Brand colors:
  - AccentColor asset catalog updated to Neon Rose (#ff0080 dark, #e20071 light).
  - StatusOK, StatusWarning, StatusCritical color assets updated to match web console.
  - All `.accentColor` / `Color.accentColor` references replaced with `LT.accent` (explicit UIColor dynamic provider to bypass iOS 26 wallpaper tinting).
  - Removed all `.indigo` / `.blue` UI tint references (except intentional URL link blue).
- Design tokens (`DesignTokens.swift`):
  - Added text hierarchy (textPrimary, textSecondary, textMuted), surface colors, animation springs, timing constants, corner radii, ISO 8601 parser.
- View modifiers (`ViewModifiers.swift`):
  - Added StatusGlowModifier, CategoryHeaderStyle (terminal-style uppercase tracked mono headers), MetricValueModifier (monospaced digits with numeric content transition).
- Tab rename: Nodes -> Devices across MainTabView, CommandPaletteView, NodesView.
- HomeView redesign: narrative-first briefing section with status glow, quick stats row, incident triage with filter pills, one-tap runbooks, category header styles.
- MoreView reorganized into 3 combined sections: Operations, Observability, System (matching web console sidebar categories).
- LoginView: brand mark + neon rose accent.
- DiscoveryView: matching brand identity.
- NodesView (Devices): added status summary row (Online/Offline/Total), switched to LT.parseISO8601.
- ServicesView, AlertsView, AlertDetailView, IncidentsView, SettingsView, DiagnosticsView, TerminalView, CommandPaletteView: all updated to use LT design tokens.
- Debug tooling: added `--lt-debug-hub`/`--lt-debug-user`/`--lt-debug-pass` launch args for simulator auto-login; push notification request skipped in simulator builds.
- Simulator iteration: fixed Live Activity notification dialog (skip Activity.request in simulator for both IncidentLiveActivityManager and SessionLiveActivityManager), fixed triage filter pill truncation (caption2 + title-only labels), color-coded triage count pills (amber/red backgrounds for non-zero values), added `--lt-debug-tab` launch arg for tab-switching screenshots.
- Verified in simulator (iPhone 17 Pro + iPhone 17, dark mode) with real hub data across all 5 tabs.
- docs/wiki rationale: iOS-only visual/branding changes; no operator workflow, API, or config changes.

## 2026-03-07 (Alternative URLs feature — merge-to-alt-URLs refactoring)
- Replaced the "merge" concept in web services with "alternative URLs" architecture.
- Backend (Go):
  - Database migration 44-45: created `web_service_alt_urls`, `web_service_url_grouping_settings`, `web_service_never_group_rules` tables; migrated old runtime settings.
  - New persistence layer: `internal/persistence/web_service_alt_urls.go` with 11 CRUD methods.
  - Renamed all merge files/types to URL grouping (`webservice_url_grouping_*.go`).
  - New API endpoints: alt URL CRUD, never-group rules, grouping settings, suggestion response.
  - Suggestion system: 70-84% confidence matches tracked in-memory, surfaced via API.
  - Service list API returns structured `alt_urls` array with source attribution.
  - Removed 6 old merge runtime setting definitions.
- Frontend (Next.js/TypeScript):
  - Renamed merge components to URL grouping (`ServiceURLGroupingSettingsModal`, `servicesURLGroupingRules`).
  - ServiceEditModal: alt URLs with source badges, add/remove controls, direct API calls.
  - Settings modal simplified: removed dry-run toggle and force-merge rules.
  - Toast suggestions with accept/deny for below-threshold grouping matches.
  - New Next.js API proxy routes for all new backend endpoints.
- Docs updated:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` — rewrote merge section as URL grouping/alternative URLs.
  - `docs/ADR.md` — updated ADR-025/027 terminology, added ADR-051 for alternative URLs.
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md` — updated merge references.
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md` — updated merge references.
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md` — updated merge references.
  - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md` — added 4 new service API endpoints.
  - `notes/TODO.md` — marked complete.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed (including URL grouping tests).
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-07 (Topology tab comprehensive bug audit)
- Systematic UI audit of every button, filter, and interaction across all three topology views (graph, list, tree).
- Bugs found and fixed:
  1. **Group By hidden in list view**: Display section in filters popover was gated on `topologyView === "graph"`, hiding the Group By dropdown in list view. Changed to `topologyView !== "tree"` so list view also shows it (Density remains graph-only).
  2. **Dead code removal**: Deleted unused 393-line `TopologyControlsCard.tsx` (leftover from pre-toolbar redesign, never imported).
  3. **Focus graph broken for compound children**: Selecting a compound child (e.g. Docker container) in the graph inspector and enabling focus showed an empty graph. Root cause: `selectFocusedGraph` didn't resolve compound children to their parent node. Fixed by adding `allChildIDs` to `CompoundNodeData` and compound child → parent resolution in `topologyGraphSelectors.ts`.
  4. **Filters popover Display section layout**: Density toggle and Group By dropdown were in a misaligned flex row. Changed to `grid grid-cols-1 gap-2 sm:grid-cols-2` matching the filter dropdown grid above.
- Files changed:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/TopologyToolbar.tsx` (fixes 1, 4)
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/TopologyControlsCard.tsx` (deleted, fix 2)
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyTypes.ts` (fix 3: added laneKey, allChildIDs to CompoundNodeData)
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyGraphModel.tsx` (fix 3: populated allChildIDs)
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyGraphSelectors.ts` (fix 3: compound child resolution)
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - none required (UI bug fixes only; no workflow/API/config contract changes).
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Visual verification via Playwright across all three views, all toolbar controls, filters popover, inspector, search.
- ADR:
  - no architecture decision change (UI bug fixes only).

## 2026-03-06 (Topology Polish: glass morphism, dark minimap, edge fix)
- Comprehensive visual polish of all topology views (graph, tree, dashboard hero).
- Changes:
  - **Glass morphism cards**: Applied `bg-[var(--panel-glass)] backdrop-blur-md` with `var(--shadow-panel)` and `border-[var(--panel-border)]` to graph asset cards (`topologyGraphModel.tsx`), `TopologyTreeCard.tsx`, `CompoundNode.tsx`.
  - **Dark-themed ReactFlow UI**: Added CSS overrides for minimap (`var(--bg)` background, dark mask), controls (glass bg, muted SVG fills), and edge paths (`stroke-linecap: round`) in `globals.css`. Added MiniMap props on both `TopologyGraphPanel` and `TopologyTreePanel`.
  - **Fixed tree view edge warnings**: Root cause was GroupBoxNode missing a source handle (only had target), and edges referencing node IDs without dagre positions. Added source handle to `GroupBoxNode.tsx`, added post-build edge filter in `topologyTreeModel.tsx`.
  - **TopologyLegend**: Replaced hardcoded hex colors with CSS variables (`var(--ok)`, `var(--warn)`, etc.), added glass panel shadow.
  - **TopologyHero dashboard**: Widened labels (130→160px), thicker edges (1→1.5px), larger hub nodes (36→40px), wider dash pattern.
  - **Terminal DNA**: Added `font-mono uppercase tracking-wider` to GroupBoxNode header, border separator.
  - **Unified status dots**: Added `ring-2 ring-current/10` glow to all status dots across card types.
  - **Tag badges**: Added `bg-[var(--surface)]` to source/type tag badges for depth.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Visual verification via Playwright: 0 React Flow warnings on graph and tree views (previously 6 warnings).
  - All three topology views (graph, tree, dashboard hero) visually verified.
- ADR: no architecture decision change (UI visual polish only).

## 2026-03-06 (UI Polish Redesign: OLED-first dark UI with Neon Rose/Deep Signal accents)
- Comprehensive visual redesign of the web console across all pages.
- Branch: `feat/ui-polish` (15 commits).
- Design:
  - Created interactive HTML design explorer (`notes/ui-design-explorer.html`) with 8 theme variants.
  - Captured designs to Figma for iteration.
  - Approved design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-06-ui-polish-design.md`.
  - Implementation plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-06-ui-polish-implementation.md`.
- Foundation (Phase 1):
  - Added Sora heading font via `next/font/google` with `--font-heading` CSS variable.
  - Replaced indigo accent system with dual-accent: Neon Rose (#ff0080) / Deep Signal (#5b8eff).
  - Added `--accent-rgb` token for use in `rgba()` expressions.
  - Added `body[data-accent="deep-signal"]` CSS overrides for all 3 base themes.
  - Updated status colors to vibrant variants (--ok: #00e68a, --warn: #f0b429, --bad: #ff3355).
  - Added keyframes: `glow-breathe`, `float-glow`, `border-travel`, `dash-flow`.
  - Added `accent`/`setAccent` to ThemeContext with localStorage persistence.
- Shared Components (Phase 2):
  - Card: glass treatment (`blur + saturate`), top-edge specular highlight, `highlight` prop for animated gradient border.
  - Button: multi-layer glow shadows (primary: 20px→30px+60px on hover), inset specular, danger glow.
  - PageHeader: Sora font, gradient text (`background-clip: text`), `// prefix` terminal DNA.
  - Badge: multi-layer glow halos (4px + 12px per status color), critical pulse.
  - MiniBar: gradient fill (`color-mix`), glow shadow, mono tabular-nums labels.
- Shell (Phase 3):
  - Console layout: accent-aware floating orbs, dot grid background, cursor-responsive glow, spring page transitions.
  - Sidebar: glass backdrop, breathing brand glow, `// category` labels, active nav radial glow, accent picker (NR/DS).
- Pages (Phases 4-6):
  - Dashboard: highlight KPI strip, `// section` headings, glass narrative, accent-aware topology.
  - Topology: accent-aware ReactFlow background dots.
  - Terminal: glass tab bar, active tab accent glow, glass layout buttons.
  - Settings: accent color picker (Neon Rose / Deep Signal) alongside theme picker.
  - All pages: hardcoded indigo colors replaced with `var(--accent)` / `rgba(var(--accent-rgb),...)`.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passes clean.
  - Design system doc (`notes/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md`) updated with new tokens, typography, and techniques.

## 2026-03-06 (Root agent-instructions parity refresh: `AGENTS.md` + `CLAUDE.md`)
- Synchronized root agent instruction docs so both files now carry the same operational contract and current project baseline.
- Implementation:
  - `AGENTS.md`
    - refreshed to a shared contract that includes mission, project state, monorepo map, validation baseline, workflow rules, slash-command shortcuts, architecture guardrails, deployment reminder, documentation contract, and definition of done.
    - added explicit parity note to keep root `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` updated together.
  - `CLAUDE.md`
    - aligned section-for-section with `AGENTS.md` using the same operational requirements and terminology.
    - retained scoped-`CLAUDE.md` note while preserving root-level parity expectations.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - no product docs (`docs/`, `docs/wiki/`) required for this slice because the change only updates root agent-governance files.
- Validation:
  - `diff -u AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md` now shows only expected file-identity wording differences in the title/purpose lines; operational content is aligned.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-boundary change; governance/instruction parity refresh only.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS VNC black-screen + immediate `ticket_expired` disconnect fix: submit stream-ticket `vnc_password` during noVNC auth challenge)
- Fixed iOS Remote Desktop behavior where agent-backed Linux VNC could connect to a black screen, then quickly disconnect with debug banner signal:
  - `ticket_expired | proto=vnc | reason=Session ended`.
- Root cause:
  - hub stream tickets now include per-session VNC credentials (`vnc_password`) for agent-backed VNC.
  - iOS desktop runtime did not decode/forward that field and noVNC `credentialsrequired` events were treated as terminal errors instead of credential submission.
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift`
    - added `DesktopStreamTicketResponse.vncPassword` (`vnc_password`) decoding.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopViewModel.swift`
    - stores trimmed ticket `vncPassword` and resets it on disconnect/state resets.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - plumbs `vncPassword` into `DesktopWebView` payload/signature; reconnect signature now includes VNC credential changes.
    - updated debug harness call site to pass the new argument.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - accepts `vncPassword` in connect options.
    - for VNC, initializes noVNC credentials with password when available.
    - on `credentialsrequired`, calls `rfb.sendCredentials({ password })` before surfacing credential error.
  - tests:
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
      - added VNC password signature-churn reconnect coverage.
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests.swift`
      - added decode coverage for `DesktopStreamTicketResponse.vncPassword`.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests` -> **31 passed**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-boundary change; this is iOS client parity with existing stream-ticket auth contract.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS VNC blank-screen after auth fix: disable forced server resize on mobile)
- Fixed iOS Remote View behavior where VNC could authenticate successfully but remain on a black/blank framebuffer.
- Root cause:
  - iOS noVNC runtime forced `rfb.resizeSession = true`.
  - during early mobile layout, viewport dimensions can briefly resolve as tiny/zero and some VNC servers accept that resize, leaving the session effectively black.
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - updated VNC mode application to keep `scaleViewport = true` but set `resizeSession = false`.
    - added inline rationale comment to preserve intent and avoid regression.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
  - focused test run is currently blocked by a pre-existing unrelated compile error in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Services/ServicesView.swift` (`builtinIconKeyPattern` JS-regex literal syntax in Swift source).
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-boundary change; this is iOS VNC client runtime stability hardening.

  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level change; this is app branding asset parity only.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS Remote View cross-origin failure hardening: native custom-origin WS + CORS-safe bundle responses)
- Fixed iOS Remote View failures that could still surface `Cross-origin script load denied by Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policy` after the custom desktop bundle loader rollout.
- Root cause:
  - iOS desktop runtime now loads from `labtether-local://bundle/...`, but hub websocket origin checks only explicitly trusted `null` and `file://` native origins.
  - bundled desktop asset responses from the custom scheme handler did not include explicit HTTP/CORS headers, which can cause stricter module-loading environments to reject dynamic imports.
- Implementation:
  - `cmd/labtether/main.go`
    - extended origin validation for native one-time-ticket stream endpoints to allow trusted `labtether-local://` origin scheme.
    - kept non-stream/unknown custom-origin requests denied.
  - `cmd/labtether/main_origin_test.go`
    - added coverage for:
      - allow `labtether-local://bundle` on native stream routes,
      - deny same origin outside native stream paths,
      - deny unrelated custom schemes.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - upgraded `DesktopBundleSchemeHandler` to return `HTTPURLResponse` with explicit `Content-Type` and CORS headers.
    - added `OPTIONS` handling and safer resource resolution for custom-scheme asset requests.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCheckSameOrigin' -count=1` -> pass.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestStreamTicketLifecycle|TestHandleDesktopStreamTicketIssuesAgentVNCPasswordWithoutQueryLeak' -count=1` -> pass.
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests test` -> **9 passed**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-boundary change; this is native-client interoperability hardening within existing desktop stream and origin-guard contracts.

- Updated iOS app icon label/display name to `LabTether`.

  - no architecture-level decision change; visual/UX polish of existing live-mode surfaces only.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS VNC `new RFB(...)` constructor crash fix)
- Fixed iOS Remote View failure where VNC startup could error with:
  - `Object is not a constructor (evaluating 'new RFB(screenEl, options.wsURL, { credentials: {} })')`
- Root cause:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html` noVNC loader resolved `module.default` first.
  - current bundled `novnc-rfb.esm.js` export shape wraps `RFB` under nested default export, so `module.default` is an object, not the constructor.
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
  - added `resolveNoVNCRFB(...)` to safely unwrap import variants and select only a function constructor.
  - `loadNoVNC()` now uses `resolveNoVNCRFB` and emits a precise error when constructor resolution fails.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level decision change; this is client-side module-loader compatibility hardening.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS widget extension: fix Xcode Show Widget request-denied for debug placeholder)
- Fixed widget-extension debug launch failure where Xcode requested `systemMedium` for the debug placeholder but the placeholder only declared `systemSmall`.
- Root cause:
  - `DebugPlaceholderWidget` in `LabTetherLiveActivitiesWidget` only advertised `.systemSmall`,
  - Xcode `Show Widget` invoked SpringBoard with a `systemMedium` request,
  - SpringBoard rejected launch with `SBAvocadoDebuggingControllerErrorDomain Code=3` (`Request widget family ... is not supported`).
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherLiveActivitiesWidget/LabTetherLiveActivitiesWidget.swift`
    - expanded debug placeholder families to:
      - `.systemSmall`
      - `.systemMedium`
      - `.systemLarge`
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level change; debug-only widget descriptor compatibility fix for Xcode tooling behavior.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS Remote View CORS/module failure fix for bundled desktop clients)
- Fixed iOS Remote View failures showing `Cross-origin script load denied by Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policy` during desktop connection startup.
- Root cause:
  - `DesktopWebView` loaded `desktop.html` from `file://` (`loadFileURL`), while the desktop runtime used dynamic ESM `import(...)` for bundled noVNC/Guacamole/SPICE clients.
  - in WKWebView, those module fetches were treated as disallowed cross-origin loads from the file-origin context, preventing client initialization before protocol handshake.
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - added `DesktopBundleAssetLoader` for deterministic custom-scheme desktop asset URLs + MIME mapping.
    - added `DesktopBundleSchemeHandler` (`WKURLSchemeHandler`) to serve bundled desktop assets from `labtether-local://bundle/...`.
    - switched desktop webview bootstrap from `loadFileURL(...)` to `load(URLRequest(url: labtether-local://bundle/desktop.html))` with `file://` fallback guard.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
    - added tests for custom entry URL scheme and MIME resolution coverage for desktop vendor bundles.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests test` -> **9 passed**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-boundary or protocol-contract change; this is iOS webview asset-loading hardening to keep existing bundled desktop clients functional.

## 2026-03-05 (Installer compatibility + faster dev-mode restart workflow)
- Reduced Linux agent install friction in mixed-version environments by switching Add Device generated commands to the compatibility-first flag `--install-vnc-prereqs` (alias remains `--auto-install-vnc`).
- Frontend and installer messaging updates:
  - `web/console/app/components/AddDeviceModal/AgentSetupStep.tsx`
    - Add Device Linux command now emits `--install-vnc-prereqs` when desktop prereq auto-install is enabled.
  - `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts`
    - updated installer-default assertion to expect `--install-vnc-prereqs`.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template.go`
    - non-TTY hint now recommends `--install-vnc-prereqs` first and keeps `--auto-install-vnc` as alias guidance.
- Dev workflow speed-ups:
  - `scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh`
    - added `--restart` and `--stop` options for faster tmux lifecycle control.
  - `scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh`
    - added `--restart` and `--stop` options for faster tmux lifecycle control.
  - `scripts/dev-up.sh` (new)
    - one-command backend+frontend startup/restart orchestration,
    - install-script readiness probe (`/install.sh`),
    - prints compatibility-first Linux install command template.
  - `Makefile`
    - new targets: `dev-up`, `dev-up-restart`, `dev-backend-bg-restart`, `dev-frontend-bg-restart`.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/SOLO_OPERATIONS.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh scripts/dev-up.sh` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript' -count=1` -> pass.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level boundary change; this is installer UX compatibility hardening and developer workflow speed optimization.

## 2026-03-05 (Node detail remote view regression: agent hosts misclassified as infra)
- Fixed missing `Remote View` panel on connected agent host node pages.
- Root cause:
  - node detail page treated all canonical `host` assets as infrastructure hosts because taxonomy infra-host fallback includes `type=host`.
  - desktop eligibility logic hides desktop panels for infra hosts unless they are Proxmox node assets.
  - result: normal `source=agent,type=host` nodes incorrectly lost `Remote View`.
- Implementation:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx`
  - panel infra classification now applies only to connector-host sources (`proxmox`, `pbs`, `truenas`, `docker`, `portainer`) instead of all canonical host types.
  - restores remote view eligibility for connected agent hosts.
- Tests:
  - `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts`
  - added regression test: `node detail shows remote view for connected agent host`.
  - retained Add Device VNC-default test coverage in same run.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/console.spec.ts --grep "node detail shows remote view for connected agent host|add device agent linux installer enables install-vnc-prereqs by default"`
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level change; this is frontend eligibility bugfix for node detail panel rendering.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS Files + mac-agent home-root parity: prevent invalid parent navigation and `HTTP 400` base-path errors)

## 2026-03-05 (alert instance deletion: backend API + iOS sync)
- Added alert-instance delete support so iOS can remove alerts and keep backend state in sync.
- Backend changes:
  - `internal/persistence/types.go`
    - added `DeleteAlertInstance(id string) error` to `AlertInstanceStore`.
  - `internal/persistence/memory_alert_instance_store.go`
    - implemented in-memory alert instance delete with not-found guard.
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_alert_instances_store.go`
    - implemented Postgres delete with rows-affected not-found behavior.
  - `cmd/labtether/alert_instances_handlers.go`
    - `DELETE /alerts/instances/{id}` is now supported from `handleAlertInstanceActions`.
    - returns `{ "status": "deleted" }` on success.
  - tests:
    - added `cmd/labtether/alert_instances_handlers_test.go` covering:
      - delete success removes instance,
      - delete missing instance returns `404`,
      - unsupported methods on `/alerts/instances/{id}` return `405`.
- iOS changes:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift`
    - added `DeleteAlertInstanceResponse`.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Alerts/AlertsViewModel.swift`
    - added `delete(_:)` flow using `DELETE /alerts/instances/{id}`.
    - optimistic local removal + cache update.
    - offline queue now supports `delete` action replay.
    - queued delete treats backend `404` as already-complete.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Alerts/AlertsView.swift`
    - added trailing swipe delete action.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Alerts/AlertDetailView.swift`
    - added delete button and dismiss-on-success behavior.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/API.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/alerts-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAlertInstanceActions(DeleteRemovesInstance|DeleteNotFound|RejectsUnsupportedMethodOnInstance)' -count=1` -> pass.
  - `go test ./internal/persistence -count=1` -> pass.
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level decision change; this is additive CRUD parity + mobile workflow completion.

## 2026-03-05 (Linux installer UX: `labtether` PATH helper for local agent uninstall/purge)
- Added a Linux installer-managed CLI helper at `/usr/local/bin/labtether` so operators can remove the agent locally without re-running `curl | bash`.
- Installer/runtime changes:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template.go`
    - defines `LABTETHER_CLI_DEST=/usr/local/bin/labtether`.
    - installs a guarded wrapper script (`LABTETHER_AGENT_WRAPPER=1`) unless a non-wrapper `labtether` command already exists.
    - wrapper supports:
      - `labtether agent status`
      - `sudo labtether agent uninstall`
      - `sudo labtether agent purge`
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template_verify_summary.go`
    - installer summary now reports the helper path when installed.
    - post-install tips now include `sudo labtether agent uninstall` / `purge`.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template_args_uninstall.go`
    - uninstall/purge completion output now points to the local helper when present.
- Tests:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
    - script-generation assertions now verify helper command snippets and wrapper marker are present.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(HandleAgentInstallScript|GenerateInstallScript)' -count=1` -> fails in current tree due a pre-existing compile error in `cmd/labtether/alert_instances_handlers_test.go` (`undefined: alerts.SeverityWarning`), so installer test execution is currently blocked.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level decision change; this is an installer/operator UX improvement within the existing Linux agent lifecycle.

## 2026-03-05 (mac-agent parity uplift: discovery/WebRTC settings contract + conditional local status polling)
- Brought the macOS wrapper (`apps/mac-agent`) up to parity with current shared `agentcore` runtime knobs and diagnostics posture.
- mac wrapper settings/runtime changes:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`
  - added local settings + env export wiring for:
    - service discovery policy (`LABTETHER_SERVICES_DISCOVERY_*`),
    - WebRTC controls (`LABTETHER_WEBRTC_*`, `LABTETHER_CAPTURE_FPS`).
  - added keychain-backed TURN password handling (`webrtcTurnPass`) to avoid persisting TURN credentials in plain defaults.
  - expanded validation coverage for:
    - WebRTC FPS bounds,
    - local/LAN port lists,
    - LAN CIDR list format/private-range constraints,
    - LAN scan host cap bounds.
- normalization/security hardening:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettingsNormalization.swift`
  - hardened hub URL normalization to reject embedded credentials/query/fragment.
  - added deterministic normalization helpers for service discovery port/CIDR lists.
- advanced settings UI parity:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`
  - added new Advanced cards:
    - `SERVICE DISCOVERY POLICY`,
    - `REMOTE DESKTOP / WEBRTC`.
  - diagnostics pane now surfaces local API posture details when available (fingerprint, bind address, local auth, insecure transport flag).
- local status transport efficiency:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LocalAPIClient.swift`
  - restored conditional polling using `ETag` / `If-None-Match` on `/agent/status`,
  - added `304` fast-path handling to avoid unnecessary decode churn while preserving reachability state.
- diagnostics export updates:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`
  - `Copy Diagnostics` now includes parity-relevant runtime settings (discovery policy + WebRTC + core policy toggles).
- tests:
  - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/AgentSettingsNormalizationTests.swift`
  - added coverage for:
    - hub URL credential/query/fragment rejection,
    - port-list normalization/dedup/sort + invalid rejection,
    - CIDR normalization and private/local validation.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `cd apps/mac-agent && swift test && swift build` -> pass.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level boundary change; this is parity and operability completion within the existing mac wrapper + shared agentcore runtime design.

## 2026-03-05 (Linux desktop reliability: VNC startup diagnostics + installer auto-install alias)
- Improved Linux/FreeBSD agent VNC startup diagnostics and headless fallback behavior.
- Backend/agent changes:
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc.go`
  - behavior:
    - VNC startup now captures `x11vnc` log output in a temp log and uses log-tail evidence when startup fails.
    - headless display fallback now triggers reliably when startup errors indicate display/X server issues, then retries via `Xvfb`.
    - startup error messages now include concise `x11vnc` log-tail context for faster operator triage.
  - tests:
    - `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc_test.go` updated for broadened display-error detection and log-tail summarization coverage.
- Installer UX/flags:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_script_install_template*.go`
  - behavior:
    - added explicit `--auto-install-vnc` alias (keeps existing `--install-vnc-prereqs` behavior).
    - non-interactive prompt guidance now points to `--auto-install-vnc`.
    - installer summary now reports desktop prerequisite status (`available`, `installed by script`, `missing`, `install failed`).
  - tests:
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go` now validates `--auto-install-vnc` appears in generated script output.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(BuildX11VNCArgsDefaults|BuildX11VNCArgsQualityMapping|BuildXvfbArgs|IsDisplayError|SummarizeProcessLogTail)' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript' -count=1`
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level decision change; this is reliability/operability hardening within existing VNC and installer flows.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS terminal first-principles keyboard docking fix: safe-area inset + input-assistant suppression)
- Resolved persistent on-device keyboard-gap regressions where the terminal helper bar appeared detached from the software keyboard.
- Root cause:
  - helper bar placement relied on manual keyboard-frame math (`keyboardHeight` bottom padding) plus keyboard safe-area bypassing, which drifted on real-device keyboard chrome.
  - WKWebView continued showing iOS input-assistant controls (up/down/check row), visually adding an extra spacer between helper bar and keyboard.
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
  - behavior:
    - switched helper bar anchoring from manual frame offsets to `safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom, spacing: 0)` so the bar docks with keyboard-native layout behavior.
    - removed manual accessory bottom offset (`.padding(.bottom, keyboardHeight)`) and keyboard-safe-area bypassing for this surface.
    - added `TerminalWebView.stripInputAssistant(in:)` and applied it during WKWebView create/update/navigation-finish to clear default iOS input-assistant button groups.
    - added `TerminalWebView.suppressSystemInputAccessory(in:)` (`WKContent*` responder class override) so the iOS previous/next/done accessory bar is fully suppressed instead of intermittently reappearing.
    - preserved layout refresh signaling (`layoutRevision`) so xterm `fit()` still tracks keyboard/accessory geometry changes.
- Docs/wiki updated:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level change; iOS terminal keyboard-layout correctness and UX polish only.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS field-debug step: connection debug banner for terminal/desktop websocket churn)
- Added an operator-visible debug banner for iOS terminal/desktop sessions to speed root-cause capture for random disconnects.
- iOS changes:

    - new key: `lt.settings.connectionDebugBanner`.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/SettingsView.swift`
    - new Behavior toggle: **Connection Debug Banner**.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`

    - added top-right compact overlay (non-debug builds too) that emits latest event telemetry:
      - event name,
      - close code/clean flag,
      - reconnect policy flags (`will`, `auto`),
      - attempt/delay metadata when present,
      - truncated reason/message for quick capture.
- Docs/wiki updated:
