  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`

## 2026-03-05 (iOS reconnect-state hotfix: keep transient websocket drops off the hard disconnect screen)
- Resolved random disconnect-screen churn in iOS terminal/desktop sessions caused by transient websocket drops.
- Root cause:
  - web runtimes emitted `disconnected` before reconnect attempts,
  - Swift state moved to `.disconnected`, which unmounted WKWebView and canceled in-flight reconnect timers.
- Implementation:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`
    - `disconnected` events now include `willReconnect` and `autoReconnectEnabled`.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - `disconnected` events now include `willReconnect` and `autoReconnectEnabled`.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalViewModel.swift`
    - non-user disconnects now stay in `.connecting` when reconnect is pending or auto-reconnect is enabled from an active session.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopViewModel.swift`
    - non-user disconnects now stay in `.connecting` when reconnect is pending or auto-reconnect is enabled from an active session.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
    - added first-principles reconnect trigger on non-user `disconnected` events (not just `ticket_expired`) to request a fresh stream when auto-reconnect is enabled.
  - tests updated:
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests.swift`
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-047)
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' test -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests` -> **pass** (11 tests).

## 2026-03-05 (iOS terminal keyboard accessory visual polish: dock helper bar flush with keyboard)
- Removed the floating/pill-style keyboard helper bar treatment in iOS terminal and switched to a docked accessory surface so the bar visually attaches to the software keyboard without the detached dark gap.

  - behavior:

    - removed horizontal/bottom outer padding that made the helper bar appear floating above keyboard.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS terminal reliability follow-up: helper input queue + safe-area accessory + websocket keepalive)
- Addressed mobile terminal regressions reported in active testing:
  - helper-key taps occasionally dropped,
  - helper bar rendering/placement issues near bottom tab region,
  - random websocket disconnect churn and stale reconnect attempts.
- iOS terminal UI/runtime changes:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
    - switched helper-key bridge from single pending string to batched queue (`pendingInputs`) so rapid key taps are preserved.
    - anchored helper bar above the active software keyboard and added keyboard-frame-driven viewport padding so keyboard/helper chrome does not overlay terminal rows.
    - added keyboard frame observers and explicit xterm `fit()` refresh on layout revision changes.
    - restyled helper bar to a compact floating capsule with high-contrast keycaps and keyboard-dismiss affordance (matched against iOS reference screenshots).
    - added ticket-expiry reconnect handoff path that requests a fresh stream via `TerminalViewModel.connect()` when auto-reconnect is enabled.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/IOSProfileHarnessView.swift`
    - updated harness binding usage to match queued terminal input model.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`
    - added websocket keepalive `ping` interval.
    - added buffered outbound input queue to avoid input drops before socket open.
    - guarded reconnect behavior for one-time ticket URLs (`ticket_expired` signal instead of stale URL retry loop).
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(HandleTerminalControlMessage|IsControlMessage|ParseTerminalSize|SessionStreamTicket)' -count=1` -> **pass**.
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level change; iOS terminal UX/runtime resilience hardening only.

## 2026-03-05 (Repo-wide best-practices audit wave 4: runtime confidence closeout + residual-risk publish)
- Completed Wave 4 runtime closeout for the repo-wide best-practices program.
- Runtime validation execution:
  - canonical `make compose-up` path was blocked by active local non-Docker listeners on canonical ports (`3000`, `8080`, `8443`), so validation ran with an isolated compose project and isolated host ports to avoid disrupting local dev services.
  - `docker compose --project-directory "$PWD" --env-file .env -f /tmp/labtether-wave4.compose.yml up -d --build` -> pass.
  - `LABTETHER_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:18080 LABTETHER_AGENT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:18090 ENV_FILE=.env COMPOSE_FILE=/tmp/labtether-wave4.compose.yml COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=labtether_wave4 ./scripts/smoke-test.sh --skip-compose --timeout 240` -> pass (`Passed: 103`, `Failed: 0`).
  - `docker compose --project-directory "$PWD" --env-file .env -f /tmp/labtether-wave4.compose.yml down` -> pass.
- Runtime defects discovered and fixed in this wave:
  - Docker build architecture defaults:
    - removed hard-coded `TARGETARCH=amd64` defaults and added `GOOS/GOARCH` fallback resolution so compose builds target the builder platform architecture by default.
    - files:
      - `build/go-service.Dockerfile`
      - `build/agent.Dockerfile`
  - Hub non-root cert/data bootstrap failure:
    - fixed `auto TLS provisioning failed: mkdir /data/certs: permission denied` by pre-seeding `/data` and `/ca` with uid/gid `65532` ownership in the hub image so named-volume initialization preserves writable permissions for the distroless runtime user.
    - file:
      - `build/go-service.Dockerfile`
- Full post-fix guardrail revalidation:
  - `make check` -> pass.
  - `make security-gosec` -> pass (`68 reviewed findings`).
  - `make check-docs` -> pass.
  - `make perf-gate` -> pass.
  - `go run honnef.co/go/tools/cmd/staticcheck@latest ./...` -> pass (`0` findings).
  - `go run golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest ./...` -> pass (`0` reachable vulnerabilities; `1` module-level vulnerability not called).
  - `cd web/console && npm audit --audit-level=moderate` -> pass (`0` vulnerabilities).
- Docs/wiki/notes updates in same workstream:
  - `docs/archive/reports/REPO_BEST_PRACTICES_AUDIT_REPORT_2026-03-05_WAVE4.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/install-docker-compose.md`
  - `notes/REPO_BEST_PRACTICES_AUDIT_PLAN.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- ADR rationale:
  - no architecture-level model change; runtime portability and container filesystem permission hardening only.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS terminal websocket failure follow-up: backend stream preflight now returns explicit WS errors)
- Improved SSH terminal stream error reporting path to avoid generic websocket transport failures when backend preflight checks fail before stream start.
- Backend change:
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_ssh.go` now upgrades websocket first, then performs SSH config checks and emits structured terminal error events over websocket (`writeTerminalError(...)`) for:
    - missing SSH/agent runtime config,
    - invalid SSH config resolution.
  - this prevents opaque browser-side websocket handshake failures for backend pre-upgrade validation errors.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(DialSSHWithRetry|StartSSHInteractiveSessionWithTimeout|HandleTerminalControlMessage|IsControlMessage|ParseTerminalSize|SessionStreamTicket|CheckSameOrigin)' -count=1` -> **pass**.
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no operator workflow/API contract change; stream transport error-path hardening only.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS terminal "stuck on connecting" regression fix)
- Resolved terminal connection deadlock in iOS UI state routing:
  - in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`, changed state rendering so:
    - `.creatingSession` and `.fetchingTicket` show loading UI,
    - `.connecting` and `.connected` both mount `TerminalWebView`.
  - this keeps the webview alive during handshake so JS/websocket events can transition state from `connecting` to `connected` (or `error`/`disconnected`) instead of staying stuck.

  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.

  - no operator-facing workflow or API contract changes; internal iOS UI state-machine rendering fix only.

    - local helper fallback values (`runtime`, `backend_hotspot_explain`),

  - removed stale entries and aligned line references.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS live-activity memory kill + SSH stream origin + Face ID-first auth hardening)
- Addressed field issues reported from iOS app-extension/runtime:
  - Live Activity extension memory pressure (`com.labtether.mobile.liveactivities` terminated due to memory issue).
  - iOS terminal/desktop stream failures from native-webview origin handling.
  - Face ID preference using passcode path too aggressively.
- iOS runtime hardening:
  - reduced Live Activity churn by skipping no-op ActivityKit updates for incident/session activities,
  - added bounded payload normalization for incident/session text fields before publish,
  - added heartbeat gating for session `updatedAt` refresh (avoids unnecessary high-frequency updates),
  - moved update calls through nonisolated activity-id lookup helpers to avoid Swift strict-concurrency data-race warnings on actor-isolated references.
- added on-device profiling helper script for repeatable repro capture:
  - `scripts/perf/ios-liveactivities-memory-trace.sh` records:
    - `Activity Monitor` all-process trend trace,
    - attached `Allocations`/`Leaks` traces when target PID is attachable,
    - device process snapshots before/after recording.
- Backend SSH/native stream hardening:
  - preserved same-origin checks while allowing native iOS `Origin: null` / `file://` only on ticket-authenticated session stream paths (`/terminal/sessions/{id}/stream`, `/desktop/sessions/{id}/stream`).
  - added origin-check regression tests in `cmd/labtether/main_origin_test.go`.
- iOS auth hardening:
  - remote access approval now attempts biometrics-first (`deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics`) and falls back to broader device-owner auth only when biometrics are unavailable.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' test` -> **TEST SUCCEEDED** (`124 passed`, `0 failed`).
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCheckSameOrigin|TestSessionStreamTicketEndpoint' -count=1` -> pass.
  - `./scripts/perf/ios-liveactivities-memory-trace.sh --device 00008140-000539A901DB001C --activity-seconds 5 --alloc-seconds 5 --skip-leaks --pre-roll-seconds 1 --output-dir /tmp/labtether-ios-liveactivities-memory-smoke4` -> pass.
- Docs/wiki updates in same workstream:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`

## 2026-03-05 (iOS Live Activities audit + debug hardening)
- Completed a full audit/debug pass on the new iOS Live Activities/session wiring and fixed concrete lifecycle regressions:
  - prevented unintended global session Live Activity termination from terminal/desktop idle/error states when no active session ID exists.
  - cleared stale terminal session state (`sessionID`, `wsURL`) at connection start to avoid reconnecting-state updates against an old session.
  - added persisted-context pruning in `SessionLiveActivityManager` so stale stored session context is removed when no matching ActivityKit session activity exists.
- Added regression tests:
  - `LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests.swift`
  - `LabTetherMobileTests/SessionLiveActivityManagerTests.swift`
- Validation:

  - `go vet ./...` -> pass.
  - `go test ./...` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
- Docs/wiki rationale:
  - no operator/API workflow changed; this work was internal correctness hardening for session Live Activity lifecycle and stale-state handling.

## 2026-03-05 (RBAC foundation + optional OIDC + Home Assistant add-on runtime)
- Completed the remaining post-onboarding foundation tracks in one workstream:

    - extended persisted auth user schema with role/provider/oidc-subject fields,
    - added built-in role semantics (`owner`, `admin`, `operator`, `viewer`),
    - enforced write-method denial for `viewer` globally in auth middleware,
    - enforced admin-only wrappers on security-sensitive route groups (runtime settings, enrollment/token admin, admin reset, auth user admin endpoints).

    - added optional OIDC provider initialization from `LABTETHER_OIDC_*` env vars,
    - added backend auth endpoints:
      - `GET /auth/providers`,

      - `POST /auth/oidc/callback`,
      - `GET/POST/PATCH /auth/users*` (admin/owner),

    - added auth provider discovery proxy route and OIDC start/callback proxy routes,
    - updated login page to show `Sign in with SSO` when OIDC is enabled while preserving local username/password login.
  - Home Assistant add-on:
    - upgraded add-on from scaffold to runnable experimental runtime:
      - real hub binary image build in add-on Dockerfile,
      - startup script validates options, supports generated credential persistence, and starts bundled local Postgres fallback when `database_url` is unset,

## 2026-03-05 (Perf-gate follow-up: query stats contract fix)
- Fixed CI `perf-gate` threshold failures caused by missing `pg_stat_statements` in the GitHub-hosted Postgres runtime.
- Implementation:
  - updated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `perf-gate` job to start Postgres with:
    - `shared_preload_libraries=pg_stat_statements`,
    - `pg_stat_statements.track=all`,
    - explicit `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_stat_statements` bootstrap before backend startup.
  - improved apples/threshold diagnostics:
    - apples summary now includes `query_stats_enabled` and `query_stats_error`,
    - threshold gate now emits an explicit query-stats-unavailable failure when query metrics are absent.
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/perf/backend-hotspot-apples.sh scripts/perf/backend-hotspot-thresholds.sh` -> pass.
  - reduced local apples verification run: `tmp/perf/runs/20260305-123310-local-ci-check2` -> pass.
  - threshold report confirms:
    - `query_stats_enabled=true`,
    - populated `batch_snapshot_lateral` and `sources_groupby_windowed` key deltas.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS observability + accessibility/performance polish: items 4 and 5)
- Completed the requested iOS workstream for production observability and accessibility/performance polish.
- Backend:
  - added authenticated mobile telemetry ingest endpoint:
    - `POST /telemetry/mobile/client` in `cmd/labtether/mobile_client_telemetry_handlers.go`,
    - route wiring in `cmd/labtether/http_handlers.go`,
    - handler tests in `cmd/labtether/mobile_client_telemetry_handlers_test.go`.
- iOS observability:
  - added `Core/Observability/MobileTelemetryCenter.swift` with bounded queueing, throttling, and best-effort delivery.
  - wired lifecycle instrumentation in `AppDelegate.swift`:
    - launch, foreground/background, terminate, memory warning, and prior-session unexpected-termination signal.
  - wired API request timing/error instrumentation in `Core/Networking/APIClient.swift` (with `/telemetry/*` recursion guard).
  - wired realtime websocket instrumentation in `Core/Realtime/EventSocket.swift`:

## 2026-03-05 (Hotspot follow-up: strict apples A/B + `QueryEvents` site projection fast path)
- Completed requested debugging items 1 + 2 with a strict apples-to-apples run and targeted `QueryEvents` mitigation:
  - strict baseline run (same load mix as prior hotspot profile):
    - `tmp/perf/runs/20260305-004207-backend-post7a-highconcurrency-apples`
    - load profile: `2400` `GET /status/aggregate` + `4800` `GET /status/aggregate/live`.
  - implemented `QueryEvents` field projection support:
    - added `FieldKeys` to `logs.QueryRequest`,
    - added Postgres fast path for `FieldKeys=["site_id"]` using projected `projected_site_id`,
    - wired site-reliability and site-filtered source-summary paths to request only `site_id`,
    - mirrored projection behavior in memory log store.
  - added regression coverage:
    - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store_test.go` field projection behavior,
    - `cmd/labtether/performance_hotspots_test.go` request-shape checks for site reliability and site-filtered source summaries.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/persistence ./cmd/labtether` -> pass.
  - `go vet ./...` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
  - backend runtime verified in `DEV_MODE=true` with authenticated `/debug/pprof/` returning `200`.
- Canonical post-change run:
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260305-005649-backend-post8e-highconcurrency-apples`.
- Observed outcome (vs strict baseline `post7a`):
  - full-fields reliability query shape (`SELECT ... message, fields, ...`) dropped from `2400` calls to `0`.
  - projected-site query shape (`... projected_site_id ...`) replaced it with `2400` calls.
  - `QueryEvents` alloc-space in pprof dropped materially:
    - flat: `1738.07MB` -> `1057.38MB` (`-39.2%`),
    - cumulative: `3095.71MB` -> `1488.40MB` (`-51.9%`).
  - `encoding/json.Unmarshal` alloc-space dropped:
    - `600.58MB` -> `55.51MB` (`-90.8%`).
  - CPU cumulative time improved on hotspot functions:
    - `PostgresStore.QueryEvents`: `1.48s` -> `1.16s`,
    - `buildSiteReliabilityComputationWithAssets`: `1.26s` -> `1.14s`.
  - DB-side execution time for the projected reliability query increased modestly versus the old full-field query (`~+4.9%` total in this apples run), but end-to-end backend allocation pressure reduced substantially.

## 2026-03-05 (Debugging wave: source-query diagnostics + watermark cache refresh + poll coalescing)
- Completed the follow-up debugging bundle across backend + console:
  - added source-query diagnostics traces for `/logs/sources` and status aggregate source-summary path with fields:
    - `caller`, `mode`, `cache_hit`, `window_start`, `window_end`, `limit`, `source_count`, `duration_ms`.
  - added optional `caller` labeling support and wired console proxy defaults:
    - `/api/status` -> `caller=console.status.full`,
    - `/api/status/live` -> `caller=console.status.live`,
    - `/api/logs/sources` -> `caller=console.logs.sources.proxy` (when missing).
  - tightened `/logs/sources` window handling by minute-bucketing parsed `from`/`to` windows consistently (not only default windows) for higher cache-key reuse.
  - replaced per-request Postgres log watermark reads with a cached watermark strategy:
    - write-through updates on successful `AppendEvent`,
    - short refresh-interval fallback query reads,
    - invalidation after retention prune deletes log rows.
  - improved console polling behavior in `StatusContext`:
    - coalesced overlapping live/full status fetches,
    - ensured polling loop restarts do not duplicate timers,
    - ignored websocket-triggered live refreshes while tab is hidden,
    - slowed full status polling cadence (`120s`) to reduce heavy source-summary churn.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
  - dev backend restarted with `DEV_MODE=true` and pprof registered.
- Fresh profiling capture:
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260305-003251-backend-post7-highconcurrency`.
- Observed outcome (compared with prior high-concurrency run):
  - windowed source aggregation dropped from dominant hotspot (`~76.7s` / `4405` calls in `post6f`) to a minor contributor (`~0.30s` / `22` calls in `post7` under the applied load).
  - `SELECT COALESCE(MAX(timestamp), ...) FROM log_events` watermark query dropped out of key deltas.
  - backend logs now emit caller-tagged source diagnostics, with cache-hit visibility during stress runs.

## 2026-03-05 (Hotspot follow-up: `/logs/sources` default windowing + telemetry batch SQL refinement)
- Continued backend hotspot mitigation using fresh dev-mode profiling runs and `pg_stat_statements` deltas:
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260304-234542-backend-post6c-highconcurrency` (pre-fix baseline),
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260305-000122-backend-post6d-highconcurrency` (after first restart),
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260305-000518-backend-post6f-highconcurrency` (after follow-up fixes).
- Implemented non-site log-source query tuning:
  - `GET /logs/sources` now defaults to recent-window aggregation (`ListSourcesSince`) with window parsing (`window` / `from` / `to`) and minute-bucketed cache keys for polling stability.
  - added explicit all-time override (`all=1`) to preserve full-history source enumeration when needed.
  - reused log-watermark keyed source cache in endpoint path to avoid repeated aggregate SQL on unchanged logs.
- Implemented Postgres telemetry batch snapshot refinement:
  - constrained snapshot reads to canonical metric names,
  - replaced broad `DISTINCT ON (asset_id, metric)` batch scan/sort with index-friendly per-`(asset, metric)` LATERAL latest-value lookups in `SnapshotMany`.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence` -> pass.
  - dev tmux backend restarted with `DEV_MODE=true`; startup logs confirm `/debug/pprof/` registration.
- Outcome:
  - all-time `logs/sources` aggregate query dropped out of dominant SQL-time contributors in latest high-concurrency run.
  - telemetry batch snapshot path shifted to lower per-call cost under load.

## 2026-03-05 (iOS profiling harness + webview reconnect churn reduction)
- Completed deep iOS profiling pass with automated terminal/desktop interaction loops:

  - removed forced SwiftUI `.id(...)` recreation in `TerminalView` and `DesktopView`,
  - added explicit coordinator reconnect logic so terminal/desktop web clients now reconnect when stream URL/protocol payloads rotate without requiring full representable remount,
  - retained explicit JS disconnect teardown in `dismantleUIView` for lifecycle safety.

  - new `WebViewReconnectTests` validates terminal reconnect behavior on stream URL rotation,
  - validates desktop reconnect behavior on protocol/password signature changes while avoiding reconnect churn on identical signatures.

## 2026-03-04 (iOS audit follow-up: realtime lifecycle correctness + webview teardown hardening)
- Performed focused iOS runtime audit for reconnect/memory correctness:
  - `xcodebuild ... analyze` (LabTetherMobile scheme, iPhone 17 Pro simulator).
  - `xcodebuild ... test` full iOS suite (`88 tests`).
  - `xctrace record --template Leaks` startup/runtime spot-check.
  - `leaks <pid>` simulator process sample check (0 leaked bytes reported in captured run).
- Implemented iOS/mobile lifecycle fixes:
  - `EventSocket` reconnect backoff now uses the correct first-delay order (1s seed before exponential growth).
  - `EventBus.disconnect()` no longer clears handler registrations, preventing post-resume realtime subscription loss.
  - `MainTabView` now re-registers realtime handlers on foreground activation before reconnecting socket transport.
  - `TerminalWebView` and `DesktopWebView` now issue explicit JS disconnect during `dismantleUIView` teardown.
  - `TerminalView` now re-keys `TerminalWebView` by `wsURL` to force clean renderer/session rotation for new ticket URLs.
  - Reduced unnecessary JS bridge churn by only pushing terminal/desktop runtime toggles when values actually change.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' test` -> **TEST SUCCEEDED** (`88 tests, 0 failures`).
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' analyze` -> **ANALYZE SUCCEEDED**.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (`ADR-032`).

## 2026-03-04 (Hotspot wave continuation: exact dead-letter totals + batch telemetry snapshots + query observability)
- Continued backend hotspot mitigation on dead-letter, telemetry overview, and log-source aggregate paths.
- Implemented exact dead-letter total support:
  - added optional `persistence.DeadLetterLogCountStore` (`CountDeadLetterEvents(from,to)`),
  - implemented count support in memory/postgres log stores,
  - updated `/queue/dead-letters` and status aggregate dead-letter snapshot paths to use count-derived totals (no projection-limit cap),
  - added status dead-letter snapshot cache keyed by log watermark + window.
- Implemented telemetry snapshot fan-out reduction:
  - added optional `persistence.TelemetrySnapshotBatchStore` (`SnapshotMany(assetIDs, at)`),
  - implemented batch snapshot support in memory/postgres telemetry stores,
  - wired `/metrics/overview` and status aggregate telemetry overview to prefer batch snapshots.
- Implemented log-source hotspot mitigation:
  - added status recent log-source summary cache keyed by log watermark + window,
  - extended memory log store with `ListSourcesSince` + `LogEventsWatermark`,
  - added Postgres migration `Version 40`: `idx_log_events_timestamp_source`.
- Added DB query hotspot observability:
  - added Postgres `TopQueryStats` support over `pg_stat_statements`,
  - added `/worker/stats` `performance` payload with `pg_stat_statements_enabled`, `top_queries`, and error reporting for unavailable extension/runtime errors.
- Added/updated regression coverage:
  - `cmd/labtether/performance_hotspots_test.go`:
    - dead-letter cache/count path coverage,
    - log-source cache path coverage,
    - batch telemetry overview coverage (`/metrics/overview` and status aggregate live payload),
  - `cmd/labtether/deadletters_credentials_api_test.go` exact-total-over-1000 regression,
  - `cmd/labtether/worker_executors_stats_test.go` worker performance snapshot behavior,
  - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store_test.go` count/sources/watermark coverage,
  - `internal/persistence/memory_telemetry_store_test.go` batch snapshot coverage.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` -> pass.
  - `go test ./...` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/API.md`,
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/performance-slow-ui.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`,
  - `docs/ADR.md` (`ADR-031: Batch Snapshot Loading, Exact Dead-Letter Totals, and Watermark Query Caches`).

## 2026-03-04 (iOS desktop protocol parity completion: RDP/SPICE/WebRTC rendering + fallback safety)
- Completed the remaining iOS remote-desktop parity gap by enabling protocol renderers directly in the mobile desktop runtime:
  - RDP via Guacamole client bridge,
  - SPICE via SPICE HTML5 client + one-time `spice-ticket` password flow,
  - WebRTC signaling/peer flow with input channel handling.
- iOS desktop model/runtime updates:
  - `DesktopViewModel` now requests `POST /desktop/sessions/{id}/spice-ticket` for SPICE sessions and stores session-scoped password for renderer bootstrap.
  - desktop web runtime (`Resources/desktop.html`) now routes by requested protocol and manages protocol-specific lifecycle cleanup/reconnect behavior.
  - added automatic WebRTC-to-VNC downgrade when signaling frames indicate non-WebRTC transport, with `protocol_fallback` surfaced back to Swift UI.
  - kept explicit protocol query enforcement in desktop websocket URL builder to prevent implicit backend routing drift.
- iOS UI bridge updates:
  - `DesktopView` now passes protocol/spice-password connect payloads into WKWebView runtime.
  - protocol options are fully selectable on iOS and in-session hint/toolbar behavior is protocol-aware.
  - protocol fallback/active protocol events now update UI state via `DesktopViewModel.handleWebViewEvent(...)`.
- Tests:
  - added SPICE ticket decode contract coverage in `LabTetherMobileTests.swift`.
  - added desktop stream URL protocol-replacement coverage in `StreamURLBuilderTests.swift`.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17,OS=26.2' test -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/StreamURLBuilderTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/LabTetherMobileTests` -> **TEST SUCCEEDED**.
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17,OS=26.2' test` -> **TEST SUCCEEDED** (`88 tests, 0 failures`).
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- Docs/wiki updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- ADR note:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update required (protocol-client parity and lifecycle hardening within existing desktop session architecture; no new architectural boundary introduced).

## 2026-03-04 (iOS parity follow-up: desktop protocol UX safety + logs live mode)
- Completed the remaining iOS parity follow-up slice identified after the operations wave:
  - desktop protocol selector/fallback UX,
  - live logs streaming UX.
- Desktop iOS updates:
  - added explicit desktop connection settings UI in `DesktopView` (protocol visibility, quality, display, recording, connect action).
  - protocol list now shows all backend modes, with iOS-native support labeling; non-VNC modes are marked web-console-only.
  - hardened desktop session correctness in `DesktopViewModel` by forcing explicit protocol query on stream URL build (`forcedProtocol`) so mobile sessions do not drift to backend implicit defaults (for example Windows auto-RDP) that noVNC cannot render.
  - surfaced active desktop protocol and compatibility notice in-session.
- Logs iOS updates:
  - added `isLiveMode` flow in `LogsViewModel` with lifecycle-safe polling (3s) and cancellation on disappear.
  - added event-driven refresh hooks for `alert.*`, `job.completed`, `docker.event`, and `heartbeat.update` while live mode is enabled.
  - added bounded + deduplicated merge behavior for live updates (`maxStoredEvents=400`) to avoid unbounded memory growth.
  - added UI live toggle and status banner in `LogsView`.
  - wired `More -> Logs` to pass shared `EventBus` so live mode can react to real-time events.
- Test updates:
  - added desktop stream URL protocol-forcing regression in `LabTetherMobileTests/StreamURLBuilderTests.swift`.
  - added logs merge/dedupe regression in `LabTetherMobileTests/LabTetherMobileTests.swift`.
  - normalized IPv6 host assertions in stream URL tests to handle bracketed vs unbracketed `URLComponents.host` behavior on current SDK/runtime.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17,OS=26.2' test` -> **TEST SUCCEEDED** (`86 tests, 0 failures`).
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17,OS=26.2' test -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/StreamURLBuilderTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/LabTetherMobileTests` -> **TEST SUCCEEDED**.
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' build` -> **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- Docs/wiki updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- ADR note:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update required (mobile UX/composition hardening on existing desktop/log transport architecture; no new architectural boundary introduced).

## 2026-03-04 (iOS operations parity wave: Actions + Sites + Recordings)

## 2026-03-04 (Hotspot wave continuation: projected dead-letter query path + partial index)
- Continued backend hotspot mitigation by removing dead-letter map-decode overhead on list/analytics paths.
- Implemented projected dead-letter query support:
  - added `logs.DeadLetterEvent` projection type and optional persistence interface `persistence.DeadLetterLogStore`,
  - implemented `QueryDeadLetterEvents(...)` in:
    - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store.go`,
    - `internal/persistence/postgres_telemetry_logs.go` (SQL field projection from `log_events.fields` + typed deliveries parse).
- Wired status and API handlers to shared projected query helper:
  - `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_payloads.go` (`statusLoadDeadLetters`),
  - `cmd/labtether/audit_deadletter_handlers.go` (`handleDeadLetters`),
  - helper path in `cmd/labtether/dead_letter_helpers.go` now prefers `DeadLetterLogStore` and falls back to single `QueryEvents` compatibility read.
- Added DB migration `Version 39`:
  - `idx_log_events_dead_letter_error_timestamp ON log_events(timestamp DESC) WHERE source='dead_letter' AND level='error'`.
- Expanded hotspot regression coverage:
  - `cmd/labtether/performance_hotspots_test.go` now asserts optimized dead-letter query path usage (`QueryDeadLetterEvents`) and verifies `QueryEvents` fallback is skipped when optimized store support exists.
  - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store_test.go` adds projected dead-letter query behavior coverage.
- Validation (targeted):

  - `go test ./internal/persistence -run 'TestMemoryLogStore(QueryEventsExcludeFields|QueryDeadLetterEventsProjectsAndFilters)$'` -> pass.

  - `go test ./...` -> pass.

## 2026-03-04 (Hotspot wave: QueryEvents mitigation + Logs/Devices route telemetry)
- Continued performance hotspot mitigation with focus on `PostgresStore.QueryEvents` and frontend route observability.
- Backend log-query mitigation:
  - added optional field projection to log queries via `logs.QueryRequest.ExcludeFields`,
  - updated `PostgresStore.QueryEvents` to skip JSON field decode/clone when fields are excluded,
  - updated selected high-volume callers to opt out of fields when not needed:
    - logs query route (`include_fields=0`, non-site-filtered path),
    - status aggregate recent logs/source aggregation (non-site-filtered path),
    - alert log-pattern evaluator,
    - TrueNAS events endpoint.
  - added DB migration `Version 38` with `idx_log_events_source_level_timestamp` index.
- Frontend route telemetry:
  - added authenticated backend ingest endpoint `POST /telemetry/frontend/perf` in `cmd/labtether/frontend_perf_handlers.go`,
  - added console proxy route `POST /api/telemetry/perf`,
  - added throttled Logs/Devices route instrumentation:
    - `request.logs_query`,
    - `render.logs_results`,
    - `compute.device_sections`,
    - `render.query_to_sections`.
  - telemetry is persisted as log events with `source=frontend_perf`.
- Tests added/updated:
  - `cmd/labtether/frontend_perf_handlers_test.go`,
  - `cmd/labtether/metrics_logs_api_test.go` (`include_fields=0` coverage + site-filter fallback behavior),
  - `internal/persistence/memory_log_store_test.go` (`ExcludeFields` behavior).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence ./internal/connectors/webservice` -> pass.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(HandleFrontendPerfTelemetry|LogsQuery|LogsQueryIncludeFieldsFalseOmitsFields|LogsQuerySiteFilterIncludeFieldsFalseStillFiltersByFieldSiteID|StatusLoadDeadLettersUsesSingleQuery|HandleDeadLettersUsesSingleQuery)$'` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
- Profiling evidence:
  - post-change capture at `/Users/michael/Documents/Projects/Vibe Coding/LabTether/tmp/perf/runs/20260304-223516-backend-post3`.
  - `QueryEvents` remains the dominant alloc-space hotspot; dead-letter field decode and aggregate-path JSON encode work still contribute significantly.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/API.md`,
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/performance-slow-ui.md`,
  - `docs/ADR.md` (`ADR-029: QueryEvents Field Projection and Frontend Route Telemetry`).

## 2026-03-04 (iOS console audit: reconnect correctness, stream URL safety, query encoding, webview lifecycle)
- Ran a full iOS audit pass focused on correctness, memory/lifecycle, and performance-sensitive paths:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F'`
  - `xcodebuild analyze ...` on the same scheme/destination.
- Implemented targeted fixes in `apps/ios-console`:
  - Reconnect correctness:
    - canceled stale reconnect tasks at `EventSocket.connect()` start and guarded reconnect scheduling while connected,
    - cleaned up failed websocket/session instances before requeue.
  - Stream URL correctness:
    - replaced string-concatenated terminal/desktop websocket URL assembly with component-based builders in:
      - `LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalViewModel.swift`
      - `LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopViewModel.swift`
    - now handles IPv6 hosts, relative stream paths, and query-item composition safely.
  - Query safety/perf:
    - added delimiter-safe query value escaping helper in `Core/Networking/APIClient.swift`,
    - migrated Files/Logs/Topology query construction to use the helper.
  - Cache concurrency hardening:
    - replaced shared static JSON encoder/decoder instances in `ResponseCache` with per-call instances to avoid cross-task mutable-state contention.
  - Webview lifecycle/reconnect cleanup:
    - added `dismantleUIView` teardown for terminal/desktop `WKWebView` wrappers (delegate + message handler removal, stop loading),
    - ensured webview JS runtimes clear pending reconnect timers on manual disconnect/unload (`terminal.html`, `desktop.html`).
- Added regression tests:
  - `LabTetherMobileTests/StreamURLBuilderTests.swift` (terminal/desktop websocket URL building for query + IPv6/relative path cases),
  - `APIClientTests.testEncodeQueryValueEscapesDelimiterCharacters`.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test` -> pass (`81` tests, `0` failures),
  - `xcodebuild analyze` -> pass.
- Docs/wiki updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`,
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/terminal-session-failures.md`,
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`.
- ADR:
  - no architecture-level decision introduced; no `docs/ADR.md` change required for this iOS bugfix wave.

## 2026-03-04 (Backend + frontend performance hotspot mitigation wave: status aggregate, logs, nodes)
- Continued the performance hotspot investigation with fresh authenticated pprof captures under concurrent load and targeted the highest-value alloc/cpu offenders on the status aggregate path and console route behavior.
- Backend mitigation 1 (web service summary allocations):
  - added `SummaryByHosts(...)` to `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator.go` to compute visible service `up/total` counts without materializing full merged service lists.
  - updated `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_collectors.go` `statusWebServiceSummary(...)` to use this summary path.
  - added regression coverage in `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator_test.go`:
    - `TestSummaryByHostsCountsVisibleServicesAndRespectsOverrides`
    - `TestSummaryByHostsEmptyFilterIncludesAllHosts`
- Backend mitigation 2 (canonical payload rebuild churn):
  - added watermark-keyed canonical payload cache in `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_payloads.go`.
  - cache key combines canonical watermark + active asset-set fingerprint; cache is bypassed when watermark support is unavailable.
  - added `apiServer` cache fields in `cmd/labtether/server_types.go`.
  - added focused tests in `cmd/labtether/performance_hotspots_test.go`:
    - `TestStatusAggregateCanonicalPayloadCacheReusesCanonicalQueries`
    - `TestStatusAggregateCanonicalPayloadCacheInvalidatesOnAssetSetChange`
  - updated existing conditional-request perf tests to reflect watermark reads now used by canonical cache key derivation.
- Frontend mitigation pass:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useLogs.ts`: debounced log query network calls (250ms, immediate on clear) to prevent per-keystroke backend fan-out.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/page.tsx`: deferred device search recomputation with `useDeferredValue(...)` for smoother typing under larger inventories.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/nodesPageUtils.ts`: reduced allocations by splitting device/non-device assets in one pass.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` -> pass.
  - `go test ./...` -> pass.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/connectors/webservice` -> pass.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestStatusAggregate(SkipsFingerprintPrecomputeWithoutConditionalRequest|ConditionalRequestUsesPrecomputeAfterCacheWarmup|CanonicalPayloadCacheReusesCanonicalQueries|CanonicalPayloadCacheInvalidatesOnAssetSetChange)$'` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
  - post-change profile capture artifacts:
    - `/Users/michael/Documents/Projects/Vibe Coding/LabTether/tmp/perf/runs/20260304-220509-backend-post`
  - directional profile signal:
    - `statusWebServiceSummary` and coordinator `ListAll`/merge contribution dropped as a share of sampled CPU in the captured run, while aggregate/log-query work remains the dominant next hotspot class.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates shipped in same workstream:
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md` (status aggregate + console route performance guardrails).
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/performance-slow-ui.md` (faster triage flow for Logs/Devices + pprof checks).
  - `docs/ADR.md` (`ADR-027: Watermark-Keyed Aggregate Reuse`).

## 2026-03-04 (Backend performance hotspots: status aggregate + alert evaluator mitigation)
- Investigated backend hot paths and prioritized two high-frequency surfaces with avoidable repeated work:
  - `/status/aggregate` conditional request handling,
  - alert evaluator suppression checks (`ListAlertSilences` + per-site maintenance queries).
- Implemented status aggregate optimizations in `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_handlers.go`:
  - made fingerprint precompute lazy so it only runs for conditional (`If-None-Match`) requests.
  - added site-key existence guard on ETag cache before running expensive precompute.
  - made fingerprint storage conditional to avoid unnecessary precompute on non-conditional requests.
  - reduced allocation pressure in `statusAggregateFingerprint(...)` by streaming parts directly into SHA-256 rather than building a large joined string.
- Implemented alert evaluator suppression prefetch in:
  - `cmd/labtether/alert_evaluator.go`,
  - `cmd/labtether/alert_evaluator_instances.go`.
  - evaluator now preloads active silences once per evaluation cycle,
  - caches resolved rule site IDs within the cycle,
  - caches maintenance suppression verdict per site ID within the cycle.
- Added focused regression/perf-guard tests in `cmd/labtether/performance_hotspots_test.go`:
  - verifies status aggregate skips fingerprint precompute on non-conditional requests.
  - verifies conditional request flow warms cache once, then serves cached 304 path without rebuilding canonical payload.
  - verifies evaluator suppression lookups are reduced to one silence query per cycle and one maintenance query per site across multiple rules.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestStatusAggregateSkipsFingerprintPrecomputeWithoutConditionalRequest|TestStatusAggregateConditionalRequestUsesPrecomputeAfterCacheWarmup|TestEvaluateAlertRulesSuppressionPrefetchReducesStoreQueries|TestAlertMaintenanceSuppression|TestAlertDedupRace' -count=1` -> pass.
- Docs/wiki parity note:
  - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` updates were required because this pass is an internal performance optimization with no API/schema/operator workflow changes.
- ADR note:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update required (no architecture boundary/tradeoff change).

## 2026-03-04 (Console dev-mode stabilization: e2e hardening + UX fixes)
- Re-ran Playwright suite in dev mode and iteratively remediated failures until green.
- Fixed settings/runtime UX and semantics:
  - removed duplicate runtime-save message rendering in `ServiceDiscoveryDefaultsCard`.
  - hid empty discovery-defaults section when no entries are present.
  - ensured terminal route has a semantic `h1` and wired quick snippet insert action into `TerminalWorkspaceHeader`.
- Fixed context menu reliability in files page:
  - hardened outside-click close behavior for the context menu.
  - removed global scroll-driven close to prevent menu-item detachment during click/scroll-to-view interactions.
- Fixed e2e mock coverage gaps causing false 404 noise:
  - added `/api/services/web/icon-library` and `/api/v1/tls/info` mock handling in shared/visual harnesses.
  - refreshed settings visual baselines for dark/light/oled snapshots.
- Stabilized resilience test timing:
  - updated PBS stop-500 test flow to wait for `Task Details` before asserting log text visibility.
- Added valid app icon assets to unblock Next image decoding in dev startup:
  - `web/console/app/icon.png`
  - `web/console/app/apple-icon.png`
  - `web/console/app/favicon.ico`
- Reduced noisy e2e runtime warnings:
  - updated `web/console/package.json` `test:e2e` script to run Playwright with `NO_COLOR` unset (`env -u NO_COLOR ...`) so Node no longer warns about `NO_COLOR` vs `FORCE_COLOR`.
- Removed standalone-mode startup advisory from Playwright web server runtime:
  - updated `web/console/playwright.config.ts` default `webServerCommand` to run the standalone server directly.
  - staged required static assets before launch by copying:
    - `.next/static` -> `.next/standalone/.next/static`
    - `public` -> `.next/standalone/public`
  - this avoids the `"next start" does not work with "output: standalone"` warning and preserves correct page boot/runtime behavior.
- Additional test stability follow-up:
  - hardened `web/console/e2e/resilience.spec.ts` PBS stop-500 test to retry selecting the backup task row when list rerender races with initial click.
- Services header UX polish follow-up:
  - consolidated low-frequency services actions into a single `Edit` dropdown in `web/console/app/(console)/services/ServicesHeaderActions.tsx`:
    - add manual service,
    - select mode toggle,
    - bulk edit,
    - arrange mode toggle,
    - discovery settings.
  - retained `Refresh` and `Pull Images` as direct top-level actions for fast access.
- Global panel translucency tuning:
  - updated shared theme tokens in `web/console/app/globals.css` so `--panel` and `--panel-glass` are 10% opaque across OLED, Dark, and Light themes.
  - this makes dropdowns/cards/modals consistently more translucent without one-off component overrides.
- Follow-up translucency adjustment:
  - increased shared `--panel` and `--panel-glass` opacity by 50% from prior values (10% -> 15%) across OLED, Dark, and Light themes in `web/console/app/globals.css`.
  - this makes overlays/cards/dropdowns more readable while preserving glass styling.
- Follow-up opacity request:
  - updated shared panel surfaces to 85% opacity in `web/console/app/globals.css`:
    - dark themes now use dark tinted `--panel`/`--panel-glass` at 0.85 alpha,
    - light theme now uses white tinted `--panel`/`--panel-glass` at 0.85 alpha.
  - preserves text contrast while making overlays substantially more solid.
- Follow-up visual polish request:
  - removed panel translucency entirely across themes:
    - `--panel` and `--panel-glass` now use fully opaque colors in `web/console/app/globals.css`.
  - removed residual per-component panel alpha utilities by normalizing `bg-[var(--panel)]/xx` to `bg-[var(--panel)]` across the console UI.
- Services card hierarchy polish follow-up (Option 1, name-first layout):
  - updated `web/console/app/(console)/services/ServiceCard.tsx` so the first row prioritizes full service name visibility (wrapping, no truncation).
  - added a dedicated metadata chip row for `source`, proxy provider, hidden state, and compatible API confidence.
  - consolidated status, response latency, and host label into a single stable footer row while preserving expanded-details content.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e` -> pass (`77 passed`).
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass, repeated `NO_COLOR` warnings no longer emitted.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass with no standalone advisory warning.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts e2e/files-browser.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/resilience.spec.ts --project=chromium --grep "handles stop backend 500"` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass after 15% token update.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass after 85% token update.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass after full opacity + alpha utility normalization.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass after services card hierarchy update.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts` -> pass after services card hierarchy update.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/services-discovery-policy.spec.ts` -> pass after services card hierarchy update.
- Docs/wiki parity note:
  - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` changes were required for this pass because behavior changes were limited to console visual hierarchy polish + test/mocking reliability; no API/config/workflow contract changes.

## 2026-03-04 (Dev-mode bring-up stabilization: queue worker + smoke TLS handling)
- Started LabTether in default dev workflow via:
  - `make dev-backend-bg`
  - `make dev-frontend-bg`
- Reproduced a live backend worker regression:
  - `jobqueue worker: claim error: ERROR: inconsistent types deduced for parameter $4 (SQLSTATE 42P08)`.
- Fixed queue stale-claim recovery SQL typing in `internal/jobqueue/queue.go`:
  - added explicit `::timestamptz` casts for `updated_at`/`completed_at` assignment (`$4`) and stale cutoff comparison (`$5`).
  - result: worker startup no longer emits repeated claim errors in dev runtime logs.
- Improved smoke harness for TLS-auto dev mode compatibility:
  - `scripts/smoke-test.sh` now detects HTTP `/healthz` redirect-hint payload (`status=redirect_active`) and auto-switches `API_BASE` to HTTPS redirect port.
  - `scripts/lib/script-common.sh` and `scripts/lib/smoke-common.sh` now use `curl -k` for HTTPS checks/requests to tolerate local self-signed certs.
  - updated smoke action command from non-allowlisted `echo smoke action` to allowlisted `uptime`.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/jobqueue -count=1` -> pass.
  - `./scripts/smoke-test.sh --skip-compose --timeout 45` -> pass (`Passed: 103, Failed: 0`).
- Docs/wiki parity:
  - updated `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/install-docker-compose.md` with TLS redirect-aware smoke test note.
- Completed local dev startup warning cleanup (`/ca` read-only):
  - extracted CA share logic into `shareCACert(...)` in `cmd/labtether/startup_bootstrap.go`.
  - default `/ca` copy is now best-effort and skipped when the default directory is absent (common on non-container local dev).
  - explicit `LABTETHER_CA_SHARE_DIR` still forces share-copy behavior and retains warnings for misconfiguration.
  - added focused tests in `cmd/labtether/startup_bootstrap_ca_share_test.go` for:
    - missing default dir skip behavior,
    - existing default dir copy behavior,
    - explicit env override behavior.
- Additional validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestShareCACert' -count=1` -> pass.
  - restarted backend with `make dev-backend-bg`; startup no longer logs `/ca` warning.
  - reran smoke path: `./scripts/smoke-test.sh --skip-compose --timeout 45` -> pass (`Passed: 103, Failed: 0`).
- Docs/wiki parity:
  - updated `docs/wiki/reference/environment-variables.md` (`LABTETHER_CA_SHARE_DIR`) to document best-effort default behavior on local non-container runs.
- ADR note:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update required (bugfix + test-harness/runtime validation improvement within existing architecture).

## 2026-03-03 (Security/performance/correctness audit remediation wave on main)
- Completed a full multi-agent remediation pass for the audit findings across backend hub, agentcore runtime, and console frontend.
- Backend security/correctness hardening delivered:
  - strict token-to-asset binding on agent websocket admission,
  - sender-asset verification on terminal/docker bridge event paths,
  - stronger command/result/session correlation checks,
  - session-aware presence/disconnect cleanup,
  - worker panic isolation and stale job-claim recovery,
  - safer policy/process guard defaults and bounds.
- Backend performance fixes delivered:
  - action-run/plan lookup N+1 reductions,
  - per-request reliability precompute reuse,
  - status aggregate early ETag short-circuit path,
  - alert evaluator fan-out reductions,
  - canonical snapshot and telemetry-path roundtrip reductions,
  - drift/failover loops now avoid repeated full-asset scans.
- Agentcore and securityruntime hardening delivered:
  - secure-transport default posture (`https`/`wss` by default),
  - insecure transport explicit opt-in gate,
  - tightened outbound/exec guardrails,
  - local endpoint lockdown defaults,
  - self-update integrity controls and expanded tests.
- Frontend security/correctness/perf hardening delivered:
  - runtime override URL validation + host restrictions,
  - mutating-route origin checks (middleware + route handlers),
  - sanitized upstream error payloads,
  - ServiceIcon custom source sanitization,
  - WebRTC signaling/session lifecycle race fixes,
  - connected-agent rerender reduction and hook abort-race cleanup.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` -> pass.
  - targeted suites pass after compatibility updates:
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/portainer ./internal/connectors/proxmox ./internal/connectors/truenas`
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run '^(TestHandlePortainerConnectorTest.*|TestHandleProxmoxConnectorTest.*|TestHandleTrueNASConnectorTest.*|TestHandlePBSConnectorTest.*|TestHandleHomeAssistantConnectorTest.*|TestHandleConnectorActions(Portainer|Proxmox|TrueNAS|PBS|HomeAssistant).*|TestIngestTrueNASAlertLogs.*|TestExecuteTrueNASCollector.*|TestTrueNASAutoLinkWrappers)$'`
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run '^TestCreateCommandUsesAuthenticatedActor$'`
  - additional fixture normalization completed in `cmd/labtether`, `internal/agentcore`, `internal/connectors/homeassistant`, and `internal/connectors/pbs`.
  - `go test ./...` -> pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass.
  - smoke runtime checks:
    - `make dev-backend-bg` and `make dev-frontend-bg` started successfully,
    - `https://localhost:8443/healthz` -> `200` with `{"service":"labtether","status":"ok",...}`,
    - `https://localhost:3000/` -> `200` (console HTML),
    - `https://localhost:3000/api/status` -> `401` unauthorized (expected without session),
    - tmux sessions present: `labtether-backend`, `labtether-frontend`.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates shipped in same wave:
  - `docs/API.md` security notes added,
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md` includes insecure-transport + forbidden-origin diagnostics,
  - `docs/ADR.md` added ADR-026 for secure-by-default transport and console mutation guards.

## 2026-03-02 (Services discovery follow-up: cycle observability + curated settings controls + mode-toggle regression)
- Completed the follow-up hardening requested after layered discovery policy rollout:
  - added discovery-cycle observability in the agent report contract (`internal/agentmgr/message.go`) with additive `discovery` payload:
    - cycle totals (`cycle_duration_ms`, `total_services`),
    - per-source stage stats (`enabled`, `duration_ms`, `services_found`) for `docker`, `proxy`, `local_scan`, `lan_scan`,
    - final source counts.
  - updated collector instrumentation in `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`:
    - stage timing/counter capture per discovery source,
    - cycle summary + source-count capture before report send,
    - cycle summary log line for operator diagnostics.
  - updated hub coordinator + API wiring:
    - `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator.go` stores latest per-host discovery snapshot and exposes `DiscoveryStats(...)`.
    - `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go` now returns `discovery_stats` with `GET /api/v1/services/web`.
- Completed Services UI observability surface:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts` now tracks `discovery_stats`.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx` now renders **Discovery Cycle Stats**:
    - source-level enabled-host counts, found counts, avg stage timings,
    - per-host latest cycle snapshot rows.
- Added curated settings controls to avoid raw-key workflows:
  - `/settings` now includes dedicated **Service Discovery Defaults** controls (`web/console/app/(console)/settings/page.tsx`) with canary rollout and LAN-scan safety guidance.
  - Node **Agent Settings** now includes dedicated **Service Discovery Policy** section (`web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/AgentSettingsTab.tsx`) for per-node overrides.
- Added end-to-end regression for mode toggles:
  - new Playwright spec `web/console/e2e/services-discovery-policy.spec.ts` verifies include/exclude behavior as Docker/proxy/local-scan/LAN-scan defaults are toggled.
- Regression coverage added/updated:
  - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go` (`countDiscoveredServicesBySource` coverage),
  - `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator_test.go` (`DiscoveryStats` snapshot + clone isolation),
  - `cmd/labtether/webservice_api_test.go` (`TestHandleWebServicesIncludesDiscoveryStats`).
- Docs/wiki/API/ADR updates:
  - `docs/API.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/settings-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (added `ADR-025: Service Discovery Cycle Observability`).
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` -> pass
  - `go test ./internal/connectors/webservice -count=1` -> pass
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebServicesIncludesDiscoveryStats|TestHandleWebServicesHiddenFilter|TestHandleWebServiceCompat|TestResolveWebServiceMergeConfigCachesRuntimeOverrides' -count=1` -> pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/services-discovery-policy.spec.ts --project=chromium` -> pass
- Deployment note:
  - Linux agent discovery/runtime changes still require `make build-agent-linux`, remote agent update/restart, and post-rollout `/api/v1/services/web/sync` verification on canary hosts before full rollout.

## 2026-03-02 (Dashboard topology hero edge density cleanup)
- Addressed operator feedback that Home topology hero lines were still visually poor in sparse infra layouts.
- Root cause:
  - `web/console/app/components/TopologyHero.tsx` was still drawing fallback edges via sorted-adjacent chaining (`selected[i] -> selected[i+1]`), which frequently created a long bottom baseline edge and awkward bends.
- Implemented line-routing fix set in `web/console/app/components/TopologyHero.tsx`:
  - added dependency-aware edge selection for Home hero (`useAssetDependencies`) so explicit infra dependencies are preferred when present.
  - replaced fallback adjacency chaining with MST shortest-path edge construction (Kruskal) to keep fallback lines short and avoid unnecessary crossings.
  - added cardinal handle selection (`top/right/bottom/left`) and per-edge handle assignment based on source->target geometry.
  - switched hero edges to straight routing with tuned inferred/explicit stroke opacity and animation.
- Added dashboard regression test in `web/console/e2e/console.spec.ts`:
  - `dashboard topology hero avoids long baseline edges in sparse infra layouts`
  - validates that sparse 3-node hero rendering does not produce a long near-horizontal baseline edge.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/start-here/navigation-map.md`
- Validation run:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts --grep "dashboard topology hero excludes service assets and keeps infrastructure|dashboard topology hero avoids long baseline edges in sparse infra layouts|dashboard fleet focus can expand from issues to full fleet"` -> `3 passed`
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a rendering/readability correction within existing dashboard topology hero architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Services follow-up: subset bulk edit targeting + custom icon rename)
- Extended Services bulk edit to support explicit subset targeting:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`:
    - added **Select** mode in header.
    - added per-card selection checkboxes in `ServiceCard`.
    - added selection banner actions (`Select All Filtered`, `Clear`).
    - bulk edit now targets:
      - selected subset when Select mode is active and items are selected,
      - full filtered scope when Select mode is off.
- Added custom icon library rename support:
  - backend: `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go` now supports `PATCH /api/v1/services/web/icon-library?id=<icon-id>` to rename library entries.
  - frontend proxy: `web/console/app/api/services/web/icon-library/route.ts` now proxies PATCH.
  - hook: `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts` now exposes `renameCustomServiceIcon(...)`.
  - UI: `web/console/app/components/ServiceEditModal.tsx` now includes per-icon rename control in the custom library grid.
- Regression coverage:
  - expanded `TestHandleWebServiceIconLibraryCRUD` in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api_test.go` to validate rename behavior.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/start-here/navigation-map.md`
- Validation run:

  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is an additive UX/API refinement within the existing Services icon-library and override architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Services: reusable custom icon library + bulk edit actions)

## 2026-03-02 (Services discovery policy expansion: source/provider toggles + layered scan controls)
- Implemented first-principles services discovery policy controls across hub defaults, per-agent overrides, and agent runtime execution so operators can explicitly choose discovery sources.
- Added fleet runtime defaults (`services.discovery_default_*`) in `internal/runtimesettings/types.go` for:
  - docker discovery,
  - proxy discovery (global and provider-specific `traefik`/`caddy`/`npm`),
  - local port scan behavior (`enabled`, `include_listening`, `ports`),
  - LAN scan behavior (`enabled`, `cidrs`, `ports`, `max_hosts`).
- Added agent-managed settings (`services_discovery_*`) in `internal/agentcore/settings_definitions.go` + runtime wiring:
  - strict normalization for comma-separated port and CIDR inputs,
  - private/local-only CIDR guardrails for LAN scan policy,
  - `HubManaged: true` + `RestartRequired: true` semantics for deterministic policy rollout.
- End-to-end runtime/settings integration:
  - `internal/agentcore/types.go`, `settings_values.go`, `config.go`, and `settings_runtime.go` now carry/load/apply all new discovery policy fields.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers.go` now maps global defaults to new agent-level discovery keys so effective settings flow from runtime defaults with per-node overrides.
- Collector execution is now policy-driven (`internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`, `internal/agentcore/run.go`):
  - conditional Docker/proxy/local-scan/LAN-scan stages from effective settings,
  - LAN scan host enumeration with bounded CIDR/host/port/concurrency guardrails,
  - endpoint de-duplication against known URLs to avoid duplicate cards,
  - scan traceability metadata on discovered services (`scan_scope`, `scan_target_host`).
- Console/API model parity:
  - `web/console/app/console/models.ts` now includes new runtime setting keys.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx` now surfaces scan scope + scanned host details when present.
- Regression coverage:
  - `internal/agentcore/settings_definitions_test.go`: validates discovery key definitions and normalization/validation behavior.
  - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`: validates LAN scan discovery metadata and dedupe behavior.
  - `internal/runtimesettings/types_test.go`: validates new runtime defaults and integer normalization.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers_test.go`: validates global-default-to-agent-setting mappings.
  - `cmd/labtether/settings_api_test.go`: validates runtime settings API exposure of new defaults.
- Docs/wiki/ADR updates:
  - `docs/API.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (added `ADR-024: Layered Service Discovery Policy Controls`).
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` -> pass
  - `go test ./internal/runtimesettings -count=1` -> pass
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(RuntimeSettingsGetUpdateAndReset|AgentSettingGlobalDefaultKeyServiceDiscoveryMappings)' -count=1` -> pass
  - `go vet ./...` -> pass
  - `go test ./... -count=1` -> pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass
- Deployment note:
  - Linux agent runtime/discovery behavior changes require rebuilding and redeploying Linux agents (`make build-agent-linux`) plus remote `labtether-agent` restart, followed by `/api/v1/services/web/sync` verification on affected assets.

## 2026-03-02 (Topology services deep-dive + Home topology visibility follow-up)
- Investigated service-visibility regressions across topology tabs/list and dashboard Topology hero.
- Implemented topology list/hierarchy fixes:

## 2026-03-02 (Topology dashboard Docker host/container link recovery for incomplete metadata)
- Investigated dashboard/topology graph issue where Docker container-host assets (for example `docker-containervm-deltaserver`) were not linking to their containers/stacks, leaving workloads orphaned and lines misleading.
- Root cause:
  - frontend topology parent-key matching for Docker relied primarily on `metadata.agent_id`.
  - older/incomplete Docker child rows without `metadata.agent_id` fell back to a generic `docker` key, which does not match per-host parent keys like `containervm-deltaserver`.
- Fix in `web/console/app/console/taxonomy.ts`:
  - added Docker key normalization helper for stable matching.
  - added Docker parent-key fallback derivation from asset ID/name conventions (`docker-host-*`, `docker-ct-*`, `docker-stack-*`, `docker-*`) when metadata is missing.
  - updated `childParentKey(...)` and `hostParentKey(...)` Docker branches to use these fallbacks before final source fallback.
- Regression coverage:
  - added Playwright test in `web/console/e2e/topology.spec.ts`:
    - `infers docker host/container hierarchy from docker asset IDs when child metadata.agent_id is missing`.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/topology-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/topology.spec.ts` -> `7 passed` (~19.1s)
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a topology matching correctness hardening within the existing frontend taxonomy/hierarchy architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Persistent device rename in Agent Settings)
- Implemented persistent device rename flow so manual name changes are not overwritten by subsequent heartbeats.
- Backend persistence/runtime changes:

    - asset update now writes updated metadata (including `name_override`) alongside name/site/tags.
- Frontend/device-settings UX:
  - added **Device Name** edit/save section in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/AgentSettingsTab.tsx`.
  - save path uses existing `PATCH /api/assets/{id}` and triggers `fetchStatus()` refresh for immediate UI consistency.
  - wired current asset name into Agent Settings renderer via `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodePanelRenderers.tsx`.
- Regression coverage:

    - `TestMemoryAssetStoreManualNameOverridePersistsAcrossHeartbeats`
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_sites_api_test.go`:

  - `docs/wiki/start-here/navigation-map.md`

  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestUpdateAssetNameAndSite|TestUpdateAssetNamePersistsAcrossHeartbeats' -count=1` -> pass
  - `go vet ./...` -> clean
  - `go test ./...` -> all packages pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a persistence/UX refinement within existing asset update and heartbeat ingestion architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Topology Docker source-filter hierarchy fix)
- Investigated operator-reported topology tree issue where Docker host/container relationships disappeared under Docker source filtering.
- Root cause:
  - hierarchy construction was computed from `allAssets` before source/status/query filters, while tree/graph renderers consumed filtered assets.
  - Docker host merge-to-agent logic then bound Docker children to a parent that could be out-of-scope for the active filter, causing missing/empty tree relationships in source-scoped views.
- Fix:
  - updated `web/console/app/(console)/topology/page.tsx` to build hierarchy from the active `filteredAssets` set (while still using shared dependency payloads), so parent-child resolution is scoped consistently with the rendered view.
- Regression coverage:
  - added test `keeps docker host/container hierarchy connected when filtering to Docker source in tree view` in `web/console/e2e/topology.spec.ts`.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/topology-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/topology.spec.ts` -> `4 passed` (~15.7s)
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a topology view-state correctness fix within the existing frontend architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Node-detail cluster topology graph: crossing-line and odd layout cleanup)
- Investigated dashboard node-detail topology graph (`ClusterTopologySection` graph tab) where lines appeared tangled and layout felt unstable.
- Root cause:
  - API host lane was sorted alphabetically while guest lane was grouped by Proxmox node, so guest->host `runs_on` edges crossed heavily.
  - Static row/block sizing made larger clusters stretch vertically and feel visually awkward in fit view.
- Implemented flow-model refinements in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/clusterTopologyFlowModel.tsx`:
  - switched to a two-pass build so API host ordering can use guest placement coordinates.
  - ordered API hosts by connected-guest vertical barycenter to reduce edge crossover.
  - grouped guest ordering within each Proxmox node by mapped host target (then by guest name) to reduce link weaving.
  - added adaptive row height / block gap scaling for larger cluster datasets.
  - preserved manual/auto/missing link semantics and mapping editor interactions.
- Updated graph fit behavior in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/ClusterTopologyGraphView.tsx` with explicit `fitViewOptions.padding`.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/node-triage.md`
- Validation run:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npx eslint 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/clusterTopologyFlowModel.tsx' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/ClusterTopologyGraphView.tsx'` -> clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a frontend layout/readability refinement within existing node-detail topology behavior.

## 2026-03-02 (Services page performance deep-dive + optimization pass)
- Completed deep performance audit of Services page with frontend and backend-path inspection.
- Root causes identified:
  - Services page was subscribed to high-frequency status context updates, triggering frequent parent rerenders.
  - Service cards were not memoized and received unstable handler props, causing large grid rerender churn.
  - `useWebServices` polling continued in background tabs and always committed new arrays even when payloads were unchanged.
- Implemented frontend performance fixes:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`:
    - wrapped `ServiceCard` with `memo`.
    - stabilized high-frequency handlers with `useCallback`.
    - switched expand toggle to ID-based callback to remove per-card inline closure churn.
    - reduced drag-over state churn with guarded updates.
    - stabilized host-name map references when payload values are unchanged.
    - memoized rendered service-group tree to avoid rebuilding large card lists on unrelated rerenders.
    - render merge settings modal only while open.
    - fixed mobile action-bar overflow by allowing Services header actions to wrap on narrow viewports.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts`:
    - added visibility-aware polling (pause when tab is hidden).
    - added payload equality guards so unchanged service lists do not trigger no-op state updates/rerenders.
- Implemented phase-2 performance hardening:
  - `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`:
    - added dedicated `useStatusAssetNameMap()` selector context with stable map identity checks so Services host-label reads no longer depend on full status-context value churn.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`:
    - replaced direct `useStatus()` subscription with `useStatusAssetNameMap()` for host lookup data.
  - `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`:
    - added TTL cache for resolved Services merge configuration (`services.merge_*` runtime settings) to avoid repeated override reads on each list/compat request.
    - added explicit cache invalidation helper.
  - `cmd/labtether/settings_handlers.go`:
    - runtime settings patch/reset now invalidate Services merge-config cache immediately.
  - Added backend regression coverage:
    - `TestResolveWebServiceMergeConfigCachesRuntimeOverrides`.
    - runtime settings API test now asserts merge-cache invalidation on update/reset.
- Added targeted frontend regression coverage:
  - new `web/console/e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts` verifies `/services` keeps a single `h1` heading and avoids horizontal overflow on mobile viewport.
- Updated docs/wiki:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `go vet ./...` -> clean
  - `go test ./...` -> all packages pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> blocked by pre-existing unrelated duplicate `selectedIDs` declarations in `web/console/app/components/TopologyHero.tsx`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/services-mobile-layout.spec.ts` -> `1 passed`
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a performance optimization/refactor within existing Services UI and polling architecture, with no new architectural boundary.

- Added state reset behavior so once the fleet returns to healthy, expanded issue-mode state is cleared for the next incident cycle.

  - `docs/wiki/start-here/navigation-map.md`
  - `notes/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md`
