## 2026-03-02 (Topology graph deep-dive: edge anchors + lane layout readability)
- Addressed topology graph readability issues in `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyGraphModel.tsx`:
  - replaced single-strip lane placement with wrapped lane packing to prevent ultra-wide graph sprawl.
  - introduced stable card-height/row-gap layout inputs and per-row height planning so graph cards stop overlapping under dense/ultra layouts.
  - added side-aware edge handle routing based on source/target placement to keep connections anchored to card edges instead of awkward bends.
  - tuned smoothstep offsets for duplicate/cross-lane edges to reduce visual clutter.
- Added consistent side handles for graph nodes:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/CompoundNode.tsx`
  - new `web/console/app/(console)/topology/AssetCardNode.tsx`
  - wired node type in `web/console/app/(console)/topology/TopologyGraphPanel.tsx`.
- Updated lane layout utility contract in `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyUtils.ts` with explicit `cardHeight` and `rowGap`.
- Hardened topology e2e assertion scoping to avoid flaky inspector selection in `web/console/e2e/topology.spec.ts`.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/topology-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/topology.spec.ts` -> `3 passed` (~16.6s)
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a frontend topology layout/interaction refinement within existing console architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Topology hierarchy root-cause remediation: host classification + relationship normalization)
- Investigated topology tree/inspector regression where valid `runs_on` links appeared as `Unlinked` and relationship panels were intermittently empty for agent-managed hosts.
- Root causes fixed:
  - `web/console/app/console/taxonomy.ts`: `isInfraHost(...)` now includes canonical host-type fallback (`host`, `hypervisor-node`, `nas`, `storage-controller`, `container-host`) for sources that do not publish a strict source→host mapping (for example agent-managed `type=host`).
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyHierarchy.ts`: explicit hosting-edge matching now normalizes relationship type case/whitespace before `runs_on`/`hosted_on` checks, and workload child classification now normalizes asset types.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyUtils.ts`: relationship priority ranking now uses normalized relationship type values so mixed-case input does not degrade edge dedupe/selection ordering.
- Regression hardening:
  - refined inspector scoping in `web/console/e2e/topology.spec.ts` so tree relationship assertions stay deterministic while still validating `from Guest VM -> runs on`.
- Docs/wiki parity:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/topology-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/topology.spec.ts` -> `3 passed` (~16.7s)
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a correctness hardening pass inside existing topology/taxonomy frontend architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (Dashboard Node Agent endpoint warning: docker-hosted runtime gating)

- Backend update in `cmd/labtether/status_aggregate_handlers.go`:

  - `TestStatusEndpointTargetsIncludesNodeAgentForConfiguredSourceWhenHubDockerHosted`
  - retained default-source omission coverage.

  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestStatusEndpointTargets' -count=1` -> pass
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a status-aggregate condition refinement within existing runtime-settings behavior.

## 2026-03-02 (Services merge settings audit + no-op fixes)
- Completed deep-dive audit for Services merge settings behavior across:
  - Services UI modal save/reset wiring.
  - Runtime settings API persistence.
  - Services merge application path in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`.
- Root-cause findings:
  - `services.merge_confidence_threshold` and `services.force_merge_rules` were exposed in UI/runtime settings but not applied during merge evaluation.
  - Services list did not refresh immediately after modal save/reset, making settings changes appear inactive until the next poll.
- Implemented backend fixes in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`:
  - `resolveWebServiceMergeConfig` now reads and applies:
    - `services.merge_confidence_threshold`
    - `services.force_merge_rules`
    - existing mode/dry-run/alias/never-merge settings.
  - Added confidence-aware merge gating (`merge_confidence_threshold`) with merge-signal scoring.
  - Added force-merge rule enforcement for explicit URL pairs.
  - Preserved undo safety by enforcing `never_merge_rules` precedence over `force_merge_rules` for the same pair.
  - Differentiated aggressive mode alias bucketing so it can merge broader canonical URL candidates than balanced/conservative.
- Implemented frontend UX fix:
  - `web/console/app/components/ServiceMergeSettingsModal.tsx` now supports `onSettingsApplied`.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx` passes `refresh` so Services update immediately after save/reset.
- Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api_test.go`:
  - `TestHandleWebServicesForceMergeRuleMergesServices`
  - `TestHandleWebServicesMergeThresholdBlocksAliasMerge`
  - `TestHandleWebServicesNeverMergeRuleOverridesForceMergeRule`
- Updated docs/wiki:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `go vet ./...` -> clean
  - `go test ./...` -> all packages pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is behavior completion/refinement within existing runtime-settings + services dedup architecture, without introducing a new subsystem boundary.

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Phase 4: Observability polish)
- Completed Phase 4 from the approved iOS roadmap (`docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-feature-roadmap-design.md`) with reliability, telemetry, topology, and updates management surfaces in mobile.
- **Reliability metrics (Phase 4A):**
  - Added `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Reliability/ReliabilityViewModel.swift` and `ReliabilityView.swift`.
  - Wired reliability data flow from:
    - `GET /sites`
    - `GET /sites/reliability?window=...`
    - `GET /sites/{id}/timeline?window=...`
  - Added site scorecards, synthetic availability timeline chart, outage history, and node/service availability rows.
- **Telemetry graphs (Phase 4B):**
  - Added `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Telemetry/TelemetryViewModel.swift` and `TelemetryView.swift` using Swift Charts.
  - Implemented fleet and per-node telemetry modes:
    - fleet snapshots from `GET /metrics/overview`,
    - per-node series from `GET /metrics/assets/{id}?window=...`,
    - fleet trend aggregation from per-node series batches.
  - Added range picker (`1h`, `6h`, `24h`, `7d`, `30d`) with explicit backend-cap note for ranges above 24h.
- **Topology map (Phase 4C):**
  - Added `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Topology/TopologyViewModel.swift` and `TopologyView.swift`.
  - Implemented simplified mobile topology map/list from:
    - `GET /assets`
    - `GET /dependencies/batch?asset_ids=...`
  - Added focused node map mode with inbound/outbound relationships and node-detail navigation.
- **Updates management (Phase 4D):**
  - Added `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Updates/UpdatesViewModel.swift` and `UpdatesView.swift`.
  - Implemented:
    - update plan list/create (`GET/POST /updates/plans`),
    - execute action with confirmation (`POST /updates/plans/{id}/execute`),
    - run history/status filtering (`GET /updates/runs`).
- **App wiring and contracts:**
  - Updated `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/MoreView.swift` with new **Observability** section linking Reliability/Telemetry/Topology/Updates.
  - Updated `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Nodes/NodeDetailView.swift` with node-scoped telemetry charts entrypoint.
  - Extended `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift` for site reliability/timeline, dependencies, updates payloads, plus richer asset/metrics fields.
  - Updated `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` for all new Phase 4 source files/groups.
- **Docs/wiki updates:**
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`: added iOS companion observability surface notes.
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`: added iOS observability fast-check section.
- Validation run:
  - `xcodebuild build -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro'` -> BUILD SUCCEEDED.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean.
  - `go vet ./...` -> fails due pre-existing backend compile error: `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go:979: goto mergedService jumps over variable declaration`.
  - `go test ./...` -> same pre-existing backend compile failure in `cmd/labtether`.
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. Phase 4 introduces iOS UI/view-model capabilities over existing backend APIs without changing architecture boundaries or service contracts.

## 2026-03-02 (Services drag-and-drop arrange mode)
- Added iOS-home-screen style arrange flow for Services in `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`:
  - new **Arrange** header action toggles layout mode.
  - services cards become draggable in layout mode and can be reordered by drag-and-drop within their category grid.
  - active drop target is highlighted during drag.
  - arrange banner includes a **Reset Layout** action.
- Layout persistence:
  - category-level service order is saved in browser local storage (`labtether.services.layout.v1`) and reused on subsequent refreshes.
  - custom order gracefully falls back to default ordering for new/missing services.
- UI polish:
  - drag handle icon added in layout mode.
  - service link clicks are suppressed while arranging to avoid accidental navigation.

  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
- Validation run:

- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is a front-end interaction enhancement within existing Services UI behavior.

## 2026-03-02 (Services dedup undo controls + never-merge enforcement)
- Added merge undo controls in `web/console/app/components/ServiceEditModal.tsx`:
  - merged URL aliases from `metadata.alt_urls` are now shown in Service Edit.
  - each merged alias has a top-right `X` action to undo that specific merge.
- Added runtime-setting persistence for undo in `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`:
  - undo action reads current runtime settings, appends a URL-pair rule to `services.never_merge_rules`, deduplicates existing pair rules, and refreshes the services list.
- Fixed backend dedup behavior to consume never-merge settings in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`:
  - parse and enforce `services.never_merge_rules` during merge evaluation.
  - support URL-pair separators `=>`, `<=>`, and comma.
  - merge indexing now keeps per-key buckets so blocked pairs stay split without preventing unrelated merges.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `TestHandleWebServicesNeverMergeRuleSkipsAliasMerge` in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api_test.go`.
- Updated merge settings UX copy in `web/console/app/components/ServiceMergeSettingsModal.tsx` to reflect URL-pair syntax and undo workflow.
- Updated docs/wiki:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebServicesAppliesAliasMergeRules|TestHandleWebServicesMergeDryRunSkipsMerge|TestHandleWebServicesNeverMergeRuleSkipsAliasMerge|TestHandleWebServicesHiddenFilter|TestHandleWebServiceCompat|TestHandleWebServiceCompatInvalidMinConfidence'` -> `ok   github.com/labtether/labtether/cmd/labtether 0.468s`
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This extends existing services dedup/runtime-settings behavior without introducing a new architecture boundary.

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Phase 3: Desktop/VNC + Settings)
- Completed Phase 3 from the approved iOS roadmap (`docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-feature-roadmap-design.md`) with desktop remote-view support and a full Settings surface.
- **Desktop/VNC feature (new files + wiring):**
  - Added `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopViewModel.swift`:
    - desktop session flow `POST /desktop/sessions` -> `POST /desktop/sessions/{id}/stream-ticket` -> mobile WebSocket URL assembly.
    - connection state machine (`idle/creatingSession/fetchingTicket/connecting/connected/error/disconnected`).
  - Added `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`:
    - `WKWebView` wrapper for noVNC, connect/retry/disconnect states, trackpad/view-only toggles, and toolbar actions.
  - Added bundled `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`:
    - noVNC web client bridge for the iOS WebView runtime with reconnect/backoff and Swift message handler events.
  - Wired `Remote Desktop` action into `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Nodes/NodeDetailView.swift` with capability-aware visibility heuristics.
- **Settings expansion (Phase 3B):**
  - Replaced stub settings with full operator settings UI in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/SettingsView.swift`:
    - hub connection section with live checks (`/healthz`) and hub switch action.
    - notification preference toggles (critical alert, node offline, service down).
    - appearance mode (system/light/dark), terminal defaults (font size/theme), behavior toggles, security toggle.
    - account/about metadata from `GET /auth/me` and diagnostics deep-link.
  - Updated `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/MoreView.swift` to pass `config` + `client` into Settings.
- **Preference/system wiring:**
  - Added shared settings contract in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Config/AppSettings.swift`.
  - Applied appearance preference at app root in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/RootView.swift`.
  - Extended terminal preference wiring:
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`
    - theme + auto-reconnect are now controlled by persisted settings.
  - Extended networking models in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift` for desktop-session responses and richer asset/auth/health metadata.
  - Updated `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/ViewModifiers.swift` to use shared haptic settings key.
  - Updated Xcode project wiring in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` for new desktop source/resource files and app settings config file.
- **Docs/wiki updates:**
  - `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`: added iOS companion desktop/VNC operator notes.
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`: added iOS remote desktop fast-check section.
- Validation run:
  - `xcodebuild build -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro'` -> BUILD SUCCEEDED.
  - `go vet ./...` -> clean.
  - `go test ./...` -> all packages pass.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean.
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This phase extends existing iOS/mobile UX and existing desktop session APIs without introducing new architectural boundaries or backend subsystem contracts.

## 2026-03-02 (Services merge settings runtime wiring fix)
- Root cause fixed: Services merge settings were previously persisted but not consumed by the services API merge path, so alias presets appeared to "save" but had no effect on duplicate output.
- Implemented runtime wiring in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`:
  - `/api/v1/services/web` and `/api/v1/services/web/compat` now resolve and apply `services.merge_*` settings at response time.
  - added alias rule parser + host pattern matcher (`*` wildcard labels) and merge application over discovered service lists.
  - merged services now preserve alternate URLs in `metadata.alt_urls` with duplicate suppression.
- Updated Services preset behavior:
  - middle-label preset now writes `*.*.<domain> => *.<domain>` and also flips to live behavior (`mode=balanced`, `dryRun=false`) so preset application is immediately effective.
  - default runtime value for `services.merge_dry_run` changed to `false`.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `TestHandleWebServicesAppliesAliasMergeRules`
  - `TestHandleWebServicesMergeDryRunSkipsMerge`
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebServicesAppliesAliasMergeRules|TestHandleWebServicesMergeDryRunSkipsMerge|TestHandleWebServicesHiddenFilter|TestHandleWebServiceCompat|TestHandleWebServiceCompatInvalidMinConfidence'` -> `ok   github.com/labtether/labtether/cmd/labtether 0.462s`
  - `go test ./internal/runtimesettings` -> `ok   github.com/labtether/labtether/internal/runtimesettings 0.355s`
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether ./internal/runtimesettings` -> clean
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. This is completion of existing settings-to-behavior wiring within current services/runtime-settings architecture.

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Phase 2: Files + Terminal)
- Implemented iOS Phase 2 from the approved 4-phase feature roadmap (design: `docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-feature-roadmap-design.md`, plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-phase2-files-terminal.md`).
- **Files feature (6 new/modified files)**:
  - Added `FileEntry`, `FileListResponse`, `FileOperationResponse` models to `Models.swift`.
  - Created `FilesViewModel.swift` with directory navigation, navigate up, refresh, delete, mkdir, upload, download, ResponseCache offline fallback, breadcrumb path components.
  - Created `FilesView.swift` with NavigationStack, loading/error/empty states, breadcrumb path bar, type-specific file icons, swipe-to-delete, new folder dialog, show/hide hidden files toggle, fileImporter for uploads, context menu download with UIActivityViewController share sheet.
  - Added `uploadRaw`/`downloadRaw` methods to `APIClient.swift` for raw byte transfers.
  - Wired "Browse Files" NavigationLink into `NodeDetailView.swift`.
- **Terminal feature (6 new/modified files)**:
  - Created `terminal.html` bundle with xterm.js v5.5.0 (CDN), fit addon, web-links addon, WebSocket bridge via `window.webkit.messageHandlers.terminal`, auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (1s→16s, 5 attempts), dark theme (#1a1a2e).
  - Created `TerminalViewModel.swift` with ConnectionState enum and 3-step connect flow (POST /terminal/sessions → POST stream-ticket → build WS URL from `client.config.hubURL`).
  - Created `TerminalView.swift` with WKWebView UIViewRepresentable wrapper, Coordinator (WKScriptMessageHandler + WKNavigationDelegate), 5 connection states, keyboard accessory bar with Esc/Tab/Ctrl modifier/arrow keys/pipe/tilde.
  - Created `TerminalNodePickerView.swift` with asset list for terminal connection.
  - Wired "Connect Terminal" into `NodeDetailView.swift` and terminal section into `MoreView.swift`.
- **Xcode project updates**: Created Files and Terminal PBXGroups under Features/, Resources group for terminal.html, added all PBXFileReference/PBXBuildFile entries.
- Validation run:
  - `xcodebuild build -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator'` → BUILD SUCCEEDED
  - `go vet ./...` → clean
  - `go test ./...` → all packages pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` → clean
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` update required. Files and Terminal features extend existing iOS app architecture within established ViewModel/View patterns; no new architectural boundary.
- 10 commits on main: data models → FilesViewModel → FilesView → wire files → terminal.html → TerminalViewModel → TerminalView → wire terminal → keyboard accessory → upload/download.

## 2026-03-02 (Traefik service dedup for shared backend aliases)
- Implemented Traefik-specific enrichment dedup in `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider.go`:
  - added backend identity canonicalization (`canonicalBackendIdentity`) and a per-run Traefik backend-to-service index so repeated routes targeting the same backend reuse one service record instead of emitting duplicates.
  - kept alias visibility by appending secondary routed URLs to `metadata.alt_urls`.
  - hardened alias handling to be idempotent:
    - `appendAltURL` now trims/normalizes entries and suppresses duplicates.
    - enrichment skips alias insertion when URL equals the service primary URL.
- Added regression coverage in `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider_test.go`:
  - `TestEnrichServicesTraefikProxyOnlyBackendDedup`
  - `TestEnrichServicesTraefikSkipsDuplicatePrimaryAlias`
  - expanded `TestAppendAltURL`
  - added `TestCanonicalBackendIdentity`
- Docs parity updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestEnrichServicesTraefik|TestAppendAltURL|TestCanonicalBackendIdentity'` -> `ok   github.com/labtether/labtether/internal/agentcore 0.395s`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore` -> `ok   github.com/labtether/labtether/internal/agentcore 1.014s`
- ADR rationale: no `docs/ADR.md` change required. This is a behavior-level dedup refinement within existing proxy enrichment architecture (no new component, contract, or system boundary).

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Phase 1: Services + Logs + Push Notifications)
- Implemented iOS app Phase 1 features from the approved 4-phase feature roadmap (design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-feature-roadmap-design.md`).
- **Services feature (5 tasks)**:
  - Added `WebService`, `WebServicesResponse`, `WebServiceCategoriesResponse` models to Models.swift.
  - Created `ServicesViewModel` with parallel fetch, ResponseCache offline fallback, category/search filtering, grouped display, sync action, and EventBus subscription (docker.event, heartbeat.update with 30s throttle).
  - Created `ServicesView` with grouped list by category, status summary (up/down counts), search, category filter menu, sync button, stale data banner.
  - Created `ServiceDetailView` with status header, metadata (Docker image, container, proxy, category), relative timestamps.
  - Wired Services tab into MainTabView with `.badge(servicesVM.downCount)`.
- **Logs feature (4 tasks)**:
  - Added `LogEvent`, `LogEventsResponse`, `LogSourcesResponse`, `JournalEntry`, `JournalEntriesResponse` models.
  - Created `LogsViewModel` with time-window query, level/source/asset filtering, search, journal per-node fetch, ResponseCache fallback.
  - Created `LogsView` with monospace log list, color-coded level indicators, filter menu, time window picker (15m-7d), search.
  - Wired Logs into MoreView (global) and NodeDetailView (per-node, pre-filtered by asset ID).
- **Backend push notification infrastructure (2 tasks)**:
  - Migration v37: `push_devices` table with `UNIQUE(user_id, device_id)`.
  - `PostgresStore` methods: `UpsertPushDevice`, `DeletePushDevice`, `GetPushDevicesForUser`, `GetAllPushTokens`.
  - `POST/DELETE /api/v1/devices/register` endpoint with auth middleware.
  - APNs notification adapter (`internal/notifications/apns.go`) implementing existing `Adapter` interface with stdlib-only ES256 JWT signing, HTTP/2 delivery, token caching. 12 tests. Registered as `ChannelTypeAPNs` in startup.
- **iOS push registration + deep-links (2 tasks)**:
  - `PushManager.swift`: requests notification permission, registers APNs token, syncs device token with hub.
  - `AppDelegate.swift`: bridges APNs token callbacks and notification tap handling into SwiftUI.
  - Deep-link routing: notification tap extracts `alert_id`, posts to NotificationCenter, MainTabView switches to Alerts tab.
  - Added `TabID` enum for type-safe programmatic tab selection.
- Validated: `xcodebuild build` (BUILD SUCCEEDED), `go vet ./...` (clean), `go test ./...` (all pass), `tsc --noEmit` (clean).
- 13 commits across iOS and backend.

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Audit: Fix 20 Issues Across 15 Files)
- Ran comprehensive iOS app audit (4 parallel agents) and fixed all 20 identified issues.
- **HIGH severity fixes (7)**:
  - ISO8601 date parsing with fractional seconds: added shared `LT.parseISO8601()` with dual-formatter strategy (`.withFractionalSeconds` primary, standard fallback). Replaced all per-call `ISO8601DateFormatter()` allocations across 6 files.
  - Incidents response wrapper: added `IncidentsResponse` struct, updated ViewModel to decode through wrapper.
  - AlertInstance annotations fallback: decoder now falls back to `annotations`/`labels` maps for `currentValue`, `threshold`, `message`, `ruleName`.
  - Query string percent-encoding: `APIClient.buildRequest()` now uses `URLComponents` when path contains `?` to avoid `appendingPathComponent` double-encoding.
  - EventBus handler accumulation: token-based deregistration (`on()` returns UUID, `off()`/`offAll()` remove by token). All 3 ViewModels store tokens and deregister before re-registering.
  - EventSocket retain cycle: `scheduleReconnect()` now captures `[weak self]` in Task closure.
- **MEDIUM severity fixes (8)**:
  - Alert fetch error propagation: `HomeViewModel.fetchAlerts()` now sets `self.error` instead of silently returning.
  - "Nodes Online" label: shows `online/total` (derived from `total - stale`) instead of just total.
  - Event handler throttling: 30s debounce on alert/heartbeat event handlers in AlertsViewModel and HomeViewModel.
  - DiagnosticsView concurrent probes: replaced sequential `await` calls with `async let` for parallel execution.
  - `scenePhase` WebSocket lifecycle: MainTabView disconnects EventBus on background, reconnects on active.
  - EventSocket APIClient reuse: lazy-initialized shared client instead of per-connect allocation.
- **LOW severity fixes (5)**:
  - Shared `JSONEncoder`/`JSONDecoder` in ResponseCache, APIClient, EventSocket.
  - Incidents cache fallback for offline viewing via ResponseCache.
- Files modified: DesignTokens.swift, Models.swift, APIClient.swift, ResponseCache.swift, EventBus.swift, EventSocket.swift, HomeViewModel.swift, HomeView.swift, AlertsView.swift, AlertDetailView.swift, AlertsViewModel.swift, IncidentsViewModel.swift, IncidentsView.swift, NodeDetailViewModel.swift, NodesViewModel.swift, DiagnosticsView.swift, MainTabView.swift.
- Validated with `xcodebuild build` (no errors).

## 2026-03-02 (Premium CLI Agent Installer)
- Transformed the Linux agent install script from plain-text echo output into a premium CLI experience.
- New UI helpers block in generated `install.sh`:
  - TTY-gated color variables (CYAN, GREEN, YELLOW, RED, BOLD, DIM) with full ASCII fallbacks for non-interactive terminals.
  - Semantic message functions: `step()`, `success()`, `warn()`, `error()`, `info()`.
  - Animated braille spinner (`spin()`) for downloads, VNC install, and startup verification.
  - Box-drawing helpers (`box_top`, `box_mid`, `box_bottom`, `box_line`, `box_kv`) for the completion summary.
- ASCII art "LabTether" banner in cyan displayed at script start (after arg parsing, before install logic).
- 6-step progressive flow: Preflight checks → Desktop prerequisites → Detecting platform → Downloading agent → Configuring → Verifying.
- Box-drawn completion summary showing hostname, fingerprint, binary path, config path, Docker status, file scope, auto-update, and TLS verify status.
- All error/warning messages throughout the script (arg parsing, validation, preflight, VNC, download, config, verify) use semantic helpers.
- Zero new Go dependencies — pure bash formatting inside existing `fmt.Sprintf` template.
- Updated 3 test snippets in `TestGenerateInstallScript` to match new message text.
- All tests pass (`go test ./cmd/labtether/ -v -count=1`), `go vet` clean.
- Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-02-premium-cli-installer-design.md`.

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Phase 2: Real-Time Event System)
- Wired existing `EventSocket` WebSocket client into all ViewModels so the UI updates live when the backend broadcasts events.
- New files:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Realtime/EventBus.swift`
    - `@MainActor @Observable` coordinator that owns `EventSocket`, demultiplexes events by type string to registered handler closures, exposes `isLive` computed property.
    - Connection guard prevents redundant connect calls.
- Modified files:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/MainTabView.swift`
    - Added `@State private var eventBus: EventBus` with connect-on-appear / disconnect-on-disappear lifecycle.
    - Calls `registerEvents(eventBus)` on all three ViewModels after WebSocket connects.
    - Added "Live" indicator overlay (green dot + `LT.ok` capsule) when `eventBus.isLive`.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Alerts/AlertsViewModel.swift`
    - `registerEvents(_:)` subscribes to `alert.fired` and `alert.resolved` — both trigger `refresh()`.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Home/HomeViewModel.swift`
    - `registerEvents(_:)` subscribes to `alert.fired`, `alert.resolved` (refresh + throttle reset), and `heartbeat.update` (30s throttled refresh).
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Nodes/NodesViewModel.swift`
    - `registerEvents(_:)` subscribes to `agent.connected`/`agent.disconnected` (optimistic `connectedAgentIDs` set mutation) and `heartbeat.update` (30s throttled refresh).
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Realtime/EventSocket.swift`
    - Fixed stale doc comment: event type example corrected from `alert_fired` to `alert.fired`.
- Validation:
  - Full clean `xcodebuild build` on iOS Simulator SDK — BUILD SUCCEEDED.
  - Verified all 3 ViewModels have `registerEvents` method via grep.
- Branch: `feat/ios-phase2-realtime-events`.

## 2026-03-02 (agent Linux install flow: optional VNC prerequisite prompt + flags)
- Addressed operator feedback that agent install completed without any prompt for desktop prerequisites.
- Backend installer changes:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`
    - extended generated `install.sh` with desktop prerequisite management for Linux:
      - detects missing `x11vnc` and/or `Xvfb`,
      - prompts in interactive installs: `Install desktop prerequisites now (x11vnc + Xvfb)? [Y/n]`,
      - supports explicit modes:
        - `--vnc-prereqs ask|install|skip`
        - `--install-vnc-prereqs`
        - `--skip-vnc-prereqs`
      - includes package-manager based install helper (`apt-get`, `dnf`, `yum`, `pacman`, `zypper`, `apk`),
      - continues agent install with warning if prerequisite install fails (does not brick enroll flow).
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
    - added assertions that generated script exposes new VNC prerequisite flags, prompt text, and mode validation snippets.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestGeneratedScriptsAreValidBashSyntax' -count=1`

## 2026-03-02 (desktop VNC prerequisite UX: install-guide prompt for missing x11vnc/binaries)
- Addressed operator report where desktop VNC failures surfaced only as raw noVNC close errors when prerequisites were missing.

    - added prerequisite detection for desktop session errors,

## 2026-03-02 (agent connected visibility latency fix: immediate Devices materialization + event refresh)
- Addressed operator report that `agent connected` appeared before node showed in Devices, with noticeable delay.
- Backend change:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`
    - on successful websocket auth/upgrade, hub now immediately upserts a host heartbeat snapshot (`source=agent`, `status=online`) via `upsertConnectedAgentAsset(...)` instead of waiting for next heartbeat tick.
  - added regression:
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`
      - `TestUpsertConnectedAgentAssetCreatesAssetImmediately`
- Frontend change:
  - `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`
    - websocket event handler now performs immediate `fetchLiveStatus(...)` on `agent.connected` / `agent.disconnected` instead of the shared 3s debounced refresh path.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestUpsertConnectedAgentAssetCreatesAssetImmediately|TestProcessAgentHeartbeatIgnoresPayloadAssetID|TestHandleAgentWebSocketReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

## 2026-03-02 (agent enrollment recovery hardening: stale persisted token no longer blocks re-enroll/pending approval)
- Investigated Linux install success + missing pending approval symptom and isolated root cause from live hub logs:
  - hub received websocket traffic from Linux node but rejected with `agentws: ... invalid agent token`,
  - agent reused stale persisted `/etc/labtether/agent-token` from previous enrollment/hub and never entered pending-enrollment path.
- Runtime fix:
  - `internal/agentcore/enroll.go`
    - introduced `ResolveTokenWithOptions(..., ResolveTokenOptions{SkipTokenFile:true})` so forced re-enrollment can bypass stale token-file load.
  - `internal/agentcore/run.go`
    - re-enrollment callback now uses `SkipTokenFile:true`, preventing auth-failure loops that previously reloaded the same stale token.
- Installer fix:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`
    - Linux `install.sh` now removes existing persisted token when `--enrollment-token` is provided, forcing fresh enrollment on explicit add-device flows.
- Tests added/updated:
  - `internal/agentcore/enroll_test.go`
    - added `TestResolveTokenWithOptions_SkipTokenFileForcesEnrollment`.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
    - added generated-script assertion for enrollment-token-driven stale token removal.
- Docs/wiki updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/node-offline.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestResolveToken|TestResolveTokenWithOptions_SkipTokenFileForcesEnrollment' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestGenerateInstallScript|TestGeneratedScriptsAreValidBashSyntax|TestHandleAgentInstallScript' -count=1`

## 2026-03-02 (iOS Phase 1: wire live backend data into existing screens)
- Completed Phase 1 of iOS feature buildout — wired real backend data into all existing stubbed screens.
- **Batch A — Node detail live data** (branch: `feat/ios-phase1-live-data`):
  - Added 9 new model structs to `Core/Networking/Models.swift`: `AssetMetricsResponse`, `MetricsAssetInfo`, `MetricSeries`, `MetricPoint`, `MetricsOverviewResponse`, `AssetTelemetryOverview`, `TelemetrySnapshot`, `ConnectedAgentsResponse`, `ConnectedAgent`.
  - Created `NodeDetailViewModel.swift` with 3 parallel `async let` fetches: `/metrics/assets/{id}?window=1h`, `/alerts/instances` (client-side filter), `/agents/connected`.
  - Rewrote `NodeDetailView.swift` to show live Gauge widgets (CPU/Memory/Disk), recent alerts (severity dot + rule name + relative time), agent presence indicator.
  - Gauge thresholds: green <70%, amber 70-90%, red >=90%. HTTP 404 treated as "not yet collected".
- **Batch B — Home telemetry + agent presence on node list**:
  - Extended `HomeViewModel` with parallel `/metrics/overview?window=1h` fetch to compute fleet-wide CPU average.
  - Replaced static "Awaiting data" in `HomeView` telemetry section with live `"\(Int(avg))%"` with color-coded thresholds.
  - Extended `NodesViewModel` with parallel `/agents/connected` fetch exposing `connectedAgentIDs` set.
  - Added `antenna.radiowaves.left.and.right` SF Symbol badge to node list rows with live agents.
  - Refactored `HomeViewModel.refresh()` to separate dashboard/alerts/metrics into independent fetch methods (dashboard success no longer discarded when alerts fail).
  - Fixed alert sorting to use ISO8601DateFormatter instead of fragile lexicographic string comparison.
- **Batch C — Response caching with offline fallback**:
  - Added per-asset cache keys to `NodeDetailViewModel` for metrics and alerts.
  - All metrics/alerts fetches now cache on success and fall back to cached data on failure.
  - Fleet CPU average cached in `HomeViewModel` for offline access.
- Validated with clean `xcodebuild` build on iOS Simulator SDK.
- No hardcoded placeholders remain — all screens show live data or conditional "No data"/"Awaiting data" fallbacks.

## 2026-03-02 (agent install TLS bootstrap hardening: self-signed CA trust + fingerprint verification in Add Device flow)
- Addressed Linux installer bootstrap failures where `curl .../install.sh` failed before script execution due to untrusted self-signed hub certs.
- Frontend changes:
  - `web/console/app/components/AddDeviceModal/AgentSetupStep.tsx`
    - switched TLS bootstrap decision source to `tls_info` from `/settings/enrollment` (same authenticated payload as enrollment command data),
    - self-signed detection now generates a one-command bootstrap call to `/api/v1/agent/bootstrap.sh?ca_fingerprint_sha256=...` with existing installer flags.
    - added UI guidance banner with CA fingerprint and bootstrap behavior details.
- Backend installer hardening:
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`
    - added new unauthenticated endpoint `GET /api/v1/agent/bootstrap.sh`:
      - downloads hub CA with bootstrap transport,
      - verifies pinned CA fingerprint,
      - installs local/system trust when available,
      - downloads `install.sh` via CA-verified TLS and executes with `--tls-ca-file`.
    - `install.sh` now enforces TLS download behavior during binary fetch:
      - `--tls-ca-file` => `curl --cacert` / `wget --ca-certificate`,
      - `--tls-skip-verify=true` => `curl -k` / `wget --no-check-certificate`,
      - validates `--tls-ca-file` exists before download.
  - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
    - extended script-generation coverage for TLS-aware download options.
  - `cmd/labtether/enrollment_handlers.go`, `cmd/labtether/tls_handlers.go`
    - added shared TLS info payload helper and included `tls_info` in `/settings/enrollment` response to eliminate bootstrap-flow fallback caused by separate TLS-info fetch variability.
  - `cmd/labtether/enrollment_api_test.go`
    - added coverage that `/settings/enrollment` includes `tls_info` with self-signed CA fingerprint.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`
  - `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (added ADR-023).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentBootstrapScript|TestGenerateBootstrapScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHandleAgentInstallScript' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - added shell syntax guardrail test: `TestGeneratedScriptsAreValidBashSyntax` (`bash -n` on generated bootstrap/install scripts).

## 2026-03-02 (iOS 26 visual polish: transform iOS console to first-party native appearance)
- Rewrote entire iOS console UI from custom dark glass morphism to iOS 26 Liquid Glass design language.
- Foundation changes:
  - `UI/DesignTokens.swift`: stripped to minimal status colors (ok/warn/bad), animation springs, and stagger constants. Removed all deprecated aliases (bg, panel, textPrimary, textSecondary, muted, accent, glows, etc.).
  - `UI/ViewModifiers.swift`: removed GlassCardModifier, ShimmerSkeletonModifier, RadarPulseModifier, ScanLineModifier, CountingNumber. Kept StaggeredAppearModifier, HapticOnAppearModifier, ShakeModifier, autocapitalizationNever.
  - `Assets.xcassets`: added AccentColor (indigo), StatusOK/StatusWarning/StatusCritical color sets with light/dark appearance variants.
  - `Info.plist`: removed `UIUserInterfaceStyle = Dark` to respect user light/dark preference.
  - `LabTetherMobileApp.swift`: removed `.preferredColorScheme(.dark)`.
  - Deleted `UI/CustomTabBar.swift` and removed all 4 `.pbxproj` references.
- Navigation:
  - `UI/MainTabView.swift`: rewrote from opacity-based custom tab bar to native iOS 26 `Tab` struct API with `.tabBarMinimizeBehavior(.onScrollDown)` and `.badge()`.
  - `UI/RootView.swift`: removed scanline overlay and custom background.
- Screen rewrites (all replaced custom chrome with native List/Form/LabeledContent/ContentUnavailableView patterns):
  - `HomeView.swift`: insetGrouped List, `.contentTransition(.numericText())`, `.symbolEffect(.bounce)`.
  - `AlertsView.swift`: native swipe actions, segment-filtered Picker.
  - `AlertDetailView.swift`: LabeledContent-based sections with status colors.
  - `NodesView.swift`: grouped Online/Offline sections with platform icons.
  - `NodeDetailView.swift`: Form with Gauge for CPU/Memory/Disk, LabeledContent details.
  - `MoreView.swift`, `SettingsView.swift`, `DiagnosticsView.swift`: Settings.app-style Forms.
  - `DiscoveryView.swift`, `LoginView.swift`: clean Setup Assistant patterns, removed animated reveals/breathing glow.
  - `IncidentsView.swift`: removed nested NavigationStack, ContentUnavailableView empty/error states.
- Liquid Glass branded accents:
  - HomeView status banner: `.glassEffect(.regular.tint(alertCount == 0 ? Color(LT.ok) : Color(LT.bad)))`.
  - AlertDetailView action buttons: `.glassEffect(.regular.tint(.indigo).interactive())` for Acknowledge, `.glassEffect(.regular.tint(LT.ok).interactive())` for Resolve.
- Key fixes during build verification:
  - `.tertiary` is `ShapeStyle`, not `Color` — used `Color(.tertiaryLabel)` in ternary expressions.
  - Explicit `Color.secondary`/`Color.accentColor` in mixed ternaries to satisfy type checker.
  - Removed nested NavigationStack in IncidentsView (broke back navigation from MoreView).
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild build` (iOS Simulator, iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.2): **BUILD SUCCEEDED**.
- Branch: `feat/ios-26-visual-polish`.
- Design doc: `docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-visual-polish-design.md`.
- Implementation plan: `docs/plans/2026-03-02-ios-visual-polish-plan.md`.

## 2026-03-01 (desktop VNC 1006 full-path audit: frontend-proxy websocket reliability + bridge regressions)
- Completed end-to-end audit of desktop VNC interaction across frontend (`web/console`), hub (`cmd/labtether` / `internal/servicehttp`), and mac agent session startup flow.
- Stability/regression fix:
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go`
    - adjusted `bridgeDesktopInput` nil-agent behavior to keep read loop alive (drop input) until session close, preserving idle-no-panic lifecycle test expectations.
- Proxy transport hardening:
  - `internal/servicehttp/servicehttp.go`
    - added `RedirectToHTTPSWithWebSocketBypass()` and wired redirect listener to bypass HTTP->HTTPS redirect for websocket upgrade requests on:
      - `/desktop/sessions/{id}/stream`
      - `/terminal/sessions/{id}/stream`
      - `/ws/events`
    - rationale: websocket clients cannot follow HTTP 301 during Upgrade, and same-origin frontend rewrites rely on upgrade pass-through.
  - `internal/servicehttp/servicehttp_test.go`
    - added redirect bypass tests for websocket upgrade pass-through and non-upgrade redirect behavior.
- Live validation:
  - direct backend desktop stream probe: `wss://localhost:8443/.../stream?ticket=...` received first binary frame (`12` bytes).
  - frontend-origin proxy probe: `wss://localhost:3000/.../stream?ticket=...` received first binary frame (`12` bytes).
  - stress loop: 5/5 successful create-ticket-connect-close cycles through frontend proxy.
  - backend log sweep after probes: no `panic`, no `repeated read on failed websocket connection`.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/servicehttp -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestBridgeDesktopInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd|TestProcessAgentDesktopClosedStoresReasonAndSignalsBridge|TestSafeWriteCloseHandlesOversizedReason' -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (tailscale metadata parsing hardening: prevent bogus version text in node network panel)
- Investigated node Network panel rendering where **Tailscale Version** displayed invalid text (`The`) instead of a semantic version string.
- Root cause:
  - platform metadata parser accepted the first token of arbitrary command output for `tailscale version` fallback, allowing non-version strings from error text.
- Backend changes:
  - `internal/agentplatform/darwin/provider_metadata.go`
  - `internal/agentplatform/linux/provider_metadata.go`
    - hardened `parseTailscaleVersion()` to extract only semantic-version-shaped tokens (`v?X.Y.Z[-suffix]`),
    - added token extraction helper and rejection of non-version output.
- Regression coverage added:
  - `internal/agentplatform/darwin/provider_metadata_test.go`
  - `internal/agentplatform/linux/provider_metadata_test.go`
    - `TestParseTailscaleVersionRejectsNonVersionOutput`
    - `TestParseTailscaleVersionExtractsFromVerboseOutput`
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md` (new Tailscale version sanity check/remediation section).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentplatform/darwin ./internal/agentplatform/linux -count=1` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (mac-agent brew parser compatibility: accept cask installed as string or array)
- Addressed mac-agent package inventory failure:
  - `failed to parse brew package output: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field ... installed of type []string`
- Backend changes:
  - `internal/agentcore/package_backend_darwin.go`
    - introduced tolerant `brewCaskInstalledVersions` unmarshal logic that accepts:
      - `installed` as `[]string`,
      - `installed` as `"string"`,
      - `null` / empty values.
    - preserves existing package version selection behavior by using normalized installed versions list.
- Regression coverage:
  - `internal/agentcore/package_backend_darwin_test.go`
    - added `TestParseBrewInstalledPackagesCaskInstalledAsString`.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestParseBrewInstalledPackages|TestBuildDarwinPackageActionArgs' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (SPICE same-origin cert trust: frontend-origin websocket proxy for Proxmox SPICE)
- Implemented SPICE transport changes so browser SPICE sessions stay on the console origin/cert instead of opening direct browser websocket connections to Proxmox hosts.
- Backend changes:
  - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_spice_proxy.go`
    - `handleDesktopSPICETicket` now issues a standard one-time desktop stream ticket and returns `stream_path` + SPICE password metadata.
    - added `handleDesktopSPICEStream` to bridge same-origin browser websocket traffic to the stored upstream Proxmox SPICE TCP endpoint.
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go`
    - `protocol=spice` now routes through `handleDesktopSPICEStream` on `/desktop/sessions/{id}/stream`.
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_session_handlers.go`
    - added SPICE proxy target state helpers (`set/take/clear`) and TTL cleanup integration.
  - `cmd/labtether/server_types.go`, `cmd/labtether/startup_server.go`
    - added in-memory SPICE proxy target map + mutex initialization.
- Frontend changes:
  - `web/console/app/api/desktop/spice-ticket/route.ts`
    - normalizes backend SPICE ticket response to same-origin stream payload (`streamPath`, `secure`, `password`) and errors when stream-path payload is unavailable.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts`
    - SPICE connect flow now builds browser ws URL from `streamPath` and stores `spiceTicket.wsUrl` (direct-host fallback removed).
  - `web/console/app/components/SPICEViewer.tsx`, `web/console/app/components/SessionPanel.tsx`
    - switched SPICE viewer wiring from `host/tls_port` to explicit `wsUrl`.
- Regression coverage added:
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`
    - `TestDesktopSPICEProxyTargetIsConsumedOnce`
    - `TestHandleDesktopStreamSPICERequiresPreissuedTicketFlow`
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/spice-proxmox.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestDesktopSPICEProxyTargetIsConsumedOnce|TestHandleDesktopStreamSPICERequiresPreissuedTicketFlow|TestNormalizeWebSocketCloseReasonTruncatesToProtocolLimit|TestSafeWriteCloseHandlesOversizedReason' -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (desktop stream lifecycle hardening: eliminate gorilla repeated-read panics and 1006 disconnects)
- Investigated mac-agent VNC disconnect reports (`Connection closed (code: 1006)`) and confirmed hub panics in desktop browser input bridge:
  - `panic: repeated read on failed websocket connection`
  - stack rooted at `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go:bridgeDesktopInput`.
- Backend fixes:
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go`
    - reworked `bridgeDesktopInput` to use blocking `ReadMessage()` with close-unblock watcher (same hardening pattern already used by terminal streams),
    - removed 1s read-deadline polling loop that could poison Gorilla read state and panic on subsequent reads,
    - added defensive panic recovery logging for desktop browser input bridge.
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go` (direct proxy path)
    - applied the same blocking-read + close-unblock pattern to direct VNC browser input loop,
    - added panic recovery logging for direct VNC browser input bridge.
- Regression coverage:
  - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`
    - added `TestBridgeDesktopInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd`.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md` (new explicit check/remediation for `repeated read on failed websocket connection`).
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` (desktop troubleshooting note for the same failure signature).
- Frontend follow-up:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts`
    - desktop session disconnect handling now surfaces backend-provided close reasons even on clean closes (except explicit user disconnect), so startup/permission failures are visible in the UI banner instead of silently returning to idle.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestBridgeDesktopInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd|TestBridgeAgentInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd|TestSafeWriteCloseHandlesOversizedReason|TestProcessAgentDesktopClosedStoresReasonAndSignalsBridge' -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (frontend websocket scheme parity: derive `ws`/`wss` from console origin only)
- Completed follow-up on same-origin websocket proxying to remove backend-port-driven scheme hints that could still force incorrect `wss://` behavior in mixed/local dev runs.
- Frontend changes:
  - `web/console/lib/backend.ts`
    - simplified `shouldUseSecureWebSocket()` to infer secure websocket usage from frontend request protocol only (`x-forwarded-proto` or request URL), no backend `:8443`/`https` heuristics.
  - updated consumers in:
    - `web/console/app/api/desktop/stream-ticket/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/api/terminal/stream-ticket/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/api/ws/events/route.ts`
- Result:
  - browser websocket URLs now consistently match console origin transport, keeping trust behavior centered on `https://localhost:3000`.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md` (explicit `http`/`https` -> `ws`/`wss` parity check).
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (infra cascade parity follow-up: collector scoping + homeassistant + remote collector boundary)
- Continued delete/site cascade audit to cover remaining connector families and collector boundaries.
- Backend changes:
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_sites_cascade.go`
    - added Home Assistant infra host mapping (`homeassistant`/`home-assistant` -> `connector-cluster`) so HA parent assets can cascade to HA entities.
    - changed Proxmox/Portainer parent-child keying to collector-scoped keys when `metadata.collector_id` is present:
      - Proxmox key: `collector_id::node`,
      - Portainer key: `collector_id::endpoint_id`.
    - added generic future-source support: non-Docker `connector-cluster` parents with `collector_id` now cascade to same-source children by collector ID.
    - updated Proxmox host parent key fallback to prefer `metadata.node` before display name.
    - introduced shared `infraChildAttachedToParent()` matcher so delete and site-cascade paths use identical matching semantics.
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_heartbeat_handlers.go`
    - switched non-Docker infra delete cascade matching to shared `infraChildAttachedToParent()` helper for parity with site assignment behavior.
- Regression coverage added:
  - `cmd/labtether/asset_delete_test.go`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_ProxmoxDeleteScopesCascadeByCollectorID`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_PortainerDeleteScopesCascadeByCollectorID`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_HomeAssistantDeleteCascadesByCollectorID`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_FutureCollectorClusterDeleteCascadesByCollectorID`
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_sites_api_test.go`
    - `TestUpdateAssetSiteCascadesToPortainerSubDevicesScopedByCollectorID`
    - `TestUpdateAssetSiteCascadesToHomeAssistantEntitiesByCollectorID`
    - `TestUpdateAssetSiteCascadesToFutureCollectorClusterChildrenByCollectorID`
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` (collector-scoped Proxmox/Portainer cascade notes + Home Assistant inclusion in delete behavior).
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md` (same cascade clarifications plus explicit note that `ssh`/`winrm`/`api` collectors are telemetry/log only and do not fan out child assets).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestDeleteAsset_(NonDockerInfraDeleteCascadesAttachedInfraChildren|ProxmoxDeleteScopesCascadeByCollectorID|PortainerDeleteScopesCascadeByCollectorID|PBSDeleteCascadesByCollectorID|HomeAssistantDeleteCascadesByCollectorID|FutureCollectorClusterDeleteCascadesByCollectorID)|TestUpdateAssetSiteCascadesTo(PortainerSubDevices|PortainerSubDevicesScopedByCollectorID|ProxmoxSubDevices|DockerSubDevicesByAgentID|HomeAssistantEntitiesByCollectorID|FutureCollectorClusterChildrenByCollectorID)' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (delete-cascade parity sweep: infra children + auto-docker collector cleanup + webservice persistence parity)
- Continued deletion-audit remediation after orphaned-dashboard issue reports.
- Backend changes:
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_heartbeat_handlers.go`
    - added non-Docker infra child cascade in `deleteAsset` (Proxmox/Portainer/PBS/TrueNAS host deletes now remove attached child assets by infra parent key).
    - added auto-Docker collector cleanup in `deleteAsset`: remove Docker collectors whose `config.agent_asset_id` matches the deleted asset and remove their collector-cluster assets.
    - retained Docker-specific attachment cleanup path for `metadata.agent_id` and legacy ID patterns.
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_sites_cascade.go`
    - added `pbs` to infra host mapping.
    - aligned parent/child key derivation for Docker (`agent_id`) and PBS/TrueNAS (`collector_id`, with source fallback).
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_lifecycle_helpers.go`
    - parent/cluster asset refresh now stamps `metadata.collector_id` for stable keying/cascade parity.
  - `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator.go`
    - `RemoveHost()` now removes persisted manual services + overrides for the host (memory store parity with Postgres FK cascade behavior).
- Regression coverage added:
  - `cmd/labtether/asset_delete_test.go`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_NonDockerInfraDeleteCascadesAttachedInfraChildren`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_PBSDeleteCascadesByCollectorID`
    - `TestDeleteAsset_RemovesAutoDockerCollectorsForDeletedParent`
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_sites_api_test.go`
    - `TestUpdateAssetSiteCascadesToDockerSubDevicesByAgentID`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_lifecycle_helpers_test.go`
    - collector_id assertions for refreshed parent assets
  - `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator_test.go`
    - `TestRemoveHostRemovesPersistedHostEntries`
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestDeleteAsset|TestUpdateAssetSiteCascadesToDockerSubDevicesByAgentID|TestKeepConnectorClusterAssetAliveUpsertsClusterHeartbeat|TestRefreshCollectorParentAssetSupportsCustomRootType' -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./internal/connectors/webservice -count=1` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (desktop VNC close-frame hardening: prevent abnormal 1006 on oversized reasons)
- Fixed a hub-side desktop stream bug where oversized WebSocket close reasons could produce invalid close control frames and force browser-side abnormal disconnects (`code: 1006`).
- Backend changes:
  - added `normalizeWebSocketCloseReason()` in `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go` to clamp close reason text to protocol-safe size (`<=123` bytes).
  - hardened `safeWriteClose()` to use normalized reasons and fallback to an empty-text close frame when close writes fail.
- Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`:
  - `TestNormalizeWebSocketCloseReasonTruncatesToProtocolLimit`
  - `TestSafeWriteCloseHandlesOversizedReason`
- Follow-up fixes after live verification:
  - improved browser websocket TLS inference in console API routes (`desktop/stream-ticket`, `terminal/stream-ticket`, `ws/events`) to avoid `ws://` downgrade against TLS port `8443`.
  - switched browser websocket transport to same-origin (`:3000`) via Next.js rewrites for:
    - `/desktop/sessions/:path*`,
    - `/terminal/sessions/:path*`,
    - `/ws/events`.
  - `buildBrowserWsUrl()` now targets current frontend origin host/port only.
  - updated `scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh` guidance to reflect same-origin websocket proxy behavior.
  - added noVNC disconnect guard in `VNCViewer` to suppress repeated teardown errors (`Tried changing state of a disconnected RFB object`) after failed connects.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/vnc.md`
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-014 consequence note on WS close-reason limit)
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestNormalizeWebSocketCloseReasonTruncatesToProtocolLimit|TestSafeWriteCloseHandlesOversizedReason|TestDesktopBridgeCloseReasonRoundTrip|TestProcessAgentDesktopClosedStoresReasonAndSignalsBridge' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (asset delete cascade fix: docker child cleanup for non-agent parents + immediate web-service cache removal)
- Fixed node deletion behavior that left orphan Docker workloads visible in Dashboard/Nodes when the deleted parent asset was not `source=agent`.
- Backend changes:
  - `deleteAsset` now always attempts attached Docker child cleanup by deleted asset ID (instead of gating by parent source).
  - `deleteAsset` now removes web-service coordinator host cache immediately for the deleted asset ID.
  - added `webservice.Coordinator.RemoveHost()` for immediate host cache eviction.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `TestDeleteAsset_NonAgentDeleteCascadesAttachedDockerAssetsAndWebServices` in `cmd/labtether/asset_delete_test.go`.
  - `TestRemoveHost` in `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator_test.go`.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run TestDeleteAsset -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./internal/connectors/webservice -count=1` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (dev runtime preference: keep only menu bar mac agent instance)
- Captured operator preference for local dev runs: keep exactly one mac agent instance, and keep the menu bar app (`LabTether Agent.app`) as the active instance.
- Do not launch an additional standalone/tmux mac agent process when the menu bar app is already running unless explicitly requested.

## 2026-03-01 (agent TLS trust-exemption hardening: runtime parsing + installer flags + diagnostics)
- Addressed agent TLS bootstrap friction when hubs use self-signed/untrusted cert chains.
- Updated agent runtime config loading:
  - `LABTETHER_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY` now parses with `strconv.ParseBool` (accepts `true/TRUE/1/t`, etc.).
  - added local agent setting keys `tls_skip_verify` and `tls_ca_file` so file-based settings can override env when needed.
  - config loading now applies those TLS setting overrides from agent settings file.
- Improved operator diagnostics:
  - websocket reconnect loop now logs explicit remediation guidance on first TLS trust failure (`LABTETHER_TLS_CA_FILE` preferred, `LABTETHER_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY=true` temporary fallback).
- Extended Linux installer script (`/install.sh`):
  - new flags `--tls-skip-verify <true|false>` and `--tls-ca-file <absolute-path>`,
  - validates option values and writes `LABTETHER_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY` / `LABTETHER_TLS_CA_FILE` into `/etc/labtether/agent.env`,
  - surfaces configured TLS values in install summary output.
- Expanded tests:
  - added `internal/agentcore/config_tls_test.go` for bool parsing and settings-over-env precedence.
  - updated install script tests to assert TLS flags and env-variable emission.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore ./cmd/labtether` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (iOS nodes decode compatibility: assets wrapper + last_seen_at mapping)
- Investigated Nodes-tab runtime failure on mobile: `Response decoding failed ... not in the correct format`.
- Root cause identified:
  - `/assets` backend response is wrapped (`{ "assets": [...] }`) while mobile Nodes view model decoded raw `[Asset]`.
- Implemented fixes:
  - switched `NodesViewModel.refresh()` to decode `AssetsResponse` wrapper.
  - added `AssetsResponse` model in mobile networking layer.
  - hardened `Asset` decode compatibility:
    - accepts both `last_seen` and `last_seen_at`,
    - derives `ipAddress` from top-level `ip_address` or `metadata["ip_address"/"ip"]`,
    - derives `agentVersion` from top-level `agent_version` or `metadata["agent_version"]`.
  - added regression coverage in `APIClientTests` for wrapped `/assets` shape and metadata-derived fields.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' build` passed (`** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`).
  - focused tests passed:
    - `xcodebuild ... -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests test` (`10 tests, 0 failures`).

## 2026-03-01 (iOS home/alerts decode compatibility: backend contract sync)
- Investigated iOS home-screen error: `Response decoding failed: The data couldn’t be read because it isn’t in the correct format.`
- Root causes identified:
  - `/alerts/instances` backend now returns wrapped payload (`{ "instances": [...] }`) while mobile decoded a raw array.
  - mobile dashboard summary expected snake_case keys while backend returns camelCase (`servicesUp`, `assetCount`, ...).
  - alert model expected legacy fields (`state`, `fired_at`) while backend emits (`status`, `started_at`, `last_fired_at`).
- Implemented iOS fixes:
  - switched Home/Alerts view models to decode `AlertInstancesResponse` wrapper.
  - updated `DashboardSummary` decoding to accept both camelCase and snake_case key variants.
  - hardened `AlertInstance` decoding to map backend fields:
    - `status -> state`,
    - `started_at/last_fired_at -> firedAt`,
    - labels fallback for `ruleName`/`assetName`/`assetID`.
  - added `APIClientTests` coverage for:
    - camelCase dashboard summary decoding,
    - wrapped alert instances payload decoding + field mapping.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' build` passed (`** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`).

## 2026-03-01 (iOS discovery/addressing follow-up: IPv4 candidates + readable API errors)
- Investigated report: app discovered IPv6 endpoint but operators expected/needed IPv4 path; observed confusing raw `APIError error 6` surfacing in UI.
- Updated iOS mDNS discovery resolver (`HubScanner`) to supplement NWConnection's chosen endpoint with DNS-resolved `<service>.local` numeric candidates:
  - collects both IPv4 and IPv6 candidates when available,
  - sorts candidates with IPv4 preference before probing.
- Preserved existing secure probing flow (`HTTPS` first, `8443` preference for legacy `8080` service advertisements).
- Added `LocalizedError` conformance for `APIError` so generic `error.localizedDescription` surfaces actionable text instead of opaque enum codes (e.g. avoids `APIError error 6`).
- Updated auth login error mapping to surface `insecureTransport` message directly.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' build` passed (`** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`).

## 2026-03-01 (iOS TLS trust follow-up: default bypass-on + mDNS HTTPS port preference)
- Addressed continued mobile `TLS trust failed` reports after enabling temporary untrusted-cert mode.
- Updated `HubConfig.allowsUntrustedTLS` default behavior:
  - when no user-default value exists yet, temporary bypass now defaults to `enabled` (first-run safe).
- Hardened iOS discovery endpoint selection in `HubScanner`:
  - for mDNS services advertising legacy `:8080`, scanner now probes secure `:8443` first and falls back to `:8080` probe second.
  - prevents selecting `https://<host>:8080` when that port is plain HTTP redirect traffic.
- Tightened TLS-trust error classification:
  - removed generic `NSURLErrorSecureConnectionFailed` from certificate-trust detection to avoid false trust labels on protocol/port mismatch.
  - narrowed auth trust-message heuristic to certificate-specific indicators only.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `HubConfigTests`: unset untrusted-TLS key defaults to enabled.
  - `AuthViewModelTests`: generic SSL/secure-connection handshake errors are no longer classified as certificate trust failures.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' build` passed (`** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`).
  - simulator test run remains flaky in this environment due launchd/SBMainWorkspace simulator app-launch errors (non-code infra issue).

## 2026-03-01 (iOS temporary untrusted-cert mode: operator toggle for self-signed hub login)
- Implemented iOS-only temporary TLS compatibility path for self-signed/untrusted hub certificates.
- Added shared runtime switch in `HubConfig` (`allowUntrustedTLSDefaultsKey`) and `HubTLSDelegate`:
  - when enabled, URLSession uses server-trust credential bypass for HTTPS challenges.
- Wired the delegate across iOS app networking paths:
  - REST API client (`APIClient`),
  - discovery probes (`HubScanner`),
  - event websocket session (`EventSocket`).
- Added Settings toggle in iOS app (`Allow Untrusted TLS`, under Security section), defaulted to `true` for immediate homelab compatibility.
- Preserved existing discovery/auth hardening and fail-fast behavior:
  - login `POST` remains non-retrying,
  - connectivity waits remain fail-fast.
- Added/updated tests:
  - `HubConfigTests` coverage for user-defaults TLS toggle read path,
  - full iOS test suite passes after change.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' test` passed (`76 tests, 0 failures`).
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' build` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (iOS auth UX reliability: remove long login spinner retries, fail fast with actionable errors)
- Investigated report: iOS login stuck on loading spinner after hub discovery.
- Updated `APIClient` interactive network behavior:
  - disabled `waitsForConnectivity` to avoid prolonged "pending network" UI hangs,
  - limited automatic retry policy to idempotent methods (`GET`/`HEAD`) only.
- Updated auth error handling in `AuthViewModel`:
  - added explicit `HTTP <status>` login failure message path,
  - retained certificate-trust guidance for TLS errors.
- Added auth test coverage for certificate-trust message heuristic (`AuthViewModelTests`).
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' test` passed (`75 tests, 0 failures`).

## 2026-03-01 (iOS hub discovery deep-dive hardening: surface TLS-untrusted hubs + IPv6-safe URL handling)
- Investigated real-world "No hubs found" reports on same LAN/Tailnet and found scanner drop points:
  - candidates were discarded on TLS trust failures,
  - URL construction was not IPv6-safe for probe/display URLs.
- Updated iOS discovery scanner (`apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Discovery/HubScanner.swift`):
  - probe pipeline now distinguishes verified, TLS-untrusted, and failed outcomes,
  - TLS-untrusted HTTPS hubs are surfaced as discoverable candidates instead of silently dropped,
  - discovery status now reports when hubs were found but still need TLS trust,
  - host URL building now correctly handles IPv6/bracket formatting (including zone escaping) for both base and `/healthz` probe URLs.
- Updated discovery UI (`Features/Auth/DiscoveryView.swift`):
  - rows now show trust state (`Verified TLS` vs `TLS trust needed`) and a warning subtitle for untrusted certs.
- Updated auth error messaging (`Features/Auth/AuthViewModel.swift`):
  - certificate-trust network failures now return explicit user guidance to install/trust the hub CA on iOS.
- Added scanner coverage (`LabTetherMobileTests/HubScannerTests.swift`) for:
  - IPv6 URL generation,
  - trust-state round-trip behavior.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' test` passed (`74 tests, 0 failures`).

## 2026-03-01 (iOS project hygiene: string catalog symbol generation enabled)
- Addressed Xcode recommendation "Enable String Catalog Symbol Generation" for `LabTetherMobile`.
- Set `STRING_CATALOG_GENERATE_SYMBOLS=YES` for project/app Debug+Release build settings in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -showBuildSettings` now reports `STRING_CATALOG_GENERATE_SYMBOLS = YES`.
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' build` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (iOS security hardening second pass: strict hub URL policy, session/cache cleanup, ATS + test stability)
- Completed a second hardening pass in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile` focused on transport/input/session hygiene:
  - added strict hub URL normalization/validation (`http[s]://host[:port]` only, no credentials/path/query/fragment).
  - startup now drops persisted insecure/invalid hub URLs from Keychain.
  - auth flow now enforces normalized URLs for discovery/manual entry and rejects non-loopback HTTP with explicit error.
  - API/WebSocket sessions now disable URLSession caching and keep TLS minimum at 1.2 for secure transport.
  - added `ResponseCache.clearAll()` and wired logout/hub-switch flows to clear offline cache plus cookie/session artifacts.
  - login-screen "Change" action now uses full logout path (prevents stale session/cache leftovers).
  - set explicit ATS policy in iOS `Info.plist` (`NSAllowsArbitraryLoads=false`, `NSAllowsLocalNetworking=true`).
- Test/build infrastructure and coverage updates:
  - added `HubConfigTests.swift` for URL normalization/allowlist and persisted insecure URL cleanup.
  - extended `ResponseCacheTests` with `clearAll` coverage.
  - updated auth tests for stricter URL-policy behavior.
  - fixed iOS test target configuration (`GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE=YES`) and added new test file to target sources.
  - resolved test actor-isolation compile errors by marking HubConfig-mutating test suites `@MainActor`.
- Documentation updates:
  - `docs/SECURITY.md` (new iOS companion security posture section),
  - `docs/wiki/operations/security-hardening-checklist.md` (iOS hardening checks),
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/auth-login-issues.md` (iOS URL policy and cache-reset recovery guidance).
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' build` passed.
  - `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' test` passed (`72 tests, 0 failures`).

## 2026-03-01 (mac-agent parity/security pass: Docker mode + writable runtime paths + validation)
- Implemented mac-agent feature/security parity improvements for shared `agentcore` settings:
  - added Docker config controls and env export wiring:
    - `LABTETHER_DOCKER_ENABLED`,
    - `LABTETHER_DOCKER_SOCKET`,
    - `LABTETHER_DOCKER_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL`.
  - added runtime policy/env wiring:
    - `LABTETHER_FILES_ROOT_MODE`,
    - `LABTETHER_AUTO_UPDATE`,
    - `LABTETHER_ALLOW_REMOTE_OVERRIDES`,
    - `LABTETHER_LOG_LEVEL`.
- Fixed macOS path parity gap by exporting user-writable Application Support paths:
  - `LABTETHER_AGENT_SETTINGS_FILE`,
  - `LABTETHER_DEVICE_KEY_FILE`,
  - `LABTETHER_DEVICE_PUBLIC_KEY_FILE`,
  - `LABTETHER_DEVICE_FINGERPRINT_FILE`,
  - retained `LABTETHER_TOKEN_FILE`.
  - this avoids non-writable `/etc/labtether` defaults for app-bundled mac runs.
- Added launch-time configuration validation in mac wrapper before agent start:
  - validates hub URL scheme/host, port range, Docker mode/endpoint/interval, files-root mode, log level, and CA file existence.
  - invalid configs now fail fast with clear UI/log errors instead of opaque runtime failures.
- Expanded mac Settings UI:
  - new Docker Integration card (mode, endpoint, discovery interval + privilege warning),
  - Runtime Policy card (file scope, remote overrides, log level),
  - startup toggle for auto-update,
  - diagnostics now surfaces settings/device identity file paths.
- Validation:
  - `cd apps/mac-agent && swift build` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (TLS finding #3 hardening: external URL downgrade guard)
- Hardened advertised hub URL resolution (discover/enroll/install/settings candidate selection):
  - added `sanitizedExternalHubURL()` guard that validates `LABTETHER_EXTERNAL_URL` and rejects `http://` overrides when hub TLS is enabled.
  - updated enroll/discover handlers, hub connection resolver, and install script URL resolution to use the guarded resolver.
- Added startup warning when `LABTETHER_EXTERNAL_URL` is invalid/insecure for current TLS mode and therefore ignored.
- Added regression coverage for:
  - discover fallback to secure request host when TLS is on and external URL is `http://`,
  - install script behavior in TLS mode with insecure external URL,
  - hub connection candidate selection ignoring insecure external URL in TLS mode.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (Security remediation wave: enrollment, desktop proxying, auth/bootstrap, and connector TLS defaults)
- Completed a full hardening pass to close the latest pentest findings across hub, agent, and console.
- Enrollment hardening:
  - added atomic enrollment token consumption in persistence stores (`ConsumeEnrollmentToken`) and switched enroll flow to single-step consume semantics.
  - clamped token creation defaults so `max_uses <= 0` no longer creates effectively unlimited enrollment tokens (default/forced to `1`).
  - sanitized public host resolution and prioritized valid `LABTETHER_EXTERNAL_URL` for generated enrollment/discovery endpoints.
- Agent TLS bootstrap hardening:
  - removed enrollment-time implicit `InsecureSkipVerify` fallback for HTTPS bootstrap in `internal/agentcore/enroll.go` (fail closed unless trust is explicitly configured).
- Desktop stream/session hardening:
  - enforced managed-asset existence for desktop session targets.
  - gated direct VNC proxy behind `LABTETHER_DESKTOP_DIRECT_PROXY_ENABLED` (default disabled).
  - removed `vnc_port` query override path and restricted direct VNC host/port resolution to stored config plus outbound policy validation.
- Websocket and runtime-surface hardening:
  - added explicit websocket read limits for browser events, terminal streams, and desktop streams.
  - wrapped DEV_MODE `pprof` routes with auth middleware.
- Auth bootstrap hardening:
  - removed non-production auto-reset/sync behavior for existing admin passwords.
  - removed weak `"password"` fallback and moved to strong generated password when none is configured.
- Connector TLS posture hardening:
  - flipped default `skip_verify` behavior to `false` across backend collector executors, connector test handlers, and console settings/test route defaults.
  - aligned connector hooks/components so UI defaults are secure-by-default.
- Added/updated regression coverage:
  - desktop target existence checks and direct-proxy disabled behavior tests.
  - DEV_MODE pprof auth requirement test.
  - enrollment default/clamp behavior and external URL preference tests.
  - updated auth bootstrap and agent enrollment tests to reflect hardened behavior.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-03-01 (Instruction second pass: domain-specific CLAUDE templates + strengthened root governance docs)
- Performed a second-pass rewrite of repo instruction files after initial standardization.
- Root governance updates:
  - expanded `AGENTS.md` with a stronger operational contract: mission, canonical terms, runtime/validation baseline, required workflow, mandatory docs/wiki contract, and definition-of-done.
  - expanded root `CLAUDE.md` with clearer purpose, architecture guardrails, development defaults, and mandatory docs/wiki sync requirements.
- Scoped guidance upgrades:
  - replaced the first-pass generic scoped templates with domain-specific variants for:
    - backend Go runtime (`cmd/labtether`, `internal/*`, `services/migrator`),
    - frontend Next.js/TypeScript (`web/console/*`),
    - Apple clients (`apps/ios-console/*`, `apps/mac-agent/*`),
    - Home Assistant integration (`integrations/homeassistant/*`),
    - deploy/install assets (`deploy/*`),
    - scripts (`scripts/*`),
    - docs/plans and notes subtrees,
    - canonical contract fixture testdata scope.
  - all scoped files continue to inherit root policy and enforce mandatory updates to `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, and execution notes.
- Net effect:
  - guidance moved from placeholder/generic text to actionable, stack-aware operational instructions while retaining a strict “update docs + wiki in the same workstream” standard.

## 2026-03-01 (Agent instruction refresh: root + scoped CLAUDE docs with mandatory docs/wiki updates)
- Rewrote root guidance files to reflect current project shape and workflow:

  - updated root `CLAUDE.md` to align architecture/workflow guidance with current repo reality.
- Replaced stale scoped `CLAUDE.md` placeholders across the monorepo with actionable subtree instructions:
  - each scoped file now defines its coverage boundary,
  - points back to root policy as source of truth,
  - enforces a mandatory documentation update policy.
- Standardized mandatory docs policy across guidance files:
