  - update `docs/wiki/` for operator/user workflow changes,
  - update `docs/ADR.md` for architecture/tradeoff decisions,

## 2026-03-01 (TLS/HTTPS: final audit fixes + WebSocket port intelligence + doc updates)
- Addressed 4 remaining findings from E2E security audit covering Browser → Next.js HTTPS :3000 → Go HTTPS :8443 chain.
- **WebSocket port resolution** (`web/console/app/lib/ws.ts`):
  - Rewrote `buildBrowserWsUrl` with smart port detection: respects `NEXT_PUBLIC_HUB_API_PORT` first, detects Next.js dev server on :3000 and auto-maps to backend ports (8443 for WSS, 8080 for WS), falls back to browser location port for production.
  - Added `BuildBrowserWsOptions` type with optional `secure` override.
  - Added TLS cross-origin cert trust documentation in JSDoc.
- **`NEXT_PUBLIC_HUB_API_PORT`** set to `8443` in `scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh` (when TLS detected) and `docker-compose.yml`.
- **Health route** (`web/console/app/api/health/route.ts`): refactored to use shared `backendBaseURLs()` and corrected endpoint to `/healthz`.
- **`.env.local` removed from git**: added `.env*.local` to `.gitignore`, ran `git rm --cached`.
- **Dev frontend script**: added TLS startup messages (WebSocket port, cert trust instructions, CA file path).
- **Documentation updates**: updated ARCHITECTURE.md (3-mode TLS system), SECURITY.md (TLS enforcement section), and 6 wiki pages with TLS configuration, environment variables, and trust model documentation.

## 2026-03-01 (Asset deletion cascade: remove attached Docker assets when deleting an agent device)
- Updated agent deletion flow for `DELETE /assets/{id}` to cascade Docker cleanup:
  - `handleAssetActions` now passes the loaded asset into delete flow so source-aware cleanup can run.
  - when deleting an `agent`/`labtether-agent` source asset, backend now deletes attached Docker assets before deleting the agent asset itself.
  - attached Docker matching uses canonical metadata (`source=docker` + `metadata.agent_id=<agent_id>`) with legacy ID-pattern fallback (`docker-host-*`, `docker-ct-*`, `docker-stack-*`, and auto collector cluster IDs).
  - in-memory Docker coordinator host cache is now cleared immediately via `dockerCoordinator.RemoveHost(assetID)` during deletion.
- Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/asset_delete_test.go`:
  - new `TestDeleteAsset_AgentDeleteCascadesAttachedDockerAssets` verifies attached Docker host/container/stack/cluster assets are removed while unrelated Docker assets remain.
- Validation completed:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run TestDeleteAsset -count=1`
  - `go vet ./...`
  - `go test ./...`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

## 2026-03-01 (TLS/HTTPS enforcement: security hardening and dev-mode HTTPS)
- Completed 3 rounds of E2E penetration testing, fixing all HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW findings.
- **Security hardening** (7 findings from first pen-test):
  - Fixed `buildBrowserWsUrl` in `web/console/app/lib/ws.ts` — replaced hardcoded port "8080" with `NEXT_PUBLIC_HUB_API_PORT` env var.
  - Added `validateCACertPEM()` in `internal/agentcore/enroll.go` — validates `IsCA=true` before saving CA cert (TOFU protection).
  - Added security headers (`X-Content-Type-Options`, `X-Frame-Options`) to `RedirectToHTTPS` handler.
  - Refactored health route (`web/console/app/api/health/route.ts`) to use shared `backendBaseURLs()` from `lib/backend.ts`.
  - Isolated CA private key: created `labtether-ca` Docker volume, hub copies `ca.crt` to `/ca/`, agent/console mount `/ca:ro`.
  - Added `TLSSkipVerify` startup warning log in `internal/agentcore/run.go`.
  - Added ADR-022: Unauthenticated TLS Endpoints in `docs/ADR.md`.
- **MinVersion enforcement**: Added `MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS12` to all `tls.Config` instances across codebase (homeassistant/stub.go, webservice_collector.go, synthetic_runner.go, enroll_test.go).
- **Dev-mode TLS enabled by default**:
  - Changed Makefile + `scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh` TLS default from `disabled` to `auto`.
  - Updated `scripts/dev-frontend-bg.sh`: auto-detects hub certs, serves HTTPS on :3000 via `--experimental-https`, sets `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` and `LABTETHER_API_BASE_URL=https://localhost:8443`.
- **Final E2E audit fixes** (4 findings):
  - Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_HUB_API_PORT=8443` in `dev-frontend-bg.sh` and `docker-compose.yml` — WebSocket URLs now target backend port 8443 instead of frontend port 3000.
  - Removed `web/console/.env.local` from git tracking, added `.env*.local` to `.gitignore`.
  - Refactored health route to use shared backend URL infrastructure.
  - Documented cross-origin cert trust requirement in `ws.ts` JSDoc and dev-frontend-bg.sh startup output.
- **Test fixes**: Updated enrollment tests with `testCACertPEM(t)` helper generating real ECDSA CA certs (old fake PEM strings failed new validation).
- **Validation**: All Go tests pass, TypeScript types pass, 3 E2E security audits passed (0 critical/high remaining).

## 2026-03-01 (TLS/HTTPS enforcement: full implementation)
- Designed and implemented HTTPS/WSS enforcement across the entire LabTether stack.
- **Design**: Built-in Go CA with auto-generated ECDSA P-256 certs, TOFU agent trust, 3 TLS modes (auto/external/disabled).
- **New packages**:
  - `internal/certmgr/` — CA generation (10yr), server cert generation (1yr), provisioning orchestrator with 30-day auto-renewal, SAN collection.
  - `internal/servicehttp/` additions — `SecurityHeaders` middleware (HSTS, nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY), `RedirectToHTTPS` handler with `/healthz` exception.
- **Hub changes** (`cmd/labtether/`):
  - `startup_bootstrap.go` — 3-mode TLS resolution, `LABTETHER_TLS_MODE` env var, auto-provisions certs via certmgr.
  - `tls_handlers.go` — `GET /api/v1/ca.crt` (PEM download), `GET /api/v1/tls/info` (JSON status with fingerprint).
  - `enrollment_handlers.go` — CA cert PEM distributed in enrollment response for TOFU.
  - `http_handlers.go` — registered new TLS routes.
- **Agent changes** (`internal/agentcore/`):
  - `enroll.go` — saves CA cert from enrollment response, skip-verify for enrollment-only bootstrap.
  - `config.go` — auto-loads saved `ca.crt` on startup if no explicit CA configured.
- **Config changes**:
  - `docker-compose.yml` — HTTPS by default (port 8443), auto TLS mode, persistent cert volume, agent/console use wss/https.
  - `Makefile` + `scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh` — dev mode defaults to TLS disabled.
- **Audit fixes** (second pass):
  - Docker Compose: agent uses `LABTETHER_TLS_CA_FILE` via shared volume instead of `LABTETHER_TLS_SKIP_VERIFY=true`.
  - Docker Compose: console uses `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` instead of `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0`.
  - Removed `KeyUsageKeyEncipherment` from ECDSA server cert (only `DigitalSignature` needed for ECDH).
  - Added server cert corruption detection in provisioner (server.crt without server.key).
  - Added `CertReloader` with background renewal loop (24h check interval) + `tls.Config.GetCertificate` callback for hot-swap cert renewal without restart.
  - Added TLS Operations docs: TOFU security model, CA rotation procedure, troubleshooting guide.
  - Added TLS Status section to console settings page (status badge, cert type, CA fingerprint, expiry, download button).
- **Commits**: 20 commits (0b8630d through 2963a43), 12 implementation tasks + review fixes + 8 audit fixes.
- **Tests**: All Go tests pass (13 certmgr tests including reloader tests), all TypeScript types pass.

## 2026-03-01 (iOS console: memory leak audit and fixes)
- Deep audit of all 29+ Swift files in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/` for memory leaks.
- Root causes identified and fixed:
  - `APIClient` (actor) created `URLSession` in init but never invalidated it. Added `deinit` calling `session.invalidateAndCancel()`.
  - `EventSocket.connect()` created a local `URLSession` variable (orphaned on each reconnect). Stored as `webSocketSession` instance property with proper invalidation on disconnect and before reconnect.
  - `EventSocket.listenForMessages()` used recursive closure pattern that grew the async call stack unboundedly. Replaced with `while` loop inside a cancellable `Task`.
  - `EventSocket.scheduleReconnect()` overwrote `reconnectTask` without cancelling the old one. Added cancel before reassignment.
  - `MoreView` created its own `APIClient` instance. Now accepts shared client from `MainTabView`.
  - `DiagnosticsViewModel` created its own `APIClient`. Now accepts shared client passed through from `MoreView`.
- Files changed:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/APIClient.swift` — added deinit with session invalidation.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Realtime/EventSocket.swift` — stored URLSession, while-loop listener, task cancellation fixes.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/MainTabView.swift` — exposed shared APIClient, passed to MoreView.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/MoreView.swift` — accepts shared APIClient parameter.
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/DiagnosticsView.swift` — accepts shared APIClient parameter.
- Validation: `xcodebuild -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' build` — BUILD SUCCEEDED, zero errors.

## 2026-02-28 (Terminal workspace: right-click tab management + pane clipboard actions)
- Implemented terminal workspace context-menu productivity pass from `notes/TERMINAL_CONTEXT_MENU_CLIPBOARD_PLAN.md`.
- Terminal tab bar updates in `web/console/app/(console)/terminal/page.tsx`:
  - added right-click tab context menu with `Rename Tab`, `Duplicate Tab`, `New Tab`, `Close Tab`, and `Close Other Tabs`.
  - added inline tab rename editor with save/cancel keyboard handling and trimmed-length validation.
  - added duplication flow that clones layout + pane target assignments.
- Terminal pane interaction updates:
  - extended `XTerminal` imperative handle (`copySelection`, `pasteFromClipboard`, `selectAll`, `clearScrollback`, `hasSelection`) in `web/console/app/components/XTerminal.tsx`.
  - added pane right-click context menu in `web/console/app/components/terminal/TerminalPane.tsx` with copy/paste/select/find/clear and reconnect/disconnect actions.
  - added focused-pane keyboard parity (`Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+C`, `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+V`, `Ctrl/Cmd+A`, existing find shortcut).
- Workspace tab hook enhancement:
  - `createTab` now returns created tab metadata for follow-up operations (`web/console/app/hooks/useWorkspaceTabs.ts`).
- E2E coverage updates:
  - added terminal workspace tests for right-click rename persistence, pane context-menu paste forwarding, and tab duplicate/close-other actions (`web/console/e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts`).
  - hardened staged SSH progress mock timings to reduce flaky early-ready races.
- Audit/debug follow-up:
  - fixed rename cancel edge case where `Escape` could still commit via blur timing race by adding skip-on-cancel guard in `web/console/app/(console)/terminal/page.tsx`.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed (`7 passed`).

## 2026-02-28 (Services root-cause fix: Traefik API discovery fallback when :8080 is not exposed)
- Performed first-principles runtime diagnosis against live API data:
  - `/api/v1/services/web` reported `20` services with `0` proxy-enriched entries (`metadata.proxy_provider` absent on all services).
  - `web_service_overrides` had `0` rows, so persisted URL overrides were not the active blocker.
  - Docker inventory showed Traefik present but without `8080` host mapping (`80/443` only), which prevented current `DetectAndConnect` from finding Traefik API.
  - Verified Traefik API was still reachable via routed dashboard host (`https://dashboard.tail.simbaslabs.com/api/http/routers` returned `200`), confirming route data existed but was discoverable through host-rule routing, not `localhost:8080`.
- Implemented code fix in `internal/agentcore/proxy_traefik.go`:
  - `DetectAndConnect` now falls back to inferring Traefik API base URL from Traefik container labels when `:8080` is not exposed.
  - New fallback path detects `api@internal` router labels, extracts host from Traefik `Host(...)` rule, and derives `http` vs `https` from entrypoint/TLS hints.
- Added regression coverage in `internal/agentcore/proxy_traefik_test.go`:
  - `falls back to dashboard host rule when API port not exposed`
  - `dashboard fallback prefers http when tls hints absent`
- Validation:
  - targeted: `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestTraefikDetectAndConnect|TestTraefikFetchRoutes|TestExtractHostsFromRule|TestIsTraefikInternal' -count=1` passed.
  - full: `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, and `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` all passed.

## 2026-02-28 (Services UI proxy-first preference: preserve reverse-proxy URLs over direct IP overrides)
- Updated Services UI override-save behavior to avoid unintentionally pinning discovered service URLs to direct IP/port values:
  - Added `listServiceOverrides` in `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts` so edit actions can preserve existing override fields without auto-creating new URL overrides.
  - Updated rename/hide actions in `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx` to retain only pre-existing `url_override`/`icon_key_override` values instead of always writing current `service.url`.
  - This keeps Traefik/Caddy/Nginx-proxied URLs free to update from discovery rather than being frozen by incidental UI actions.
- Improved proxy visibility and preference in Services cards:
  - Normalized proxy provider labels to operator-friendly names (`Traefik`, `Caddy`, `Nginx`).
  - Added per-category service sorting to prioritize proxy-enriched services before direct IP/non-proxy entries.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-28 (Functionality audit completion: LT-OPS-01 compose deploy + smoke)
- Closed the last unstarted audit workflow (`LT-OPS-01`) with compose-backed smoke verification and deployment hardening.
- Initial ops audit blockers discovered:
  - `make compose-up` failed local precheck because port `3000` was occupied by active non-Docker processes.
  - compose startup required `LABTETHER_ADMIN_PASSWORD` in env.
  - subsequent isolated compose runs exposed two real deployment defects:
    - `web-console` image build failure (`bash scripts/patch-novnc.sh` assumptions incompatible with container context),
    - `labtether` false-unhealthy service status because compose healthcheck called `wget` in a distroless image without that binary.
- Fixes implemented:
  - `web/console` install/build portability:
    - switched `web/console/package.json` `postinstall` to `sh scripts/patch-novnc.sh`,
    - rewrote `web/console/scripts/patch-novnc.sh` to be POSIX-compatible and package-root relative (no monorepo-only path assumptions),
    - updated `web/console/Dockerfile` deps stage to copy `scripts/` before `npm ci`.
  - hub container healthcheck reliability:
    - updated `build/go-service.Dockerfile` to copy busybox probe binary into distroless runtime image,
    - updated `docker-compose.yml` healthcheck to `/busybox wget ... /healthz`.
- Validation:
  - smoke against already-running local stack (no compose): `./scripts/smoke-test.sh --skip-compose --no-build` -> `103 passed`, `0 failed`.
  - full isolated compose-backed smoke (alternate host ports + isolated compose project/env):
    - `COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=labtether_audit ... ./scripts/smoke-test.sh --timeout 240` -> `103 passed`, `0 failed`.
  - `sh web/console/scripts/patch-novnc.sh` executed successfully.

## 2026-02-28 (Services discovery follow-up: listening-port augmented scan coverage)
- Addressed operator feedback that Services discovery still felt unchanged by strengthening first-principles host discovery.
- Updated `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`:
  - `scanCandidatePorts()` now augments candidate ports from local listening TCP sockets (`/proc/net/tcp`, `/proc/net/tcp6`) instead of fixed defaults only.
  - added `LABTETHER_WEBSVC_PORTSCAN_INCLUDE_LISTENING=false` opt-out flag for listening-port augmentation.
  - added listening-candidate cap (`maxListeningScanPorts`) to bound scan cost.
  - improved open-port prioritization so known-service and likely-web ports are processed before unknown/non-web ports, reducing noisy unknown saturation against `maxUnknownScannedServices`.
  - added Linux proc parser helper `parseProcNetListeningPorts(...)` and supporting helpers.
- Added regression coverage in `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`:
  - `TestParseProcNetListeningPorts`,
  - `TestScanCandidatePortsCanDisableListeningAugment`.
- Validation:
  - targeted collector suite passed (`go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestParseProcNetListeningPorts|TestScanCandidatePortsCanDisableListeningAugment|TestDiscoverPortScannedServices|TestHealthCheckSwitchesToHTTPS|TestHealthCheckSwitchesToHTTP|Test.*WebService.*|TestParsePortList|TestExtractHostPortForService|TestAlternateSchemeURL' -count=1`).
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-28 (Terminal connect latency root-cause fix: blocking tmux probe removed)
- Investigated slow terminal connect behavior after stream-stability fixes.
- Root cause confirmed from live backend logs:
  - repeated `terminal: tmux probe timed out for containervm-deltaserver` immediately before each `agent session started`,
  - connect path was synchronously waiting on `probeAgentTmux(...)` timeout before starting shell.
- Fix implemented in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_handlers.go`:
  - changed tmux probe flow from blocking wait to cached, non-blocking async probe,
  - added per-agent connection metadata cache keys:
    - `terminal.tmux.has`
    - `terminal.tmux.path`
    - `terminal.tmux.probe_pending`
  - `processAgentTerminalProbed(...)` now writes probe results back to connection metadata cache.
- Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_lifecycle_test.go`:
  - `TestProbeAgentTmuxUsesCachedConnectionMetadata`
  - `TestProcessAgentTerminalProbedCachesProbeResultOnConnection`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestProbeAgentTmuxUsesCachedConnectionMetadata|TestProcessAgentTerminalProbedCachesProbeResultOnConnection|TestBridgeAgentInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd|Test.*Terminal.*' -count=1` passed.
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether` passed.
  - live timing probe (`/tmp/terminal_connect_timing.go`) after backend restart:
    - `create=5.893708ms ticket=556.625µs ws-dial=2.002917ms first-bytes=6.016167ms total=14.469417ms`.

## 2026-02-28 (Terminal SSH blank-cursor regression: websocket panic fix)
- Investigated operator-reported terminal behavior where SSH/agent terminal opened with a blinking cursor but no usable shell output.
- Root cause identified from live backend logs:
  - repeated panic in `bridgeAgentInput` (`repeated read on failed websocket connection`) at `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_handlers.go:318`,
  - caused by 1s read-deadline polling loop on Gorilla websocket; timeout marked read state failed and subsequent reads panicked.
- Fix implemented:
  - rewired `bridgeAgentInput` to use blocking reads,
  - added closed-session watcher goroutine that force-unblocks reads by closing the websocket when `closedCh` closes,
  - added panic guard log in input bridge for defensive hardening.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `TestBridgeAgentInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd` in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_lifecycle_test.go` (covers prior idle-timeout panic path and close behavior).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestBridgeAgentInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd|Test.*Terminal.*' -count=1` passed.
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether` passed.
  - live probe against running backend:
    - opened terminal websocket, idled >3s (previous panic window), sent `echo __LT_TERM_OK__`, and received marker output successfully,
    - backend logs show terminal session start with no websocket panic.

## 2026-02-28 (Terminal multipane create-tab runtime error: root-cause + hardening)
- Investigated runtime error surfaced in terminal multipane flow: `failed to create workspace tab`.
- Root cause confirmed: local DB schema lagged behind terminal uplift migrations.
  - Diagnostic check showed `schema_migrations` max version was `32` while terminal workspace tables are introduced at migration `34`.
  - `terminal_workspace_tabs` relation was missing, causing backend insert failure in `createWorkspaceTab`.
- Applied migration fix:
  - ran `make db-migrate` successfully,
  - re-verified schema state (`max_migration=36`) and successful insert into `terminal_workspace_tabs`.
- Added resilience and UX hardening:
  - `cmd/labtether/terminal_workspace_handlers.go` now detects schema drift SQL states (`42P01`, `42703`) and returns actionable message:
    - `terminal workspace schema is outdated; run db migrations`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/terminal/page.tsx` now catches create/delete tab promise rejections and shows inline error banner instead of triggering unhandled runtime overlay.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWorkspaceTabs.ts` now parses API error payloads more defensively (`readAPIError`) so create/delete failures show clearer messages.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./cmd/labtether` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*WorkspaceTab|Test.*Terminal.*' -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed (`2/2`).

## 2026-02-28 (Services Dashboard Phase 3d: first-principles discovery hardening)

- **Hub/agent sync path**:

    - scans candidate web ports, skips ports already represented by Docker/proxy-discovered services, emits `source=scan` services, and keeps unknown scan results bounded.

## 2026-02-28 (Terminal feature audit + multipane stability fixes)
- Performed a step-by-step audit pass over terminal workspace features (layout/panes, target selection, snippets, keyboard toolbar, reconnect/session behavior, and settings hydration) with targeted frontend validation.
- Fixed high-impact multipane/state consistency regressions:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useWorkspaceTabs.ts`:
    - debounced workspace-tab updates now merge pending patches per tab instead of dropping earlier updates when a second change lands within debounce window;
    - tab delete now clears pending timers/patches;
    - newly created tab is now activated after creation/fetch.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/terminal/page.tsx`:
    - clamped focused pane index on tab/layout changes to prevent focus targeting a non-existent pane after switching layouts.
- Fixed terminal input/control correctness issues:
  - `web/console/app/components/terminal/KeyboardToolbar.tsx` now sends key sequences to remote via `sendData(...)` instead of local-only `write(...)`.
  - `web/console/app/components/XTerminal.tsx` `sendData(...)` now also triggers `onDataCapture` so broadcast mode can mirror programmatic sends (snippets/toolbar) consistently.
- Fixed terminal settings persistence/hydration mismatch:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useTerminalPreferences.ts` now supports proxied payload envelope shape `{ preferences: ... }` (and flat fallback), so saved preferences load/apply correctly instead of silently reverting to defaults.
- Fixed previously dead terminal auto-reconnect preference wiring:
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts` now supports terminal-session auto-reconnect (non-clean disconnect, bounded backoff attempts) when enabled;
  - wired through from both terminal surfaces:
    - `web/console/app/components/terminal/TerminalPane.tsx`
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/TerminalTab.tsx`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/resilience.spec.ts -g 'terminal reconnect reuses prior session id after abnormal disconnect' --project=chromium` passed (1/1).
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed (1/1).
  - Added dedicated terminal workspace e2e coverage in `web/console/e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts`:
    - multipane layout switching updates pane count and persists across reload;
    - broadcast mode mirrors snippet-driven input across multiple active panes.
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed (2/2).

## 2026-02-28 (Services Dashboard Phase 3c: protocol deduction + smarter Docker port selection)
- Addressed operator-reported service discovery gaps where URLs were misclassified (`http` vs `https`) and containers with mixed port mappings selected the wrong endpoint.
- Updated `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`:
  - Added active dual-scheme health probing (`http` + `https`) with response scoring and HTTPS-preferred tie-breaking.
  - Health checks now auto-correct `svc.URL` to the working scheme when the initial guess is wrong.
  - Added `alternateSchemeURL` helper and shared probe path used by both normal checks and `raw_url` fallback checks.
  - Replaced naive first-port selection with `extractHostPortForService` web-first heuristics:
    - prefer known mapped web default ports,
    - then likely web public/private ports,
    - then TCP fallback.
  - Tightened raw host-port fallback to TCP mappings only.
  - Expanded initial HTTPS guess ports (`8006`, `8007`, `7443` in addition to prior ports) while keeping runtime probe correction authoritative.
- Expanded tests in `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`:
  - port selection heuristics (`extractHostPortForService`),
  - scheme swap helper (`alternateSchemeURL`),
  - runtime health-check scheme correction (`http -> https`, `https -> http`),
  - updated URL/port expectations and TCP/UDP filtering behavior.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-28 (Premium Remote View Phase 3: WebRTC Streaming Pipeline)
- Implemented the Phase 3 WebRTC pipeline across agent, hub signaling, and frontend remote-view surfaces with transparent VNC fallback.
- **Validation**:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
  - `make build-agent-linux` passed (built `build/labtether-agent-linux-amd64` and `build/labtether-agent-linux-arm64`).
- **Agent protocol + capability surface**:
  - Added WebRTC message types/payloads (`webrtc.capabilities`, `webrtc.start`, `webrtc.offer/answer`, `webrtc.ice`, `webrtc.input`, `webrtc.stop`, lifecycle events) in `internal/agentmgr/message.go`.
  - Extended heartbeat capabilities and agent-connection metadata plumbing so hub routing can detect per-agent WebRTC readiness.
- **Agent WebRTC runtime**:
  - Added Linux-first GStreamer capability detection and encoder/source selection (`internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect.go`).
  - Added WebRTC settings/runtime wiring for STUN/TURN and capture controls.
  - Added WebRTC session manager (`internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler.go`) using Pion peer connections plus `gst-launch-1.0` RTP subprocesses, ICE/SDP handling, DataChannel input handling, and lifecycle teardown.
  - Wired WebRTC manager into agent receive loop and startup capability advertisement.
- **Hub signaling and stream routing**:
  - Added WebRTC signaling bridge handlers (`cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go`) and router integration in `agent_ws_handler.go`.
  - Extended desktop protocol normalization and stream cascade to prefer WebRTC when requested and available, else fallback to VNC.
  - Persisted/derived `webrtc_available` metadata from heartbeat and capability updates for protocol selection logic.
- **Frontend integration**:
  - Added browser signaling hook (`useWebRTCSignaling`) and `WebRTCViewer` component with offer/answer/ICE flow, remote media rendering, and input channel support.
  - Integrated WebRTC into session panel, desktop tab protocol picker/transport badge, and remote-view toolbar audio controls.
  - Added protocol support updates in session and desktop session API layers.
- **Runtime packaging/hardening**:
  - Added required runtime tools to agent Docker image (`gstreamer` plugin set + `xdotool`).
  - Added `gst-launch-1.0`, `gst-inspect-1.0`, and `xdotool` to security runtime allowlist.

## 2026-02-28 (Premium Remote View Phase 1: UI Polish)
- **9 features shipped** transforming the VNC remote view from basic embedded viewer to premium remote desktop experience.
- **Validation**: `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, `tsc --noEmit` — all clean.
- **Floating overlay toolbar** (`RemoteViewToolbar.tsx`): Translucent dark overlay with backdrop blur, auto-hides after 3s, reveals on mouse near top edge (40px hot zone), pinnable via grab handle. Contains latency indicator (color-coded: green/yellow/red), quality selector (Low/Med/High segmented toggle), scaling mode selector (Fit/1:1/Fill), pointer lock toggle, view-only toggle, Ctrl+Alt+Del, fullscreen toggle, disconnect button.
- **Fullscreen mode** (`useFullscreen.ts`): Browser Fullscreen API wrapper hook. Toggle from toolbar, ESC exits. CSS strips border-radius/border in fullscreen state.
- **Three scaling modes** in VNCViewer: Fit (scaleViewport=true), Native/1:1 (scrollable, clipViewport+dragViewport), Fill+Resize (scaleViewport+resizeSession). Applied live via useEffect without reconnecting.
- **Auto-reconnect** in useSession: On non-clean desktop disconnect, exponential backoff retry (2s→4s→8s, max 3 attempts). Reconnect counter resets on success. Manual disconnect cancels pending retries.
- **Connection latency HUD** (`useLatency.ts`): Pings `/api/health` every 5s, measures RTT via performance.now(). Health proxy route (`/api/health/route.ts`) forwards to hub.
- **Pointer lock** (relative mouse): requestPointerLock/exitPointerLock methods on VNCViewer handle. Toggle from toolbar, ESC releases. Pointer lock state tracked via document event listener.
- **View-only mode**: rfb.viewOnly toggled live via useEffect. Toggle from toolbar with eye icon indicator.
- **Xvfb headless fallback** (`desktop_vnc.go`): When x11vnc fails with display error on Linux/FreeBSD, auto-launches `Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1920x1080x24` and retries. Covers headless servers, Raspberry Pis, VMs without virtual displays.
- **Persistent session context** (`DesktopSessionContext.tsx`): Tracks active desktop session across navigation. Sidebar shows active session indicator (Monitor icon + node name + pulsing green dot) linking back to the node.
- **DesktopTab rewritten**: External SessionToolbar replaced with pre-connection Card (quality + connect) and in-viewer RemoteViewToolbar overlay. SessionPanel passes through scalingMode and viewOnly to VNCViewer.
- Design: `docs/plans/2026-02-28-premium-remote-view-design.md`. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-02-28-premium-remote-view-phase1.md`.

## 2026-02-28 (Top 12 missing features implemented across all layers)
- **Feature audit** across 5 domains (Hub API, Frontend, Agent, Internal packages, Infrastructure) identified 164 missing features (37 High, 65 Medium, 62 Low). Top 15 selected for implementation, 12 shipped (1 deferred to TODO).
- **Validation**: `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, `tsc --noEmit` — all clean.
- **Task 1 — Retention expansion**: Extended pruning to alert_instances (resolved only), alert_evaluations, notification_history, alert_silences (expired only). Four new Settings fields with configurable windows (30-90 days), updated all three presets (Conservative/Balanced/Aggressive).
- **Task 2 — Composite alert evaluation**: Implemented AND/OR logic over sub-rule firing state. Extracts `rule_ids` and `operator` from composite rule condition, checks firing instances per sub-rule, applies boolean logic with short-circuit evaluation.
- **Task 3 — Pagination offset**: Added `parseOffset` helper, `Offset int` field to all filter structs (IncidentFilter, AlertRuleFilter, AlertInstanceFilter, AuditEventFilter), `OFFSET $N` clauses to all SQL queries, and `offset` parameter to `ListAssets` and `ListActionRuns` interfaces. ~25 files updated.
- **Task 4 — WS/health/CI**: WebSocket read limit (32MB) on agent connections, deep `/healthz` with DB pool ping (503 on failure), HTTP server timeouts (Read=30s, Write=60s, Idle=120s), CI Go version bumped 1.22→1.24.
- **Task 5 — Dashboard sparklines**: `useSparklineHistory` hook accumulates rolling 30-point window from fleet poll data. KPI cards (devices online, CPU/mem/disk avg, issues) now show mini Sparkline below each value. Added sparkline CSS classes.
- **Task 7 — DB backup**: `scripts/db-backup.sh` with pg_dump+gzip, 7-day rotation, env var config. `make db-backup` Makefile target.
- **Task 8 — Alert inbox sort**: Client-side sort by time or severity with direction toggle. Severity ordering: critical→high→medium→low. Sort controls added to filter bar.
- **Task 9 — Agent file search**: `file.search`/`file.search_result` message types, `FileSearchData`/`FileSearchResultData` payloads. `handleFileSearch` with `filepath.WalkDir`, glob matching, 10s timeout, max 500 results, `.git`/`node_modules`/`.cache` exclusion.
- **Task 10 — Container registry release**: GitHub Actions workflow triggered on `v*` tags, builds hub + web-console images for linux/amd64+arm64, publishes to GHCR with version + latest tags.
- **Task 11 — Notification retry**: `RetryCount`/`MaxRetries`/`NextRetryAt` fields on notification records, exponential backoff (30s→60s→120s, cap 10min), migration v33, `ListPendingRetries`/`UpdateRetryState` store methods, 30s retry loop.
- **Task 12 — Process kill/signal**: `process.kill`/`process.kill_result` message types, `ProcessKillData`/`ProcessKillResultData` payloads. Platform-specific: Unix `syscall.Kill` with SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGINT/SIGHUP mapping, Windows `os.FindProcess`+`Kill()`. PID ≤ 1 rejection guard.
- **Task 13 — Makefile targets**: `make check` (go vet + go test + tsc), `make build` (go build to build/labtether).
- Plan: `docs/plans/2026-02-28-top-features-plan.md`.

## 2026-02-28 (Comprehensive codebase audit: 141 issues fixed across all layers)
- **Full-stack audit** across 5 domains (Hub API, Internal packages, Agent core, Frontend, Infrastructure) identified 141 issues (14 Critical, 31 High, 45 Medium, 41 Low).
- **All 141 issues resolved** across 10 parallel task groups. 66 files changed, 871 insertions, 368 deletions.
- **Validation**: `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, `tsc --noEmit` — all clean.
- **Task 1 — WebSocket concurrency** (Critical):
  - `sync.Once` for VNC done-channel close, write mutex for browser-bound goroutine writes.
  - Write deadlines (10s) on both sides of Proxmox VNC bridge.
  - Write deadline in agent WebSocket `Send()` to prevent indefinite blocking under mutex.
- **Task 2 — Agent security hardening** (Critical/High):
  - `io.LimitReader` (100MB) on self-update downloads; streaming SHA256 hashing.
  - Container ID regex validation on all Docker API calls and exec start.
  - Session limits: max 10 terminal sessions, max 10 desktop VNC sessions.
  - Netplan backup path validation (must be inside os.TempDir).
- **Task 3 — Database performance** (Critical/High):
  - N+1 → batch query for alert rule targets (`WHERE rule_id = ANY($1::text[])`).
  - Telemetry series: 6 queries → 1 (`WHERE metric = ANY($2::text[])`).
  - CTE cycle detection with visited arrays in dependency graph traversal.
  - `SELECT FOR UPDATE` transaction isolation for alert rule updates.
- **Task 4 — Hub API hardening** (High/Medium):
  - Authentication added to `/api/v1/discover` endpoint.
  - Audit logging before admin reset operations.
  - `sanitizeUpstreamError()` strips URLs/IPs from all 502/503 responses.
  - Cache size limits (200 runtime, 1000 TrueNAS) on all in-memory maps.
  - Rate limit pruning throttled to once per minute.
- **Task 5 — Internal packages** (High/Medium):
  - `sync.RWMutex` on job worker handler registration (race condition fix).
  - `PurgeOldJobs()` for dead letter queue cleanup.
  - `json.Marshal` error handling in Docker compose actions (was silently swallowed).
  - Shell command blocklist expanded: token-level matching for shutdown/reboot/halt/poweroff/init + systemctl variants.
  - 12 new security runtime test cases.
- **Task 6 — Frontend critical/high** (Critical/High):
  - XTerminal callback refs to prevent effect teardown/reconnect cycles.
  - Error boundaries at console and root levels (`error.tsx`).
  - StatusContext: `siteFilterRef` pattern to prevent WebSocket reconnection on filter changes; stale closure fix.
  - ThemeContext: `useMemo` on provider value.
  - AbortController added to 6 fetch hooks (telemetry, logs, incidents, node telemetry, node logs, node actions).
- **Task 7 — Frontend medium**:
  - Toast ID generator: module-level `let` → `useRef` (SSR safety).
  - Node detail: non-null assertion → `NonNullable<typeof asset>`.
  - Login page: `javascript:` and `data:` protocol blocking on redirect URL.
  - Settings form: `useMemo` for runtimeSettingsByScope computation.
  - CommandPalette: ARIA roles (`dialog`, `listbox`, `option`).
- **Task 8 — Frontend low**:
  - `"use client"` directive added to useEnrollment hook.
  - Empty string defaults for update plan form fields.
  - Live tail polling paused when tab hidden (`document.visibilityState`).
  - `useCallback` wrapping for submitConnectorAction.
- **Task 9 — Infrastructure**:
  - Dockerfiles updated to Go 1.24; agent runs as non-root user.
  - Docker Compose: health checks with `service_healthy` depends_on, resource limits.
  - Makefile: removed hardcoded encryption key fallback.
  - `.dockerignore` added for web console.
- **Task 10 — Agent medium/low**:
  - `AssetID()` accessor for WS transport encapsulation.
  - Parent context threading in Docker logs handler.
  - Log handler uses transport accessor instead of direct field access.
- Plan: `docs/plans/2026-02-28-codebase-audit-fixes-plan.md`.

## 2026-02-28 (Services Dashboard Phase 3b: reverse proxy bug fixes and gap closures)
- **Senior-level audit** identified 9 bugs and 5 feature gaps across the reverse proxy implementation.
- **Bug fixes** (all validated: `go test`, `go vet`, `tsc --noEmit` clean):
  - **Bug 1**: Removed `extractTraefikURL` pre-enrichment in `buildServicesFromContainers` that was corrupting `raw_url` — Traefik labels are now handled exclusively by the proxy enrichment pipeline.
  - **Bug 2**: Removed `len(containers) > 0` gate on proxy enrichment — proxy providers now run even when Docker is unavailable, enabling proxy-only service discovery.
  - **Bug 3**: Added `raw_url = backend_url` to `buildProxyOnlyService` so `healthCheckWithFallback` can fall back to backend URL on DNS failure.
  - **Bug 4**: Added `webServiceCoordinator.MarkHostDisconnected()` call to agent disconnect handler + periodic `CleanExpired` loop (60s interval) — stale services now properly expire after agent disconnect.
  - **Bug 5**: Fixed Caddy/NPM `DetectAndConnect` early return (`return "", false` → `continue`) — now correctly scans all containers when first match lacks admin port.
  - **Bug 6**: Updated `extractHostFromRule` → `extractHostsFromRule` using `FindAllStringSubmatch` — Traefik multi-host OR rules (`Host(a) || Host(b)`) now produce separate routes for each domain.
  - **Bug 7**: Added deterministic `sort.Slice` to `ListAll()` (by HostAssetID + ID) — eliminates UI card reshuffling on 30s polls.
  - **Bug 8**: Added `ambiguousPorts` tracking to `enrichServicesWithRoutes` — Strategy 2 fallback now skipped for ports shared by multiple services.
  - **Bug 9**: Added `backend_url` and `router_name` fields to frontend expanded detail panel.
- **Feature gaps closed**:
  - **Manual proxy URLs**: Added `LABTETHER_TRAEFIK_URL`, `LABTETHER_CADDY_URL`, `LABTETHER_NPM_URL` env vars to skip container detection (non-Docker deployments).
  - **Traefik basic auth**: Added `LABTETHER_TRAEFIK_USER`/`LABTETHER_TRAEFIK_PASSWORD` env vars with `traefikGet` helper for authenticated API access.
  - **Source filter**: Added Source dropdown (All/Docker/Proxy) to services page filter bar.
  - **NPM token caching**: Added `getToken` method that caches JWT for 1 hour, avoiding re-authentication every 60s cycle.
- Files modified: `webservice_collector.go`, `proxy_provider.go`, `proxy_traefik.go`, `proxy_caddy.go`, `proxy_npm.go`, `coordinator.go`, `agent_ws_handler.go`, `webservice_api.go`, `startup_background.go`, `proxy_traefik_test.go`, `services/page.tsx`.

## 2026-02-28 (Services Dashboard Phase 3: reverse proxy service discovery)
- Designed and implemented reverse proxy provider integration for the services dashboard.
- **ProxyProvider interface** (`proxy_provider.go`): `ProxyRoute` type, `ProxyProvider` interface with `DetectAndConnect`/`FetchRoutes`, `enrichServicesWithRoutes` with container port bridging (PrivatePort→ContainerID→service matching), double-enrichment guard, multi-domain support (`alt_urls`), proxy-only service creation via registry lookup.
- **Traefik provider** (`proxy_traefik.go`): Zero-config detection (image `traefik`, port 8080), array-based API parsing (`/api/http/routers` + `/api/http/services`), `@internal` router filtering, `websecure` entrypoint TLS detection, `per_page=1000` pagination.
- **Caddy provider** (`proxy_caddy.go`): Detects `caddy` and `lucaslorentz/caddy-docker-proxy` images, admin API port 2019, full JSON config parsing (`/config/`), TLS via listen port suffix matching, `reverse_proxy` handler extraction.
- **NPM provider** (`proxy_npm.go`): JWT authentication (`LABTETHER_NPM_EMAIL`/`LABTETHER_NPM_PASSWORD` env vars), `POST /api/tokens` → `GET /api/nginx/proxy-hosts`, disabled host filtering, multi-domain support per proxy host.
- **WebServiceCollector integration** (`webservice_collector.go`): Pipeline rewritten to Docker discovery → proxy enrichment → health checks → send. `enrichFromProxies` runs all providers concurrently. `healthCheckWithFallback` retries with `raw_url` when proxied URL DNS fails. Provider registration gated by `LABTETHER_PROXY_DISABLED` env var.
- **Frontend** (`services/page.tsx`): Proxy provider badge in card header, expanded detail panel shows proxy provider, direct URL, and alt URLs.
- **Tests**: 30+ tests across proxy_provider, proxy_traefik, proxy_caddy, proxy_npm — all passing.
- **Validation**: `go test ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `tsc --noEmit` — all clean.
- Design: `docs/plans/2026-02-28-reverse-proxy-discovery-design.md`. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-02-28-reverse-proxy-discovery-plan.md`.

## 2026-02-28 (devices page card click reliability fix)
- Root cause found in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/DeviceCard.tsx`:
  - the click guard considered `[role='link']` interactive descendants, but each card container itself is `role="link"`.
  - result: most clicks were treated as "interactive child clicks" and card navigation was skipped, creating a multi-click/unreliable feel.
- Fix implemented:
  - updated `isInteractiveDescendant(...)` to compare against the current card container and ignore self-matches.
  - updated click/keyboard handlers to pass `event.currentTarget` into the guard, so only nested controls (like the footer Docker link) suppress card navigation.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (linux agent remote desktop/vnc root-cause fix)
- Root cause identified for Linux agent Remote View failures:
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc.go` used `x11vnc` flags `-quality` / `-compress`, which are not portable/supported across `x11vnc` builds and can cause immediate process exit.
  - Linux systemd agent service templates used `ProtectHome=true`, blocking read access to common X11 auth paths (`/home`, `/run/user`) needed for desktop session attachment.
- Implemented fixes:
  - refactored Linux x11vnc arg construction into `buildX11VNCArgs(...)`.
  - switched quality mapping to valid `x11vnc -speeds` presets (`low=modem`, `medium/default=dsl`, `high=lan`).
  - added `-auth guess` for X authority discovery in root/systemd contexts.
  - added regression tests in `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc_test.go` to lock argument behavior and prevent unsupported-flag regressions.
  - updated Linux systemd service config from `ProtectHome=true` to `ProtectHome=read-only` in:
    - installer-generated service (`cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`)
    - deploy template (`deploy/agents/systemd/labtether-agent.service`)
  - extended installer script structure test coverage (`cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`) for `ProtectHome=read-only`.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run TestBuildX11VNCArgs -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS' -count=1`
  - `go vet ./...`
  - `go test ./...`
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit`

## 2026-02-27 (Living Lab UI redesign: complete visual overhaul)
- Executed 21-task implementation plan across 6 phases for Living Lab UI redesign.
- Phase 1 (Foundation): Installed framer-motion + Radix UI deps, updated globals.css with full design token system.
- Phase 2 (Component Library): Redesigned Card (glass-morphism), Badge (status glow), Button (accent), Input (accent focus), Modal (Radix Dialog + Framer Motion). Created EmptyState, Skeleton, DataValue components.
- Phase 3 (Sidebar + Ambient): Redesigned Sidebar (16px icons, accent active state, brand mark glow, health dot). Added ambient glow layer to layout.
- Phase 4 (Dashboard): Created NarrativeSummary, TopologyHero, ResourceRanking. Rewrote Dashboard from 800 to 256 lines — removed triage system, added topology visualization + narrative briefing + resource hotspots.
- Phase 5 (All Pages): Applied glass-card styling, EmptyState, Skeleton loading, and DataValue across Devices, Node Detail, Alerts, Sites, Topology, Files, Actions, Incidents, Logs, Reliability, Updates, Settings.
- Phase 6 (Animation): Added Framer Motion page transitions with AnimatePresence, added prefers-reduced-motion support.
- Design philosophy: "SpaceX mission control meets Bloomberg terminal" — narrative over numbers, anomaly-first display, topology hero, the lab feels alive.
- Updated `notes/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` with complete Living Lab design spec: color tokens, animation tokens, component redesigns, layout architecture, page designs, and implementation notes.
- Branch: feature/living-lab-ui

## 2026-02-27 (docker container terminal keystrokes ignored)
- Fixed Docker container terminal stdin not working from the web UI (`keys do not register` symptom).
- Root cause:
  - agent Docker exec stream wrapper (`internal/agentcore/docker_exec.go`) accepted writes but discarded bytes in `execConn.Write(...)`.
  - this made container terminals appear connected/output-capable while silently dropping keyboard input.
- Additional compatibility gap addressed:
  - some daemon/socket combinations expose a readable exec response stream via net/http without a writable upgraded body.
  - added unix-socket raw hijack fallback to guarantee bidirectional I/O when the HTTP response body does not implement `io.Writer`.
- Additional reliability/diagnostics hardening:
  - docker exec sessions now track close reasons (stdin write failure, stream read failure, closed-by-hub) and return those reasons over `docker.exec.closed`.
  - hub docker exec bridge now preserves and surfaces close reasons to the browser instead of generic “closed unexpectedly”.
  - implemented docker exec PTY resize handling on agent (`POST /exec/{id}/resize`).
  - switched hub default container shell command from `"/bin/sh"` to `"sh"` for broader PATH-based compatibility across images.
  - terminal UI now auto-focuses xterm on open/connect and on container click to avoid no-focus key-drop UX.
- Follow-up root cause fix for daemon error `exec command ... is already running`:
  - root cause: unix-socket startup path could attempt mixed fallback startup (`HTTP start` then `raw unix hijack`) on the same exec ID.
  - fix: `startAndHijack(...)` now selects a single startup strategy per session; unix socket endpoints use raw hijack directly (no second start attempt).
  - added regression test `TestStartAndHijackUnixPrefersRawPathWithoutHTTPFallback`.
- Fix:
  - updated exec stream startup to require a writable upgraded stream (`resp.Body` implementing `io.Writer`) and return an explicit error if Docker does not expose writable stdin.
  - updated `execConn` to forward writes to the underlying upgraded stream writer instead of returning success for dropped input.
  - added raw unix-socket upgrade fallback path (`startAndHijackRawUnix`) and buffered pre-read handoff wrapper (`hijackedConn`).
  - tracked unix socket endpoint in Docker client (`dockerClient.unixPath`) for fallback dialing.
  - preserved outbound URL policy hardening by using `securityruntime.NewOutboundRequestWithContext` + `securityruntime.DoOutboundRequest`.
- Regression coverage:
  - added `TestExecConnWriteForwardsBytes`.
  - added `TestExecConnWriteFailsWithoutWriter`.
  - added `TestDockerExecInputWritesToSessionConn`.
  - added `TestDockerExecSessionReasonSetOnce`.
  - added hub bridge tests:
    - `TestDockerExecBridgeReasonLifecycle`
    - `TestProcessAgentDockerExecClosedStoresReason`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestDockerExec|TestExecConnWrite' -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestDockerExecBridge|TestProcessAgentDockerExecClosedStoresReason|TestHandleDocker' -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (docker/container tab navigation race fix)
- Fixed an intermittent tab navigation bug on node detail pages where switching tabs (especially `terminal` -> `overview`) could require multiple clicks or snap back unexpectedly.
- Root cause:
  - tab changes optimistically updated local `activeTab` and triggered `router.replace(...)`, but `useSearchParams()` could briefly expose stale query values.
  - a URL->state effect re-applied the stale `tab` value during that transition, overriding the user’s new selection.
- Fix:
  - added a pending URL-sync guard (`pendingTabSyncRef`) in node detail tab logic to ignore stale query-state frames until router transition catches up.
  - tab sync now only accepts URL updates that match the in-flight requested tab parameter, then clears the pending guard.
- File touched:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDocker' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (docker node tab-reset + stacks view correctness)
- Resolved Docker node-detail tab reset behavior where selecting a non-default tab could unexpectedly jump back to `containers` or `overview`.
- Root cause:
  - tab selection state (`activeTab`) was not written back to URL query state, while a separate effect repeatedly re-applied `?tab=...` from URL whenever `availableTabs` changed (status/context refreshes), causing stale query tab values to override in-memory selection.
- Fix:
  - synchronized tab clicks to URL query (`?tab=`) in `handleTabChange(...)`.
  - switched URL tab hydration to `searchParams`-based sync (removed direct `window.location` parsing).
  - added invalid-tab fallback guard: when current tab is no longer available, route to requested valid tab or first available tab.
- Resolved Docker stacks rendering issues in stacks view.
- Root cause:
  - stack health badge treated only exact `"running"` as healthy, but backend stack status is formatted as `"running(n)"` (for example `running(2)`), causing false-bad indicators.
  - stack ordering was unsorted, producing unstable list ordering between refreshes.
- Fix:
  - updated running-state check to `startsWith("running")`.
  - added explicit status text rendering in row chips.
  - added stable alphabetical sorting for stack list rendering.
- Files touched:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/DockerStacksTab.tsx`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDocker' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (docker UX expansion: container detail + terminal + create/deploy flows)
- Completed a Docker UX expansion pass to deliver Portainer-style container workflows.
- Follow-up UX refinement completed:
  - moved container/compose creation out of crowded host tabs into dedicated pages:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/new-container/page.tsx`
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/new-compose/page.tsx`
  - replaced inline create/deploy forms in host tabs with focused CTA buttons (`New Container`, `New Compose`).
  - fixed node tab resolution precedence so Docker container pages always include `Terminal`, `Logs`, `Stats`, and `Inspect` even when template tab bindings are present.
- Backend/API additions:
  - added `POST /api/v1/docker/hosts/{id}/action` (host-target actions for `container.create`, `stack.deploy`, image actions).
  - added `GET /api/v1/docker/containers/{id}/logs` (tail/timestamps passthrough -> `container.logs` action).
  - added Docker exec stream bridge wiring so terminal sessions targeting Docker container assets route to in-container exec.
- Agent/runtime additions:
  - added Docker action support for `container.create` and `container.logs`.
  - added Docker compose support for `stack.deploy` (compose YAML write + `up -d`).
  - added immediate post-success Docker state refresh (discovery + stats) after container/compose lifecycle actions so UI updates do not wait for periodic collector ticks.
- Frontend additions:
  - Docker host `Containers` tab now supports quick-launch form (image/name/cmd/env/ports).
  - Docker host `Stacks` tab now supports compose deploy form (stack name + YAML).
  - Docker container node tabs now include `Terminal`, `Logs`, `Stats`, and `Inspect`.
  - container rows now deep-link to container node details; host/container proxy routes now support required POST/GET passthroughs.
  - Docker tabs now surface backend action/load errors explicitly (instead of silent empty states) to make unsupported-agent capabilities diagnosable in UI.
- Regression coverage added:
  - new Docker API handler tests for host action path + container logs path (`cmd/labtether/docker_api_test.go`).
  - new coordinator tests for host-target `container.create` and `stack.deploy` action routing (`internal/connectors/docker/coordinator_test.go`).
  - new agentcore parser/decoder tests for docker action helpers (`internal/agentcore/docker_actions_test.go`).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/connectors/docker -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestSplitDockerParamList|TestParseDockerPortBindings|TestDecodeDockerLogPayload|TestDocker|TestInferComposeStacks|TestComposeCommandArgs' -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDocker' -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (topology audit remediation: correctness + UX parity + scaling)
- Completed a topology deep-dive remediation pass focused on user-expected behavior and correctness.
- Topology UX/correctness updates:
  - lane collapse now applies consistently to both graph and list views.
  - list sections/stats now use only visible (non-collapsed) lanes and show an explicit empty state when all lanes are hidden.
  - relationship filter default changed from `explicit` to `all` (including reset behavior) so topology shows complete context by default.
  - fixed inferred Docker parent mapping for multi-host setups by keying Docker parent/child matching on `metadata.agent_id` (with safe fallback).
- Relationship loading/scaling updates:
  - replaced topology N-per-asset dependency fetches with one bulk request path.
  - frontend topology hook now fetches `/api/dependencies/batch` once per asset ID set and no longer drops non-source edges client-side.
  - added backend bulk endpoint `GET /dependencies/batch?asset_ids=<csv>&limit=<n>`.
  - added Postgres-optimized bulk dependency query via `ListAssetDependenciesBatch(assetIDs, limit)`.
  - backend handler auto-uses the bulk query when available and falls back to merged per-asset listing for compatibility.
- Files touched:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/{page.tsx,TopologyListView.tsx,useTopologyFilters.ts,useAssetDependencies.ts}`
  - `web/console/app/console/taxonomy.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/dependencies/batch/route.ts`
  - `cmd/labtether/dependencies_handlers.go`
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_dependencies_store.go`
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (docker containers tab empty: proxy route mismatch fix)
- Root cause confirmed: Next.js Docker proxy routes were forwarding to `${base.api}/docker/...` while backend Docker APIs are mounted at `/api/v1/docker/...`.
- Impact:
  - Docker host/container/image/stack proxy requests returned backend 404s.
  - Docker UI tabs swallowed fetch errors and rendered empty-state (`No containers`) even when data existed.
- Fix implemented across all Docker proxy routes:
  - `web/console/app/api/docker/hosts/route.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/docker/hosts/[id]/route.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/docker/hosts/[id]/[sub]/route.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/docker/containers/[id]/route.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/docker/containers/[id]/[sub]/route.ts`
  - `web/console/app/api/docker/stacks/[id]/action/route.ts`
  - each route now forwards to `${base.api}/api/v1/docker/...`.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - authenticated console proxy verification:
    - `GET /api/docker/hosts` -> 200 with 1 host.
    - `GET /api/docker/hosts/containervm-deltaserver/containers` -> 200 with 11 containers.
    - `GET /api/docker/hosts/docker-containervm-deltaserver/containers` -> 200 with 11 containers (legacy alias path).
  - frontend dev logs show Docker API proxy routes returning 200 after fix.

## 2026-02-27 (topology containers categorized as services)
- Updated topology categorization logic so container assets no longer appear under `Compute` in topology category lanes.
- Implementation:
  - in `web/console/app/(console)/topology/topologyUtils.ts`, `topologyCategoryKey(...)` now treats service-typed assets (including containers) as `services` before falling back to canonical taxonomy mapping.
  - expanded `SERVICE_ASSET_TYPES` to include `container` and `docker-container`, which also fixes topology `Devices / services` summary counts.
- Scope control:
  - kept the change topology-local (did not alter global taxonomy in `web/console/app/console/taxonomy.ts`) to avoid unintended category changes in non-topology screens.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - `go test ./...` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (agent heartbeat docker auto-provisioning)
- Implemented automatic Docker hub-collector provisioning when an agent heartbeat advertises Docker connector availability.
- Added new backend helper in `cmd/labtether/docker_auto_collector.go`:
  - detects Docker in heartbeat connector metadata (`type=docker`),
  - ensures idempotency by skipping creation if a Docker collector already exists,
  - creates a non-colliding collector asset id: `docker-cluster-<normalized-agent-id>`,
  - creates/refreshes the collector cluster asset heartbeat (`source=docker`, `type=connector-cluster`),
  - creates an enabled Docker collector with default 60s interval,
  - starts an immediate collector run when runtime connector registry is available.
- Wired into heartbeat flow in `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go` via `processAgentHeartbeat`.
- Added discovery-trigger refresh path to close first-discovery race:
  - on agent `docker.discovery` messages, hub now attempts an immediate Docker collector run (`triggerDockerCollectorRunForDiscovery`) when collector runs are stale enough.
  - added short min-gap throttling to avoid excessive run spam.
- Fixed Docker host API compatibility for legacy node route IDs:
  - `GET /api/v1/docker/hosts/{id}[/containers|/images|/stacks]` now accepts:
    - normalized host id (`containervm-deltaserver`),
    - canonical asset alias (`docker-host-containervm-deltaserver`),
    - legacy alias (`docker-containervm-deltaserver`).
  - this unblocks container tab rendering for older/stale node routes in the web UI.
- Hardened Docker host ID derivation in node detail frontend:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx` now prefers `asset.metadata.agent_id` (normalized) for docker-host API calls before falling back to asset-id parsing.
  - this reduces route mismatch failures from stale browser routes/local cache variants.
- Added targeted regression tests in `cmd/labtether/docker_auto_collector_test.go`:
  - collector creation path,
  - skip path when Docker collector already exists,
  - heartbeat integration path,
  - Docker connector detection normalization,
  - discovery-trigger collector selection with recent-run throttling.
- Added Docker API regression tests in `cmd/labtether/docker_api_test.go` for legacy host-id alias resolution.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestAutoProvisionDockerCollectorIfNeeded|TestProcessAgentHeartbeatAutoProvisionsDockerCollector|TestHeartbeatAdvertisesDockerConnector|TestSelectDockerCollectorForDiscoveryKick' -count=1` passed.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleDockerHostContainersSub|TestHandleDockerHostContainersSubLegacyDockerPrefixAlias|TestHandleDockerHostDetailDockerHostAssetAlias|TestHandleDockerHostDetail|TestHandleDockerHostNotFound' -count=1` passed.
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - restarted backend and verified live docker inventory in status aggregate (`docker_ct=11`, `docker_host=1`) and healthy collector status.

## 2026-02-27 (linux install-time docker integration flow)
- Added install-time Docker configuration flow to generated Linux installer script in `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`.
- New installer options:
  - `--docker-enabled <auto|true|false>`
  - `--docker-endpoint <path|unix://path|http(s)://host:port>`
  - `--docker-discovery-interval <seconds>`
  - `--docker-wizard` (interactive prompts when TTY is available)
  - `--help`
- Installer behavior updates:
  - validates Docker mode/endpoint/interval inputs before install.
  - writes Docker runtime env vars into `/etc/labtether/agent.env`:
    - `LABTETHER_DOCKER_ENABLED`
    - `LABTETHER_DOCKER_SOCKET`
    - `LABTETHER_DOCKER_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL`
  - warns if configured local Docker socket is missing at install time.
  - runs a post-install Docker connectivity check message path.
  - prints explicit guidance that dashboard Docker assets require `Add Device -> Docker` hub collector setup.
- Updated install-script tests in `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go` to assert new Docker flags/env/tip snippets.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS' -count=1` passed.
  - verified live `/install.sh` output includes the new Docker flow snippets.

## 2026-02-27 (linux installer fingerprint unavailable fix)
- Fixed a first-install Linux agent issue where installer output could show:
  - `Device fingerprint: unavailable (check /etc/labtether/device-fingerprint)`
- Root cause:
  - generated systemd unit used `ProtectSystem=full` but did not grant write access to `/etc/labtether`, blocking device identity/token persistence.
- Implementation:
  - updated generated unit in `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go` to include:
    - `ReadWritePaths=/etc/labtether`
    - `ReadWritePaths=/usr/local/bin`
  - kept hardening enabled while allowing required writes (identity/token/settings + self-update binary replacement path).
  - extended installer script assertions in `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go` for both `ReadWritePaths` lines.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (linux installer uninstall purge mode)
- Added first-class purge uninstall support to generated Linux install script in `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`.
- New script behavior:
  - `--uninstall`: remove service/binary/env file and preserve token/device identity for easy reinstall.
  - `--purge` (or `--uninstall --purge`): perform uninstall plus full credential/identity cleanup:
    - `/etc/labtether/agent-token`
    - `/etc/labtether/device-key`
    - `/etc/labtether/device-key.pub`
    - `/etc/labtether/device-fingerprint`
    - `/etc/labtether` directory
- Added argument parsing and usage messaging for installer flags, including unknown-arg failure handling.
- Updated install-script tests in `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go` to assert:
  - `--purge` flag presence,
  - key/fingerprint cleanup paths,
  - purge completion messaging.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS' -count=1` passed.

## 2026-02-27 (secure agent identity implementation: fingerprint + challenge/proof + UI gate)
- Implemented end-to-end secure pending-enrollment identity verification for agents.
- Agent identity lifecycle:
  - added per-device Ed25519 identity generation/loading in `internal/agentcore/device_identity.go`.
  - default files:
    - private key: `/etc/labtether/device-key`
    - public key: `/etc/labtether/device-key.pub`
    - fingerprint: `/etc/labtether/device-fingerprint`
  - wired config fields in `internal/agentcore/{types.go,config.go}` and runtime startup in `internal/agentcore/run.go`.
  - agent static metadata now includes:
    - `agent_device_fingerprint`
    - `agent_device_key_alg`
- Agent protocol hardening:
  - added new protocol messages in `internal/agentmgr/message.go`:
    - `enrollment.challenge` (hub -> agent)
    - `enrollment.proof` (agent -> hub)
  - added challenge signing in `internal/agentcore/command_handler.go` using device private key.
  - added shared identity helpers in `internal/agentidentity/identity.go` for canonical fingerprinting + signing payload.
- Hub-side pending enrollment verification:
  - extended pending enrollment model/API in `cmd/labtether/agent_enrollment.go` with:
    - `device_fingerprint`, `device_key_alg`, `identity_verified`, `identity_verified_at`.
  - hub now issues enrollment challenge on pending connect and verifies signed proof before approval.
  - `POST /api/v1/agents/approve` now blocks with `409` until identity is verified.
  - on approval, heartbeat metadata includes:
    - `agent_device_fingerprint`
    - `agent_device_key_alg`
    - `agent_identity_verified_at`
- Console/UI updates:
  - pending approvals banner now shows fingerprint and verification badge, and disables approve until verified:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/nodesPageComponents.tsx`
  - pending API type updated:
    - `web/console/app/api/agents/pending/route.ts`
  - node metadata mapping includes identity fields:
    - `web/console/app/console/models.ts`
- Linux install UX:
  - installer script now prints hostname + device fingerprint after startup and explicit verify-before-approve guidance:
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers.go`
  - install handler tests updated:
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_install_handlers_test.go`
- Tests and validation:
  - added backend proof verification tests:
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_enrollment_test.go`
      - valid proof accepted
      - invalid signature rejected
      - approval blocked when unverified
  - targeted backend/agent tests passed:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS|TestHandleListPendingAgents|TestVerifyPendingEnrollmentProof|TestHandleApproveAgentRequiresIdentityVerified' -count=1`
    - `go test ./internal/agentmgr -run 'TestIsKnownMessageType|TestKnownMessageTypesCountMatchesConstants' -count=1`
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestEnrollmentHeadersWhenNoToken|TestUpdateTokenResetsAuthFailures|TestSaveAndLoadTokenRoundTrip' -count=1`
  - frontend checks passed:
    - `cd web/console && npm run lint`
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit`
  - broader package sweep note:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/agentcore ./internal/agentmgr -count=1` hit known flaky TrueNAS test once; targeted rerun of the failing subtest passed.
- Runtime/deploy verification:
  - rebuilt installer-served Linux agent binaries:
    - `make build-agent-linux`
    - refreshed `build/labtether-agent-linux-{amd64,arm64}` artifacts.
  - restarted dev runtime:
    - backend: `labtether-backend` (`:8080`)
    - frontend: `labtether-frontend` (`:3000`)
  - verified install script output includes:
    - `WS_URL=\"ws://localhost:8080/ws/agent\"`

## 2026-02-27 (agent identity security planning)
- Added a dedicated deep-dive plan for secure device identity verification and enrollment hardening:
  - `notes/AGENT_DEVICE_IDENTITY_SECURITY_PLAN.md`
- Plan covers:
  - current-state analysis of pending enrollment trust model,
  - threat model (hostname spoofing, token replay/impersonation, operator confusion),
  - options analysis (metadata-only, fingerprint-only, signed challenge, TPM path),
  - recommended phased architecture:
    - Phase 1: install-time + UI fingerprint display,
    - Phase 2: enrollment proof-of-possession challenge,
    - Phase 3: token + key binding on every agent reconnect,
    - Phase 4: rotation/recovery + optional TPM hardening,
  - data model/API/protocol/UI/testing impacts and rollout guardrails.

## 2026-02-27 (cross-platform file-browser audit: linux + mac + backend + frontend)

## 2026-02-27 (UI audit and race-condition hardening sweep)
- Ran a full console UI audit for stale/empty-state races and silent proxy failures; validated with:
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run lint`
- Fixed pending-agent approval flow breakage and UX desync:
  - corrected API proxy upstream paths to `/api/v1/agents/{pending,approve,reject}` in:
    - `web/console/app/api/agents/pending/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/api/agents/approve/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/api/agents/reject/route.ts`
  - removed optimistic row removal in pending approvals banner and added failure feedback + button busy state in:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/nodesPageComponents.tsx`
- Fixed file-browser stale response races (global + node-level):
  - added monotonic request guards to ignore out-of-order list responses in:
    - `web/console/app/hooks/useFiles.ts`
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/FilesTab.tsx`
  - kept the earlier auto-load-on-connect fix for global Files page:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/files/page.tsx`
- Fixed session/viewer async race hazards:
  - added connect-attempt guard to prevent stale connect responses from overriding newer session state in:
    - `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts`
  - added cancellation guard around async noVNC init so stale `wsUrl` effects cannot attach after cleanup:
    - `web/console/app/components/VNCViewer.tsx`
- Fixed task/action stale-state UI issues:
  - added request-order guards for PBS details/task fetches:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/PBSTab.tsx`
  - prevented stale action-run data from persisting after failed refresh:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodeDetailData.ts`
- Reduced silent API fallback behavior that masked backend/auth failures:
  - pending-agent list proxy now propagates backend failure status (instead of success-empty fallback):
    - `web/console/app/api/agents/pending/route.ts`
  - connected-agent proxy now propagates non-OK status and context clears stale connected set on failed refresh:
    - `web/console/app/api/agents/connected/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/contexts/ConnectedAgentsContext.tsx`
  - enrollment/agent-token settings proxies now propagate backend error status instead of synthetic `200` empty payloads:
    - `web/console/app/api/settings/enrollment/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/api/settings/agent-tokens/route.ts`
  - status/live status proxies now propagate backend failure status (instead of success-empty fallbacks):
    - `web/console/app/api/status/route.ts`
    - `web/console/app/api/status/live/route.ts`
- Hardened status polling merge against cross-filter stale overwrites:
  - added scope generation + request sequencing guards in:
    - `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`
  - resetting cached full-status snapshot/ETag on site-filter changes now prevents older in-flight responses from overriding newer site-filter data.

## 2026-02-26 (global Files page empty-state race after agent connect)
- Root-caused and fixed a console-side race where the global Files page could stay empty after the selected target later became connected.
  - behavior before fix: auto-list only ran when `target` changed; if `target` was selected while disconnected and agent presence flipped to connected afterward, no list request was triggered.
  - confirmed backend/agent path was healthy via live calls:
    - `GET /agents/connected` returned `Michael-MBP.local`,
    - `GET /status/aggregate` included the same asset ID,
    - `GET /files/Michael-MBP.local/list?path=~` returned entries.
- Updated `web/console/app/(console)/files/page.tsx`:
  - auto-list effect now reacts to `targetHasAgent` transition and triggers `listDir(target, "~")` when the selected target becomes connected.
- Updated `web/console/app/hooks/useFiles.ts`:
  - stabilized `listDir` callback by reading hidden-file state through `showHiddenRef`, preventing dependency churn and ensuring connectivity-driven effect behavior is deterministic.
- Validation completed:
  - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit`

## 2026-02-25 (security hardening follow-up: runtime command/outbound guards + log sanitization)
- Implemented shared runtime security guard package: `internal/securityruntime`.
  - `command.go`:
    - added guarded command constructors/wrappers (`NewCommand*`, `Command*Output`, `ValidateExecBinary`),
    - added shell-command policy checks (`ValidateShellCommand`) with configurable allowlist/blocked-substring controls.
  - `outbound.go`:
    - added outbound URL/host validation with configurable allowlist/private/loopback controls,
    - added guarded HTTP request+execution wrappers and guarded TCP dial helper (`NewOutboundRequestWithContext`, `DoOutboundRequest`, `DialOutboundTCPTimeout`).
  - `log.go`:
    - added centralized log argument sanitization and `Logf` wrapper to neutralize control-character/newline injection and bound field length.
- Integrated command guard usage across dynamic execution surfaces:
  - `cmd/labtether/worker_command_executor.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/{command_handler,cron_backend_launchd,desktop_vnc,docker_compose,log_backend_darwin,log_backend_journalctl,network_handler,network_stats_darwin,package_backend_darwin,package_backend_linuxpm,service_backend_launchd,service_backend_systemd,terminal_handler,users_handler}.go`
  - `internal/agentplatform/{linux/provider_metadata,darwin/provider_metadata,temperature/temperature}.go`
- Integrated outbound guard usage across HTTP/socket surfaces:
  - collectors/status/proxy surfaces:
    - `cmd/labtether/{hub_collector_remote,hub_collector_winrm,status_aggregate_handlers,desktop_stream_handlers,proxmox_stream_socket,truenas_stream_helpers}.go`
  - agent/runtime/connector/notification/telemetry surfaces:
    - `internal/agentcore/{docker_client,docker_exec,enroll,publisher,self_update}.go`
    - `internal/connectors/{homeassistant/stub,pbs/client,portainer/client,proxmox/client}.go`
    - `internal/notifications/{slack,webhook}.go`
    - `internal/telemetry/prometheus.go`
- Integrated sanitized logging wrapper in high-signal dynamic log paths:
  - `cmd/labtether/{agent_enrollment,agent_ws_handler,canonical_model_runtime,file_handlers_read_write,desktop_stream_handlers,proxmox_stream_desktop_proxy,proxmox_stream_terminal_proxy,proxmox_stream_socket,truenas_stream_helpers}.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/disk_handler.go`
- Deployment/runtime config updates:
  - added hardening env knobs to `.env.example` and `docker-compose.yml` for command/outbound/log policies.
- Scanner baseline impact:
  - refreshed `security/gosec_allowlist.tsv` from `109` reviewed findings to `31` reviewed findings.
  - remaining reviewed rules: `G117 (18)`, `G304 (13)`.
  - eliminated reviewed findings in: `G204`, `G704`, and `G706`.
