- [2026-02-25] `gosec` policy closure completed with strict reviewed-allowlist enforcement.
  - Security hardening implementation updates:
    - fixed unsafe integer conversions flagged by `G115` using explicit clamp/convert helpers in startup worker, job handlers, and PTY resize paths.
    - replaced reconnect jitter `math/rand` usage with crypto-backed jitter generation (`G404`) in `internal/agentcore/ws_transport.go`.
    - hardened agent install URL/host derivation by sanitizing configured external URL and inbound host headers before script generation.
    - added targeted safety comments/suppressions for intentional insecure-toggle paths (`G402`/`G106`) and validated-command/file patterns where data is trusted or constrained.
  - Enforcement and CI:
    - added `security/gosec_allowlist.tsv` as the explicit baseline (`rule_id`, `path`, `line`, `justification`) for current unresolved findings.
    - added executable gate script `scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh` (fails on new unallowlisted findings and stale allowlist entries).
    - added `make security-gosec` and integrated a dedicated `gosec-allowlist` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
  - Validation:
    - `./scripts/check-gosec-allowlist.sh` passed (`109` reviewed findings).
    - `make security-gosec` passed.
    - `go vet ./...` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
- [2026-02-27] Docker pipeline audit completed (backend + agent + web UI) with targeted test validation.
  - Validation run:
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/docker -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Docker|docker|Exec|Compose|Logs' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Docker|docker|terminal|Terminal' -count=1` passed.
  - Audit findings captured for follow-up:
    - coordinator host-state snapshot safety/data-race risk (mutable map/slice pointers exposed to handlers).
    - stale Docker stats lifecycle (no prune/empty update path) causing stale UI metrics.
    - unix socket availability/test path false positives (socket-exists vs daemon/API reachable).
    - docker exec/terminal cleanup branch risk where close signal may be skipped in output-before-start branch.
    - Docker UI fidelity gaps (stats stale-clear behavior and silent image-fetch failures).
- [2026-02-27] Docker pipeline audit fixes implemented and validated.
  - Hub/coordinator safety:
    - switched Docker coordinator host/container lookups (`ListHosts`, `GetHost`, `GetHostByNormalizedID`, `FindContainer`) to return detached deep-copy snapshots to avoid mutable shared-state exposure during API responses.
    - added regression tests for snapshot detachment and stats replacement lifecycle in `internal/connectors/docker/coordinator_test.go`.
  - Stats staleness lifecycle:
    - agent now always emits docker stats payloads (including empty) so stopped/removed containers can clear stale metrics.
    - hub docker stats ingest now replaces host stats snapshot atomically (pruning stale IDs).
    - host/detail container responses now include optional `cpu_percent`, `memory_percent`, `memory_bytes`, `memory_limit` fields by joining container inventory with latest stats.
  - Docker endpoint reachability correctness:
    - upgraded unix endpoint availability checks from socket-exists to real Docker API ping in `internal/agentcore/docker_collector.go` and connector discovery reachability in `internal/agentcore/connector_discovery.go`.
    - strengthened remote docker endpoint test command generation (`dockerConnectivityTestCommand`) to verify daemon/API reachability instead of only socket existence.
  - Docker exec cleanup:
    - docker exec terminal stream now always sends `docker.exec.close` via deferred cleanup after successful start-dispatch, covering all startup branches.
  - UI fidelity:
    - Docker stats tab now clears stale stats/history when backend reports no stats for a container.
    - Docker images tab now surfaces fetch errors instead of silently showing empty state.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/docker -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Docker|docker|connector' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Docker|docker|AgentSettings|agent.settings|terminal|Terminal' -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-27] Files target picker now shows only actionable devices.
  - Root cause: Files page selector rendered full `status.assets` list even when a device had no connected agent, so unusable Docker/discovered targets appeared.
  - Fix: `web/console/app/hooks/useFiles.ts` now filters assets to `connectedAgentIds` and clears stale selections when a chosen target drops out.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx eslint app/hooks/useFiles.ts` passed.

- [2026-02-27] Files navigation now respects agent sandbox root.
  - Root cause: Files breadcrumb root button and up-navigation requested `/`, but agent file manager is sandboxed to its base directory (`$HOME` by default), so `/` returned `path "/" is outside the allowed base directory`.
  - Fixes:
    - `web/console/app/hooks/useFiles.ts` now tracks the resolved root path from initial `~` listing and clamps `navigateToPath`/`navigateUp`/folder navigation within that root.
    - `web/console/app/(console)/files/FilesBrowserCard.tsx` now renders root breadcrumb as `~` (or `/` only when true root), builds breadcrumb segments relative to the sandbox root, and hides `up` at root.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npx eslint app/hooks/useFiles.ts 'app/(console)/files/FilesBrowserCard.tsx' 'app/(console)/files/page.tsx` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-27] Added opt-in full-filesystem mode for agent file access.
  - Added hub-managed agent setting `files_root_mode` with values `home` (default sandbox) and `full` (entire filesystem), marked restart-required.
  - Agent runtime now reads/writes this setting via env (`LABTETHER_FILES_ROOT_MODE`), local settings file, and remote apply pipeline.
  - File manager now derives base directory from mode (`home` -> user home, `full` -> filesystem root) while retaining traversal/symlink safety checks.
  - Linux install script now supports `--files-root-mode <home|full>` and writes `LABTETHER_FILES_ROOT_MODE` into `/etc/labtether/agent.env`.
  - Files UI now probes whether `/` listing is allowed and, when allowed, sets root navigation to `/`; otherwise it stays sandboxed to home root.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestValidatePath|TestResolveFileBaseDir|TestCopyPathRecursive|TestFileWrite' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS' -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx eslint app/hooks/useFiles.ts 'app/(console)/files/FilesBrowserCard.tsx' 'app/(console)/files/page.tsx' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/AgentSettingsTab.tsx' && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-27] Moved install-script option builder into Add Device -> Agent setup flow.
  - Updated `web/console/app/components/AddDeviceModal/AgentSetupStep.tsx` to show Linux installer options and generate a copyable one-line install command directly in the add-agent wizard.
  - Linux options now include Docker mode/endpoint/discovery interval, file root mode, and a toggle to include one-time enrollment token.
  - Retained manual binary command as advanced fallback in the same step.
  - Extended installer script support with `--enrollment-token` and env passthrough (`LABTETHER_ENROLLMENT_TOKEN`) so generated wizard commands can still auto-enroll immediately.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestGenerateInstallScript|TestHTTPURLToWS' -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx eslint app/components/AddDeviceModal/AgentSetupStep.tsx && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-27] Enrollment/install connection targets now auto-discover Tailscale + LAN endpoints (instead of defaulting to localhost).
  - Backend:
    - Added `resolveHubConnectionSelection` in `cmd/labtether/hub_connection_resolver.go` to build ranked, deduplicated connection candidates (`external`, `request`, `tailscale`, `lan`, fallback).
    - Added network-interface discovery for Tailscale (`100.64.0.0/10` / `fd7a:115c:a1e0::/48`) and non-loopback LAN addresses with noisy virtual-interface filtering.
    - `/settings/enrollment` now returns `hub_candidates` alongside `hub_url` and `ws_url`.
    - Install-script URL resolution now reuses the same selection logic so generated install commands can target real reachable endpoints.
  - Web console:
    - `useEnrollment` now parses and stores `hub_candidates`, tracks selected target URL, and drives derived `hubURL/wsURL` from that selection.
    - Added connection-target picker in Settings and Add Device -> Agent setup so operators can explicitly choose Tailscale vs LAN endpoint.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestResolveHubConnectionSelectionPrefersTailscaleForLoopbackHost|TestEnrollmentTokensIncludesHubCandidates|TestHandleAgentInstallScript|TestEnrollmentTokenCreateAndList'` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint:types` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether` currently has an existing unrelated fixture mismatch (`TestStatusAggregateIncludesCanonicalPayload`: expected `provider=labtether-agent`, got `provider=agent`).

- [2026-02-27] Fixed nodes page hydration warning from nested anchor tags in device cards.
  - Root cause: `DeviceCard` wrapped the entire card with `Link`, and `WorkloadSummaryFooter` rendered a nested Docker host `Link`, producing invalid `<a>` inside `<a>` markup.
  - Fix: refactored outer card container in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/DeviceCard.tsx` to a keyboard-accessible clickable `div` (`role="link"`, `tabIndex`, Enter/Space support) that routes with `useRouter`, while preserving the footer Docker host `Link`.
  - Added interactive-target guard so clicks/keys on nested interactive elements do not trigger parent navigation.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-27] Repo simplicity + efficiency refactor audit (backend + frontend) completed.
  - Scope:
    - backend production hotspots (`cmd/labtether`, `internal/*`) for coupling/dispatch complexity and duplication.
    - frontend production hotspots (`web/console/app`) for high-LOC/high-churn modules and duplicated workflows.
  - Evidence gathered:
    - frontend hotspot: `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx` (`687 LOC`, recent churn leader).
    - frontend hotspot: files UX split across `web/console/app/(console)/files/page.tsx`, `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/FilesTab.tsx`, and `web/console/app/hooks/useFiles.ts` (duplicate clipboard/context-menu/file-op flows).
    - frontend duplication: connector settings API routes (`web/console/app/api/settings/*/route.ts`) duplicate helper stacks (`stringValue`, `boolValue`, `numberValue`, `clampInterval`, `normalizeBaseURL`, `safeJSON`).
    - backend hotspot: `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go` large message dispatch switch for agent protocol handling.
    - backend hotspot: `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_connector_exec.go` collector-specific run flow duplication.
  - Recommended refactor tracks:
    - Node detail modularization (panel registry + split data hooks).
    - File workspace unification (shared file ops/workspace state for global files + node files tab).
    - Settings route/hook consolidation (factory/adapter pattern for connector settings).
    - Agent WS dispatch router extraction (table-driven handlers).
    - Collector execution template/registry extraction (shared run lifecycle helpers).

- [2026-02-28] Terminal uplift audit follow-up fixes (SPICE client packaging + alert global-target correctness).
  - SPICE runtime packaging (no CDN/global script dependency):
    - added `@spice-project/spice-html5` dependency in `web/console/package.json`.
    - updated `web/console/app/components/SPICEViewer.tsx` to load SPICE client via module import (`@spice-project/spice-html5/src/main.js`) instead of expecting `window.SpiceMainConn`.
    - added local type declaration `web/console/types/spice-html5.d.ts` for strict TypeScript compatibility.
  - Alert target resolution correctness:
    - fixed `cmd/labtether/alert_predicates.go` so global fallback only applies to truly empty target sets (non-matching explicit targets no longer expand to all assets).
    - added regression test `TestResolveRuleTargetAssets_GlobalRuleWithNonMatchingTargetsDoesNotFallbackToAllAssets` in `cmd/labtether/alert_canonical_predicates_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run TestResolveRuleTargetAssets_GlobalRuleWithNonMatchingTargetsDoesNotFallbackToAllAssets -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Terminal uplift audit follow-up fixes (desktop stream backpressure + recording retention file cleanup).
  - Desktop stream correctness/performance:
    - updated `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go` so `desktopBridge.trySendOutput(...)` no longer drops output when the buffer is full; it now waits for capacity or bridge close.
    - added bridge shutdown propagation on websocket write failure (`bridge.close()` + websocket close) to unblock pending desktop output senders cleanly.
    - added regression test `TestDesktopBridgeTrySendOutputBlocksWhenBufferFull` in `cmd/labtether/stream_bridge_safety_test.go`.
  - Recording retention correctness:
    - updated `internal/persistence/postgres_runtime_credentials.go` to prune recordings with:
      - `status <> 'recording'` (do not prune active sessions)
      - `COALESCE(stopped_at, created_at) < cutoff` (age by finalized stop time when available)
      - `DELETE ... RETURNING file_path` (collect files for cleanup)
    - after successful transaction commit, retention now removes associated recording files from disk and logs cleanup failures.
    - added test `TestRemoveRecordingFiles_RemovesExistingAndMissingAsSuccess` in `internal/persistence/postgres_runtime_credentials_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run TestDesktopBridgeTrySendOutput -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/persistence -run TestRemoveRecordingFiles_RemovesExistingAndMissingAsSuccess -count=1` passed.
    - `make check` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Terminal uplift audit follow-up fixes (desktop viewer session stability).
  - Reconnect churn + callback stability:
    - updated viewer disconnect contracts for Guacamole/SPICE/WebRTC to return structured `{ clean, reason }` details.
    - removed forced `clean: false` wrapping in `web/console/app/components/SessionPanel.tsx` so viewers can report true clean/manual disconnects.
    - stabilized WebRTC lifecycle callbacks using refs (`onConnectRef`/`onDisconnectRef`) and made `closePeer(...)` independent of changing parent callback identities.
  - Manual disconnect semantics:
    - Guacamole and SPICE now explicitly mark user-initiated disconnects as clean.
    - WebRTC imperative disconnect now reports clean/manual disconnect.
  - Reconnect timer cleanup:
    - added centralized reconnect timer cleanup in `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts` (on connect/disconnect/connected/unmount) to prevent lingering reconnect timeouts.
  - Session context cleanup:
    - `DesktopTab` now clears active desktop session context when leaving connected state and on panel disconnect callbacks.
  - Validation:
    - `make check` passed (`go vet`, `go test`, `tsc --noEmit`).

- [2026-02-28] Terminal uplift audit follow-up fix (Guacamole keyboard capture scoping).
  - Updated `web/console/app/components/GuacamoleViewer.tsx` keyboard handling to:
    - bind `Guacamole.Keyboard` to the viewer container element instead of `document` (prevents global keystroke capture outside remote view focus).
    - initialize a single keyboard instance per component mount (prevents listener accumulation across reconnects).
    - route key events through `clientRef` and focus container on mouse interaction.
    - reset keyboard state during stream cleanup to release stuck modifier state.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `make check` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Terminal uplift audit follow-up fix (Guacamole mouse/clipboard isolation + cleanup symmetry).
  - Updated `web/console/app/components/GuacamoleViewer.tsx` to:
    - keep clipboard handling scoped to viewer focus:
      - local paste (`ClipboardEvent`) is forwarded to remote only when the viewer container is focused, via `createClipboardStream("text/plain")`.
      - remote clipboard updates (`client.onclipboard`) are written to local clipboard only when the viewer container is focused.
    - align cleanup symmetry for reconnect/unmount:
      - remove container paste listener.
      - null mouse handlers (`onmousedown`/`onmouseup`/`onmousemove`).
      - clear client callbacks (`onclipboard`/`onstatechange`/`onerror`) before disconnect.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
    - `make check` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Terminal uplift audit follow-up fixes (desktop recording lifecycle + routing regressions).
  - Desktop WebRTC routing guard hardening:
    - added `shouldUseWebRTC(...)` in `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go` so protocol routing consistently checks connected-agent capability metadata before entering WebRTC path.
    - kept explicit fallback logging and VNC downgrade when WebRTC is requested but unavailable.
  - Recording lifecycle race correctness:
    - added per-session serialized recording operations on `desktopBridge` via `startRecordingLocked(...)` and `stopRecordingLocked(...)`.
    - updated `startRecordingRequest` and `stopRecordingBySession` in `cmd/labtether/recording_handlers.go` to use serialized operations, preventing duplicate recording creation under concurrent start/stop requests.
  - Desktop close/timeout cleanup correctness:
    - extracted `finalizeAgentDesktopSession(...)` and switched handler defer path to use it, guaranteeing started agent sessions always emit `desktop.close` on all exits (including timeout/error paths).
  - Added regression tests in `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`:
    - `TestShouldUseWebRTCReadsAgentCapabilities`

    - `TestFinalizeAgentDesktopSessionSkipsCloseBeforeStart`

    - `make check-docs` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-01 critical connector hardening.
  - Hardened connector test egress policy enforcement in backend:
    - added `securityruntime.ValidateOutboundURL` checks in:
      - `cmd/labtether/connector_test_handlers_truenas.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/connector_test_handlers_homeassistant.go`
    - requests with disallowed/invalid `base_url` now fail fast with `400` before dial attempts.
  - Hardened Home Assistant connector transport safety:
    - moved request construction/execution to securityruntime wrappers in `internal/connectors/homeassistant/stub.go`:
      - `securityruntime.NewOutboundRequestWithContext`
      - `securityruntime.DoOutboundRequest`
    - capped response reads at `32MiB` with deterministic overflow error (`home assistant response exceeded ...`).
  - Added regression coverage:
    - expanded TrueNAS handler tests: `cmd/labtether/connector_handlers_truenas_test.go` (disallowed outbound URL case).
    - added Home Assistant handler test suite: `cmd/labtether/connector_handlers_homeassistant_test.go` (validation, rate limit, credential fallback, redaction, outbound policy).
    - added Home Assistant connector unit tests: `internal/connectors/homeassistant/stub_test.go` (success, outbound policy rejection, oversized response cap).
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleTrueNASConnectorTest|TestHandleHomeAssistantConnectorTest|TestHandleConnectorActionsHomeAssistantTestRateLimit' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/homeassistant -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-TERM-02 terminal stream reliability hardening.
  - Fixed a panic-prone type assertion bug in terminal agent message processing:
    - `terminalBridges` map is shared between probe channels (`probe:*`) and live terminal bridge structs.
    - handlers `processAgentTerminalStarted`, `processAgentTerminalData`, and `processAgentTerminalClosed` previously did direct `.(*terminalBridge)` casts after map load.
    - crafted/colliding session IDs (or unexpected map values) could panic the hub process.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_handlers.go`:
    - replaced unsafe casts with guarded type assertions and ignore non-bridge entries safely.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_lifecycle_test.go`:
    - `TestProcessAgentTerminalHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries` verifies all three handlers ignore probe-channel entries without panic.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestProcessAgentTerminalHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries|TestCloseTerminalBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly|TestFinalizeAgentTerminalSession' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*Terminal.*Stream' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-02 WinRM collector transport hardening.
  - Identified unbounded SOAP response reads in `winrmSOAPRequest` (`cmd/labtether/hub_collector_winrm.go`) that could allow oversized upstream responses to pressure hub memory.
  - Added bounded read enforcement:
    - introduced `maxWinRMSOAPResponseBytes = 8 * 1024 * 1024`,
    - switched to `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(..., max+1))`,
    - return deterministic overflow error when response exceeds the cap.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_winrm_test.go`:
    - `TestWinRMSOAPRequestRejectsOversizedResponse`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestExecuteWinRMCommandRejectsInvalidCAPEM|TestWinRMSOAPRequestRejectsOversizedResponse' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'WinRM' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-05 TrueNAS outbound policy hardening.
  - Identified connector/runtime policy gap in `internal/connectors/truenas/jsonrpc.go`:
    - TrueNAS WebSocket endpoint generation existed, but no centralized outbound policy validation was enforced before dial in `Call` or `Subscribe`.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - added `validatedWSEndpoint()` using `securityruntime.ValidateOutboundURL`,
    - enforced validated endpoint usage in both `Client.Call` and `Client.Subscribe` before websocket dial.
  - Added regression coverage in `internal/connectors/truenas/jsonrpc_test.go`:
    - `TestJSONRPCCallRejectsDisallowedOutboundEndpoint`,
    - `TestJSONRPCSubscribeRejectsDisallowedOutboundEndpoint`,
    - both assert policy failure occurs before dial invocation.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/truenas -run 'TestJSONRPCCallRejectsDisallowedOutboundEndpoint|TestJSONRPCSubscribeRejectsDisallowedOutboundEndpoint' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/truenas -run 'TestJSONRPC' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-DESK-01 desktop disconnect lifecycle hardening.
  - Identified session lifecycle gap:
    - agent websocket disconnect teardown closed terminal bridges but not desktop bridges.
    - active desktop sessions could remain stale/hanging when the backing agent connection dropped unexpectedly.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - added `closeDesktopBridgesForAsset(assetID)` in `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go`,
    - invoked desktop bridge sweep during agent disconnect defer in `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go` (alongside existing terminal sweep).
  - Added regression coverage:
    - `TestCloseDesktopBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly` in `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCloseDesktopBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly|TestFinalizeAgentDesktopSession|TestProcessAgentDesktopClosedStoresReasonAndSignalsBridge' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-03 docker exec disconnect lifecycle hardening.
  - Identified session cleanup gap:
    - agent websocket disconnect teardown did not close Docker exec bridges.
    - active container terminal sessions could hang stale after container-host agent disconnect.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - added `closeDockerExecBridgesForAsset(assetID)` in `cmd/labtether/docker_exec_stream_handlers.go`,
    - wired docker exec bridge sweep into agent disconnect defer in `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`,
    - added safe `*dockerExecBridge` assertions in docker exec agent message handlers.
  - Added regression coverage:
    - `TestCloseDockerExecBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly` in `cmd/labtether/docker_exec_stream_handlers_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCloseDockerExecBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly|TestProcessAgentDockerExecClosedStoresReason|TestDockerExecBridgeReasonLifecycle' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*DockerExec' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-DESK-02 WebRTC signaling lifecycle hardening.
  - Identified WebRTC bridge lifecycle/integrity gaps:
    - agent disconnect teardown did not close active WebRTC signaling bridges,
    - WebRTC agent message handlers used unsafe bridge type assertions,
    - signaling event handlers did not enforce bridge-agent ownership matching.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go`:
    - added `closeWebRTCBridgesForAsset(assetID)` and bridge `matchesAgent(...)`,
    - wired WebRTC bridge sweep into agent disconnect defer (`cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`),
    - converted WebRTC signaling handlers to safe bridge assertions and agent-match guards (`processAgentWebRTCStarted/Answer/ICE/Stopped`).
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers_test.go`:
    - `TestCloseWebRTCBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly`,
    - `TestProcessAgentWebRTCHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries`,
    - `TestProcessAgentWebRTCStoppedSkipsMismatchedAgent`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCloseWebRTCBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly|TestProcessAgentWebRTCHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries|TestProcessAgentWebRTCStoppedSkipsMismatchedAgent' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*WebRTC' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: cross-workflow bridge assertion hardening.
  - Identified repeated unsafe `sync.Map` type assertion pattern across multiple agent response handlers:
    - desktop stream handlers still used direct `.(*desktopBridge)` assertions (`desktop.started`, `desktop.data`, `desktop.closed`),
    - recording start/stop paths asserted desktop bridge types directly,
    - file and node-ops response handlers (`process`, `service`, `network`, `disk`, `package`, `cron`, `users`, `journal`, `desktop.displays`) also performed direct assertions.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - replaced direct assertions with guarded bridge casts in:
      - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/recording_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/file_handlers_responses.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/process_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/service_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/network_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/disk_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/package_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/cron_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/users_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/journal_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/wol_handlers.go`
    - non-bridge entries are now ignored safely (recording start/stop now treats non-bridge entries as not-found/no-op).
  - Added regression coverage:
    - extended `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_handlers_test.go` with `TestProcessAgentDesktopHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries`,
    - added `cmd/labtether/recording_handlers_test.go` (`TestStartRecordingRequestRejectsNonBridgeEntry`, `TestStopRecordingBySessionIgnoresNonBridgeEntry`),
    - added `cmd/labtether/bridge_hardening_test.go` (`TestProcessAgentNodeOpsHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries`, `TestProcessAgentFileHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries`).
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestProcessAgentDesktopHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries|TestStartRecordingRequestRejectsNonBridgeEntry|TestStopRecordingBySessionIgnoresNonBridgeEntry|TestProcessAgentNodeOpsHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries|TestProcessAgentFileHandlersIgnoreNonBridgeEntries|TestCloseDesktopBridgesForAssetClosesMatchingSessionsOnly|TestProcessAgentDesktopClosedStoresReasonAndSignalsBridge' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-ALERT-02 evaluator lifecycle hardening.
  - Identified suppressed alert lifecycle correctness gap in `fireOrRefireAlert`:
    - suppressed alerts create `pending` instances (expected),
    - when suppression lifts, existing `pending` instances were only `last_fired_at` refreshed and never transitioned to firing,
    - pending instances could also be evaluated for auto-incident timing on refire path.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/alert_evaluator_instances.go`:
    - added explicit pending-instance lifecycle branch in `fireOrRefireAlert`,
    - while suppression remains active: only refresh `last_fired_at`,
    - when suppression lifts: resolve pending placeholder and create/dispatch a fresh firing instance,
    - retained existing refire path (`firing`/`acknowledged`) and extracted firing-instance creation+dispatch into local helper closure.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/alerts_incidents_api_test.go`:
    - `TestAlertSilenceSuppressionTransitionsToFiringAfterSilenceEnds`,
    - `TestAlertSuppressedPendingDoesNotAutoCreateIncident`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestAlertSilenceSuppression|TestAlertSilenceSuppressionTransitionsToFiringAfterSilenceEnds|TestAlertSuppressedPendingDoesNotAutoCreateIncident|TestAlertAutoIncidentTiming|TestAlertMaintenanceSuppression' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestAlert.*|Test.*Alert.*|Test.*Incident.*' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-ALERT-01 rule-validation consistency hardening.
  - Identified create/update validation mismatch in alert rule timing fields:
    - create allowed explicit `0` for `window_seconds` / `evaluation_interval_seconds`,
    - update validation rejected `0` for the same fields.
  - Impact:
    - inconsistent API contract for rule CRUD workflows,
    - edit/save flows could reject semantically valid values already accepted on create.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/alerts_validation.go`:
    - aligned update validation for these fields to non-negative (`>= 0`) semantics,
    - corrected create-rule kind validation message to include supported `synthetic_check`.
  - Added regression coverage in new `cmd/labtether/alerts_validation_test.go`:
    - `TestValidateCreateAlertRuleRequestAcceptsSyntheticCheckKind`,
    - `TestValidateUpdateAlertRuleRequestAllowsZeroWindowAndEvaluationInterval`,
    - `TestValidateUpdateAlertRuleRequestRejectsNegativeWindowAndEvaluationInterval`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestValidateCreateAlertRuleRequestAcceptsSyntheticCheckKind|TestValidateUpdateAlertRuleRequestAllowsZeroWindowAndEvaluationInterval|TestValidateUpdateAlertRuleRequestRejectsNegativeWindowAndEvaluationInterval|TestAlertRuleCreateListGetUpdateAndEvaluation' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-AUTH-01/02 runtime hardening.
  - Identified nil-pointer panic risk in auth handlers under degraded/misconfigured runtime states:
    - `handleAuthLogin` dereferenced `s.authStore` unguarded,
    - `handleAuthLogout` dereferenced `s.authStore` when a session cookie was present.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/auth_handlers.go`:
    - login now returns `503` (`authentication unavailable`) when auth store is not configured,
    - logout now only attempts session validation/deletion when `authStore != nil`, while still clearing the session cookie.
  - Added regression coverage in new `cmd/labtether/auth_handlers_test.go`:
    - `TestHandleAuthLoginReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutAuthStore`,
    - `TestHandleAuthLogoutClearsCookieWhenAuthStoreUnavailable`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAuthLoginReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutAuthStore|TestHandleAuthLogoutClearsCookieWhenAuthStoreUnavailable' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-04 Proxmox collector-routing hardening.
  - Identified action-routing ambiguity:
    - direct `node/vmid` Proxmox action targets without `collector_id` would default to the first active collector.
    - in multi-collector deployments, this could execute VM/CT lifecycle actions against the wrong Proxmox cluster.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/proxmox_action_runtime.go`:
    - added `resolveProxmoxActionCollectorID` to deterministically resolve collector selection,
    - preserved auto-resolution when exactly one active Proxmox collector is present,
    - enforced explicit `collector_id` when multiple active Proxmox collectors exist.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/proxmox_action_runtime_test.go`:
    - `TestExecuteProxmoxActionDirectNodeVMIDTargetRequiresCollectorWhenMultipleConfigured`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestExecuteProxmoxActionDirectNodeVMIDTargetUsesCollectorParam|TestExecuteProxmoxActionDirectNodeVMIDTargetRequiresCollectorWhenMultipleConfigured|TestExecuteProxmoxActionDirectUsesCollectorRuntime|TestExecuteProxmoxActionRuntimeFailureAndInvokeValidation' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*Proxmox.*' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/proxmox -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-06 PBS multi-collector routing hardening.
  - Identified runtime selection ambiguity in `loadPBSRuntime`:
    - when `collector_id` was omitted, runtime selection defaulted to the first active PBS collector,
    - task status/log/stop flows could therefore target the wrong backup cluster in multi-collector deployments.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/pbs_runtime_loader.go`:
    - added multi-collector guard so omitted `collector_id` is rejected when more than one active PBS collector exists,
    - preserved backward-compatible auto-selection when exactly one active PBS collector exists.
  - Added regression coverage:
    - extended `TestLoadPBSRuntimeBranches` with explicit multi-collector `collector_id` guard assertion,
    - added `TestHandlePBSTaskHandlersRequireCollectorWhenMultipleCollectors` in `cmd/labtether/pbs_api_handlers_helpers_test.go` to verify task handlers fail fast and make zero upstream calls under ambiguous routing.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestLoadPBSRuntimeBranches|TestHandlePBSTaskHandlersRequireCollectorWhenMultipleCollectors|TestHandlePBSTaskHandlersUpstreamErrorsAndLogLimits|TestHandlePBSTaskRoutesAndHandlerGuards|TestResolvePBSAssetRuntimeFallbackAndErrors' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*PBS.*' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/pbs -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-07 Portainer API-key validation hardening.
  - Identified validation-contract mismatch in `handlePortainerConnectorTest`:
    - API-key auth path required `token_id` even though the Portainer client only uses the API key secret.
    - this caused false `400` failures for valid API-key requests and credential profiles with blank username/token-id.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/connector_test_handlers_portainer.go`:
    - updated API-key validation to require only `base_url` and `token_secret` (or `credential_id`).
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/connector_handlers_portainer_test.go`:
    - updated validation error expectation for missing API-key fields,
    - added `TestHandlePortainerConnectorTestAPIKeyDoesNotRequireTokenID` to ensure successful API-key auth without `token_id`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandlePortainerConnectorTestValidationErrors|TestHandlePortainerConnectorTestDefaultsAuthMethodAndHonorsSkipVerify|TestHandlePortainerConnectorTestAPIKeyDoesNotRequireTokenID|TestHandlePortainerConnectorTestSuccessWithCredentialFallback|TestHandleConnectorActionsPortainerTestRateLimit|TestHandleConnectorActionsDispatchesPortainerTest' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*Portainer.*' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/portainer -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-CONN-08 Home Assistant rate-limit alias hardening.
  - Identified connector-test throttling bypass path in `handleConnectorActions`:
    - Home Assistant accepts both `homeassistant` and `home-assistant` route forms,
    - rate-limit keying used the raw path connector id, so alternating aliases bypassed the intended shared test burst cap.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/connector_handlers.go`:
    - connector test rate-limit key now uses `resolvedConnectorID` (normalized canonical connector id).
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/connector_handlers_homeassistant_test.go`:
    - `TestHandleConnectorActionsHomeAssistantTestRateLimitSharesAliasBuckets` validates mixed-alias requests are throttled under one shared bucket.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleConnectorActionsHomeAssistantTestRateLimit|TestHandleConnectorActionsHomeAssistantTestRateLimitSharesAliasBuckets|TestHandleHomeAssistantConnectorTestRejectsDisallowedOutboundURL|TestHandleHomeAssistantConnectorTestSuccessAndFailure' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*HomeAssistant.*' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/homeassistant -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-AUTH owner-token runtime guard hardening.
  - Identified nil-pointer panic risk in owner-token validation paths:
    - `withAuth` middleware called `s.authValidator.ValidateRequest(r)` without guarding `authValidator`,
    - `handleBrowserEvents` and `handleAgentWebSocket` had the same direct dereference.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - added shared nil-safe helper `validateOwnerTokenRequest`,
    - switched middleware/browser-events/agent-websocket bearer-token auth checks to the helper so missing validator states return `401` instead of panicking.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`:
    - `TestWithAuthReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator`,
    - `TestHandleBrowserEventsReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator`,
    - `TestHandleAgentWebSocketReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestKnownMessageTypes|TestWithAuthReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator|TestHandleBrowserEventsReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator|TestHandleAgentWebSocketReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-AGENT-02 transport identity binding hardening.
  - Identified cross-asset integrity gap in `/ws/agent` ingestion:
    - heartbeat, telemetry, and log handlers trusted payload-provided `asset_id` fields instead of authenticated websocket identity (`conn.AssetID`),
    - a connected agent could spoof writes for another asset ID.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`:
    - heartbeat upserts now always use `conn.AssetID`,
    - telemetry sample writes now always use `conn.AssetID`,
    - log stream/batch writes now always use `conn.AssetID`.
  - Added spoofing regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler_test.go`:
    - `TestProcessAgentHeartbeatIgnoresPayloadAssetID`,
    - `TestProcessAgentTelemetryIgnoresPayloadAssetID`,
    - `TestProcessAgentLogHandlersIgnorePayloadAssetID`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestProcessAgentHeartbeatIgnoresPayloadAssetID|TestProcessAgentTelemetryIgnoresPayloadAssetID|TestProcessAgentLogHandlersIgnorePayloadAssetID' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*(Agent|Enrollment|Auth|Browser).*' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/agentmgr -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-AGENT-01 pending enrollment ID hardening.
  - Identified pending enrollment asset-ID collision risk:
    - temporary IDs were derived from `hostname + unixMillis`,
    - concurrent requests for the same hostname in the same millisecond can collide and overwrite pending entries in `pendingAgents`.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/agent_enrollment.go`:
    - added `buildPendingEnrollmentAssetID` to generate IDs as normalized host component + cryptographic request ID,
    - bounded/sanitized host component for ID safety (`maxPendingHostnameIDLen`, allowed `[a-z0-9._-]`, fallback `unknown`),
    - replaced timestamp-based pending ID generation in `handlePendingEnrollment`.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/agent_enrollment_test.go`:
    - `TestBuildPendingEnrollmentAssetID` validates normalization, unknown-host fallback, max-length truncation, and uniqueness across repeated generations.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestBuildPendingEnrollmentAssetID|TestPendingAgents|TestHandleListPendingAgents|TestVerifyPendingEnrollmentProof|TestHandleApproveAgentRequiresIdentityVerified|TestWithAuthReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator|TestHandleBrowserEventsReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator|TestHandleAgentWebSocketReturnsUnauthorizedWithoutAuthValidator|TestProcessAgentHeartbeatIgnoresPayloadAssetID|TestProcessAgentTelemetryIgnoresPayloadAssetID|TestProcessAgentLogHandlersIgnorePayloadAssetID' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*(Agent|Enrollment|Auth|Browser).*' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-AGENT-03 settings route-shape hardening.
  - Identified route-dispatch correctness gap in `handleAgentSettingsRoutes`:
    - dispatcher accepted extra trailing path segments (for example `/api/v1/agents/node-1/settings/update-agent/extra`) and still executed action handlers.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers.go`:
    - added strict path-segment count guard (`len(parts) > 3` -> `404`) so only exact settings action routes are dispatched.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers_test.go`:
    - `TestHandleAgentSettingsRoutesRejectsExtraPathSegments`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentSettingsUpdateAgentInvalidPayload|TestHandleAgentSettingsUpdateAgentRequiresConnectedAgent|TestHandleAgentSettingsUpdateAgentRequiresAgentManager|TestHandleAgentSettingsRoutesRejectsExtraPathSegments' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*(Agent|Enrollment|Auth|Browser).*' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Services discovery hardening: Traefik route/service correlation correctness.
  - Identified a deterministic mapping gap in proxy enrichment:
    - when multiple containers share the same backend private port (common `:80`), Traefik routes could map to the wrong discovered service due to first-match port bridging.
    - routes without an explicit backend port could fall through to proxy-only service creation despite matching Docker services.
  - Hardening implemented in `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider.go`:
    - added Traefik-only Strategy 0 matching before port strategies:
      - `route.RouterName` -> normalized router key
      - container label extraction from `traefik.http.routers.<name>.*`
      - unique router-to-service index correlation via container IDs.
    - retained existing Strategy 1 (private-port bridging) and Strategy 2 (direct public-port fallback) as secondary paths.
  - Added regression coverage in `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider_test.go`:
    - `TestEnrichServicesTraefikRouterLabelCorrelation` verifies correct service enrichment under shared private-port ambiguity and no-port backend route cases.
    - `TestTraefikRoutersFromLabels` verifies router label parsing/normalization behavior.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Traefik|EnrichServices|Proxy|RoutersFromLabels' -count=1` passed.
    - `go vet ./...` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-SERV-01 manual web-service route-shape hardening.
  - Identified route-dispatch correctness gap in `handleWebServiceManualActions`:
    - manual-service action paths with extra segments (for example `/api/v1/services/web/manual/{id}/extra`) were truncated and still dispatched against `{id}`.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`:
    - replaced prefix-truncation parsing with strict segment parsing (`len(parts) == 1`) so only exact `/api/v1/services/web/manual/{id}` action paths are accepted.
  - Added regression coverage in `cmd/labtether/webservice_api_test.go`:
    - `TestHandleWebServiceManualActionsRejectsExtraPathSegments`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebServiceManualCRUD|TestHandleWebServiceManualActionsRejectsExtraPathSegments|TestHandleWebServicesHiddenFilter|TestHandleWebServiceSyncMethodNotAllowed|TestHandleWebServiceSyncNoConnectedAgents|TestHandleWebServiceSyncHostNotConnected|TestHandleWebServiceSyncInvalidBody' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/webservice -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Agent Settings version card hardening: remove `Unknown`/hash-only confusion.
  - Investigated operator-reported Agent Version card output:
    - `Current: Unknown` occurred when `asset.metadata.agent_version` was absent/stale.
    - `Latest: sha256:...` occurred when no explicit release version env was provided and lookup fell back directly to binary hash.
  - Runtime + transport hardening implemented:
    - `internal/agentcore/config.go` now derives agent version as:
      - semantic version when available,
      - else `git:<short-revision>` (+ `-dirty`),
      - else `dev`.
    - `internal/agentcore/ws_transport.go` now sends `X-Agent-Version` during WS handshake.
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go` now records connection-level `agent_version` metadata from handshake header and heartbeat metadata.
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers.go` now falls back to connected-agent metadata when persisted asset version is missing.
  - Latest-version lookup hardening:
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers.go` now extracts release version from binary build info (semantic or `git:<short-revision>`) before hash fallback.
  - Regression coverage added:
    - `internal/agentcore/config_version_test.go` (`TestDeriveAgentVersionFromBuildInfo`),
    - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers_test.go` (`TestAgentVersionFromBuildInfo`).
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'TestDeriveAgentVersionFromBuildInfo|TestBuildLocalStatus|TestReconnectLoop|TestEnrollment|TestClassifyConnectError' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAgentSettingsUpdateAgent|TestDetermineAgentVersionStatus|TestNormalizeAgentRelease|TestAgentVersionFromBuildInfo|TestHandleAgentSettingsRoutesRejectsExtraPathSegments|TestProcessAgentHeartbeatIgnoresPayloadAssetID' -count=1` passed.
    - `go vet ./...` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-ALERT-03 notification channel route-shape hardening.
  - Identified route-dispatch correctness gap in `handleNotificationChannelActions`:
    - channel action paths with extra segments (for example `/notifications/channels/{id}/extra`) were still treated as channel-id actions.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/notification_handlers.go`:
    - added strict segment parsing guard (`len(parts) == 1`) and explicit empty-id checks so only exact `/notifications/channels/{id}` action paths are dispatched.
  - Added regression coverage in new `cmd/labtether/notification_handlers_test.go`:
    - `TestHandleNotificationChannelActionsRejectsExtraPathSegments`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleNotificationChannelActionsRejectsExtraPathSegments|TestDispatchAlertNotifications_SendsWebhookAndEmail|TestDispatchAlertNotifications_RecordsFailureWhenAdapterSendFails' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-ASSET-01/LT-TELE-01/LT-LOG-01 `siteStore` degraded-runtime hardening.
  - Identified nil-pointer panic risk on site-filter/site-assignment paths:
    - `handleAssets` dereferenced `siteStore` for `site_id` filtering and site-assignment update validation without a nil guard,
    - `handleMetricsOverview` dereferenced `siteStore` for `site_id` filtering without a guard,
    - `handleLogsQuery` and `handleLogSources` dereferenced `siteStore` for `site_id` filters without a guard.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - added explicit `siteStore` availability checks returning `503 site store unavailable` before site lookups in:
      - `cmd/labtether/assets_sites_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/metrics_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/log_handlers.go`
  - Added regression coverage:
    - `TestAssetsSiteOperationsReturnServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore` in `assets_sites_api_test.go`,
    - `TestMetricsOverviewSiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore`,
    - `TestLogsQuerySiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore`,
    - `TestLogSourcesSiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore` in `metrics_logs_api_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestAssetsSiteOperationsReturnServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore|TestAssetsAndMetricsFilterBySite|TestMetricsOverviewSiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore|TestLogsQuerySiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore|TestLogSourcesSiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore|TestMetricsOverview|TestLogsQuery' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestAsset.*|TestMetrics.*|TestLogs.*|TestLogSources.*' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-INC-01 `siteStore` degraded-runtime hardening.
  - Identified nil-pointer panic risk in incident site-context paths:
    - `handleIncidents` list path dereferenced `siteStore` for `site_id` filtering without a nil guard,
    - `validateIncidentReferences` dereferenced `siteStore`/`assetStore` without availability checks, affecting incident create/update when site/asset references are provided.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - added explicit `siteStore` availability guard for `GET /incidents?site_id=...`,
    - added sentinel errors for unavailable incident reference stores in `validateIncidentReferences`,
    - mapped unavailable-store reference validation errors to `503` in incident create/update handlers.
  - Added regression coverage in new `cmd/labtether/incidents_handlers_test.go`:
    - `TestHandleIncidentsListSiteFilterReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore`,
    - `TestHandleIncidentsCreateReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore`,
    - `TestHandleIncidentActionsUpdateReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore`.
  - Validation:

    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test.*Incident.*|Test.*Incidents.*' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Terminal reliability + UX hardening sweep (5-part follow-up completion).
  - SSH startup reliability and performance hardening:
    - implemented strict SSH startup dial policy in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_handlers.go` with per-attempt timeout (`6s`), one retry (`2` total attempts), and bounded retry delay.
    - added explicit stream lifecycle events (`status`/`ready`/`error`) so frontend can surface real connection stages instead of blank cursor waits.
    - added bounded shell-start initialization timeout (`8s`) with explicit user-facing error on startup stall.
  - Agent terminal capability pre-warm:
    - added shared async probe dispatcher `startAgentTmuxProbeAsync(...)` and reused it from `probeAgentTmux(...)`.
    - wired pre-warm call into agent websocket connect path (`cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`) so tmux capability metadata is primed before first terminal launch.
  - Retention prune correctness fix:
    - fixed `alert_silences` retention prune mismatch in `internal/persistence/postgres_runtime_credentials.go` by dynamically selecting `expires_at` or `ends_at` based on live schema columns (eliminates `column "expires_at" does not exist` runtime failures on legacy/current schemas).
  - Frontend connection progress staging:
    - extended `useSession` with phase-aware progress model (`creating-session`, `requesting-ticket`, `opening-stream`, `starting-shell`, `reconnecting`, `connected`) + elapsed timers.
    - enhanced `XTerminal` control-message handling to parse backend stream events and trigger `onStreamStatus` / `onStreamReady`.
    - updated terminal surfaces (`TerminalPane`, `SessionPanel`, node detail `TerminalTab`) to consume staged progress and transition to connected on backend `ready`/first output.
  - Regression coverage expansion:
    - backend tests added in `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_lifecycle_test.go`:
      - `TestStartAgentTmuxProbeAsyncResetsPendingOnSendFailure`
      - `TestDialSSHWithRetryRetriesOnceThenSucceeds`
      - `TestDialSSHWithRetryReturnsLastErrorAfterMaxAttempts`
    - persistence tests added in `internal/persistence/postgres_runtime_credentials_test.go`:
      - `TestAlertSilencePruneColumnPrefersExpiresAtWhenAvailable`
      - `TestAlertSilencePruneColumnFallsBackToEndsAt`
      - `TestAlertSilencePruneColumnHandlesMissingColumns`
    - e2e terminal tests expanded in `web/console/e2e/terminal-workspace.spec.ts`:
      - default tab auto-create path when workspace list is empty
      - staged SSH progress visibility through ready/connected transition
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(StartAgentTmuxProbeAsyncResetsPendingOnSendFailure|DialSSHWithRetryRetriesOnceThenSucceeds|DialSSHWithRetryReturnsLastErrorAfterMaxAttempts|BridgeAgentInputIdleNoPanicAndClosesOnSessionEnd|ProbeAgentTmuxUsesCachedConnectionMetadata|ProcessAgentTerminalProbedCachesProbeResultOnConnection)' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/persistence -run 'Test(AlertSilencePruneColumn|RemoveRecordingFiles)' -count=1` passed.
    - `go vet ./cmd/labtether` passed.
    - `go vet ./internal/persistence` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s test:e2e -- terminal-workspace.spec.ts` passed (`4/4`).

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: actions/updates/settings/security hardening sweep.

  - LT-SET-02 degraded-runtime hardening:

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-JOB-01 dead-letter callback semantics hardening.
  - Identified worker callback coverage/accuracy gap in `internal/jobqueue/worker.go`:
    - final-attempt jobs with unregistered handlers were dead-lettered in queue state but did not trigger dead-letter callback telemetry,
    - callback telemetry could still fire even when `Fail(...)` persistence failed, producing false dead-letter signals.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - introduced `processQueue` seam and `processOneWithQueue(...)` worker path for deterministic unit coverage,
    - no-handler flow now:
      - persists failure state,
      - triggers dead-letter callback on final attempt only when fail persistence succeeds,
    - handler-error flow now suppresses dead-letter callback when fail persistence fails.
  - Regression coverage added in new `internal/jobqueue/worker_process_test.go`:
    - `TestProcessOneWithQueueNoHandlerDeadLetterCallbackOnFinalAttempt`,
    - `TestProcessOneWithQueueNoHandlerDoesNotDeadLetterBeforeMaxAttempts`,
    - `TestProcessOneWithQueueNoHandlerSkipsCallbackWhenFailPersistFails`,
    - `TestProcessOneWithQueueHandlerFailureDeadLettersOnFinalAttempt`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/jobqueue -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-DB-01 startup bootstrap fail-fast hardening.
  - Identified startup correctness gap in admin bootstrap path:
    - `bootstrapAdminUser` logged critical failures (admin lookup/create/hash/update failures and weak production password guard) but returned without error,
    - hub startup (`runHub`) continued even when admin bootstrap failed, risking boot into a state with no valid local admin login path.
  - Hardening implemented:
    - refactored `bootstrapAdminUser` to return explicit errors across critical failure paths,
    - switched bootstrap dependency to interface-backed store contract for focused unit testing,
    - wired `runHub` startup to fail fast when admin bootstrap returns an error.
  - Added regression coverage in new `cmd/labtether/auth_bootstrap_test.go`:
    - `TestBootstrapAdminUserRejectsWeakPasswordInProduction`,
    - `TestBootstrapAdminUserCreatesDevDefaultPasswordWhenMissing`,
    - `TestBootstrapAdminUserSyncsExistingDevAdminPassword`,
    - `TestBootstrapAdminUserReturnsLookupError`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestBootstrapAdminUser.*|TestActionRunQueueUnavailable|TestUpdatePlanCreateAndExecuteQueueUnavailable|TestActionAndUpdateSiteFiltersReturnServiceUnavailableWithoutSiteStore|TestRetentionSettingsReturnServiceUnavailableWithoutRetentionStore|TestHandleAuditEventsReturnsServiceUnavailableWithoutAuditStore|TestHandleWebServiceManualActionsRejectsExtraPathSegments|TestHandleWebServiceManualCRUD|TestHandleWebServiceSyncMethodNotAllowed|TestHandleWebServiceSyncNoConnectedAgents|TestHandleWebServiceSyncHostNotConnected|TestHandleWebServiceSyncInvalidBody|TestHandleWebServicesHiddenFilter' -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/jobqueue -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/persistence -run 'TestPostgresMigrationsRecoverAfterMarkerDeletion|TestPostgresMigrationsCreateCanonicalTables|TestNormalizedSchemaMigrations.*|TestDBStatementTimeout.*' -count=1` passed.

- [2026-02-28] Functionality audit continuation: LT-SET-01 runtime-settings auditability hardening.
  - Identified privileged-change traceability gap:
    - runtime settings mutation paths (`PATCH /settings/runtime`, `POST /settings/runtime/reset`) persisted config changes without emitting audit events.
  - Hardening implemented in `cmd/labtether/settings_handlers.go`:
    - added `settings.runtime.updated` audit event on successful override save,

    - both events include actor context and deterministic affected key metadata (`keys`, `count`).

- [2026-03-01] HTTPS/TLS/WSS audit remediation across hub, console, and agents.
  - Backend websocket origin hardening:
    - switched agent websocket upgrader to same-origin policy for requests that send `Origin`, while preserving non-browser agent compatibility (no Origin header).
    - switched WebRTC signaling websocket upgrader to same-origin checks.
    - expanded response hardening headers with `Referrer-Policy` and restrictive API CSP defaults in secure and redirect handlers.
    - files: `cmd/labtether/agent_ws_handler.go`, `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go`.
  - Frontend websocket correctness fixes:
    - fixed `/api/health` proxy route to call backend `/healthz`.
    - changed `/api/ws/events` route to return `streamPath` + `secure` rather than Docker-internal absolute ws URL.
    - updated browser WS URL builder with secure override and robust port derivation (`3000 -> 8443/8080` dev mapping).
    - wired `secure` handling into terminal/desktop stream-ticket routes and consumers (`useSession`, `StatusContext`).
    - files: `web/console/app/api/health/route.ts`, `web/console/app/api/ws/events/route.ts`, `web/console/app/lib/ws.ts`, `web/console/app/api/terminal/stream-ticket/route.ts`, `web/console/app/api/desktop/stream-ticket/route.ts`, `web/console/app/hooks/useSession.ts`, `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx`.
  - Enrollment correctness fix:
    - preserved request-host enrollment URL behavior while honoring explicit `LABTETHER_EXTERNAL_URL` scheme/host for `hub_api_url` and `hub_ws_url`.
    - file: `cmd/labtether/enrollment_handlers.go`.
  - iOS security/correctness fixes:
    - removed trust-all certificate delegate usage from discovery and realtime websocket paths.
    - made discovered hubs carry explicit URL scheme and updated discovery to be TLS-first with secure dedupe behavior.
    - moved mobile API auth semantics to cookie-based session handling (no bearer header derived from `session_id`).
    - tightened manual URL policy to require `https://` (allow `http://` only for localhost loopback).
    - files: `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Discovery/HubScanner.swift`, `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Realtime/EventSocket.swift`, `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/APIClient.swift`, `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift`, `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/AuthViewModel.swift`, `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/DiscoveryView.swift`.
  - mac-agent default posture hardening:
    - updated default hub URL to `wss://localhost:8443/ws/agent`.
    - removed insecure default API token placeholder.
    - files: `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`.
  - Validation:
    - `go vet ./...` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

- [2026-03-01] mac-agent correctness/performance/security audit remediation (startup + settings regressions).
  - Root-cause fixes for perceived slow startup and broken start-state feedback:
    - duplicate-instance `pgrep` fallback now runs only for non-bundled debug launches (bundle-ID launches skip fallback path),
    - replaced broad `pkill -f` orphan cleanup with absolute-path scoped PID discovery (`ps`) and targeted kills,
    - added explicit `isStarting` lifecycle state and immediate `Starting…` UI feedback in menu + settings controls.
    - files: `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/App.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentProcess.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentStatus.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/MenuBarView.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`.
  - Settings reliability and validation hardening:
    - normalized hub URL handling now accepts `http/https/ws/wss` host inputs and canonicalizes to websocket URL,
    - auto-start path now respects full validation errors and surfaces actionable error text,
    - settings defaults explicitly anchored to `com.labtether.agent` suite with legacy migration retained.
    - file: `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`.
  - Security hardening:
    - migrated `apiToken`/`enrollmentToken` persistence from plain defaults into macOS Keychain storage,
    - added legacy secret migration + cleanup from defaults stores,
    - persisted API token to `LABTETHER_TOKEN_FILE` with locked-down file permissions (`0600`) to reduce env-secret exposure,
    - ensured app-support directory is created/forced with restrictive permissions (`0700`),
    - hardened Screen Sharing admin command username quoting to prevent shell-injection edge cases.
    - files: `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/KeychainSecretStore.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentSettings.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/ScreenSharingMonitor.swift`.
  - UX correctness hardening:
    - Start-at-Login toggle now detects registration failures and reports actionable error when not running as installed `.app`.
    - file: `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/LoginItemManager.swift`, `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/SettingsView.swift`.
  - Validation:
    - `cd apps/mac-agent && swift build` passed after each remediation slice.
    - isolated launch sanity check (`HOME=$(mktemp -d) ./apps/mac-agent/.build/debug/LabTetherAgent`) confirmed process comes up in fresh profile.

- [2026-03-01] mac-agent follow-through: packaged `.app` QA sweep + focused Swift tests.
  - Added targeted testable helpers:
    - `AgentSettingsNormalization` (hub URL canonicalization + docker endpoint validation),
    - `SettingsSecretMigrator` with `SecretStore` abstraction and keychain adapter.
  - Added SwiftPM test target and focused tests:
    - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/AgentSettingsNormalizationTests.swift`,
    - `apps/mac-agent/Tests/LabTetherAgentTests/AgentSettingsMigrationTests.swift`.
  - Validation:
    - `cd apps/mac-agent && swift test` passed (`9 tests`, `0 failures`).
  - Packaged QA run (`make mac-agent`, launch from assembled `.app`) produced this matrix:
    - PASS: fresh launch process startup, no-autostart when no token, duplicate-instance guard, settings persistence across relaunch, stability with `startAtLogin=false`.
    - FAIL (open investigation): configured auto-start did not produce child `labtether-agent` process in non-interactive harness; crash-restart check blocked by missing child process; app-support/token permission checks blocked by missing runtime artifact creation.
  - Additional correction:
    - removed nonsensical `UserDefaults(suiteName: "com.labtether.agent")` legacy probe from migration path to avoid runtime warnings under app-bundle execution.

- [2026-03-01] mac-agent packaged startup fix: orphan process scan deadlock remediation.
  - Root cause:
    - `AgentProcess.orphanedAgentPIDs` launched `/bin/ps` and called `waitUntilExit()` before draining stdout.
    - On larger process tables this can deadlock on pipe buffer backpressure, blocking startup and preventing child agent launch.
  - Fix:
    - drain `ps` pipe (`readDataToEndOfFile`) before `waitUntilExit()`.
    - file: `apps/mac-agent/Sources/LabTetherAgent/AgentProcess.swift`.
  - Verification:
    - packaged `.app` QA matrix now passes end-to-end:
      - `FreshLaunch` PASS
      - `FreshInstallNoTokenNoAutoStart` PASS
      - `DuplicateInstanceGuard` PASS
      - `ConfiguredAutoStart` PASS
      - `LocalAPIReachable` PASS
      - `SettingsPersistenceAcrossRelaunch` PASS
      - `CrashAutoRestart` PASS
      - `AppSupportDirPermissions` PASS (`700`)
      - `TokenFilePermissions` PASS (`600`)
      - `StartAtLoginFalseStable` PASS
    - test/build validation still green: `cd apps/mac-agent && swift test` (10 tests, 0 failures), `swift build`.

- [2026-03-01] terminal workspace UX fix: tab context menu now opens at cursor.
  - Root cause:
    - tab context menu used viewport `clientX/clientY` with a `fixed` overlay rendered inside an animated (`transform`) console wrapper.
    - transformed ancestor changed the positioning containing block, causing visible cursor/menu offset when right-clicking a tab.
  - Fix:
    - switched tab context menu overlay from `fixed` to host-relative `absolute`.
    - added `tabMenuHostRef` and converted click coordinates to host-local space before clamping.
    - clamping now supports explicit bounds so menu placement is correct inside the terminal workspace container.
    - file: `web/console/app/(console)/terminal/page.tsx`.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` edits required (UI-only positioning fix with no API/config/workflow change).

- [2026-03-01] console UX sweep: fixed right-click menu cursor offsets across terminal + files surfaces.
  - Scope:
    - terminal pane context menu (`TerminalPane`) now uses host-relative coordinates and host-bounded clamping.
    - file workspace context menus now use host-relative coordinates and host-bounded clamping in both:
      - `/files` page,
      - node detail `FilesTab`.
    - shared file workspace context menu hook extended to accept optional clamp bounds (`boundsWidth`/`boundsHeight`).
  - Root cause:
    - menus relied on viewport coordinates with `fixed` overlays rendered under transformed page wrappers, producing visual offset from cursor position.
  - Files:
    - `web/console/app/components/terminal/TerminalPane.tsx`
    - `web/console/app/(console)/files/page.tsx`
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/FilesTab.tsx`
    - `web/console/app/(console)/files/useFileWorkspaceState.ts`
  - CrowdSec audit:
    - reviewed codebase for CrowdSec-specific metadata/version parser path; no equivalent parser path currently exists (registry-only service definition), so no parallel parser hardening change was required in this sweep.
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` edits required (UI positioning behavior only; no API/config/workflow change).

- [2026-03-01] agent webservice discovery: ambiguous-port metadata fix.
  - Root cause:
    - service inference by port used first-match registry behavior (`LookupByPort`) even when many known services share the same default port (for example `3000`, `8080`), which could mislabel discovered services.
  - Fix:
    - added `LookupUniqueByPort` in the known-service registry, backed by a unique-port index that only resolves ports used by exactly one service.
    - switched port-scan classification/sorting to unique-port inference so ambiguous ports remain generic (`Port <n>`, empty service/icon keys, `CatOther`).
    - switched proxy-only route labeling to unique-port inference to avoid incorrect service metadata on ambiguous backend ports.
    - files: `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry.go`, `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`, `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider.go`.
  - Regression coverage:
    - added registry tests for unique-port success and ambiguous-port miss.
    - added collector metadata inference test proving ambiguous port `3000` yields generic metadata.
    - added proxy-only service test proving ambiguous backend port `3000` does not assign known service labels.
    - files: `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry_test.go`, `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`, `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` edits required (operator workflow unchanged; fix only prevents incorrect auto-labeling on ambiguous ports).

- [2026-03-01] agent webservice discovery: LabTether fingerprint classification for unresolved scan/proxy services.
  - Root cause:
    - after ambiguous-port hardening, services on shared ports no longer mislabelled, but LabTether endpoints on `:3000` (console) / `:8443` (hub API) stayed generic without a positive fingerprint.
  - Fix:
    - added `labtether` to known service registry (`CatManagement`, `/healthz`, docker image aliases).
    - added runtime fingerprint pass for unresolved `scan`/`proxy` services before health checks.
    - proxy fingerprint probing now prefers `raw_url` / `backend_url` to avoid DNS failures on public proxy hostnames during classification.
    - LabTether detection signatures:
      - backend: `/healthz` or `/version` JSON payload includes `"service":"labtether"`;
      - frontend: `/api/health` JSON shape (`status: ok|error`) plus page marker containing `LabTether` (`/login` or `/`).
    - follow-up hardening: frontend detector now handles proxy-auth-protected `/api/health` (`401 unauthorized`) by using fallback signature (`/login` contains LabTether marker + `/api/auth/login` exists with method-signature status like `405`).
    - added LabTether multi-surface normalization:
      - classify `service_key=labtether` services as `LabTether Console` or `LabTether API` based on URL/backend port and health hints,
      - auto-hide `LabTether API` (`metadata.hidden=true`) when `LabTether Console` is present on the same host so the dashboard defaults to the frontend entry.
    - added hub-side fallback normalization in `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator.go` so existing/stale discovered records are also presented as Console/API with API hidden-by-default even before an updated agent report arrives.
    - applies scheme fallback (`http`/`https`) and self-signed TLS fallback via existing insecure probe client.
    - files: `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry.go`, `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`, `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator.go`.
  - Regression coverage:
    - backend fingerprint classification test (`/healthz` signature),
    - frontend fingerprint classification test (`/api/health` + LabTether UI marker),
    - negative guard test to avoid misclassifying Grafana-like `/api/health` responses.
    - added normalization tests for Console/API differentiation and API auto-hide behavior when Console exists.
    - added coordinator normalization tests (with and without overrides) to ensure server-side presentation remains consistent and user overrides can still unhide API.
    - files: `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`, `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry_test.go`, `internal/connectors/webservice/coordinator_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./internal/connectors/webservice -count=1` passed.
    - live validation (`/api/v1/services/web`): `:3000` and `:8443` now both classify as `LabTether` (`service_key=labtether`) on `Michael-MBP.local`.
    - live validation (`/api/v1/services/web` vs `include_hidden=true`): default list now shows `LabTether Console`; hidden list additionally contains `LabTether API` with `metadata.hidden=true`.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` edits required (discovery behavior correction only; no operator workflow/config/API changes).

- [2026-03-01] agent webservice discovery: connector-compatible API autodiscovery and operator surfacing.
  - Scope:
    - extended agent webservice fingerprint flow to apply compatibility metadata (`compat_connector`, `compat_confidence`, `compat_auth_hint`, `compat_profile`, `compat_evidence`) for discovered `scan`/`proxy`/`docker` services.
    - added compatibility probes and confidence scoring for:
      - Portainer (`/api/status` + UI marker),
      - Home Assistant (`/api` + UI marker),
      - TrueNAS (`/api/v2.0/system/version` + UI marker),
      - Proxmox VE / PBS (`/api2/json/version` + UI markers),
      - Docker Engine API (`/version` and `/_ping` on 2375/2376).
    - added docker API ports (`2375`, `2376`) to webservice scan heuristics and likely web-port classification.
    - added hub endpoint `GET /api/v1/services/web/compat` with host/connector/min-confidence filters and hidden-service filtering behavior aligned with webservice list defaults.
    - added console proxy route `GET /api/services/web/compat`.
    - services UI now renders compatibility badges and expanded details (connector/auth/profile/evidence/confidence) directly from service metadata.
  - Files:
    - agent: `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`, `internal/agentcore/webservice_compat.go`
    - hub: `cmd/labtether/webservice_api.go`, `cmd/labtether/http_handlers.go`
    - console: `web/console/app/api/services/web/compat/route.ts`, `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`
  - Regression coverage:
    - agent compatibility metadata tests added for service-key fast path and Home Assistant probe classification.
    - hub API tests added for `services/web/compat` filtering + validation.
    - files: `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`, `cmd/labtether/webservice_api_test.go`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebServiceCompat|TestHandleWebServiceCompatInvalidMinConfidence|TestHandleWebServicesHiddenFilter' -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - updated `docs/API.md` with service discovery endpoint references.
    - updated `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` Services workflow with compatibility-hint usage.
    - updated wiki:
      - `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
      - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-connector-infrastructure.md`
  - ADR:
    - no architecture-level decision change; existing webservice discovery + connector onboarding architecture remains unchanged.

- [2026-03-02] Services discovery hardening: duplicate suppression + broader image matching.
  - Scope:
    - strengthened known-service image matching by normalizing registry-host-prefixed image references before lookup (supports mirrored paths such as `lscr.io/...`, `ghcr.io/...`, and private `host:port/...` registries).
    - prevented duplicate service cards from port scanning when an existing discovered service already points to the same backend via metadata (`raw_url`, `backend_url`, `public_port`, `private_port`, `alt_urls`).
  - Files:
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`
