  - ADR:
    - added `ADR-023: Service Discovery Identity Normalization` to `docs/ADR.md`.

- [2026-03-02] Services dashboard: bulk Docker image pull action for discovered services.
  - Scope:
    - added one-click **Pull Images** action in Services UI header.
    - action builds a per-host deduplicated pull plan from discovered Docker services and executes Docker `image.pull` for each unique host/image pair.
    - scope honors current host filter and returns operator-visible succeeded/failed/skipped summary.
  - Discovery metadata:
    - Docker-backed discovered services now include `metadata.image` with the exact running container image reference.
    - enables reliable pull planning for the dashboard bulk action.
  - Files:
    - `web/console/app/(console)/services/page.tsx`
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - updated `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` services workflow with Pull Images step.
    - updated `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md` with Pull Images operational step.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture-level change (feature uses existing docker action transport and webservice discovery flow).

- [2026-03-02] Services discovery baseline classification expansion (name/domain fallback).
  - Scope:
    - added normalized service-hint index in known service registry for fallback classification via:
      - service key/name/icon aliases,
      - docker image tail segments,
      - explicit baseline aliases (`npm` -> Nginx Proxy Manager, `mosquitto` -> MQTT, `node-red` -> Node-RED, `code-server` -> code-server).
    - Docker container discovery now falls back to hint classification from container name/image string when exact image mapping misses.
    - proxy-only route discovery now falls back to hint classification from route domain/router name when backend-port lookup is ambiguous/unavailable.
  - Files:
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_registry_test.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`
    - `internal/agentcore/proxy_provider_test.go`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -count=1` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - updated `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` discovery notes.
    - updated `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md` discovery notes.
  - ADR:
    - expanded `ADR-023` decision/rationale/consequences with fallback hint-based classification policy.

- [2026-03-05] iOS bundled web-client vendoring for terminal/desktop reliability.
  - Scope:
    - removed remaining runtime CDN dependencies from iOS embedded terminal/desktop webviews.
    - vendored noVNC/Guacamole/SPICE ESM bundles under app resources and switched `desktop.html` imports to local paths.
    - retained local xterm.js vendoring for terminal and regenerated the Xcode project so all vendor assets are copied into the app bundle.

    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`

    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`
  - Validation:

    - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/StreamURLBuilderTests test` passed.
    - build logs confirm vendor assets copied into app bundle (`novnc-rfb.esm.js`, `guacamole-common.esm.js`, `spice-html5-main.esm.js`).

- [2026-03-05] iOS crash + Live Activities follow-up fixes after on-device regression report.
  - Scope:
    - fixed bundled HTML asset paths for iOS webviews after confirming Xcode resource copy flattening (no nested `vendor/...` folders in app bundle).
    - added missing Face ID privacy usage description to avoid runtime termination when biometric approval is requested for remote access actions.
    - removed nonisolated ActivityKit update helper paths that triggered Swift 6 main-actor/sendability warnings and updated activities via main-actor references directly.

    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`

    - verified built app `Info.plist` contains `NSFaceIDUsageDescription` and `NSSupportsLiveActivities`.

- [2026-03-05] WebSocket LAN stability hardening for iOS terminal/desktop streams.
  - Scope:
    - added shared backend browser-stream websocket keepalive helper with server ping, pong handler, and rolling read deadlines.
    - wired keepalive into terminal/desktop stream bridges (SSH, agent terminal/desktop, docker-exec terminal, direct VNC, SPICE proxy, Proxmox terminal/desktop bridge, and TrueNAS terminal bridge).
    - updated iOS desktop web client reconnect behavior so one-time ticket URLs trigger fresh stream acquisition instead of stale URL reuse.
    - added iOS desktop Swift reconnect orchestration parity with terminal (`ticket_expired` event with bounded backoff).
  - Files:
    - backend:
      - `cmd/labtether/stream_websocket_keepalive.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/stream_websocket_keepalive_test.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_ssh.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_agent_bridge.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/docker_exec_stream_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_agent_bridge.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_routing_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_spice_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_desktop_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_terminal_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/truenas_stream_helpers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/guacd_stream_handlers.go`
    - iOS:
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/desktop.html`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether` passed.
    - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -sdk iphonesimulator -configuration Debug build` passed.
  - Docs/wiki:
    - updated `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md` and `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md` with websocket keepalive + one-time ticket reconnect guidance.
  - ADR:
    - added `ADR-046` documenting websocket liveness policy and one-time ticket reconnect tradeoff.

- [2026-03-05] iOS terminal cursor UX fix (single cursor, no blink).
  - Scope:
    - removed duplicate visible cursor effect in the iOS terminal webview by hiding the native helper-textarea caret.
    - disabled xterm cursor blinking so the terminal keeps a steady cursor indicator.
  - Files:
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`
  - Validation:
    - verified terminal web client config now sets `cursorBlink: false`.
    - verified CSS now suppresses native helper textarea caret (`.xterm-helper-textarea { caret-color: transparent !important; }`).
  - Docs/wiki:
    - no API/architecture/operator workflow change; no `docs/` or `docs/wiki/` update required.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture decision change.

- [2026-03-05] Terminal/desktop websocket liveness hardening follow-up for random iOS SSH exits.
  - Scope:
    - added shared read-deadline refresh helper for browser websocket streams and wired it to all browser->hub stream read loops that use `startBrowserWebSocketKeepalive` (SSH terminal, agent terminal/desktop, docker exec terminal, direct VNC, RDP/guacd, SPICE, Proxmox bridges, TrueNAS terminal).
    - kept existing ping/pong keepalive behavior, but removed dependency on control-frame pong visibility by extending deadlines on each successfully-read browser frame.
    - added targeted backend diagnostics for SSH stream teardown points (browser read, control message processing, stdin write) to improve root-cause visibility in logs.
    - added terminal web-client reconnect telemetry event (`reconnecting` with attempt/delay/close metadata) so iOS connection debug banner captures retry context.
  - Files:
    - backend:
      - `cmd/labtether/stream_websocket_keepalive.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_ssh.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/terminal_stream_agent_bridge.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/docker_exec_stream_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_agent_bridge.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/desktop_stream_routing_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/guacd_stream_handlers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_spice_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_desktop_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/proxmox_stream_terminal_proxy.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/truenas_stream_helpers.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/stream_websocket_keepalive_test.go`
    - iOS:
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Resources/terminal.html`
    - docs/wiki:
      - `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(StartBrowserWebSocketKeepaliveSendsPingAndStopsCleanly|TouchBrowserWebSocketReadDeadlineExtendsDeadline)$'` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether` passed.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture decision change (liveness policy refinement within existing websocket keepalive design).

- [2026-03-05] iOS SSH disconnect trace-correlation instrumentation (phase 1).
  - Scope:
    - added per-session browser stream trace extraction/sanitization (`lt_trace`) on hub side and included trace context in terminal routing fallback logs.
    - wired iOS terminal stream URLs to append a stable trace id per user-initiated connect attempt, then surfaced that trace in terminal debug banner events.
    - added explicit SSH stream teardown reason logging (`reason=...`) for setup failures and runtime exits (websocket close/error/timeout, control channel failure, stdin write failure), each with `session`, `target`, and `trace`.
    - kept reconnect behavior unchanged while improving observability signal for deterministic root-cause isolation.
  - Files:
    - backend:

- [2026-03-05] iOS terminal route lifetime hardening for random `browser_ws_closed_code_1005` drops (phase 3).
  - Scope:
    - correlated user repro trace (`ios-605577c5-e62f-42e8-93b8-7bdd715a15a2`) with hub logs showing `terminal-agent: stream_ended reason=browser_ws_closed_code_1005` after ~11s while agent session remained healthy.
    - identified client-side lifecycle risk from constructing fresh `TerminalViewModel` instances inline at route call sites.
    - introduced `TerminalSessionView` wrapper (inside `TerminalView.swift`) that owns a stable `@State` `TerminalViewModel` instance per presented terminal route lifetime.
    - switched terminal launch call sites to the stable wrapper in node detail, terminal picker, and live-session launch flow.

- [2026-03-05] Agent-backed Linux VNC per-session password hardening (no plaintext-at-rest).

- [2026-03-05] iOS services visibility fix: web-service payload decode contract hardening.
  - Root cause:
    - iOS `WebService` model expected legacy field shapes (`id` as `Int`, `response_time_ms`, `asset_id`, `container_name`) while the hub emits canonical backend fields (`id` string, `response_ms`, `host_asset_id`, `container_id`).
    - strict decode failure on `/api/v1/services/web` caused mobile Services/Operations lists to appear empty.
  - Scope:
    - iOS:
      - hardened `WebService` decode compatibility in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/Models.swift`:
        - accepts string/numeric IDs,
        - supports current + legacy key aliases,
        - derives fallbacks from `metadata` and `health.last_checked_at`.
      - added regression coverage in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests.swift` for both current backend and legacy service payload shapes.
    - docs/wiki/notes:
      - `docs/API.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
      - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-050)
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.2' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/APIClientTests test` passed (`13 tests, 0 failures`).
    - attempted `cd apps/ios-console && swift test`, but it is not valid for this UIKit-based target in this environment (`no such module 'UIKit'`); simulator `xcodebuild` was used as the authoritative validation path.
  - ADR:
    - updated `docs/ADR.md` with ADR-050 (iOS web-service decode compatibility policy).

- [2026-03-05] Linux agent Docker socket root-cause fix (unix socket blocked by outbound URL policy).
  - Scope:
    - reproduced break with a new regression test showing unix-socket Docker ping failed under secure defaults with:
      - `insecure url scheme "http" requires LABTETHER_ALLOW_INSECURE_TRANSPORT=true`.
    - fixed Docker client request path so unix-socket endpoints bypass outbound URL policy checks (local IPC), while remote HTTP(S) Docker endpoints continue using securityruntime policy enforcement.
    - unified case-insensitive `unix://` endpoint handling across:
      - Docker client creation,
      - connector discovery reachability checks,
      - docker endpoint setting normalization,
      - hub-side docker connectivity test command generation.
    - normalized `docker_endpoint` during agent config load with fallback to `/var/run/docker.sock` on invalid values.
  - Files:
    - backend/agent:
      - `internal/agentcore/docker_client.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/connector_discovery.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/settings_definitions.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/config.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_http_handlers.go`
    - tests:
      - `internal/agentcore/docker_client_test.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/settings_definitions_test.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/config_tls_test.go`
      - `cmd/labtether/agent_settings_handlers_test.go`
    - docs/wiki/notes:
      - `docs/API.md`
      - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
      - `docs/ADR.md`
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run "Docker|Config|Normalize" -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run DockerConnectivity -count=1` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `go vet ./...` passed.
  - ADR:
    - updated `docs/ADR.md` with ADR-049 (Docker unix socket IPC policy treatment).

- [2026-03-05] iOS Live Activity memory-guard throttling hardening.
  - Scope:
    - reduced session stale heartbeat publish cadence from 90s to 5 minutes to limit lock-screen extension refresh churn.
    - hardened incident publishing behavior:
      - raised incident hub-sync floor from 12s to 20s,
      - coalesced incident `updatedAt`-only changes behind a 5-minute stale publish window unless material fields changed.
    - stopped scheduling incident live-activity sync from low-signal realtime `heartbeat.update` bus events.
    - added deterministic unit coverage for incident publish coalescing behavior (noop-within-window, stale-window publish, and material-change immediate publish).

## 2026-03-08 (iOS audit remediation hardening)
- Closed the audit findings around transport trust, deep-link mutation auth, logout cleanup, and session invalidation.
  - Scope:
    - flipped `Allow Untrusted TLS` to secure-by-default (`off`) across hub config, settings UI, and terminal/desktop recovery flows.
    - added shell-wide auth invalidation on HTTP 401 so cookie/session expiry now clears local session artifacts and routes back to login cleanly.
    - required local device-owner approval before Live Activity incident acknowledge/resolve actions issue their PATCH requests.
    - deregistered APNs routing and cleared persisted mobile offline queues/audit state on sign-out and hub switch.
    - removed duplicated native reconnect-on-disconnect loops for terminal/desktop so embedded clients own reconnect churn and native code only refreshes one-time stream tickets when necessary.
    - simplified `MainTabView` structure after the new logout/session listeners pushed SwiftUI type checking past compiler limits.
  - Files:
    - iOS app:
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Config/HubConfig.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Networking/APIClient.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Operations/MobileRunbookCenter.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Push/PushManager.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Core/Realtime/EventSocket.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/LabTetherMobileApp.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/MainTabView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/RootView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/AuthViewModel.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Alerts/AlertsViewModel.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Incidents/IncidentsViewModel.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/MoreView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/More/SettingsView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
    - tests:
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/HubConfigTests.swift`
    - docs/wiki/notes:
      - `docs/ADR.md`
      - `docs/SECURITY.md`
      - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
      - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/auth-login-issues.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA'`
      - passed (`170` tests, `0` failures).
  - ADR:
    - `docs/ADR.md`
      - added `ADR-064` for the secure-by-default iOS session hardening decisions.

- [2026-03-06] Agent TLS handshake spam reduction during web-service discovery probes.
  - Scope:
    - identified backend TLS spam source as local macOS agent discovery traffic (`192.168.0.118`) rather than iOS client traffic.
    - changed web-service discovery probe request selection to prefer the insecure probe client for HTTPS discovery/fingerprinting calls, with safe fallback behavior preserved.
    - retained scheme fallback behavior only for non-responsive probes to avoid mixed-scheme churn.
    - added regression coverage to ensure HTTPS discovery probes use the insecure client first in both health and body/fingerprint request paths.
    - verified behavior with timed tmux pane captures:
      - prior `remote error: tls: bad certificate` bursts no longer reproduced,
      - remaining sparse `TLS handshake error ... EOF` events correlate with mac agent reconnect cycles.
  - Files:
    - backend agent runtime:
      - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/webservice_fingerprinting.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/webservice_collector_test.go`
    - notes:
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore` passed.
    - `make mac-agent` passed.
    - backend tmux pane capture before/after confirmed elimination of `bad certificate` spam.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture decision change (discovery probe client selection and logging-noise reduction only).

- [2026-03-06] Final `TLS handshake error ... EOF` cleanup on mac agent connect path.
  - Scope:
    - traced remaining sparse backend TLS EOF lines to service-discovery local port scan reachability checks (`connect` + immediate `close`) against HTTPS listeners.
    - updated scan reachability probe behavior to send a TLS ClientHello on likely HTTPS ports before closing, preventing handshake-EOF log noise on TLS-enabled services while preserving open-port detection.
    - added focused unit coverage for scan probe transport behavior:
      - TLS-preferred probe writes handshake bytes,
      - plain TCP probe remains payload-free.
    - verified with timed backend tmux-pane captures after mac-agent rebuild/restart:
      - no `TLS handshake error`, `bad certificate`, or HTTP-on-HTTPS spam observed across multiple scan intervals.
  - Files:
    - backend agent runtime:
      - `internal/agentcore/webservice_scan_discovery.go`
      - `internal/agentcore/webservice_scan_discovery_test.go`
    - docs/wiki:
      - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
      - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
    - notes:
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./internal/agentcore` passed.
    - `make mac-agent` passed.
    - `make build-agent-linux` passed.
    - backend capture file `/tmp/backend-pane-eof-final.log` stayed free of TLS handshake errors during active mac-agent runtime.
    - Linux endpoint rollout/restart pending on affected assets.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture decision change (scan probe transport behavior only).

- [2026-03-08] iOS UI chrome glitch pass: removed grey safe-area/nav-band fallbacks across mobile routes.
  - Scope:
    - normalized premium navigation chrome coverage across the iOS console so pushed screens, sheets, and full-screen session views stop falling back to default UIKit grey bars.
    - expanded the shared app backdrop to render in both dark and light/system appearance modes, preventing grouped-background bleed-through around the top safe area.
  - iOS app:
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/ViewModifiers.swift`
      - added `premiumScreenBackground()` for full-screen backdrop coverage.
      - made `premiumNavBar()` and `premiumTabBar()` keep their material backgrounds visible and adapt toolbar color scheme to the active appearance.
      - updated `premiumList()` to use the shared full-screen backdrop.
    - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/Components.swift`
      - extended `LTGradientBackground` and `LTDotGrid` with light-mode rendering so system/light appearance no longer exposes plain grey UIKit surfaces.
    - feature screens:
      - applied shared screen background coverage to discovery, alerts, devices, services, logs, files, incidents, terminal picker, and command palette surfaces.
      - applied `premiumNavBar()` to previously unstyled routes including files, command palette, terminal, desktop, sites, updates, actions, recordings, Docker/service/agent detail flows, and the debug stress harness.
      - set `Home` and `More` to inline navigation titles after simulator review showed those routes still leaving an oversized empty top band.
  - Simulator sweep:
    - ran authenticated screenshot checks against the local hub (`https://localhost:8443`, dev login `admin/password`) across light/dark appearances and all top-level tabs.
    - confirmed the original grey/nav-fallback issue was cleared on the top-level routes; the only follow-up regression found was the oversized top band on `Home`/`More`, fixed in the same pass.
  - Validation:
    - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=1E62B633-7517-44F9-9A3C-FD0E56C01F6F' -quiet` passed.
    - Xcode emitted pre-existing warnings in Live Activities and several mobile tests; no new build failures were introduced by the UI chrome changes.
  - Docs/wiki rationale:
    - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this was a presentation-only polish/fix with no workflow, setup, API, or architecture change.

- [2026-03-08] Alerts console rule-builder pass.
  - Scope:
    - turned the existing alerts rules tab into a working console rule-creation flow and moved the starter presets into the shared backend alert-template catalog.
  - Frontend:
    - fixed alert rule severities to match backend values (`critical`, `high`, `medium`, `low`) instead of the stale `warning`/`info` UI-only values.
    - updated rule creation payloads to use the backend `targets` contract for asset/site scoped rules.
    - switched the rules tab from hardcoded starter presets to the backend `/alerts/templates` catalog so the UI reflects shared templates instead of local-only presets.
    - wired the rules form to the live inventory so site/asset-scoped rules can pick targets from current sites/assets instead of relying on manual raw IDs in the common case.
    - corrected composite rule payload generation to send `rule_ids` and the selected `and`/`or` operator.
  - Backend:
    - expanded the alert template catalog with starter templates for `Agent Offline`, `CPU Saturation`, `Memory Pressure`, `Disk Nearly Full`, and `Error Burst`.
    - extended template enable requests to accept target overrides for site/asset-scoped templates.
    - changed template dedupe matching from `template_id` only to `template_id + target set`, so starter templates can be enabled for multiple assets without collapsing into one global rule.
  - Docs/wiki/notes:
    - updated `README.md`, `docs/OPERATIONS.md`, `docs/wiki/core-workflows/alerts-workflow.md`, `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`, and `notes/TODO.md`.
  - Validation:
    - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleAlertTemplatesListsMobileTemplates|TestHandleAlertTemplateActionsEnableCreatesAndDeDupeRules|TestHandleAlertTemplateActionsEnableServiceTemplate|TestHandleAlertTemplateActionsEnableStarterTemplateForAssetTargets' -count=1` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run lint` passed.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture change; this was a console workflow and UX alignment pass on existing alerting APIs.

- [2026-03-08] iOS performance hardening wave: logs, shell lifecycle, and remote-session cleanup.
  - Scope:
    - reduced iOS live logs cost by switching to compact `/logs/query` payloads, lengthening the live poll interval, lowering retained event volume, and removing the heavier live-row chrome.
    - coalesced shell foreground resume work in `MainTabView`, delayed realtime disconnect on background, and limited realtime handler registration to the visible tab so hidden Alerts/Devices/Services flows stop refreshing in the background.
    - made terminal/desktop reconnects lifecycle-bound, cancelled banner/live-activity helper tasks on dismissal, serialised connect flows in the remote-session view models, and moved terminal input-accessory suppression out of the per-update path.
    - batched desktop JS action injection so active keyboard/pointer helpers stop issuing one `evaluateJavaScript` call per queued action.
    - gated the remaining repeat-forever upgrade/status glow animations behind the same reduce-motion / low-power policy used by the rest of the iOS shell.
  - Files:
    - iOS app:
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Logs/LogsView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Logs/LogsViewModel.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/MainTabView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalViewModel.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopViewModel.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/RootView.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/Components.swift`
      - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/ViewModifiers.swift`
    - docs/wiki/notes:
      - `docs/ADR.md`
      - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
      - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA'` passed (`178 tests`, `0 failures`).
  - ADR:
    - documented in `ADR-066` (`iOS Performance Guardrails Prefer Visible-Tab Refresh, Compact Live Logs, And Lifecycle-Bound Remote Recovery`).

- [2026-03-06] Alternative URLs feature (replacing Merge).
  - Scope:
    - replaced the "merge" concept in web services with "alternative URLs" — a conceptual redesign where services can have multiple access URLs that are persisted in the database.
  - Database:
    - 2 new migrations (44-45) creating `web_service_alt_urls`, `web_service_url_grouping_settings`, and `web_service_never_group_rules` tables.
    - migration 45 copies old runtime settings (`services.merge_*`) into new DB tables and removes old keys.
  - Backend:
    - renamed all merge files/types/functions to URL grouping (`webservice_url_grouping_*.go`).
    - added persistence layer for alt URLs, grouping settings, and never-group rules.
    - added new API endpoints for alt URL CRUD (`POST/DELETE /api/v1/services/web/{id}/alt-urls`), never-group rules, and grouping settings.
    - added suggestion tracking for below-threshold matches with accept/deny endpoints.
  - Frontend:
    - renamed all merge components/types to URL grouping (`servicesURLGroupingRules.ts`, `ServiceURLGroupingSettingsModal.tsx`).
    - updated ServiceEditModal with alt URLs array, source badges, add/remove controls.
    - simplified grouping settings modal (removed dry-run, force-merge).
    - added toast notifications with accept/deny for grouping suggestions.
  - Cleanup:
    - removed old merge runtime setting definitions.
    - updated config loader to read from new DB tables.
    - fixed duplicate function build error.
  - Files:
    - backend:
      - `cmd/labtether/webservice_url_grouping_*.go` (config, identity, mutation, settings, suggestions)
      - `internal/persistence/postgres_web_service_alt_urls.go`
      - `internal/persistence/postgres_web_service_url_grouping.go`
      - `services/migrator/migrations/000044_*.sql`, `services/migrator/migrations/000045_*.sql`
    - frontend:
      - `web/console/app/(console)/services/servicesURLGroupingRules.ts`
      - `web/console/app/(console)/services/ServiceURLGroupingSettingsModal.tsx`
      - `web/console/app/(console)/services/ServiceEditModal.tsx`
    - docs/wiki/notes:
      - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/services-workflow.md`
      - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
      - `docs/wiki/reference/environment-variables.md`
      - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
      - `notes/TODO.md`
  - Validation:
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
    - `go vet ./cmd/labtether` passed.
  - ADR:
    - no architecture decision change (feature redesign within existing services subsystem; merge -> URL grouping rename with database persistence replacing runtime settings).
  - Commits: 12 on `feat/alternative-urls` branch.

- [2026-03-09] Device hierarchy feature: replace sites with hierarchical groups.
  - Scope:
    - replaced flat `sites` table with recursive `groups` table supporting unlimited nesting via `parent_group_id` self-reference.
    - added group CRUD REST API, recursive tree queries, bulk asset move, link suggestions, and auto-detection.
    - added `contains` dependency type for infrastructure containment in topology.
    - updated console devices page with hierarchical tree UI, inline group management, and drag-and-drop reordering.
    - updated topology page to consume group hierarchy for layout.
    - updated iOS app and Home Assistant integration for `site_id` to `group_id` rename.
  - Database:
    - migration creating `groups` table (self-referencing `parent_group_id`, `sort_position`, `location`, `icon`).
    - migration creating `asset_link_suggestions` table for cross-connector auto-detection.
    - migration renaming `asset.site_id` to `asset.group_id` and converting site rows to group rows.
    - added `contains` as a valid `asset_dependencies.dependency_type`.
  - Backend:
    - `internal/persistence/`: `GroupStore` interface and Postgres implementation (CRUD, tree query, reorder, move).
    - `internal/persistence/`: `LinkSuggestionStore` for cross-connector link suggestion persistence.
    - `cmd/labtether/`: group HTTP handlers (CRUD, tree, move, reorder), link suggestion handlers (list, accept/dismiss, manual create), bulk asset move handler.
    - `cmd/labtether/`: auto-detect link suggestions during connector discovery polls (IP/hostname/MAC matching).
    - `cmd/labtether/`: sites redirect handler (`GET /sites` -> 301 `/groups`).
    - removed `internal/sites/` package and `SiteStore` interface.
  - Frontend:
    - `web/console/app/console/models.ts`: `Group` type replaces `Site`, added `LinkSuggestion` type.
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/`: hierarchical device tree with collapsible groups, manage mode (create/edit/delete/reorder groups, move assets), link suggestions banner.
    - `web/console/app/(console)/topology/`: topology page consumes group tree for containment layout.
    - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/`: device detail panels updated for `group_id`.
  - iOS:
    - `apps/ios-console/`: `group_id`/`group_name` replaces `site_id`/`site_name` across models, views, and view models.
  - Home Assistant:
    - `integrations/homeassistant/`: coordinator, sensors, and entity base updated for `group_id`/`group_name`.
  - Docs:
    - `docs/API.md`: documented group CRUD, tree, link suggestion, and bulk move endpoints.
    - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: updated package tree (`sites/` -> `groups/`), added hierarchical device organization section.
    - `docs/ADR.md`: ADR-063 documenting sites-to-groups migration decision.
  - Validation:
    - `go vet ./...` passed.
    - `go test ./...` passed.
    - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` passed.
  - Commits: 14+ on `device-hierarchy` worktree.

### iOS Tailscale TLS Login Fix (2026-03-08)
- Investigated on-device iOS login failure with `Allow Untrusted TLS` enabled against a Tailscale hub saved as raw `100.100.216.70`.
- Root cause: discovery/auth flow still treated a raw Tailscale IP as "already Tailscale" and therefore skipped upgrading to an advertised MagicDNS `*.ts.net` hostname; discovered hubs also persisted `address` instead of preferring the advertised hostname when available.
- Fixes shipped:
  - `DiscoveredHub.url` now prefers an advertised `*.ts.net` hostname for Tailscale discoveries.
  - `AuthViewModel.preferredHubURL(...)` now upgrades raw Tailscale IP URLs to advertised MagicDNS hostnames.
  - `login()` now applies the preferred host upgrade before constructing the API client, so existing saved raw-IP configs self-correct on sign-in.
- Added iOS regression coverage:
  - `HubScannerTests.testTailscaleHubPrefersMagicDNSHostnameWhenAvailable`
  - `AuthViewModelTests.testPreferredHubURLUpgradesTailscaleIPAddressToMagicDNSHostname`
- Updated troubleshooting docs for iOS Tailscale TLS/MagicDNS recovery guidance.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/HubScannerTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests`
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA'`
  - both passed; Xcode still emits unrelated passcode-protected physical-device service warnings while simulator tests succeed.
### iOS macOS VNC Credential Prompt Fix (2026-03-09)
- Investigated iOS remote-desktop failures against macOS agents where Screen Sharing required interactive credentials but the iOS viewer only surfaced a generic `Desktop credentials required` error.
- Root cause: the bundled iOS `desktop.html` handled noVNC `credentialsrequired` by auto-submitting only password-only tickets and otherwise falling straight into a generic error path; there was no native username/password prompt bridge for macOS Screen Sharing auth challenges.
- Fixes shipped:
  - `desktop.html` now forwards `credentialsrequired` metadata (`types`, username requirement, password presence) back to Swift and exposes `window.desktopAPI.sendCredentials(...)`.
  - `DesktopViewModel` now tracks pending desktop credential requests and keeps the session in a reconnect/authenticating posture while credentials are pending instead of immediately dropping into the generic error screen.
  - `DesktopView` now presents a native inline authentication prompt for username/password or password-only VNC challenges and forwards submitted credentials into the active noVNC session.
  - `IOSProfileHarnessView` was updated for the new `DesktopWebView` credential-submission binding.
- Added/updated regression coverage in `WebViewReconnectTests` for:
  - desktop credential bridge script generation,
  - credential-request state capture,
  - error handling while a credential prompt is pending,
  - clearing the pending prompt after a successful connect event.
- Updated desktop troubleshooting docs with the new macOS VNC credential prompt recovery path.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests`
  - `xcodebuild build -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA'` passed.
  - the targeted `WebViewReconnectTests` suite now launches and the new credential-flow assertions execute, but the overall run still fails in this simulator environment after repeated crashes in `testDesktopCoordinatorReconnectsWhenSignatureChanges` and `testDesktopCoordinatorReconnectsWhenVNCPasswordChanges` (`objc: Attempt to use unknown class ...` during WKWebView/coordinator execution).
  - follow-up needed: isolate the simulator/WebKit crash in those reconnect tests and confirm the new native prompt on a physical iPhone/iPad against a macOS Screen Sharing target.
### iOS Terminal Previous Sessions Simplification (2026-03-09)
- Reworked the iOS terminal launcher after operator feedback that the saved SSH flow was too model-heavy and made users think in terms of app sections instead of "pick up where I left off."
- Root cause: the prior `Terminal Connections Hub` split terminal entry into Favorites, Recent Connections, Saved Profiles, and All Nodes even though the data already supported a simpler previous-sessions mental model.
- Fixes shipped:
  - `TerminalNodePickerView` now leads with a single `Previous Sessions` list that automatically merges recent history, saved terminal configs, pins, and local session labels, with `Browse Nodes` as the secondary list.
  - row actions now support `Rename`, `Pin/Unpin`, `Save/Edit Connection`, and `Delete Session`, while the hero card resumes the most recent session directly.
  - `TerminalConnectionsStore` now supports removing recent entries and clearing local session presentation metadata.
  - `TerminalConnectionsViewModel` now exposes merged previous-session ordering, browse-only rows, local rename handling, and delete orchestration.
  - `APIClient` and the hub now support deleting per-asset terminal configs through `DELETE /assets/{id}/terminal/config`, so removed saved sessions do not reappear after refresh.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `TerminalConnectionsStoreTests` now cover removing recents and clearing local session presentation metadata.
  - `TerminalConnectionsViewModelTests` now cover previous-session ordering and local session deletion cleanup.
  - backend API coverage now verifies create/get/delete/get for asset terminal configs.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/terminal-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/start-here/navigation-map.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run TestCredentialProfilesCreateRotateAndAssetTerminalConfig` passed.
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalConnectionsStoreTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalConnectionsViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests` passed.
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is a UX simplification and CRUD completion within the existing terminal/session architecture, not a new architectural direction.
### iOS Terminal Visual Consistency Pass (2026-03-09)
- Tightened the new `Previous Sessions` screen so it feels more in-family with the rest of the iOS app instead of reading like a one-off utility surface.
- Refinements shipped:
  - replaced the hero's health-orb treatment with a terminal `LTIconBox` so the top card uses the same icon language seen across `More`, `Nodes`, and other premium cards.
  - removed the redundant `Pinned` chip because the pin glyph already carries that state, reducing row noise.
  - changed the previous-session overflow action from a full-width `More` button to an icon-only menu button, matching existing list/action patterns elsewhere in the app.
  - converted destructive action feedback in the hero from plain red text into the same capsule notice language used across other status surfaces.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalConnectionsStoreTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalConnectionsViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/TerminalViewModelTests` passed.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` change required because this pass only refines visual presentation and interaction weight; the terminal workflow and capabilities are unchanged.
### iOS Terminal Previous Sessions Follow-up Polish (2026-03-09)
- Closed the remaining UX gaps in the new `Previous Sessions` launcher so the behavior matches the visual emphasis.
- Fixes shipped:
  - pinned sessions now sort ahead of ordinary recents, so `Pin` actually keeps important hosts prominent instead of letting newer unpinned sessions push them down.
  - session removal is now split into `Delete Everywhere` and `Remove From This iPhone`, with local hidden-state tracking so hub-backed saved sessions stay dismissed locally until the operator reconnects or saves them again.
  - the icon-only session overflow control now has an explicit accessibility label and hint for VoiceOver.
  - saving or reconnecting a previously removed session clears the local hidden flag so the item can naturally return to `Previous Sessions`.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `TerminalConnectionsStoreTests` now cover hidden-session clearing when a recent connection is recorded again.
  - `TerminalConnectionsViewModelTests` now cover pinned ordering priority and local-only removal for saved sessions.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/terminal-workflow.md`
  - `docs/wiki/start-here/navigation-map.md`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this is still a launcher UX and local-state refinement within the existing terminal/session architecture.
### iOS TLS Trust Helper + Advanced WebRTC Parity (2026-03-09)
- Closed the remaining iOS login trust follow-up and the advanced remote-viewer WebRTC parity items from `notes/TODO.md`.
- iOS login trust helper:
  - added a login-screen `Trust CA` action beside the `Allow Untrusted TLS` toggle in `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Auth/LoginView.swift`.
  - the helper now opens the hub CA download path (`/api/v1/tls/ca.crt`) and shows the iPhone profile-install and certificate-trust steps in-app so the safer fix is one tap away.
- Remote viewer WebRTC parity:
  - `web/console/app/components/WebRTCViewer.tsx` now renders multi-display Linux WebRTC desktops as a stitched layout using agent-reported display geometry.
  - WebRTC stats now classify the selected candidate-pair route (`direct`, `reflexive`, or `relay`) so the viewer can surface route-aware transport labels.
  - `web/console/app/hooks/useProtocolSwitch.ts` now auto-demotes degraded relay-backed WebRTC sessions to VNC faster than direct paths and can recover back to WebRTC automatically after the fallback session stabilizes, unless the operator has explicitly pinned a protocol.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/DesktopTab.tsx`, `RemoteViewerShell.tsx`, and `SessionPanel.tsx` now thread stitched-layout geometry and route-aware transport labels through the desktop viewer shell.
- Added regression coverage:
  - `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts` now verifies stitched WebRTC multi-monitor rendering and relay-backed WebRTC fallback/recovery behavior.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/auth-login-issues.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/webrtc.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/multi-monitor-and-file-transfer.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/protocol-selection.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md` (ADR-062 consequences/decision expanded for stitched layout + auto-recovery)
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts --grep "relay-backed WebRTC|stitched layout|protocol switching hides stale display"`
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/AuthViewModelTests -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/HubConfigTests`
### iOS WebView Reconnect Test Stabilization (2026-03-09)
- Closed the remaining simulator-only `WebViewReconnectTests` crash follow-up for the desktop reconnect coordinator cases.
- Fixes shipped:
  - introduced a lightweight `LTScriptEvaluating` bridge in the iOS app so reconnect coordinator logic can target any JavaScript evaluator instead of requiring a concrete `WKWebView`.
  - updated `DesktopWebView.Coordinator.connectIfNeeded(...)` and `TerminalWebView.Coordinator.connectIfNeeded(...)` to use the evaluator bridge for connect/disconnect script emission.
  - replaced the reconnect test harness `RecordingWKWebView` subclass with a plain `RecordingScriptEvaluator`, removing the need to instantiate real WebKit views for coordinator-only assertions.
- Why this fixes the simulator crash:
  - the failing cases were only asserting emitted JavaScript and signature churn behavior; they did not need WebKit rendering, navigation, or a live `WKWebView` object.
  - by removing the real `WKWebView` subclass from those tests, the suite no longer depends on simulator WebKit Objective-C class state for pure coordinator logic.
- Updated files:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Terminal/TerminalView.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests.swift`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were needed because this change only stabilizes internal iOS unit-test harnessing and does not change shipped user or API behavior.
- Validation:
  - `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj -scheme LabTetherMobile -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=8C306CA5-2E92-40C6-B9BD-37B80B0585BA' -only-testing:LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests`
  - repeated targeted simulator runs of `LabTetherMobileTests/WebViewReconnectTests` to confirm the reconnect cases no longer rely on a live `WKWebView` instance.
### iOS WebView Helper Extraction Cleanup (2026-03-09)
- Followed up on the reconnect stabilization by moving the shared `LTScriptEvaluating` bridge into a dedicated iOS runtime file.
- Updated:
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/UI/WebViewScriptEvaluating.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile/Features/Desktop/DesktopView.swift`
  - `apps/ios-console/LabTetherMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`
  - `notes/PROGRESS_LOG.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - terminal and desktop now share the evaluator bridge from a neutral `UI` location instead of depending on a helper nested inside the desktop feature implementation.
  - `WebViewReconnectTests` continue to target the same shared bridge without coupling the test harness to desktop-owned code.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were needed because this is an internal iOS code-organization cleanup with no workflow, API, or architecture behavior change.
- Validation:
  - reran the targeted iOS `WebViewReconnectTests` suite after moving the helper into the shared file.
### iOS VNC Repro Capture Workflow Hardening (2026-03-09)
- Turned the open iOS VNC black-screen follow-up into a repeatable capture workflow instead of relying on ad hoc `/tmp` log scrapes.
- Fixes shipped:
  - added `scripts/capture-vnc-repro.sh`, which snapshots the target asset state, frontend/backend tmux panes, optional desktop smoke output, and macOS unified logs into a dated report immediately after a repro.
  - the helper accepts copied iOS Connection Debug Banner lines (`--banner-line` / `--banner-file`) and auto-detects `trace=ios-...` when present so hub `desktop-agent` lines can be matched directly.
  - the report now starts with a `summary` section that surfaces the latest banner line, the key backend connect/close lines, the strongest macOS `:5900` / `screensharingd` evidence, and a best-effort likely close-source label.
  - `DesktopViewModel.buildDesktopWebSocketURL(...)` now appends desktop `lt_trace` values, and `DesktopView` now includes that same trace in the iOS rolling debug banner to give the client and hub a shared join key during black-screen triage.
  - extended `StreamURLBuilderTests` with desktop trace-query coverage.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/OPERATIONS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/ADR.md` change required because this work adds operator diagnostics and trace correlation on top of the existing desktop stream architecture; it does not change protocol, auth, or system design.
- Validation:
  - `bash -n scripts/capture-vnc-repro.sh`
  - `./scripts/capture-vnc-repro.sh --help`
  - targeted iOS tests covering the desktop stream URL builder.
  - live baseline capture against `Michael-MBP.local` now produces a top-of-report summary that surfaces the key `desktop-agent: stream_connected`, `desktop-agent: stream_ended reason=browser_ws_closed_code_1006`, and matching macOS `:5900` / `screensharingd` clues without scanning the raw sections first.
### Console Playwright Self-Signed HTTPS Harness (2026-03-09)
- Added a dedicated self-signed HTTPS Playwright mode for the console so browser coverage can run against `https://127.0.0.1` with an invalid local certificate instead of only plain HTTP.
- Updated:
  - `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
  - `web/console/playwright.config.ts`
  - `web/console/package.json`
  - `web/console/scripts/start-playwright-https.mjs`
  - `web/console/e2e/certs/playwright-selfsigned-cert.pem`
  - `web/console/e2e/certs/playwright-selfsigned-key.pem`
  - `web/console/e2e/resilience.spec.ts`
  - `web/console/e2e/helpers/consoleApiMocks.ts`
  - `web/console/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts`
  - `web/console/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts-snapshots/visual-settings-dark-chromium-darwin.png`
  - `web/console/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts-snapshots/visual-settings-light-chromium-darwin.png`
  - `web/console/e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts-snapshots/visual-settings-oled-chromium-darwin.png`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodePanelRenderers.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodePanelRenderContext.ts`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx`
- Result:
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_SELF_SIGNED_HTTPS=1` now boots the standalone console behind a local HTTPS proxy with a bundled self-signed cert and enables Playwright certificate bypass on both the browser and web-server readiness path.
  - same-origin mutation coverage now works under HTTPS because the proxy forwards the external host/protocol headers expected by the CSRF/origin guard.
  - the visual harness now mocks `/api/alerts/templates`, and the small settings screenshot drift was re-baselined after confirming it was stable, low-pixel noise rather than a layout regression.
  - GitHub Actions now uploads Playwright HTML/raw artifacts for the standard `web-e2e` lane as well as the separate `web-e2e-https` and `web-visual-https` gates, so HTTP and HTTPS failures can all be inspected directly from Actions.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were needed because this change only affects internal frontend test infrastructure and local validation workflows, not shipped operator behavior or public architecture.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run build`
  - `cd web/console && npx eslint --no-ignore --no-warn-ignored 'playwright.config.ts' 'scripts/start-playwright-https.mjs' 'e2e/resilience.spec.ts' 'e2e/helpers/consoleApiMocks.ts' 'e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/nodePanelRenderers.tsx' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useNodePanelRenderContext.ts' 'app/(console)/nodes/[id]/page.tsx'`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_SELF_SIGNED_HTTPS=1 npx playwright test e2e/visual-regression.spec.ts`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts e2e/resilience.spec.ts`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_SELF_SIGNED_HTTPS=1 npx playwright test e2e/console.spec.ts e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts e2e/resilience.spec.ts`
### Outbound Private Target Runtime Setting (2026-03-09)
- Followed up on the secure private-HTTPS outbound policy change so operators can control it from the hub UI instead of only through env files.
- Updated:
  - `internal/securityruntime/command.go`
  - `internal/securityruntime/outbound_test.go`
  - `internal/runtimesettings/types.go`
  - `internal/runtimesettings/types_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/securityruntime_runtime_state.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/securityruntime_runtime_state_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/startup_bootstrap.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/settings_api_test.go`
  - `docs/API.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - added a tri-state Advanced Settings entry, `security.outbound_allow_private`, with `auto`, `true`, and `false` modes.
  - the hub now refreshes that runtime override into `internal/securityruntime` so outbound connector validation changes take effect without editing `.env`.
  - `auto` preserves the current homelab-friendly default for secure `https://` / `wss://` private targets, while `false` restores the strict deny-private posture.
  - verified the restarted live hub no longer rejects `https://proxmox-deltaserver.simbaslabs.com` as a private host; the request now advances to the real network dial stage instead.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/securityruntime ./internal/runtimesettings ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(RuntimeSettingsGetUpdateAndReset|BuildSecurityRuntimeEnvOverrides)' -count=1`
  - live backend restart via `make dev-backend-stop` and `make dev-backend-bg`
  - live probe: `POST https://localhost:8443/connectors/proxmox/test` with `base_url=https://proxmox-deltaserver.simbaslabs.com` now returns a network dial error instead of `outbound private host ... is not allowed`

- Fixed the Portainer add flow so a successful test now proves the credential can list Portainer environments/endpoints instead of only reaching the version endpoint.

- Result:
  - Portainer connection tests now fail early when the API key can hit `/api/system/version` but cannot list environments/endpoints or sees zero visible endpoints, which matches the collector's real discovery requirements.
  - the console no longer forces a Proxmox-style `token_id` format for Portainer; Token ID is now an optional label, and saves only require the base URL plus API key secret.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/CONNECTORS.md` and `docs/wiki/connectors/portainer.md` because this changes shipped operator behavior and troubleshooting guidance.

- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:

  - no `docs/ADR.md` update was needed because this is a presentation/naming fix inside the existing collector model.

### Hub Collector Isolation And Partial Sync Outcomes (2026-03-09)
- Reworked hub collector execution so one degraded connector run does not fan out into unrelated device/connector failures.
- Updated:
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_runner.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_handlers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_lifecycle_helpers.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_pbs_exec.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_connector_exec_portainer.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_connector_exec_proxmox.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_truenas_exec.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_homeassistant_exec.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/connector_test_handlers_pbs.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_lifecycle_helpers_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_runner_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/connector_handlers_pbs_test.go`
  - `cmd/labtether/hub_collector_runner_truenas_collector_test.go`
  - `web/console/app/components/AddDeviceModal/collectorSync.ts`
  - `docs/CONNECTORS.md`
  - `docs/wiki/connectors/overview.md`
  - `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-connector-infrastructure.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/connector-sync-failures.md`
  - `docs/ADR.md`
  - `notes/TODO.md`
- Result:
  - due collectors now dispatch through a bounded concurrent lane instead of the background loop running every due collector strictly serially.
  - duplicate-run protection remains per collector ID, so concurrent dispatch improves fleet isolation without allowing overlap on the same collector.
  - PBS, Portainer, Proxmox, TrueNAS, and Home Assistant collectors now classify reachable-but-empty inventory and partial persistence as `partial` instead of collapsing them into hard `error` status.
  - manual sync toasts now distinguish warning completion from true failure.
  - PBS connector test now preflights datastore visibility, warning when a token authenticates but sees zero datastores.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/CONNECTORS.md` plus operator-facing wiki pages because this changes shipped collector semantics and troubleshooting guidance.
  - updated `docs/ADR.md` because bounded concurrent dispatch plus `partial` collector outcomes is an architecture/runtime policy decision rather than a one-off connector fix.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(CollectorLifecycle.*|RunPendingCollectorsStartsDueCollectorsConcurrently|HandlePBSConnectorTest.*|ExecuteTrueNASCollectorNoAssetsAndSuccess|ExecuteTrueNASCollectorNoAssetsLog|ExecuteTrueNASCollectorNoAssetsStatus)' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(ExecuteCollector.*|RunPendingCollectors.*|HandlePBSConnectorTest.*|ExecuteTrueNASCollector.*)' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
### Development Repo Relocation And Backend Compile Recovery (2026-03-11)
- Moved the LabTether working tree out of `~/Documents` into `/Users/michael/Development/LabTether` to stop dev/build churn from living under CloudDocs-managed storage.
- Restored backend compilation for the partially landed site/failover feature slice by:
  - adding missing persistence contracts for reliability history, sites, site profiles, and failover pairs
  - restoring backward-compatible `asset.site_id` reads from `GroupID` for stale site feature call sites
  - removing a duplicate `loadUpdatePlansByID` implementation
  - updating site reliability/timeline log queries to the current `logs.QueryRequest` shape
  - adding the missing site coordinate validation helper
- Result:
  - `make dev-backend` compiles and serves again from the new repo path.
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether/...` passes again.
  - frontend continues serving from `https://localhost:3000`.
  - `make dev-backend-bg` now uses a real PID-file stop/restart path on macOS and waits for the backend listener before reporting success.
  - `make dev-backend-stop` now actually stops the macOS background backend instead of exiting through the tmux-only path.
  - `make dev-backend-bg` can be validated end-to-end inside a single shell invocation (`./scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh; sleep 3; curl -kI https://localhost:8443`), but this Codex runner still reaps detached children once the shell exits, so long-lived background persistence was only validated indirectly here.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no product/operator docs or wiki pages changed because this was a local dev-runtime recovery and repo relocation, not a shipped product/API change.
  - updated `Makefile` help text so it no longer incorrectly describes the macOS backend runtime as a tmux session.
- Validation:
  - `make dev-backend`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether/...`
  - `curl -kI https://localhost:3000`
  - `bash -n scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh`
  - `LABTETHER_BACKEND_PID_FILE=/tmp/labtether-test-stop.pid ./scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh --stop`
  - `./scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh; sleep 3; curl -kI https://localhost:8443`

- Result:
  - persistent terminal session create/attach now re-checks interactive policy, closing the policy bypass.
  - terminal auditing/logging paths now degrade safely when audit/log stores are unavailable instead of nil-dereferencing.
  - network/package/service/display/file agent bridges now bind responses to the expected asset connection instead of trusting `request_id` alone.
  - file downloads now fail explicitly on mid-stream agent errors instead of returning a silent partial payload after the first chunk.
  - schema setup now uses advisory locking and compatibility follow-through so the migration/hub runtime drift identified in the audit is covered by the current worktree.
  - auth bootstrap now keeps the operator in place on transient `/api/auth/me` failures and offers an explicit retry UI instead of forcing a logout/setup redirect.
  - nodes page/device-tree rendering now shows canonical type labels, includes Home Assistant hub cards correctly, avoids nested-link semantics, and expands deterministically on first render so the audit Playwright regressions no longer flap.
  - route-integrity mocks now include link-suggestion APIs, eliminating the remaining 404 during the core mobile route sweep.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - updated `docs/API.md`, `docs/SECURITY.md`, and troubleshooting wiki pages because the shipped behavior changed for persistent terminal authorization and browser auth/bootstrap recovery.
  - no `docs/ADR.md` update was needed because this wave tightened existing behavior and testing rather than introducing a new architectural policy.
- Validation:
  - `go vet ./...`
  - `go test ./...`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS=1 npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/console.spec.ts --grep 'canonical kind labels|Home Assistant detail panels|search narrows visible device cards|single history entry'`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS=1 npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/route-integrity.spec.ts e2e/services-discovery-policy.spec.ts`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS=1 npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/console.spec.ts --grep 'search narrows visible device cards'`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS=1 npm run -s test:e2e`

### Performance Audit Kickoff (2026-03-12)
- Started a fresh website + backend performance audit workstream and recorded the execution plan in `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-plan.md`.
- Captured the first provisional hotspot ranking in `notes/2026-03-12-performance-audit-findings.md`.
- Measured and verified the current baseline tooling/coverage:
  - `go vet ./...` -> pass
  - `go test ./...` -> pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` -> pass
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint` -> pass
  - `./scripts/perf/backend-hotspot-gate.sh` -> pass
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS=1 npm run -s test:e2e -- e2e/resilience.spec.ts --grep 'hidden-tab pauses fast polling and visible-tab resumes without duplicate loops'` -> pass
- Initial audit observations recorded in the plan:
  - current local console runtime is HTTPS `next dev` on `:3000`
  - direct unauthenticated `/api/status*` proxy measurements return `401`, so authenticated or mocked harnesses are required for meaningful frontend timing data
  - `scripts/perf/baseline.sh` is currently brittle under `set -e` and exited before printing useful process stats
  - `scripts/perf/profile.sh` still assumes direct, unauthenticated `http://localhost:8080/debug/pprof/` access, which no longer matches the auth-gated DEV_MODE reality
- Initial ranked hotspot shortlist now recorded:
  - `StatusContext` global polling/fanout and deep equality cost
  - `/status` and `/status/live` aggregate paths
  - route-local churn in `useLogs` and `useWebServices`
  - metrics overview full-inventory read before filtering
  - perf tooling gaps that currently block reliable sign-off
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed for this kickoff because this was audit planning and baseline capture only, not a shipped behavior change.

- Extended `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx` to report proxy vs browser-visible request overhead separately.
- Result:
  - on the current empty local dataset, the Next.js proxy is not the main request-side bottleneck on most routes
  - `services` and `devices` are currently dominated by browser-visible request overhead outside the proxy
  - `topology` spends a larger share inside the proxy, but still not enough to explain its full request-to-paint gap
  - React commit work remains too small to explain the remaining gap, so the next wave should target post-request scheduling and long-task attribution
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this wave only added internal performance instrumentation and audit evidence
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
  - interactive browser telemetry rerun across `/`, `/services`, `/topology`, and `/nodes`
### Performance Audit Post-Response Attribution (2026-03-13)
- Extended `web/console/app/contexts/StatusContext.tsx` with:
  - post-response-to-paint timing
  - uncategorized timing after subtracting measured request/parse/compare/merge cost
  - best-effort long-task attribution with `PerformanceObserver`
  - first-frame vs settled-frame hooks for follow-up calibration
- Result:
  - no overlapping long tasks were observed in the measured empty-fleet windows
  - the remaining gap is still dominated by uncategorized post-response time rather than React commit or long-task CPU
  - `topology` full refresh remains the only strong outlier in this bucket and needs a rerun once first-frame metrics are visible
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this was internal performance instrumentation and audit evidence only
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
  - interactive browser telemetry rerun across `/`, `/services`, `/topology`, and `/nodes`
### Performance Audit First-Frame Calibration (2026-03-13)
- Fixed the measurement harness for frontend perf calibration by using an explicit authenticated browser session and by attaching first-frame values to the already-stable `status.*.paint` event metadata.
- Result:
  - the earlier empty-fleet uncategorized bucket was partly inflated by the deliberate settled-after-paint measurement window
  - most routes now show a stable settle delay around `8 ms`
  - visible first-frame delay after response completion is much smaller than the raw paint number on most routes
  - `topology` full refresh still shows an unusually large settle-delay sample and should be rechecked on a non-empty dataset before optimization work starts there
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this was internal performance instrumentation, harness calibration, and audit evidence only
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
  - authenticated browser telemetry calibration pass across `/`, `/services`, `/topology`, and `/nodes`
### Performance Audit Non-Empty Dataset Rerun (2026-03-13)
- Ran the first warmed non-empty performance pass and then cleaned all temporary fixtures back out of the local runtime.
- Measurement runtime:
  - frontend remained on the existing HTTPS `next dev` server at `:3000`
  - backend was run directly from `./build/labtether` with `DEV_MODE=true`, HTTPS on `:8443`, and redirect listener on `:18080`
- Seeded temporary workload before cleanup:
  - `25` assets
  - `24` manual web services
  - `31` dependencies
  - `25` telemetry overview rows
- Key artifacts:
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260313-110359-status-baseline/summary.json`
  - `tmp/perf/runs/20260313-110844-perf-audit-wave2-nonempty/summary.json`
  - `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773360445493.json`
- Result:
  - backend aggregate still stayed small under load (`/status/aggregate` p95 `13.073 ms`, `/status/aggregate/live` p95 `11.939 ms`)
  - warmed `topology` no longer reproduced the earlier full-refresh outlier, so that route is not the next optimization target
  - `services` now shows a real route-local cost on non-empty data (`request.services_fetch` `39.3 ms`, `render.services_results` `68.1 ms`)
  - `devices` remains dominated by status acceptance / first-frame cost rather than tree derivation (`compute.device_tree` `0 ms`, but full request `175.6 ms`, full parse `56.2 ms`, full first frame `255.3 ms`)
  - warmed `dashboard` came back down to a more ordinary steady-state live path (`24.2 ms -> 41.3 ms`), so its earlier scary samples were cold-route noise
  - discovered a separate backend correctness blocker while seeding:
    - `POST /assets/heartbeat` with `group_id` returned `500 {"error":"failed to record heartbeat"}`
    - plain ungrouped heartbeats worked
- Cleanup:
  - deleted the temporary perf assets, manual services, dependencies, groups, and debug heartbeat fixtures after measurement
  - stopped the temporary direct backend session after the run
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this wave produced internal performance evidence only and intentionally cleaned up its temporary runtime fixtures
### Performance Audit Services Wave 1 (2026-03-13)
- Applied the first scoped frontend optimization pass to the measured `services` hotspot:
  - `web/console/app/components/ServiceIcon.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/ServiceCard.tsx`
  - `web/console/app/(console)/services/ServicesGroupedGrid.tsx`
- Changes:
  - replaced built-in service icon rendering from `next/image` to a plain lazy `<img>` path for the local SVG icon set
  - added explicit render containment and intrinsic sizing hints on service category sections and service cards
  - reduced repeated per-card work by caching host hue and tag splits inside the card render
  - added a custom `memo` prop comparator for service cards so unrelated parent rerenders do not fan out through the grid
- Why this wave:
  - the warmed non-empty pass showed `services` as the clearest route-local hotspot with `request.services_fetch` `39.3 ms` and `render.services_results` `68.1 ms`
  - backend status aggregation remained too small to justify starting there first
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this wave only adjusted frontend rendering strategy and did not change operator workflow, API shape, or architecture
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- Post-change measurement:
  - reran the warmed non-empty browser telemetry pass with:
    - `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773361264819.json`
    - `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773361348446.json`
  - result:
    - the UI-only services cut did not materially improve the hotspot
    - earlier baseline was `request.services_fetch` `39.3 ms`, `render.services_results` `68.1 ms`
    - post-change reruns landed at:
      - pass 1: `request.services_fetch` `77.7 ms`, `render.services_results` `106.0 ms`
      - pass 2: `request.services_fetch` `44.8 ms`, `render.services_results` `71.1 ms`
    - the second rerun was closer to baseline, but still not a convincing win
- Follow-up still required:
  - move the next services optimization wave into payload shaping or normalization work in `useWebServices` / `/api/services/web`

### Performance Audit Services Wave 2 (2026-03-13)
- Landed the first payload/backend cut for the `services` hotspot:
  - `/api/v1/services/web` now supports `detail=compact|full` and `service_id`
  - the console services page now polls the compact list payload and lazily fetches full detail for expanded cards
  - the compact list path no longer persists auto alt-URL aliases on every refresh
  - the dedicated alt-URL endpoint now synthesizes grouped auto aliases on demand so the edit modal keeps full alias visibility without paying compact-path write cost
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebServices|TestHandleWebServiceAltURLs' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- Warmed measurement after the backend/payload cut:
  - discarded one overlapping/noisy artifact: `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773362678532.json`
  - clean rerun 1: `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773362755261.json`
    - `request.services_fetch` `53.0 ms`
    - `render.services_results` `79.3 ms`
  - clean rerun 2: `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773362835212.json`
    - `request.services_fetch` `45.8 ms`
    - `render.services_results` `74.2 ms`
    - `status.live.request` `35.8 ms`
    - `status.live.paint` `54.4 ms`
    - `status.full.request` `99.5 ms`
    - `status.full.paint` `112.2 ms`
- Result:
  - the compact/full split plus compact-path alt-URL write removal was safe and correct, but it still did not beat the earlier warmed baseline of `39.3 ms / 68.1 ms`
  - the next likely lever remains `useWebServices` normalization/equality/grouping work rather than more list-field trimming
- Cleanup:
  - deleted the temporary perf assets/services/dependencies from `/tmp/perf-audit-flat-20260313114429-manifest.json`
  - verified the local runtime returned to its preexisting baseline shape (`6` assets / `6` telemetry items / `0` services)
  - stopped the temporary direct backend session after measurement

### Performance Audit Services Wave 3 (2026-03-13)
- Applied a hook-level reconciliation pass in `web/console/app/hooks/useWebServices.ts`:
  - the hook now reconciles incoming service payloads against the current state instead of fully normalizing first and then deep-comparing the whole normalized list
  - unchanged services keep object identity, nested metadata/health/alt-URL structures are reused when possible, and a fully unchanged poll keeps the existing array
  - discovery stats and grouping suggestions now compare against ref-backed current state before scheduling updates

  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s lint`
- Warmed measurement after the hook reconciliation cut:

    - `render.services_results` `76.2 ms`
  - clean rerun 2: `/tmp/frontend-pass-warm-1773363337337.json`
    - `request.services_fetch` `34.1 ms`
    - `render.services_results` `61.7 ms`
    - `status.live.request` `24.1 ms`
    - `status.live.paint` `67.3 ms`
    - `status.full.request` `70.3 ms`
    - `status.full.paint` `100.8 ms`
- Result:
  - this is the first services-focused wave with a genuinely better-than-baseline warmed rerun, but it is still not consistent enough across both reruns to mark the hotspot solved
  - the evidence now points more strongly at frontend reconciliation/derivation than backend list payload size
- Cleanup:
