### Tailscale Serve For Docker Planning (2026-03-13)
- Wrote the implementation plan in `notes/2026-03-13-tailscale-serve-docker-plan.md`.
- Key decisions captured:
  - recommend host-Tailscale + Docker-LabTether as the primary model
  - treat Tailscale Serve as the easy HTTPS path for Docker deployments
  - position Tailscale as optional but strongly recommended, not required
  - support both guided-manual mode and managed mode, instead of requiring privileged host control for the feature to exist
  - keep normal LabTether auth even when arriving over Tailscale Serve
  - avoid Funnel, `tsnet`, and Tailscale-identity-based app auth in v1
- Plan covers:
  - deployment modes
  - setup-flow UX
  - settings/runtime/env model
  - backend endpoints and state model
  - Docker/network constraints
  - audit/drift/failure behavior
  - test matrix and implementation order
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were made in this step because it was planning only; implementation will require full doc updates in the same workstream

### Strict Group Model Cleanup (2026-03-13)
- Completed the next strict group-model sweep across the active runtime, persistence layer, and console naming.
- Backend/runtime changes:
  - renamed remaining active backend type packages/imports to `groupmaintenance`, `groupprofiles`, and `groupfailover`
  - renamed live persistence/store contracts to `GroupMaintenanceStore`, `GroupProfileStore`, and `GroupAssetStore`
  - removed the remaining active runtime `siteStore` / `siteProfileStore` naming from `apiServer`
  - switched live persistence queries from `site_*` table names to `group_*` table names
  - renamed alert-route group scoping from `site_filter` to `group_filter`
  - renamed provider scope output from `site` to `group`
  - added schema migration `53` (`group_table_and_filter_canonicalization`) to rename the remaining live `site_*` tables and `alert_routes.site_filter`
- Console changes:
  - renamed residual node/topology UI components and props from `site` to `group`
  - updated topology controls copy and group-filter naming to match the canonical model
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether ./internal/persistence`

### Tailscale Agent Enrollment Hardening (2026-03-13)
- Fixed the public Next.js proxy used by the Tailscale HTTPS console origin so proxied bootstrap/enrollment responses no longer forward stale `Content-Length` metadata after decompression.
  - before the fix, `POST /api/v1/enroll` could fail with truncated HTTP/1.1 bodies or `HTTP/2 INTERNAL_ERROR` on the `ts.net` URL
- Fixed auto-enrollment hub URL selection so `/api/v1/enroll` now returns the same public connection target selected by the discovery/install flow instead of collapsing back to `https://localhost:8443`
  - this keeps post-enrollment API + WebSocket reconnects on the healthy Tailscale HTTPS/WSS origin
- Polished the Linux installer summary so token-based installs now say auto-enrollment is configured instead of incorrectly telling operators to wait for manual approval
- Fixed the Add Device agent step so it also detects success from newly issued agent tokens, not only from a net-new asset count bump
  - this unblocks reinstall/same-hostname flows where the agent is online but the modal could stay stuck on `Waiting for device to check in...`
- Fixed console asset taxonomy so agent-enrolled `type=node` assets are treated as device-tier hosts
  - before the fix, the node was present in `/api/status` but filtered out of `Devices`, dashboard device counts, and related frontend host views
- Fixed browser terminal/desktop websocket origin checks for proxied frontend/Tailscale launches
  - the hub now accepts `X-Forwarded-Host` / `Forwarded: host=` from trusted loopback/private proxy hops when validating browser websocket origins, so SSH/VNC launched from the `ts.net` console no longer fail with `request origin not allowed by Upgrader.CheckOrigin`
- Fixed desktop protocol switching so a manual choice of `VNC` no longer gets auto-snapped back to `WebRTC` by the desktop protocol preference state
  - before the fix, the desktop tab could keep reopening the recommended WebRTC path even after the operator explicitly switched transports
- Improved the Linux headless VNC fallback so Xvfb-backed sessions open a visible bootstrap shell instead of an empty black framebuffer
  - the agent now starts an `xterm` window inside the fallback display when no live X session is available
  - installer desktop prerequisites now include `xterm` alongside `x11vnc`, `Xvfb`, `xdotool`, and GStreamer so fresh Linux installs have something visible to render on the fallback desktop
- Removed the global Add Device `Beginner` / `Advanced` mode split from the live modal
  - the source picker now presents a single default setup flow
  - the agent install step keeps the common settings inline by default (file access scope, VNC/desktop prerequisites, auto-update)
  - uncommon agent overrides now live behind an inline `Advanced settings` section instead of a modal-wide mode toggle
  - connector/helper copy was updated to refer to the default setup flow rather than `Beginner` mode
- Docs/wiki/ADR:
  - updated `docs/REMOTE_ACCESS.md`, `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`, `docs/wiki/start-here/new-user-setup-guide.md`, `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`, `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`, `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-connector-infrastructure.md`, `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`, and `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
  - no additional `docs/` / `docs/wiki/` / `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed for the frontend follow-ups because they corrected existing UI detection/classification behavior without changing operator workflow
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(EnrollFullFlow|InstallScript|HandleAgentInstallScript|HandleTailscaleServeStatus)' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestCheckSameOrigin' -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(BuildX11VNCArgs|BuildXvfbArgs|StartLinuxVNCServer|StartDesktopBootstrapShell|DesktopManagerHandleDesktopStartStreamsOutputAndCleansUpOnEOF)' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestInstallScriptDesktopPrereqsInstallIncludesGStreamerAndInputTools' -count=1`
  - `make build-agent-linux`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - live websocket handshakes returned `101 Switching Protocols` for both:
    - `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net/terminal/sessions/.../stream`
    - `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net/desktop/sessions/.../stream`

### TLS Management And Public-Trust Agent Bootstrap (2026-03-13)
- Implemented the first shipped slice of both March 13 TLS plans on `main`.
- Backend/runtime changes:
  - added tracked TLS source state (`built_in`, `deployment_external`, `ui_uploaded`, `disabled`) plus default/startup source bookkeeping on `apiServer`
  - added a swappable hub certificate provider so a running TLS listener can change served certificates without recreating the listener
  - added admin-only `/settings/tls` handlers to inspect active TLS state, upload/apply an operator-managed certificate/key pair, and clear the uploaded override
  - uploaded private keys are encrypted with the existing secrets manager before persistence; cert/key material is written to `data/certs/uploaded/` for active listener use
  - startup now loads any persisted uploaded TLS override and makes it the active serving source ahead of the startup baseline
  - `/api/v1/tls/info` now reports richer TLS source and certificate metadata
  - hub candidate resolution now annotates connection targets with trust mode, preferred reason, and pinned bootstrap URL/strategy metadata
- Console/operator changes:
  - replaced the old read-only TLS status card with a full TLS Management card in Settings
  - added a new console proxy route for `/api/settings/tls`
  - added an admin restart endpoint and a `Restart Backend` action in the TLS Management card when a TLS change still needs a process restart
  - Settings and Add Device agent setup now explain why a connection target is preferred and whether it uses public trust, LabTether CA bootstrap, or plain HTTP
  - Linux installer command generation now uses the pinned bootstrap endpoint automatically for built-in-CA direct targets and standard `install.sh` for public-cert targets
- Docs/wiki/ADR:
  - updated `docs/API.md`, `docs/SECURITY.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/ADR.md`, `docs/wiki/reference/settings-reference.md`, `docs/wiki/core-workflows/add-agent-node.md`, `docs/wiki/install-upgrade/agent-install-commands-by-os.md`, and `docs/wiki/reference/api-quick-reference.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleTLS|TestResolveHubConnectionSelection|TestEnrollmentTokensIncludesHubCandidates' -count=1`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

### Real X11 Display Auth Discovery For Linux WebRTC (2026-03-14)
- Fixed the remaining live Linux WebRTC black-screen gap on `containervm-deltaserver`.
  - Root cause: the host had a real LightDM/Xorg session on `:0`, but generic X11 clients launched by the agent (`xrandr`, `gst-launch-1.0`, `xdotool`) were still missing the real display's auth cookie while `x11vnc` kept working via its own `-auth guess` logic.
  - Added real-display X11 auth discovery from Xorg/Xwayland `-auth` process arguments plus LightDM/GDM fallback paths in `internal/agentcore/x11_auth.go`.
  - `buildX11ClientEnv(...)` now auto-injects discovered `XAUTHORITY` for real displays, `startLinuxVNCServer(...)` passes it into the primary `x11vnc` launch, and Linux display enumeration now runs `xrandr --listmonitors` with the same resolved auth context.
- Live validation:
  - `curl -sk -b /tmp/labtether-backend-cookies.txt https://127.0.0.1:8443/api/v1/nodes/containervm-deltaserver/displays` now returns the real monitor list instead of `exit status 1`.
  - rebuilt with `make build-agent-linux`, forced self-update on `containervm-deltaserver`, and re-tested live WebRTC against `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net/nodes/containervm-deltaserver?panel=desktop`.
  - browser verification after redeploy showed `data-webrtc-video-tracks="1"`, `data-webrtc-audio-tracks="1"`, `readyState=4`, `1920x1080`, and advancing `currentTime` on the real `:0` desktop.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(BuildX11ClientEnvIncludesDisplayAndXauthority|BuildX11ClientEnvOmitsXauthorityWhenUnavailable|BuildX11ClientEnvDiscoversRealDisplayXauthority|StartLinuxVNCServerUsesExistingDisplayWhenAvailable|DiscoverDisplayXAuthorityFromProc|IsDisplayAvailableUsesActiveUserSession|AppendDetectedActiveDisplaysAddsSessionBackedDisplay|BuildGStreamerVideoPipeline|BuildGStreamerAudioPipeline|ResolveWebRTCDisplay|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStart|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStop|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCInput|DecodeWebRTCInputEvent|ICECandidateSendDelay)'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`

### Dev Backend Restart Hardening And Session Bootstrap Recovery (2026-03-13)
- Fixed the local macOS/Linux background backend launcher so stale pidfiles and stale backend processes no longer make a dead fresh process look healthy.
  - `scripts/dev-backend-bg.sh` now:
    - finds real running `build/labtether` processes instead of trusting only `/tmp/labtether-dev-backend.pid`,
    - stops all matching backend PIDs during restart/stop,
    - waits for the newly spawned PID to stay alive while health checks pass,
    - reports `backend exited before becoming ready` when the new process dies instead of silently succeeding against an old listener,
    - explicitly `disown`s the detached backend after `nohup`.
- Improved frontend session-bootstrap UX during backend restart windows.
  - `web/console/app/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` now treats transient `/api/auth/me` failures as backend-unavailable/restarting conditions instead of framing them as a generic auth-service outage.
  - the auth bootstrap shell now retries automatically every few seconds and tells the operator the page should recover once the hub is reachable again.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` updates were made for this slice because it fixes local runtime supervision and transient bootstrap messaging rather than changing shipped product configuration or operator workflow.

### Group Creation + Topology Alignment Regression Coverage (2026-03-13)
- Added a stateful console regression in `web/console/e2e/topology.spec.ts` that exercises the current pre-release group workflow end to end:
  - create a new group from the Groups page
  - assign mixed-source infrastructure roots (Proxmox, Docker, TrueNAS) into that group through the live `/api/assets/:id` mutation path
  - cascade the assignment to their dependency-linked child assets inside the mocked runtime state
  - verify topology location filtering, group lane grouping, and inspector labels all stay aligned with the created group
- This specifically covers the operator complaint that group creation/assignment and topology could drift apart for heterogeneous assets.
- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:
  - no `docs/`, `docs/wiki/`, or `docs/ADR.md` changes were needed because this pass added regression coverage and debugged current behavior without changing the shipped operator workflow or API contract.
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && npx playwright test e2e/topology.spec.ts --reporter=line`
  - backend-focused validation for this pass remains blocked by an unrelated current compile error in `cmd/labtether/tls_runtime.go` (`undefined: x509.Name`)

### Tailscale Serve + TLS Management Validation Landing (2026-03-13)

Validated and landed the Tailscale Serve integration, UI-managed TLS certificate upload, and agent enrollment preference work:

**Backend:**
- Tailscale Serve status API (`GET/POST /settings/tailscale/serve`) with detection, guided/managed mode, apply/disable — 8 tests.
- TLS settings API (`GET/POST/DELETE /settings/tls`) with cert upload, validation, encrypted key storage, hot-reload, rollback.
- Restart API (`POST /settings/restart`) for cert changes needing process restart.
- Hub connection resolver auto-prefers healthy Tailscale HTTPS for agent enrollment — 5 resolver tests.
- Runtime settings: `remote_access.mode` (serve/manual/off), `remote_access.tailscale_serve_target`.
- Fixed 5 `TestDiscoverEndpoint*` tests that broke when real Tailscale on the dev machine caused the resolver to return a live ts.net URL — added `disableTailscaleResolutionForTest(t)` to isolate each test from host Tailscale state.

**Frontend:**
- TailscaleServeCard in Settings: mode selection, target config, managed actions, status, recommendation banner.
- TLSManagementCard in Settings: cert upload, active cert details, rollback, restart warning.
- `useTailscaleServeStatus` hook, Next.js API proxy routes for tailscale/tls/restart.

**Architecture decisions:**
- Tailscale Serve as reverse proxy (no cert extraction) — ADR-071.
- Built-in CA remains universal fallback; agent trust is additive.
- Add Device auto-prefers ts.net URL when Tailscale Serve is healthy.

**Docs/wiki:**
- Added Tailscale HTTPS and TLS upload troubleshooting sections to `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/quick-diagnostics.md`.
- All other docs (API, SECURITY, ARCHITECTURE, ADR, REMOTE_ACCESS, wiki references) were already updated in prior slices.

**Validation:**
- `go vet ./...` — clean
- `go test ./cmd/labtether/... -count=1` — all pass (including 22 focused Tailscale/TLS/restart/resolver tests + fixed discover endpoint tests)
- `go test ./internal/... -count=1` — all pass
- `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit` — clean

### Desktop Remote View Coverage Sweep (2026-03-14)

Validated the full desktop remote-view feature sweep for WebRTC and VNC and fixed the regressions the pass exposed:

- restored VNC monitor selection persistence in session requests/reconnects while still relying on the Linux agent to sanitize non-X11 monitor labels before launch
- fixed automatic WebRTC fallback/recovery so quality-driven protocol switches do not become sticky as if the operator explicitly selected them
- fixed the desktop Playwright VNC mock so React Strict Mode throwaway mounts no longer consume credential prompt plans and hide reconnect regressions
- added explicit WebRTC drag-and-drop file-transfer coverage through the `file-transfer` data channel alongside the existing clipboard/recording checks

Coverage now exercised in `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`:

- WebRTC:
  - stitched multi-monitor rendering
  - browser recording
  - clipboard pull/push
  - relay fallback to VNC and automatic recovery back to WebRTC
  - fullscreen toolbar controls
  - drag-and-drop file upload over the WebRTC file-transfer channel
- VNC:
  - selected monitor persistence on initial connect and reconnect
  - focus, keyboard capture, toolbar shortcuts, and fallback cursor behavior
  - reconnect recovery / exhaustion / try-again flow
  - audio unavailable -> reconnect recovery flow
  - username+password prompt retry flow
  - password-only auto-submit, reconnect reset, and repeat-prompt fallback

- Docs/wiki/ADR rationale:

### Live Desktop Clipboard/File Verification + Linux Upload Home Fix (2026-03-14)

- Finished the real-host desktop feature sweep on `containervm-deltaserver` and fixed the last live failures affecting clipboard/file workflows.

    - added an authenticated Next proxy for `/api/v1/nodes/<asset>/clipboard/{get,set}` so the public console origin can reach the hub clipboard bridge instead of returning `404`.
  - `internal/agentcore/clipboard_linux.go`
    - made Linux clipboard reads/writes inherit the active desktop session environment (`DISPLAY`/`XAUTHORITY` for X11, `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`/`XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` for Wayland), which fixed live clipboard sync on the real desktop host after installing `xclip`.
  - `internal/agentcore/file_handler.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/file_paths.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/file_handler_test.go`
    - fixed Linux file-home resolution so `~` no longer blindly maps to an unwritable service home like `/root`; the agent now prefers a writable home (active desktop/session user when needed) and falls back to a dedicated writable staging home only if necessary.
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useDesktopTabState.ts`
  - `web/console/app/components/RemoteViewerShell.tsx`
  - `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`
    - moved desktop drag-drop default target to `~/Downloads`, kept WebRTC uploads on the `file-transfer` data channel, and made VNC drag-drop use the existing files API fallback instead of incorrectly trying to call the WebRTC uploader.

### Live Linux Black-Screen Fix For WebRTC + VNC (2026-03-14)

- Root-caused the shared black-screen regression on `containervm-deltaserver` to the real X11 session being alive but blanked/locked under LightDM/XFCE while the agent still defaulted X11 attach paths without explicitly waking the display.
  - host evidence showed a real XFCE session for `michael` on `:0`, a LightDM greeter on `:1`, `light-locker` running in the user session, healthy WebRTC signaling, and both WebRTC/VNC rendering a live but all-black framebuffer.
- Fixed the Linux agent X11 attach path:
  - `internal/agentcore/x11_display.go`
    - added `preferredX11Display()` so desktop features prefer the detected logged-in X11 session before falling back to scanned displays / `:0`
    - added `wakeX11Display()` to send a best-effort `xset` + `xdotool` wake before desktop capture attaches
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/display_list_linux.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_session.go`
    - wired the preferred-display/wake path into VNC, WebRTC, and monitor enumeration
    - updated session scoring so logged-in `Class=user` desktops outrank `Class=greeter`
  - `internal/securityruntime/command.go`
    - allowlisted `xset` for the new wake path
- Added regression coverage:
  - `internal/agentcore/x11_display_test.go`
  - `internal/agentcore/desktop_session_test.go`
  - updated `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect_test.go`
  - updated `internal/agentcore/desktop_vnc_test.go`
  - updated `internal/securityruntime/command_test.go`
- Rebuilt + redeployed:
  - `make build-agent-linux`
  - `POST /api/v1/agents/containervm-deltaserver/settings/update-agent {"force":true}`
  - live update result: `forced update applied to sha256:c1285b067be6`
- Live verification after redeploy:
  - WebRTC now renders the real LightDM login screen instead of a black frame.
  - VNC now renders the real LightDM login screen instead of a black frame.
  - Live keyboard input works through both protocols (verified by typing into the LightDM password field over WebRTC and VNC).
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore`
  - `go test ./internal/securityruntime`
  - live browser checks against `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net/nodes/containervm-deltaserver?panel=desktop`

### Live WebRTC Keyboard Stability Fix On Linux (2026-03-14)

- Fixed the remaining Linux WebRTC keyboard instability after the black-screen repair.
  - root cause:
    - the X11 injector was treating browser DOM `keyCode` values as raw X11 keysyms, which is wrong for normal browser typing and especially brittle for lock/modifier keys
    - the X11 path also used `xdotool keydown --clearmodifiers ...` on every key press, which fought normal modifier state
    - the browser viewer had no pressed-key cleanup when focus changed, so keys could remain logically held after blur/disconnect
  - code:
    - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler.go`
      - added DOM-code / DOM-key to X11 keysym mapping for Linux WebRTC input
      - removed blanket `--clearmodifiers` from X11 keydown injection
    - `web/console/app/components/WebRTCViewer.tsx`
      - track pressed keys client-side
      - ignore repeated keydown spam for already-held keys
      - release held keys on blur / visibility loss
    - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler_test.go`
      - added focused coverage for DOM-code preference and keysym fallback
- Rebuilt + redeployed:
  - `make build-agent-linux`
  - `POST /api/v1/agents/containervm-deltaserver/settings/update-agent {"force":true}`
  - live update result: `forced update applied to sha256:3464585b301f`
- Live verification:
  - WebRTC typing into the live Linux desktop now behaves normally again.
  - explicit Caps Lock toggle test on the visible remote text field produced `ABCdef`, confirming Caps Lock toggled on and back off instead of getting stranded.
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(DecodeWebRTCInputEventAcceptsFallbackPayload|X11KeyArgumentPrefersDOMCodeMapping|X11KeyArgumentFallsBackToKeysymCode|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCInput|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStartSetsManagedDisplayX11Env)'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - live browser verification against `https://michaelmacbook-pro.taildaf69.ts.net/nodes/containervm-deltaserver?panel=desktop`

### WebRTC Quality Badge Stabilization For Static Screens (2026-03-14)

- Investigated operator feedback that the WebRTC toolbar badge kept flipping between `poor` and `fair` even when the client was on a wired connection.
  - live browser `getStats()` samples on the real desktop session showed the stream holding steady at `29-30 fps`, `0` packet loss, and advancing video time while encoded bitrate hovered around `569-790 kbps`
  - that proved the session was not transport-failing; the badge was misclassifying a mostly static login screen because the existing heuristic treated `<800 kbps` as `poor` by itself
- Fixed the console-side classifier in `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/desktopTabMetrics.ts`.
  - `poor` is now reserved for low-FPS / high-latency sessions, or for severe bitrate starvation paired with another degraded signal
  - low bitrate by itself now settles at `fair`, which keeps static-but-healthy WebRTC sessions from looking unstable or tripping the auto-fallback logic incorrectly
- Updated operator docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/webrtc.md`
- Validation:
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - live browser stats inspection against the active `containervm-deltaserver` WebRTC session confirmed the original oscillation came from bitrate-only classification, not FPS/loss instability

### Desktop Native-Scaling And WebRTC Quality Parity Fixes (2026-03-14)

- Followed up on operator feedback that VNC looked sharper than WebRTC and that VNC `1:1` mode stopped remote click-drag from working reliably.
- Root cause:
  - the WebRTC path was still ignoring the selected desktop quality preset entirely; the browser/hub preserved `quality`, but `webrtc.start` never forwarded it to the agent so Linux WebRTC capture stayed on the same default encoder bitrate for `Low`, `Med`, and `High`
  - VNC native mode enabled noVNC `dragViewport`, so click-drag gestures panned the local viewport instead of reaching the remote desktop
  - WebRTC native scaling still behaved differently from VNC because the viewer never switched into the same scrollable `1:1` container mode
- Fixed:
  - `internal/agentmgr/message.go`
    - added `quality` to `WebRTCSessionData`
  - `cmd/labtether/webrtc_stream_handlers.go`
    - now forwards the selected stream `quality` query into the agent `webrtc.start` payload
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_handler.go`
    - added Linux WebRTC bitrate mapping for desktop quality presets (`low=1 Mbps`, `medium=5 Mbps`, `high=20 Mbps`) and passed the chosen bitrate into the GStreamer video pipeline
  - `web/console/app/components/VNCViewer.tsx`
    - stopped enabling noVNC `dragViewport` in native mode so remote drag gestures keep working in `1:1`
  - `web/console/app/components/WebRTCViewer.tsx`
    - aligned WebRTC native scaling with the VNC viewer by switching `1:1` into the same scrollable native container semantics instead of leaving WebRTC on fit-style sizing rules
  - `web/console/e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts`
    - added regressions for WebRTC native `1:1` container behavior and for VNC native mode keeping remote drag input enabled
### Group Membership Heartbeat Persistence Fix (2026-03-14)

- Fixed grouped devices falling back to `Unassigned` after routine heartbeats or connector refreshes.
- Root cause:
  - asset heartbeat upserts treated a blank incoming `group_id` as an explicit clear, even though most recurring agent and connector heartbeat paths omit `group_id`
  - that let background refresh traffic undo manual group assignment changes made from the console
- Fixed:
  - `internal/persistence/postgres_assets_groups.go`
    - preserve the existing stored `group_id` on heartbeat upsert when the incoming heartbeat omits/blankes `group_id`
  - `internal/persistence/memory.go`
    - mirror the same preserve-existing-group behavior in the in-memory asset store
  - `cmd/labtether/assets_groups_api_test.go`
    - added an API-level regression covering manual group assignment followed by a later heartbeat without `group_id`
  - `internal/persistence/memory_assets_test.go`
    - added a store-level regression covering grouped assets surviving later heartbeats without `group_id`
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/reference/environment-variables.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./internal/persistence -run 'TestMemoryAssetStore.*' -count=1`
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'Test(UpdateAssetNamePersistsAcrossHeartbeats|ManualGroupAssignmentPersistsAcrossHeartbeatsWithoutGroupID|GroupsCreateListAndAssignAsset|UpdateAssetNameAndGroup)' -count=1`

- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/webrtc.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
- Validation:
  - `go test ./cmd/labtether -run 'TestHandleWebRTCStreamForwardsBrowserSignalsAndSendsStop'`
  - `go test ./internal/agentcore -run 'Test(ResolveWebRTCDisplay|ResolveWebRTCDisplayWaylandIgnoresX11Selection|WebRTCVideoBitrateForQuality|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCStartSetsManagedDisplayX11Env|WebRTCManagerHandleWebRTCInputQueuesEvent)'`
  - `cd web/console && npm run -s tsc -- --noEmit`
  - `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npx playwright test e2e/desktop-remote-view.spec.ts --grep 'desktop (WebRTC native scaling uses a scrollable 1:1 container|VNC native scaling keeps remote drag input enabled)'`
### Linux WebRTC Software Encoder Preference Fix (2026-03-14)

- Investigated operator feedback that Linux WebRTC still felt markedly laggier than VNC even after black-screen, keyboard, quality-selector, and Tailscale Serve fixes.
- Root cause:
  - software-only Linux hosts advertising `VideoEncoders=["vp8","x264"]` were still selecting `vp8enc` because the runtime priority order preferred VP8 ahead of software `x264`
  - that meant real-desktop WebRTC on machines like `containervm-deltaserver` was screen-capturing with `ximagesrc` and then software-encoding VP8 instead of using `x264enc tune=zerolatency speed-preset=ultrafast`
  - for interactive desktop control, that software VP8 path was materially worse than the existing x264 path and could make WebRTC feel laggy/horrible even when the node itself was healthy
- Fixed:
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect.go`
    - reordered software encoder preference so Linux now picks `x264` before `vp8` when no hardware H.264 encoder is available
  - `internal/agentcore/webrtc_detect_test.go`
    - added regression coverage for the new priority order and for `bestVideoEncoder` preferring `x264` over `vp8` on software-only capability sets
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/webrtc.md`
  - `docs/wiki/troubleshooting/desktop-connection-failures.md`
### Linux Desktop Protocol Preference Update (2026-03-14)

- Updated console-side Linux desktop protocol ordering so agent-backed Linux X11 sessions now prefer `vnc` ahead of `webrtc` by default.
- Rationale:
  - recent live debugging showed that, although Linux WebRTC now works, the current X11 WebRTC path is still more sensitive to encoder/capture/runtime conditions than the established VNC path
  - operators wanted the default Linux desktop connect flow to favor the more predictable path instead of dropping into WebRTC first
  - Wayland remains the exception: when VNC is only a fallback desktop path, the console still keeps WebRTC first for the real session
- Fixed:
  - `web/console/app/(console)/nodes/[id]/useDesktopProtocolState.ts`
    - Linux X11/headful sessions now order protocols as `vnc -> webrtc`
    - Linux Wayland sessions with no real-desktop VNC support still order as `webrtc -> vnc`
    - protocol labels now mark only the first-choice item as `(Recommended)`
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `docs/wiki/remote-desktop/protocol-selection.md`

### Manual Device & Protocol Management — Post-MVP Items Verified Complete (2026-03-20)

Confirmed four follow-up items from the Manual Device & Protocol Management workstream are fully implemented and closed out:

- **RSA 4096 fallback** (`hub_identity.go`): SSH hub key push falls back to RSA 4096 when Ed25519 is rejected by an older server.
- **SSH hub key rotation endpoint** (`hub_key_rotation.go`): `POST /settings/ssh-hub-key/rotate` with audit trail.
- **Protocol config migration** (migration 69 in `postgres_schema_migrations.go`): existing `asset_terminal_configs` and `asset_desktop_configs` rows unified into `asset_protocol_configs`.
- **Periodic connection health checks** (`protocol_health_checker.go`): `runProtocolHealthChecker` background goroutine performs scheduled test-connection polls for protocol configs.

Two items remain as genuine future features and are kept open in TODO.md:
- IPMI SOL (Serial Over LAN) protocol support.
- Serial port/console protocol support (for BMCs, network switches, terminal servers).

### Windows Agent Phase 1 Complete (2026-03-21)

All 15 tasks of the Windows agent plan are shipped. Summary of delivered work:

**Build targets**
- `make build-agent-windows-amd64` and `make build-agent-windows-arm64` produce `labtether-agent.exe` binaries for both architectures.
- Cross-compilation works from macOS/Linux CI via `GOOS=windows`.

**Windows Service wrapper**
- `internal/agentplatform/windows/service_windows.go`: SCM integration using `golang.org/x/sys/windows/svc`.
- Reports `SERVICE_RUNNING`, `SERVICE_STOP_PENDING`, etc. to SCM; handles pause/continue signals.
- Fallback to console mode when not running under SCM.

**Platform-specific paths**
- Runtime data stored under `%ProgramData%\LabTether\` with `%LOCALAPPDATA%\LabTether` fallback.
- Log, config, and token files follow Windows conventions; no path collisions with macOS/Linux layouts.

**Enhanced Windows platform provider**
- OS metadata: Windows edition, build number, architecture.
- Capability flags: SCM services, Event Log, Task Scheduler, Hyper-V, WinGet/Choco.
- Tailscale IP detection via Windows-specific interface enumeration.

**Tailscale metadata deduplication refactor**
- Extracted shared Tailscale metadata logic from per-platform providers into `internal/agentplatform/tailscale_metadata.go`.
- All four platform providers (linux, darwin, windows, freebsd) now call the shared path; per-platform duplication eliminated.

**Self-update (rename-dance)**
- Windows does not allow overwriting a running executable.
- Self-update renames the current binary to `labtether-agent.exe.old`, writes the new binary, then restarts.
- Stale `.old` files are cleaned up on the next successful start.

**7 agent backends**
- `services_windows.go`: SCM service enumeration and control via `sc.exe`.
- `packages_windows.go`: WinGet primary, Chocolatey fallback; install/uninstall/upgrade operations.
- `logs_windows.go`: Windows Event Log ingestion (System, Application, Security channels); structured forwarding to hub log store.
- `tasks_windows.go`: Task Scheduler enumeration via `schtasks /query`; trigger and last-run metadata.
- `network_windows.go`: Network interface and route enumeration via `netsh interface` and `netsh route`.
- `updates_windows.go`: Windows Update status and history via PSWindowsUpdate / WUA COM; reboot-pending detection.
- `hyperv_windows.go`: Hyper-V VM enumeration and basic state reporting via `Get-VM` PowerShell cmdlet.

**MSI packaging**
- WiX toolset project under `deploy/windows/` produces a signed MSI installer.
- MSI registers the Windows Service, sets `%ProgramData%\LabTether\` directories, and places uninstall entries in Add/Remove Programs.

**Install/uninstall scripts**
- `deploy/windows/install.ps1`: downloads correct architecture binary, installs MSI or direct binary, registers and starts the service.
- `deploy/windows/uninstall.ps1`: stops and removes the service, removes program data (with optional `-KeepData` flag).

### Remote View Tab Scaffolding Complete (2026-03-21)

All 14 tasks of the Remote View tab plan are shipped. Summary of delivered work:

**Frontend — types and state**

- `app/components/RemoteViewNewTab.tsx` — new-tab landing page with recent bookmarks grid and quick-connect form.
- `app/components/RemoteViewSession.tsx` — session wrapper component: renders the toolbar and viewer area, owns per-tab connection lifecycle.

**Frontend — Next.js API route**
- `app/api/remote-view/bookmarks/[[...path]]/route.ts` — thin proxy forwarding bookmark CRUD to the hub API.

**Backend — bookmarks store and API**
- `internal/hubapi/remoteview/` package: `types.go`, `store.go` (Postgres CRUD for `remote_view_bookmarks`), `handlers.go` (collection + item HTTP handlers), `deps.go`.
- Migration 70: `remote_view_bookmarks` table with `asset_id`, `protocol`, `host`, `port`, `label`, `credential_ref`, `options` (JSONB), timestamps.
- Routes registered under `POST/GET /api/v1/remote-view/bookmarks` and `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/remote-view/bookmarks/:id`.

**Validation**
- TypeScript: no errors (`npx tsc --noEmit`).
- Go vet: clean (`go vet ./...`).

### E2E + Security Audit Stabilization (2026-03-22)

Completed a full follow-through on the previously failing frontend audit slices and the security gate drift.

**Frontend audit repairs**
- Stabilized existing-server Playwright runs by serializing workers in `web/console/playwright.config.ts` when `PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1`.
- Updated stale console/settings/accessibility/topology e2e expectations to match the current console IA and detail panels.
- Restored topology audit coverage by teaching the e2e harness to infer Docker/runtime relationships consistently across `/api/topology` and `/api/edges`.
- Aligned topology tree/canvas code paths so stored topology connections can participate in containment rendering instead of only the separate edges feed.

**Security gate**
- Refreshed `security/gosec_allowlist.tsv` to the current repo layout and current scanner output so `make security-gosec` passes again after the `cmd/labtether` to `internal/hubapi` decomposition and related file moves.

**Validation**
- `cd web/console && npm run lint`
- `cd web/console && PLAYWRIGHT_USE_EXISTING_SERVER=1 npx playwright test e2e/settings-user-access.spec.ts e2e/accessibility.spec.ts e2e/topology.spec.ts e2e/console.spec.ts`
- `make security-gosec`
